### UC-22.8.27 · SOC 2 A1 — Capacity Planning Signals for In-Scope Production Services
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Performance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Observability
- **Regulations:** SOC 2
- **Value:** A1 addresses availability commitments. Forecasting CPU, memory, queue depth, or connection pool utilization against committed headroom demonstrates proactive capacity management—not reactive firefighting after outages.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Observability Cloud / Infrastructure monitoring, or `metrics` index
- **Data Sources:** `index=metrics` `metric_name IN ("cpu.util","mem.util","queue.depth")` `entity=prod_payments_*` earliest=-90d
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=metrics metric_name="cpu.util" entity=prod_payments_* earliest=-90d
| timechart span=1d avg(_value) as avg_cpu
| predict avg_cpu as forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=14 period=7
| where forecast>85
| table _time, avg_cpu, forecast
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Set threshold to contractual headroom; (2) join with change calendar for planned traffic spikes; (3) feed capacity committee slide deck; (4) document assumptions in system description; (5) alert infra 30 days before sustained breach predicted.
- **Visualization:** Line chart (avg_cpu + forecast), Single value (days until threshold), Table.
- **CIM Models:** Performance
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t avg(Performance.cpu_load_percent) as agg_value from datamodel=Performance.CPU by Performance.host span=1d | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
- **References:** [CIM: Performance](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Performance)

---

### UC-22.8.28 · SOC 2 A1 — Disaster Recovery Test Execution and Evidence Timestamps
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Resilience
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOC 2
- **Value:** A1 expects recovery commitments to be tested. Indexing DR runbook executions, RTO/RPO measurements, and failover test results with immutable timestamps proves tests occurred at the required frequency and outcomes were recorded.
- **App/TA:** HTTP Event Collector, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
- **Data Sources:** `index=resilience` `sourcetype="dr:test_result"` (test_id, region_pair, rto_min, rpo_min, pass_fail, executed_by, _time); optional `snow:cmdb_ci` for scope
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=resilience sourcetype="dr:test_result" earliest=-400d
| bin _time span=90d as quarter
| stats latest(pass_fail) as outcome, latest(rto_min) as rto, latest(rpo_min) as rpo by quarter, region_pair
| eval ok=if(outcome="pass",1,0)
| eventstats sum(ok) as passes, count as quarters by region_pair
| where passes<4
| table region_pair, quarter, outcome, rto, rpo
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require one test per in-scope region_pair per quarter minimum—tune to policy; (2) integrate orchestration tool webhooks; (3) store raw logs in WORM; (4) failed `pass_fail` opens severity-2 problem; (5) map to customer SLA annex.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (tests), Table (gaps), Single value (region_pairs behind).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)

---

### UC-22.8.29 · SOC 2 C1 — Confidential Information Disposal and Secure Destruction Evidence
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOC 2
- **Value:** C1 addresses confidential information. Tracking destruction certificates, crypto-shredding events, and DLP “secure delete” workflows demonstrates media and logical disposal met policy when assets retired or contracts ended.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect, vendor APIs (Iron Mountain, secure erase tools) via HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance` `sourcetype="disposal:cert"` (asset_id, method, cert_number, destroyed_ts, witness); `index=endpoint` BitLocker or secure erase tool logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="disposal:cert" earliest=-365d
| eval destroyed=strptime(destroyed_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| join type=left asset_id [
    search index=cmdb sourcetype="cmdb:asset" earliest=-400d
    | fields asset_id, retired_date, classification
    | eval retired=strptime(retired_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
  ]
| where retired>relative_time(destroyed,"+30d@d")
| table asset_id, classification, retired, destroyed, cert_number
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Flip logic per policy: flag retirements without cert within 30 days; (2) restrict `classification=confidential` scope; (3) integrate ITAD vendor feed; (4) legal hold exceptions via `legal_hold_assets.csv`; (5) annual sample for auditors with cert PDF links (out of band).
- **Visualization:** Table (late disposal), Single value (assets past SLA), Bar by classification.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---

### UC-22.8.30 · SOC 2 PI1 — Processing Completeness Validation Across Multi-Stage Pipelines
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Data Quality
- **Splunk Pillar:** Observability
- **Regulations:** SOC 2
- **Value:** PI1 expects processing to be complete, valid, accurate, timely, and authorized. Reconciling record counts and checksums between ingest, transform, and load stages catches silent drops that would undermine financial or subscriber reporting integrity.
- **App/TA:** HTTP Event Collector from orchestration (Airflow, dbt, mainframe bridge)
- **Data Sources:** `index=dataops` `sourcetype="pipeline:reconcile"` (pipeline_id, stage, batch_id, row_count, checksum, _time)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=dataops sourcetype="pipeline:reconcile" earliest=-7d
| stats latest(eval(if(stage="ingest",row_count,null()))) as ingest_rows,
        latest(eval(if(stage="load",row_count,null()))) as load_rows,
        latest(eval(if(stage="ingest",checksum,null()))) as c_in,
        latest(eval(if(stage="load",checksum,null()))) as c_out
  by batch_id, pipeline_id
| eval row_match=if(ingest_rows=load_rows,1,0), chk_match=if(c_in=c_out,1,0)
| where row_match=0 OR chk_match=0
| table pipeline_id, batch_id, ingest_rows, load_rows, c_in, c_out
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Emit reconcile events at stage boundaries; (2) allow known shrinkage rules via `pipeline_tolerance.csv`; (3) alert data owners on mismatch; (4) retain batch_id for auditor replay; (5) map `pipeline_id` to PI1 subservice in documentation.
- **Visualization:** Table (mismatches), Line chart (ingest vs load over time), Single value (failed batches).
- **CIM Models:** N/A


- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
### 22.9 Compliance Trending

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686) for GRC database pulls.

**Data Sources:** `index=compliance` / `index=grc` (framework scores, control tests, audit findings), `sourcetype="compliance:framework_score"`, `sourcetype="grc:audit_finding"`, `sourcetype="compliance:control_test"`, `` `notable` `` (compliance-tagged incidents), `sourcetype="dlp:violation"` / `sourcetype="policy:enforcement"`, `sourcetype="qualys:policy"`, `sourcetype="nessus:sc:compliance"`, CIM (Authentication, Change) where policy events are normalized.


---

### UC-22.9.1 · Compliance Posture Score Trending
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Value:** Rolling quarterly posture scores across NIST CSF, ISO 27001, and SOC 2 show whether investments in controls are improving measurable outcomes—not just checkbox activity—so executives and regulators see trajectory, not a one-time snapshot.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (GRC export), custom HTTP poller, or indexed CSV from Archer/ServiceNow GRC
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance` OR `index=grc` `sourcetype IN ("compliance:framework_score","grc:posture")` — fields `framework`, `overall_score`, `_time`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=compliance OR index=grc sourcetype IN ("compliance:framework_score","grc:posture") earliest=-730d
| eval fw=case(
    like(framework,"%NIST%"),"NIST_CSF",
    like(framework,"%ISO%"),"ISO27001",
    like(framework,"%SOC%"),"SOC2",
    1=1,framework)
| where isnum(overall_score)
| bin _time span=90d
| stats avg(overall_score) as avg_score by _time, fw
| stats avg(avg_score) as portfolio_score by _time
| timechart span=90d avg(portfolio_score) as avg_portfolio_score
| trendline sma3(avg_portfolio_score) as sma_posture
| predict avg_portfolio_score as posture_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2 period=4
```
```spl
index=compliance OR index=grc sourcetype IN ("compliance:framework_score","grc:posture") earliest=-730d
| eval fw=case(like(framework,"%NIST%"),"NIST_CSF",like(framework,"%ISO%"),"ISO27001",like(framework,"%SOC%"),"SOC2",1=1,framework)
| timechart span=3mon avg(overall_score) as avg_score by fw
| trendline sma2(NIST_CSF) as roll_NIST_CSF sma2(ISO27001) as roll_ISO27001 sma2(SOC2) as roll_SOC2
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Land GRC or continuous-control scores into `compliance` or `grc` with stable `framework` labels and numeric `overall_score` (0–100); (2) align calendar quarters to your audit cycle (`span=90d` vs fiscal); (3) schedule weekly and snapshot results to a summary index for year-over-year evidence; (4) validate `predict` against low-volume series—disable or widen `period` if confidence bands explode; (5) pair the portfolio panel with the by-framework panel for board-ready trending.
- **Visualization:** Line or area chart (portfolio score, `sma_posture`, `posture_forecast`), multiseries line (scores by `fw`), overlay confidence bands from `predict`.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---

### UC-22.9.2 · Audit Finding Closure Rate Trending
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Value:** Trending open versus closed audit findings over ninety days makes backlog and closure velocity visible before external audits, while mean time to remediate highlights whether remediation playbooks and ownership are working.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk DB Connect for GRC findings, or custom `grc:audit_finding` HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` OR `index=compliance` `sourcetype="grc:audit_finding"` — `finding_id`, `status`, `closed_time` or `remediated_time`, `_time`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc OR index=compliance sourcetype="grc:audit_finding" earliest=-90d
| eval is_closed=if(match(status,"(?i)closed|resolved|verified"),1,0)
| eval finding_key=coalesce(finding_id,uuid,ticket_id,number)
| timechart span=1d dc(eval(if(is_closed=0,finding_key,null()))) as open_findings
    dc(eval(if(is_closed=1,finding_key,null()))) as distinct_closed_entities
| trendline sma7(open_findings) as open_smoothed
| predict open_findings as open_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=14
```
```spl
index=grc OR index=compliance sourcetype="grc:audit_finding" earliest=-90d
| eval is_closed=if(match(status,"(?i)closed|resolved|verified"),1,0)
| where is_closed=1
| eval mttr_days=if(isnotnull(closed_time),(closed_time-_time)/86400,
    if(isnotnull(remediated_time),(remediated_time-_time)/86400,null()))
| where isnum(mttr_days) AND mttr_days>=0 AND mttr_days<365
| timechart span=1w avg(mttr_days) as mean_mttr_days perc95(mttr_days) as p95_mttr_days
| trendline sma4(mean_mttr_days) as mttr_trend
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(mean_mttr_days) as prev_w_mttr
| eval wow_mttr_pct=if(isnotnull(prev_w_mttr) AND prev_w_mttr>0,round(100*(mean_mttr_days-prev_w_mttr)/prev_w_mttr,1),null())
| predict mean_mttr_days as mttr_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure each finding emits at least one event with stable `finding_key` and transition events or daily snapshots for `status`; (2) normalize `closed_time` to epoch seconds; (3) if only snapshot data exists, switch `dc()` panels to `latest()` by key in a summary index; (4) alert when `open_forecast` rises week over week or when `mean_mttr_days` exceeds your SLA; (5) export MTTR trend for audit workpapers.
- **Visualization:** Dual-axis line (open vs closed counts), area chart (`open_smoothed`), line chart (`mean_mttr_days`, `mttr_trend`, `mttr_forecast`), single value (`wow_mttr_pct`).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---

### UC-22.9.3 · Control Effectiveness Trending
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Value:** A ninety-day pass ratio by control domain exposes domains where tests are failing more often or trending down—so you prioritize control owners, evidence collection, and automation before a failed external assessment.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Tenable (Splunkbase 4060) for scan-based control checks, DB Connect for ITGC spreadsheets, or `compliance:control_test` automation feeds
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance` `sourcetype IN ("compliance:control_test","nessus:sc:compliance","qualys:policy")` — `control_domain`, `test_result` or `status`, `_time`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=compliance sourcetype IN ("compliance:control_test","nessus:sc:compliance","qualys:policy") earliest=-90d
| eval outcome=if(match(coalesce(test_result,status,result),"(?i)pass|passed|success|green"),1,0)
| eval domain=coalesce(control_domain,pluginFamily,"UNCLASSIFIED")
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count as tests, sum(outcome) as passes by _time, domain
| eval pass_ratio=if(tests>0, round(100*passes/tests, 2), null())
| sort domain, _time
| streamstats window=4 avg(pass_ratio) as eff_trend by domain
| table _time, domain, tests, pass_ratio, eff_trend
```
```spl
index=compliance sourcetype IN ("compliance:control_test","nessus:sc:compliance") earliest=-90d
| eval outcome=if(match(coalesce(test_result,status),"(?i)pass|passed|success"),1,0)
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count as tests, sum(outcome) as passes by _time
| eval org_pass_ratio=if(tests>0, round(100*passes/tests,2), null())
| sort _time
| trendline sma3(org_pass_ratio) as ratio_trend
| eventstats avg(org_pass_ratio) as portfolio_mean
| eval gap=round(org_pass_ratio-portfolio_mean,2)
| predict org_pass_ratio as ratio_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2 period=6
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map vendor fields (`pluginFamily`, Qualys title) to internal `control_domain` via lookup `control_domain_map.csv`; (2) dedupe repeated tests per asset/control daily to avoid skew; (3) review domains where `eff_*` slopes negative for four consecutive buckets; (4) tune `span=7d` to match test frequency; (5) store weekly CSV exports for assessors.
- **Visualization:** Multiseries line or area (pass_ratio by domain), heatmap (domain x week), line (`org_pass_ratio`, `ratio_trend`, `ratio_forecast`).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities (when Tenable/Qualys maps to CIM); otherwise N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest span=7d | sort - agg_value
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Tenable](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

---

### UC-22.9.4 · Regulatory Incident Response Time Trending
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1048, T1562
- **Value:** Mean time to resolve compliance-tagged incidents by quarter proves that regulatory and policy breaches are handled with discipline—supporting supervisory expectations and internal KPIs beyond generic IT MTTR.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263)
- **Data Sources:** `` `notable` `` — filter with `tag`/`category`/`rule_name` for compliance/regulatory work; fields `closed_time`, `_time`
- **SPL:**
```spl
`notable` earliest=-730d (tag="compliance" OR category="*compliance*" OR rule_name="*regulatory*" OR like(rule_name,"%compliance%"))
| eval mttr_sec=if(isnotnull(closed_time) AND closed_time>_time, closed_time-_time, null())
| where isnotnull(mttr_sec)
| timechart span=90d avg(mttr_sec) as avg_mttr_sec perc95(mttr_sec) as p95_mttr_sec
| eval avg_mttr_h=avg_mttr_sec/3600
| trendline sma2(avg_mttr_sec) as mttr_trend
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(avg_mttr_sec) as prev_q_mttr
| eval vs_prev_q_pct=if(isnotnull(prev_q_mttr) AND prev_q_mttr>0,round(100*(avg_mttr_sec-prev_q_mttr)/prev_q_mttr,1),null())
| predict avg_mttr_sec as mttr_forecast algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2 period=4
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define a consistent ES tag or naming convention for regulatory notables; (2) confirm `closed_time` population for closed incidents (`| fieldsummary closed_time`); (3) exclude false positives with a lookup of excluded `rule_name` values; (4) compare quarterly MTTR to IT-wide MTTR in a separate panel for context; (5) document scope (which jurisdictions or policies) in the dashboard subtitle.
- **Visualization:** Line chart (`avg_mttr_h` or `avg_mttr_sec`, `mttr_trend`, `mttr_forecast`), column chart (`p95_mttr_sec` by quarter), single value (`vs_prev_q_pct`).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---

### UC-22.9.5 · Policy Violation Volume Trending
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078, T1562
- **Value:** Quarterly violation counts by category—data handling, access, encryption—show whether policy drift, training gaps, or technical misconfigurations are improving or worsening, which steers awareness campaigns and control investments.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for Windows (Splunkbase 742), Enterprise Security data models
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance` OR `index=sec` `sourcetype IN ("dlp:violation","policy:enforcement","ms:o365:management")` — `violation_category`, `PolicyName`, `Workload`; optional `sourcetype="qualys:*"` / `sourcetype="nessus:*"` for encryption posture drift correlated to policy
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=compliance OR index=sec sourcetype IN ("dlp:violation","policy:enforcement") earliest=-730d
| eval cat=case(
    match(_raw,"(?i)encrypt|crypto|tls|bitlocker"),"encryption",
    match(_raw,"(?i)access|privileged|login|permission"),"access",
    match(_raw,"(?i)dlp|classification|label"),"data_handling",
    1=1,coalesce(violation_category,"other"))
| bin _time span=90d
| stats count by _time, cat
| sort cat, _time
| streamstats window=2 avg(count) as cat_trend by cat
| eventstats sum(count) as quarter_total by _time
| sort _time, cat
| table _time, cat, count, cat_trend, quarter_total
```
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload IN ("Dlp","Security") earliest=-730d
| eval cat=case(
    like(Operation,"%encryption%") OR like(SensitiveInfoType,"%encryption%"),"encryption",
    match(Operation,"(?i)login|access|role"),"access",
    1=1,"data_handling")
| bin _time span=90d
| stats count by _time, cat
| sort cat, _time
| streamstats window=2 avg(count) as o365_trend by cat
| table _time, cat, count, o365_trend
```
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload IN ("Dlp","Security") earliest=-730d
| timechart span=90d count as o365_violation_total
| trendline sma2(o365_violation_total) as o365_trend
| predict o365_violation_total as o365_fcst algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2
```
```spl
index=vm OR index=compliance sourcetype IN ("nessus:sc:*","qualys:host") earliest=-730d
| eval enc_gap=if(match(_raw,"(?i)ssl|tls|cipher|encrypt") AND match(_raw,"(?i)fail|weak|deprecated"),1,0)
| timechart span=90d sum(enc_gap) as encryption_policy_gaps
| trendline sma3(encryption_policy_gaps) as enc_trend
| predict encryption_policy_gaps as enc_fcst algorithm=LLP future_timespan=2
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize DLP and CASB events into shared `violation_category` values via `case` or lookup; (2) align `span=90d` to fiscal or regulatory reporting quarters; (3) correlate spikes with change tickets (`index=itsm`) using `join` on `_time` windows; (4) use the total-violations panel (`o365_violation_total`) when category columns are too sparse for `predict`; (5) retain quarterly PDF snapshots for compliance archives.
- **Visualization:** Stacked column or area (counts by `cat` over time), line (`o365_violation_total`, `o365_trend`, `o365_fcst`), line (`encryption_policy_gaps`, `enc_trend`), heatmap (category x quarter).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055), [Splunk Add-on for Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

---

### UC-22.9.6 · Compliance Trending — SOC 2 Control Test Pass Rate vs Prior Quarter Baseline
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1562
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Multiple
- **Value:** Framework-specific trending shows whether SOC 2 Type II control tests are improving or eroding compared to your own prior quarter baseline—not just a static “percent pass today”—so leadership sees trajectory for trust services commitments.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (GRC export), or `index=compliance` HEC from Archer/ServiceNow GRC
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance` `sourcetype="grc:control_test"` (framework, control_id, test_result, test_date, period_label)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="grc:control_test" framework="SOC2" earliest=-400d
| eval pass=if(lower(test_result) IN ("pass","effective","no_exception"),1,0)
| eval test_epoch=strptime(test_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| bin test_epoch span=90d as period
| stats avg(pass) as pass_rate by period
| sort period
| streamstats window=1 global=f latest(pass_rate) as prev_pass_rate by framework
| eval delta_pp=round(100*(pass_rate-prev_pass_rate),2)
| table period, pass_rate, prev_pass_rate, delta_pp
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize `test_result` vocabulary via lookup; (2) align `period` to fiscal calendar if needed; (3) exclude duplicate tester reruns via `dedup control_id test_date`; (4) annotate dashboard with SOC subservice scope; (5) export quarterly screenshot for audit committee pack.
- **Visualization:** Line chart (pass_rate), Column chart (delta_pp), Single value (latest pass_rate).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---

### UC-22.9.7 · Compliance Trending — ISO 27001 Statement of Applicability Control Exception Burn-Down
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Multiple
- **Value:** ISO trending should reflect SoA reality. Tracking open control exceptions (compensating controls, overdue remediation) by Annex A theme shows whether your ISO program is actually burning down risk or accumulating deferred work ahead of certification surveillance.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect, `index=grc` HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype="iso:soa_exception"` (annex_control, exception_id, opened_date, target_close_date, status, framework="ISO27001")
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="iso:soa_exception" status!="closed" earliest=-730d
| eval tgt=strptime(target_close_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>tgt,1,0)
| timechart span=30d sum(overdue) as overdue_exceptions by annex_control
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map `annex_control` to SoA rows; (2) require `target_close_date` on all non-closed exceptions; (3) monthly ISO steering committee view; (4) correlate with internal audit themes; (5) keep framework tag explicit for multi-framework tenants.
- **Visualization:** Stacked area (overdue_exceptions), Table (current backlog), Single value (total overdue).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---

### UC-22.9.8 · Compliance Trending — Auditor Evidence Pack Generation Volume and Deficiency Rate
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Multiple
- **Value:** External audits consume evidence packs. Trending how many packs were produced, how often they were rejected or re-requested for gaps, and median assembly time shows whether the organization is maturing its evidence factory—not scrambling at year-end.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), document system webhooks
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype="audit:evidence_pack"` (engagement_id, pack_id, created_ts, sent_ts, deficiency_flag, reviewer_comment_len)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="audit:evidence_pack" earliest=-730d
| eval created=strptime(created_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), sent=strptime(sent_ts,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval assemble_h=round((sent-created)/3600,1)
| bin sent span=90d as quarter
| stats count as packs, sum(deficiency_flag) as deficient, median(assemble_h) as med_assemble_h by quarter
| eval deficiency_rate=round(100*deficient/packs,1)
| sort quarter
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define `deficiency_flag` from auditor workflow state; (2) redact `reviewer_comment_len` only—never store full comments with PII; (3) integrate with secure file transfer metrics optionally; (4) target downward trend on `deficiency_rate`; (5) map `engagement_id` to regulator vs commercial audit classes.
- **Visualization:** Combo chart (packs + deficiency_rate), Line (med_assemble_h), Table by quarter.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)

---

### UC-22.9.9 · Compliance Trending — Regulatory Change Feed Impact Score on In-Scope Controls
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Multiple
- **Value:** Laws and supervisory guidance change continuously. Scoring incoming regulatory change items by how many in-scope controls they touch—and trending that score—helps compliance prioritize policy updates, training, and control amendments before effective dates.
- **App/TA:** HTTP Event Collector from regulatory intelligence vendor or manual curator tool
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype="reg:change_item"` (item_id, jurisdiction, effective_date, summary_tokens, impacted_control_ids, impact_score, _time)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="reg:change_item" earliest=-730d
| eval eff=strptime(effective_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where eff>now() OR eff>relative_time(now(),"-90d@d")
| timechart span=30d sum(impact_score) as rolling_impact by jurisdiction
| trendline sma3(rolling_impact) as impact_trend
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain mapping table from `impacted_control_ids` to owners; (2) human validates `impact_score`—do not fully automate legal severity; (3) integrate effective-date countdown alerts; (4) deduplicate vendor spam with `item_id`; (5) cross-link to policy management tickets.
- **Visualization:** Line chart (rolling_impact + impact_trend), Table (upcoming items), Single value (next-90d sum).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

---

### UC-22.9.10 · Compliance Trending — Weighted Compliance Posture Composite and Driver Attribution
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Multiple
- **Value:** A single posture score communicates status to executives while driver attribution explains why it moved. Weighting domains (identity, data, resilience, third party) by materiality yields a composite that trends over time and complements—not replaces—framework-specific panels.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect, `index=compliance` normalized score feeds
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance` `sourcetype="posture:domain_score"` (domain, score_0_100, weight, as_of_date)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
| eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| bin d span=7d as week
| stats avg(score_0_100) as avg_score, latest(weight) as weight by week, domain
| eval contrib=avg_score*weight
| stats sum(contrib) as composite by week
| sort week
| streamstats window=2 global=f first(composite) as prev_composite
| eval wow_delta=round(composite-prev_composite,2)
| join type=left week [
    search index=compliance sourcetype="posture:domain_score" earliest=-400d
    | eval d=strptime(as_of_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
    | bin d span=7d as week
    | stats avg(score_0_100) as s by week, domain
    | sort 0 week -s
    | dedup week
    | rename domain as top_driver_domain, s as top_driver_score
    | fields week, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
  ]
| table week, composite, wow_delta, top_driver_domain, top_driver_score
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize `weight` to sum to 1 per week in ETL if not already; (2) simplify driver logic in production with explicit `driver_domain` from upstream model instead of `dedup` shortcut; (3) governance committee approves weights annually; (4) document formula in methodology PDF; (5) never use this score as sole regulatory metric.
- **Visualization:** Line chart (composite), Overlay (wow_delta), Single value (latest composite), Table (driver).
- **CIM Models:** N/A


- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)
### 22.11 PCI DSS v4.0

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Stream (1809), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** `index=netfw` / `index=firewall` (Palo Alto `pan:traffic`, `pan:system`, `pan:config`, Cisco ASA), `index=windows` (`WinEventLog:Security`, `WinEventLog:System`), `index=linux` / `index=os` (auditd, syslog), `index=vm` (Tenable `tenable:sc:vuln`, Qualys `qualys:host`, `qualys:vm`), `index=edr` (CrowdStrike `crowdstrike:hosts`, `crowdstrike:detection`), `index=stream` (TLS metadata), `index=aws` (`aws:cloudtrail`, ELB/ALB access logs), CIM-accelerated Authentication, Network_Traffic, Malware, Vulnerabilities, Certificates, Change.


---

### UC-22.11.1 · Scheduled Firewall Rule Review Evidence for CDE NSCs (PCI DSS Req 1.1.6, 1.2.8)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Produces a repeatable inventory of firewall policy changes touching the cardholder data environment so assessors can see that network security controls are formally reviewed at least every six months.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `sourcetype IN ("pan:config","pan:system")` — commits, admin user, rule object names; optional `sourcetype="cisco:asa"` configuration events
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype IN ("pan:config","pan:system") earliest=-185d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)commit|policy|rule|security|nat")
| lookup pci_cde_device_groups.csv device_group OUTPUT in_cde
| where in_cde="true"
| rex field=_raw "(?i)admin@?(?<fw_admin>\\S+)"
| bin _time span=30d
| stats values(fw_admin) as reviewers, dc(_raw) as change_events by _time, host
| sort - _time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `pci_cde_device_groups.csv` mapping Panorama device groups or firewall hostnames to `in_cde`. (2) Align the search window to your documented six-month review cycle. (3) Export the stats table monthly and attach the export ID to the GRC ticket. (4) Exclude template pushes that do not alter enforced policy using a `commit_id` dedupe if available.
- **Visualization:** Column chart (change_events by month), table (reviewers per device group), single value (distinct review months with activity).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.dest span=30d | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

---

### UC-22.11.2 · NSC Configuration Change Correlation to Change Tickets (PCI DSS Req 1.1.2, 1.2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Links live firewall configuration changes to approved change records, evidencing that modifications to network security controls follow change management and are traceable for PCI interviews.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:config"`; `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:change_request"` or `sourcetype="jira:changelog"` with change number in description
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:config" earliest=-7d
| eval chg_hint=if(match(_raw,"CHG\\d{7}"),replace(_raw,".*?(CHG\\d{7}).*","\\1"),null())
| where isnotnull(chg_hint)
| join type=left max=1 chg_hint [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-14d
    | rename number as chg_hint
    | fields chg_hint, state, start_date, end_date, assigned_to
  ]
| eval missing_ticket=if(isnull(state),"no_matching_chg",state)
| table _time, host, chg_hint, missing_ticket, assigned_to
| sort _time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require engineers to embed `CHG#######` in commit descriptions or Panorama notes. (2) Tune `rex` for your ITSM number format. (3) Alert on `missing_ticket="no_matching_chg"` for CDE-tagged hosts. (4) Retain joined results in a summary index for QSA sampling.
- **Visualization:** Table (exceptions first), pie (matched vs missing), timeline (_time vs change state).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

---

### UC-22.11.3 · CDE Boundary Traffic — Unexpected Corporate-to-Payment Flows (PCI DSS Req 1.2.3, 1.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Highlights allowed sessions from general corporate segments into payment subnets so segmentation and NSC rules can be validated between assessments, not only during annual penetration tests.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` — `src`, `dest`, `src_zone`, `dest_zone`, `action`, `rule`; lookups `pci_corp_zones.csv`, `pci_payment_subnets.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" action=allowed earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_corp_zones.csv zone AS src_zone OUTPUT corp_zone
| lookup pci_payment_subnets.csv cidr AS dest OUTPUT payment_tier
| where corp_zone="true" AND payment_tier="card_processing"
| stats count, values(dest_port) as ports, values(app) as apps by src, dest, rule
| where count>5
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate CIDR lookups from network diagrams approved for PCI. (2) Whitelist jump-host rules via `where NOT match(rule,"(?i)bastion|jump")`. (3) Feed hits to the segmentation validation runbook. (4) Map findings to Req 11 segmentation testing follow-up.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (src_zone x dest_zone counts), table (top flows), Sankey optional for zone paths.
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.11.4 · Denied Inbound Attempts to Payment Application Ports (PCI DSS Req 1.2.7, 1.3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces blocked connection attempts targeting card-processing listeners so you can prove NSCs are actively enforced and tune rules before attackers find a misconfiguration.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` with `action` in (deny, drop, reset-client)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| where action IN ("deny","drop","reset-client","reset-server")
| lookup pci_payment_listener_ports.csv dest_port OUTPUT sensitive_listener
| where sensitive_listener="true"
| stats count by src, dest, dest_port, app, rule
| where count>=50
| sort - count
| head 100
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define listener ports (database, queue, admin API) in the lookup. (2) Enrich `src` with threat intel in ES if available. (3) Set threshold based on baseline scanning noise. (4) Archive weekly top-100 for operational review evidence.
- **Visualization:** Map or geomap (if GeoIP enabled), bar chart (dest_port), table (top sources).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port, All_Traffic.app | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.11.5 · DMZ Originated Sessions Hitting CDE Internal Segments (PCI DSS Req 1.3.4, 1.3.7)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Detects allowed paths from DMZ assets into internal CDE networks that violate one-way DMZ design, reducing the chance a compromised web tier pivots into systems that store or process account data.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"`; asset lookup `pci_dmz_assets.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" action=allowed earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_dmz_assets.csv ip AS src OUTPUT in_dmz
| lookup pci_cde_internal.csv ip AS dest OUTPUT in_cde_internal
| where in_dmz="true" AND in_cde_internal="true"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by _time, src, dest, app, dest_port
| eventstats perc95(count) as p95 globally
| where count>p95 AND p95>0
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Refresh DMZ and CDE internal inventories weekly from CMDB. (2) Validate expected admin SSH paths. (3) Create ES notable for sustained spikes. (4) Document compensating controls if architecture requires exceptions.
- **Visualization:** Line chart (hourly counts), bubble chart (src size by count), table (spike windows).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.app, All_Traffic.dest_port span=1h | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.11.6 · Wireless Client Pools Reaching CDE Hosts (PCI DSS Req 1.2.3, 2.2.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Shows successful flows from wireless DHCP scopes to CDE destinations, supporting evidence that wireless is segmented from cardholder environments or that compensating detective controls exist.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Stream (1809)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"`; optional `index=stream` for decrypted metadata tagging
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" action=allowed earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_wlan_dhcp_scopes.csv subnet AS src OUTPUT wlan_ssid
| lookup pci_cde_subnets.csv subnet AS dest OUTPUT in_cde
| where isnotnull(wlan_ssid) AND in_cde="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen, count by wlan_ssid, src, dest, app
| eval session_span_sec=last_seen-first_seen
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Export DHCP superscopes per SSID from IPAM. (2) Exclude corporate VPN concentrator egress if pooled with WLAN. (3) Pair with 802.1X authentication logs when available. (4) Report zero-result runs as quarterly attestation attachment.
- **Visualization:** Table (sessions), pie (by wlan_ssid), timeline (first_seen/last_seen).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.app | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.11.7 · Outbound Service Allow-List Violations from CDE Servers (PCI DSS Req 1.2.1, 1.2.6)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Compares observed egress from in-scope servers against the documented approved service list, proving that outbound traffic from the CDE is limited to what the standard permits.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"`; `lookup pci_cde_server_subnets.csv`, `lookup pci_approved_egress.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" action=allowed earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_cde_server_subnets.csv subnet AS src OUTPUT cde_server
| where cde_server="true"
| eval svc=lower(concat(dest_port,"#",app))
| lookup pci_approved_egress.csv svc OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved="false"
| stats count by src, dest, dest_port, app, dest_zone
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build the egress lookup from the PCI network standard appendix. (2) Refresh when new SaaS dependencies are onboarded. (3) Weekly ops review of the stats output. (4) Store signed CSV with each policy version.
- **Visualization:** Treemap (by app), bar (dest_port), table (detail).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port, All_Traffic.app | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.11.8 · Default and Vendor Account Authentications on In-Scope Systems (PCI DSS Req 2.2.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Detects interactive use of factory or vendor default accounts on systems in the CDE, directly supporting the requirement to change all vendor defaults before production use.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security"` EventCode 4624/4625; `index=linux` `sourcetype="linux:secure"` or `auditd`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4624,4625))
    OR (index=linux (sourcetype="linux:secure" OR sourcetype="linux:auditd")) earliest=-24h
| eval acct=coalesce(TargetUserName, acct, audit_user)
| lookup pci_default_accounts.csv account AS acct OUTPUT is_default
| lookup pci_in_scope_hosts.csv host OUTPUT in_scope
| where is_default="true" AND in_scope="true"
| stats count by host, acct, EventCode, action
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Seed the default-account lookup from CIS benchmarks and vendor lists. (2) Map `host` to PCI inventory. (3) Alert on any successful 4624 for default accounts. (4) Track remediation tickets to closure.
- **Visualization:** Table (hits), single value (distinct hosts), column chart (by account name).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest, Authentication.action | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.9 · Configuration Drift vs CIS Hardening Benchmark on Windows CDE Members (PCI DSS Req 2.2.1, 2.2.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Aggregates configuration assessment findings against a documented hardening standard so you can show continuous alignment with industry-accepted secure configurations for in-scope Windows systems.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:compliance","qualys:policy")` — `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`, `compliance_status`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:compliance","qualys:policy") earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_windows_cde_assets.csv fqdn AS host_fqdn OUTPUT cde_member
| where cde_member="true"
| eval fail=if(match(compliance_status,"(?i)failed|error|critical"),1,0)
| stats sum(fail) as failed_checks, count as total_checks by host_fqdn
| eval fail_pct=if(total_checks>0, round(100*failed_checks/total_checks,2), null())
| where fail_pct>5
| sort - fail_pct
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map scanner asset names to PCI inventory FQDNs. (2) Scope policies to CIS Windows Server benchmarks used in your standard. (3) Track `fail_pct` trend weekly. (4) Attach exports to secure configuration evidence repository.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (fail_pct by host), heatmap (plugin families), single value (mean fail_pct across CDE).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Tenable](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [Splunk Add-on for Qualys](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2964), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

---

### UC-22.11.10 · Listening Services and Daemons on Linux Payment Middleware (PCI DSS Req 2.2.4, 2.2.5)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces unexpected listening ports reported by endpoint telemetry on payment middleware hosts, supporting the requirement to enable only necessary services and to document business justification for each.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=edr` `sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts"` Network listening metadata if forwarded; or `index=linux` scripted `netstat`/`ss` HEC sourcetype `linux:network_listeners`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=linux sourcetype="linux:network_listeners" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_payment_middleware.csv host OUTPUT tier
| where tier="middleware"
| lookup pci_allowed_listeners.csv proto_port OUTPUT allowed
| where isnull(allowed)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen, values(process) as processes by host, proto_port
| sort host, proto_port
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Schedule a scripted input to emit `proto_port` and owning `process` every hour. (2) Maintain allowed listener lookup per role. (3) Auto-open tickets for new tuples. (4) Review quarterly for stale rules.
- **Visualization:** Table (host, proto_port, processes), mosaic or heatmap (host x port), single value (new listeners per day).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5994)

### UC-22.11.11 · System Component Inventory Reconciliation — New In-Scope Hosts (PCI DSS Req 2.1.1, 2.1.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Flags hosts observed in CDE traffic or authentication logs that are missing from the authoritative inventory so the documented list of system components stays accurate between annual reviews.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"`; `lookup pci_component_inventory.csv` (host, in_scope, owner)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_cde_subnets.csv subnet AS dest OUTPUT in_cde
| where in_cde="true"
| stats dc(dest) as uniq_dest by dest
| rename dest as ip
| lookup pci_component_inventory.csv ip OUTPUT inventory_id
| where isnull(inventory_id)
| stats count by ip
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize internal IP inventory to one row per address. (2) Deduplicate NAT pools using a second lookup. (3) Route new IPs to asset management within five business days. (4) Snapshot weekly results for PCI evidence.
- **Visualization:** Table (unknown IPs), single value (count new vs prior week), map if subnets geolocated internally.
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(All_Traffic.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.11.12 · Removal of Vendor Default SNMP and Community Strings (PCI DSS Req 2.2.2, 2.2.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces SNMP or other management protocols still using well-known community names on devices that touch the CDE, supporting timely removal of insecure defaults from network infrastructure.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netops` `sourcetype IN ("snmp:trap","syslog:cisco")` or `index=linux` `sourcetype="linux:auditd"` for snmpd edits
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netops sourcetype IN ("snmp:trap","syslog:cisco") earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_network_in_scope.csv device OUTPUT in_pci_scope
| where in_pci_scope="true"
| where match(_raw,"(?i)public|private|community\\s*=\\s*public")
| stats count by device, _raw
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Forward SNMP-related syslog to a dedicated index. (2) Tune for encrypted SNMPv3 success noise. (3) Correlate with CMDB management IP. (4) Track remediation in change system.
- **Visualization:** Table (device, sample _raw), column chart (hits by device model if extracted).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)

### UC-22.11.13 · Security Parameter Drift on In-Scope Routers from Gold Config Hash (PCI DSS Req 2.2.1, 2.2.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Compares periodic configuration snapshots to an approved gold hash per device so unintended drift in security-relevant parameters is detected between formal audits.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance` `sourcetype="compliance:network_config_digest"` — `device`, `config_hash`, `baseline_hash`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=compliance sourcetype="compliance:network_config_digest" earliest=-48h
| lookup pci_perimeter_devices.csv device OUTPUT pci_tier
| where pci_tier IN ("cde_border","dmz")
| eval drift=if(config_hash!=baseline_hash,1,0)
| where drift=1
| stats latest(_time) as last_drift by device, config_hash, baseline_hash
| sort device
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Nightly job pushes SHA-256 of normalized running-config. (2) Store `baseline_hash` only after CAB approval. (3) Auto-create incident on drift. (4) Retain hash history 13 months for PCI logging retention alignment.
- **Visualization:** Table (drifted devices), sparkline if hashes trend over time in summary index, single value (drift count).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

---

### UC-22.11.14 · Primary Account Number Pattern Discovery in Application Indexes (PCI DSS Req 3.3.1, 3.4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Identifies log fields or raw text that resemble payment card numbers in non-tokenization indexes so PAN can be purged or masked before it violates protection of stored account data requirements.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=app` OR `index=web` high-volume text; restrict to CDE-related `sourcetype` list
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=app OR index=web) sourcetype IN ("payment:api:json","java:log4j","nginx:access") earliest=-24h
| regex _raw="\\b(?:4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?|5[1-5][0-9]{14}|3[47][0-9]{13}|6(?:011|5[0-9]{2})[0-9]{12})\\b"
| eval pan_like=replace(_raw,".*?(\\b(?:4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?|5[1-5][0-9]{14})\\b).*","\\1")
| eval pan_redacted=replace(pan_like,"(\\d{6})\\d+(\\d{4})","\\1******\\2")
| stats count by index, sourcetype, host
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Run in a restricted role; mask outputs in dashboards. (2) Validate with Luhn in a external script if false positives are high. (3) Route hits to DLP response. (4) Never store full PAN in summary indexes—store counts only. (5) Document sampling methodology for QSA.
- **Visualization:** Bar (by sourcetype), table (host counts only), single value (total matches week over week).
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Web)

---

### UC-22.11.15 · Key Management Operations from HSM and KMS Audit Trails (PCI DSS Req 3.5.1, 3.6.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Centralizes HSM partition and cloud KMS administrative events so cryptographic key custodians can prove dual-control and logging for keys that protect stored account data.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), custom HSM syslog parser
- **Data Sources:** `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` (`eventName` CreateKey, DisableKey, ScheduleKeyDeletion); `index=hsm` `sourcetype="thales:luna:audit"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" eventSource="kms.amazonaws.com" earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=hsm sourcetype="thales:luna:audit" earliest=-24h)
| eval op=coalesce(eventName, hsm_command, operation)
| where match(op,"(?i)delete|disable|import|unwrap|clone|partition")
| stats count by user, op, src_ip, host
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map IAM user/role to human custodian IDs. (2) Require ticket ID in CloudTrail session context where possible. (3) Alert on `ScheduleKeyDeletion` without CAB record. (4) Retain 12+ months in compliant storage tier.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (operations), table (custodian, op), pie (op mix).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.src, Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.16 · Data Retention Job Failures for Cardholder Data Stores (PCI DSS Req 3.2.1, 3.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks scheduled purge and truncation jobs for databases that held PAN so retention limits are enforced operationally, not only on paper.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=app` `sourcetype="sqlagent:job"` or `index=linux` cron logs, `sourcetype="batch:retention_job"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=app sourcetype="batch:retention_job" earliest=-7d
| where match(job_name,"(?i)pci|pan|retention|purge|truncate")
| eval success=if(match(status,"(?i)success|completed"),1,0)
| stats latest(_time) as last_run, sum(success) as ok_runs, count as total_runs by host, job_name
| eval ok_rate=if(total_runs>0, round(100*ok_runs/total_runs,2), null())
| where ok_rate<100 OR total_runs=0
| sort job_name
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Instrument retention scripts to emit JSON lines with `job_name` and `status`. (2) Align job list with data retention standard table. (3) Page on two consecutive failures. (4) Attach weekly success rate to compliance dashboard.
- **Visualization:** Single value (aggregate ok_rate), table (failing jobs), column chart (runs per day).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)

### UC-22.11.17 · Cryptographic Erasure Verification After Decommission (PCI DSS Req 3.2.1, 3.5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Correlates disk wipe or crypto-shred completion events with asset retirement tickets so media and VMs that held account data cannot return to production without verified erasure evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=sec` `sourcetype="disk:wipe:log"`; `index=aws` `eventName` DeleteVolume; `index=itsm` retirement tickets
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:cmdb_ci" earliest=-30d
| where match(short_description,"(?i)decommission|retire") AND match(short_description,"(?i)CDE|PCI|payment")
| rename sys_id as asset_id, number as chg_ticket
| join type=left max=0 asset_id [
    search index=sec sourcetype="disk:wipe:log" earliest=-30d
    | stats max(wipe_status) as wipe_ok by asset_id
  ]
| where isnull(wipe_ok) OR wipe_ok!="VERIFIED"
| table chg_ticket, asset_id, short_description, wipe_ok
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure wipe tooling emits `asset_id` matching CMDB. (2) Define `wipe_status` enumeration with `VERIFIED`. (3) Escalate open rows weekly. (4) Store closure export for PCI media controls.
- **Visualization:** Table (exceptions), gauge (% verified), timeline (ticket opened vs wipe event).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [Splunk Add-on for Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

---

### UC-22.11.18 · PAN Masking Validation in Point-of-Sale and Web Receipt Logs (PCI DSS Req 3.3.3, 3.4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Confirms that customer-visible channels emit only first six and last four digits, catching accidental logging of full PAN in receipt or audit trails.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=pos` `sourcetype="pos:receipt"`; `index=web` `sourcetype="access_combined"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=pos sourcetype="pos:receipt") OR (index=web sourcetype="access_combined") earliest=-24h
| where match(_raw,"\\b[0-9]{13,19}\\b")
| where NOT match(_raw,"\\b[0-9]{6}[\\*Xx-]{3,}[0-9]{4}\\b")
| stats count by index, sourcetype, host, uri
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune URI exclusions for internal test environments. (2) Pair with secure SDLC defect workflow. (3) Redact `_raw` in dashboards. (4) Monthly compliance review of zero vs non-zero results.
- **Visualization:** Table (top URIs), single value (violations), trendline week over week.
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Web)

### UC-22.11.19 · Sensitive Authentication Data (SAD) in Auth Broker Logs (PCI DSS Req 3.3.1, 3.2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Detects track data, CVC, or PIN block patterns in authentication or tokenization service logs so SAD is not retained after authorization, per stored data prohibitions.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Stream (1809)
- **Data Sources:** `index=app` `sourcetype="oauth:gateway:json"`; `index=stream` TLS payloads only where policy allows mirroring metadata
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=app sourcetype="oauth:gateway:json" earliest=-24h
| where match(_raw,"(?i)track[12]|;\\d{15,}|%B\\d{15,}|pin\\s*block|cvc2?\\s*[:=]\\s*\\d{3,4}")
| rex max_match=0 field=_raw "(?i)(?<redacted>track[12][^\\n]{0,40})"
| stats count by host, endpoint, ip
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Restrict search to security admin role. (2) Integrate with DLP blocking on the log path. (3) Mandatory incident for any positive hit. (4) Legal/privacy review of regex scope annually.
- **Visualization:** Table (aggregated only), single value (incident count), map of source IP if needed.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.20 · Hash and Truncation Method Changes on Tokenization Database (PCI DSS Req 3.3.1, 3.5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Alerts when DDL or configuration changes alter hashing algorithms or truncation rules applied to account data, preserving integrity of non-reversible rendering approaches approved by the assessor.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk DB Connect (if DB audit ingested)
- **Data Sources:** `index=db` `sourcetype="mssql:audit"` or `oracle:audit` — `object_name`, `statement`, `action`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=db sourcetype IN ("mssql:audit","oracle:audit") earliest=-7d
| where match(statement,"(?i)alter\\s+table|create\\s+index|hashbytes|truncate\\s+column|token")
| lookup pci_token_db_objects.csv object_name OUTPUT in_token_vault
| where in_token_vault="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen, values(statement) as stmts by object_name, db_user
| sort last_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable fine-grained audit on token vault schema. (2) Map `db_user` to break-glass accounts only. (3) Require pre-approved DDL ticket in correlation. (4) Retain statements hashed if raw SQL is sensitive.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (DDL events), table (object_name, db_user), heatmap (hour of day).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

### UC-22.11.21 · Cryptographic Key Rotation and Custodian Acknowledgement Trail (PCI DSS Req 3.6.1, 3.6.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks scheduled key rotation events and custodian acknowledgements so the organization can demonstrate keys are replaced on policy cadence and custodianship is documented.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail"` KMS `RotateKey`; `index=sec` `sourcetype="vault:audit"`; `index=itsm` CAB tasks
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" eventName="RotateKey" earliest=-365d)
 OR (index=sec sourcetype="vault:audit" operation="rotate" earliest=-365d)
| eval key_id=coalesce(requestParameters.keyId, key_id)
| bin _time span=90d
| stats count as rotations by _time, key_id
| lookup pci_key_rotation_policy.csv key_id OUTPUT expected_days
| eval expected_rotations=365/expected_days
| where rotations<1
| table _time, key_id, rotations, expected_rotations
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate `expected_days` per key from the cryptographic procedures. (2) Align `span` to rotation policy (annual vs quarterly). (3) Append manual HSM rotation logs via HEC with same `key_id`. (4) Archive quarterly attestation PDFs externally.
- **Visualization:** Line chart (rotations per bucket), table (keys under-rotated), single value (count of keys needing action).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)

---

### UC-22.11.22 · TLS 1.2 Minimum Version Violations on Payment APIs (PCI DSS Req 4.1.1, 4.2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces handshakes negotiated below TLS 1.2 on in-scope listeners so weak transmission protections are remediated before external vulnerability scanning deadlines.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Stream (1809), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876)
- **Data Sources:** `index=stream` `sourcetype="stream:tls"`; `index=aws` `sourcetype="aws:elb:accesslogs"` with `ssl_protocol`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=stream sourcetype="stream:tls" earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=aws sourcetype="aws:elb:accesslogs" earliest=-24h)
| eval tls_ver=coalesce(tls_version, ssl_protocol)
| lookup pci_payment_endpoints.csv dest_ip OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true"
| where tls_ver IN ("TLSv1","TLSv1.0","TLSv1.1","SSLv3") OR match(tls_ver,"(?i)^ssl")
| stats count by tls_ver, dest_ip, src_ip, server_name
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize TLS version strings per data source. (2) Map `dest_ip` to payment VLANs. (3) Exclude lab subnets via lookup. (4) Feed to vulnerability management for ASV correlation.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (by tls_ver), table (top client IPs), geomap optional.
- **CIM Models:** Certificates
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Certificates.All_Certificates by All_Certificates.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Certificates](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Certificates)

### UC-22.11.23 · Weak Cipher Suites Offered by Internal TLS Terminators (PCI DSS Req 4.1.1, 4.2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Highlights servers advertising RC4, 3DES, or NULL ciphers on CDE-facing services, supporting the strong cryptography requirement for transmissions of PAN and connection data.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Stream (1809), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964)
- **Data Sources:** `index=stream` `stream:tls` (`cipher_suite`); `index=vm` `qualys:host` SSL QIDs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=stream sourcetype="stream:tls" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_tls_terminators.csv server_ip OUTPUT pci_listener
| where pci_listener="true"
| where match(cipher_suite,"(?i)RC4|3DES|NULL|EXPORT|anon")
| stats count by server_ip, cipher_suite, tls_version
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest Stream metadata from SPAN or tap on DMZ switches. (2) Refresh listener inventory when load balancers change. (3) Pair with Qualys SSL scan for corroboration. (4) Track remediation SLA in ITSM.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (server_ip x cipher_suite), pie (weak vs strong), table detail.
- **CIM Models:** Certificates
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Certificates.All_Certificates by All_Certificates.ssl_subject_common_name, All_Certificates.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809), [Splunk Add-on for Qualys](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2964), [CIM: Certificates](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Certificates)
