### UC-22.11.24 · Certificate Expiry Risk for Public-Facing Payment Hostnames (PCI DSS Req 4.2.1, 4.2.1.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Operations
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Counts days-to-expiry for certificates protecting CHD transmission paths so renewals happen before outages or downgrade attacks during renewal gaps.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` `tenable:sc:vuln` plugin 51192; `qualys:host` cert fields if mapped
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype="tenable:sc:vuln" earliest=-24h
| where pluginID=51192 OR match(plugin_name,"(?i)certificate.*expir")
| lookup pci_public_payment_hosts.csv dns_name AS dns OUTPUT in_business_scope
| where in_business_scope="true"
| eval days_left=round((notAfter_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| where days_left<45 AND days_left>0
| stats min(days_left) as soonest_expiry by dns, ip
| sort soonest_expiry
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map Tenable `dns` to customer-facing FQDNs. (2) Ingest `notAfter_epoch` via TA field alias. (3) Page at 30/14/7 days thresholds. (4) Document emergency renewal procedure linkage.
- **Visualization:** Table (dns, soonest_expiry), column chart (certs per expiry week), single value (min days_left).
- **CIM Models:** Certificates
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Certificates.All_Certificates by All_Certificates.ssl_subject_common_name, All_Certificates.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Tenable](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [Splunk Add-on for Qualys](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2964), [CIM: Certificates](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Certificates)

### UC-22.11.25 · Cleartext PAN Indicators in HTTP Headers or Query Strings (PCI DSS Req 4.1.1, 3.4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Detects PAN-like sequences in unencrypted HTTP request lines so cardholder data is not sent in cleartext over open networks, satisfying encryption and rendering unreadable expectations.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Stream (1809), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=stream` `sourcetype="stream:http"`; `index=web` `sourcetype="access_combined"` where scheme=http
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=stream sourcetype="stream:http" earliest=-24h uri=*)
 OR (index=web sourcetype="access_combined" earliest=-24h)
| where match(coalesce(uri,url,_raw), "http://")
| where match(coalesce(uri,url,_raw), "\\b(?:4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?)\\b")
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, coalesce(uri,url) as request_line
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Block at WAF based on same patterns where possible. (2) Restrict Splunk role for this search. (3) Immediate incident for positives. (4) Redact dashboard fields.
- **Visualization:** Table (aggregated URI patterns only), single value (violations), timeline.
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.src, Web.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Web)

### UC-22.11.26 · Wireless Link Encryption Downgrade for Store WLAN Carrying Payment Terminals (PCI DSS Req 4.1.1, 2.2.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces association frames or controller logs showing legacy WPA or open auth on SSIDs used by payment terminals, evidencing review of wireless encryption for account data environments.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Cisco (WLAN syslog), custom Meraki `meraki:radio` if used
- **Data Sources:** `index=wireless` `sourcetype="wlan:controller"` — SSID, security mode, AP name
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wireless sourcetype="wlan:controller" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_store_ssid.csv ssid OUTPUT carries_pos
| where carries_pos="true"
| where match(_raw,"(?i)open|OWE|WEP|WPA[^2]|TKIP")
| stats count by ap_name, ssid, security_mode
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Classify SSIDs that can see payment devices. (2) Exclude guest SSIDs with captive portal if physically isolated. (3) Integrate with store opening checklist. (4) Weekly store ops digest.
- **Visualization:** Table (SSID, security_mode), map of APs if geo available, bar chart (by store region).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

---

### UC-22.11.27 · Anti-Malware Agent Coverage Gaps on CDE Windows Servers (PCI DSS Req 5.2.1, 5.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Compares CrowdStrike or legacy AV presence signals to the PCI server inventory so every in-scope system is actively protected against malware as required for all system components.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=edr` `sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts"`; `index=windows` `WinEventLog:System` for legacy AV vendor
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_windows_cde_assets.csv hostname OUTPUT cde_member
| where cde_member="true"
| eval protected=if(match(agent_status,"(?i)normal|protected|enabled"),1,0)
| where protected=0 OR isnull(agent_status)
| stats values(agent_status) as status_vals by hostname, local_ip
| sort hostname
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize hostname case with `lower()`. (2) Reconcile VMs cloned without sensor. (3) Auto-ticket missing agents within SLA. (4) Monthly attestation export for Req 5.
- **Visualization:** Table (unprotected hosts), single value (% coverage), pie (protected vs not).
- **CIM Models:** Malware
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks by Malware_Attacks.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5994), [CIM: Malware](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Malware)

### UC-22.11.28 · Malware Definition and Sensor Policy Update Lag (PCI DSS Req 5.2.2, 5.3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Measures hours since last successful policy or definition update on CDE endpoints so protection stays current and assessors see operational discipline beyond initial installation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082)
- **Data Sources:** `index=edr` `crowdstrike:hosts` — `last_seen`, `policy_id`, `version`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_windows_cde_assets.csv hostname OUTPUT cde_member
| where cde_member="true"
| eval lag_hours=round((now()-last_seen)/3600,2)
| where lag_hours>48
| stats max(lag_hours) as max_lag_h by hostname, platform_name, version
| sort - max_lag_h
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune `lag_hours` to vendor SLA. (2) Exclude maintenance windows via lookup. (3) Correlate with network outages. (4) Weekly compliance report attachment.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (max_lag_h by host), table, single value (hosts breaching 48h).
- **CIM Models:** Malware
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks by Malware_Attacks.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5994), [CIM: Malware](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Malware)

### UC-22.11.29 · Scheduled Malware Scan or On-Demand Scan Failures (PCI DSS Req 5.3.1, 5.3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Captures failed or skipped AV scans on systems that can affect account data so periodic and real-time detection requirements remain demonstrable during operational reviews.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` `WinEventLog:Application` vendor AV EventCode; `sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Windows Defender/Operational"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Windows Defender/Operational" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_windows_cde_assets.csv ComputerName OUTPUT cde_member
| where cde_member="true"
| where EventCode IN (1008,1010,5001,5007)
| stats count by ComputerName, EventCode, EventDescription
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map Defender EventCodes to human-readable descriptions via lookup. (2) Include legacy AV sourcetypes in append. (3) Alert on any failure in CDE. (4) Track re-scan completion in ITSM.
- **Visualization:** Table (host, EventCode), column chart (failures by day), single value (open failures).
- **CIM Models:** Malware
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks by Malware_Attacks.action, Malware_Attacks.signature, Malware_Attacks.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Malware](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Malware)

### UC-22.11.30 · Malware Detection Volume Trend by Store and Server Tier (PCI DSS Req 5.2.1, 5.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Trends malware blocks and quarantines where payment systems exist so regional infection campaigns are visible before they spread into the CDE.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=edr` `sourcetype="crowdstrike:detection"`; ES `malware` data model if accelerated
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:detection" earliest=-30d
| lookup pci_site_codes.csv aid OUTPUT store_region, pci_tier
| where pci_tier IN ("pos","store_server","corp_payment")
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time, store_region, pci_tier
| eventstats sum(count) as daily_total by _time
| eval share_pct=if(daily_total>0, round(100*count/daily_total,2), null())
| sort _time, store_region
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map CrowdStrike `aid` to store metadata. (2) Filter out benign test detections via `severity` threshold. (3) Compare to seasonal retail baseline. (4) Include in monthly security committee pack.
- **Visualization:** Stacked area (count by pci_tier), line (`share_pct` by region), heatmap (region x day).
- **CIM Models:** Malware
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks by Malware_Attacks.action, Malware_Attacks.signature, Malware_Attacks.dest span=1d | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5994), [CIM: Malware](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Malware)

### UC-22.11.31 · Phishing Simulation Click-Through Rates for Users with CDE Access (PCI DSS Req 5.3.3, 12.6.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Measures phishing exercise outcomes for staff who can reach payment systems so security awareness training effectiveness supports malware defense and human-layer controls.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055) if simulations logged; or `sourcetype="phishme:results"`
- **Data Sources:** `index=sec_aware` `sourcetype="phishme:results"` — `campaign`, `user`, `clicked`, `reported`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=sec_aware sourcetype="phishme:results" earliest=-90d
| lookup pci_cde_users.csv email AS user OUTPUT has_cde_access
| where has_cde_access="true"
| eval clicked_flag=if(match(clicked,"(?i)yes|true|1"),1,0)
| stats sum(clicked_flag) as clicks, count as sent by campaign
| eval click_rate=if(sent>0, round(100*clicks/sent,2), null())
| where click_rate>15
| sort - click_rate
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Sync HR email to `pci_cde_users.csv` after access reviews. (2) De-duplicate multiple sends per user per campaign. (3) Trigger mandatory remedial training over threshold. (4) Quarterly leadership summary.
- **Visualization:** Bar (click_rate by campaign), table (campaign, sent, clicks), gauge (CDE user click rate).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)

### UC-22.11.32 · Anti-Malware Tamper and Bypass Attempt Telemetry (PCI DSS Req 5.2.1, 5.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces attempts to disable real-time protection or unload drivers on payment-facing hosts, evidencing detective controls when malware defenses are targeted.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=edr` `crowdstrike:detection` with technique “defense evasion”; `index=windows` Defender operational events for tamper protection
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=edr sourcetype="crowdstrike:detection" earliest=-7d tactic="Defense Evasion")
 OR (index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Windows Defender/Operational" EventCode=5001 earliest=-7d)
| lookup pci_windows_cde_assets.csv coalesce(hostname,ComputerName) as host_key OUTPUT cde_member
| where cde_member="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as first, latest(_time) as last, values(description) as titles by host_key
| sort first
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune out known patch reboot sequences. (2) Map to insider-threat procedure if user context exists. (3) Immediate SOC escalation. (4) Retain raw events 12 months.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (first/last), table (host_key, titles), single value (distinct hosts).
- **CIM Models:** Malware
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks by Malware_Attacks.action, Malware_Attacks.signature, Malware_Attacks.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5994), [CIM: Malware](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Malware)

### UC-22.11.33 · Critical and High Vulnerabilities on Payment Application Servers (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 11.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Prioritizes open critical and high findings on CDE application tiers from authenticated scans so secure development and vulnerability management requirements stay aligned with PCI risk ranking.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` `sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host")` — `severity`, `plugin_name`, `host_fqdn`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc:vuln","qualys:host") earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_app_tier.csv fqdn AS host_fqdn OUTPUT tier
| where tier="payment_app"
| where severity IN ("Critical","High",4,5)
| where match(state,"(?i)open|active|reopened)")
| stats dc(plugin_id) as open_plugins, values(plugin_name) as sample_plugins by host_fqdn
| sort - open_plugins
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize severity strings across vendors. (2) Deduplicate by `plugin_id`+asset. (3) Sync with patch CAB weekly. (4) Export for ASV/internal scan evidence crosswalk.
- **Visualization:** Table (hosts with counts), bar (open_plugins), treemap by business unit lookup.
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Tenable](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [Splunk Add-on for Qualys](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2964), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.11.34 · Critical CVE Remediation SLA Breach Tracking (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 6.3.3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Computes age in days for critical vulnerabilities against the organization’s documented remediation SLA so PCI expectations for timely flaw correction are measurable.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` `tenable:sc:vuln` — `first_found`, `last_found`, `severity`, `host_fqdn`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype="tenable:sc:vuln" severity="Critical" earliest=-90d
| lookup pci_in_scope_hosts.csv fqdn AS host_fqdn OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true"
| where match(state,"(?i)open|reopened)")
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_found)/86400,1)
| eval sla_days=30
| where age_days>sla_days
| stats max(age_days) as max_age by host_fqdn, plugin_name
| sort - max_age
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Adjust `sla_days` per risk policy (15 for internet-facing). (2) Join patch ticket state from ITSM to exclude in-progress. (3) Weekly governance email. (4) Document exceptions with compensating controls.
- **Visualization:** Bar (max_age by host), table (plugin_name), single value (count of SLA breaches).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Tenable](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

---

### UC-22.11.35 · Web Application Firewall Blocks and Anomalies on Checkout URIs (PCI DSS Req 6.4.1, 6.4.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Aggregates ModSecurity, AWS WAF, or commercial WAF denies on payment paths so public-facing web applications retain active attack detection between code releases.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=waf` `sourcetype="aws:waf:logs"`; `sourcetype="modsec:json"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=waf (sourcetype="aws:waf:logs" OR sourcetype="modsec:json") earliest=-24h
| where match(coalesce(uri,httpRequest.uri),"(?i)/checkout|/payment|/cart|/token")
| where action IN ("BLOCK","block","deny","403")
| stats count by terminatingRuleId, coalesce(uri,httpRequest.uri) as path, clientIp
| sort - count
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field names with CIM aliases. (2) Enrich `clientIp` with threat intel. (3) Tune out scanner noise using rate limits. (4) Feed spikes to application on-call.
- **Visualization:** Column chart (by rule ID), geomap (clientIp), table (path, count).
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Web)

### UC-22.11.36 · Pull-Request and Code Review Evidence for Payment Microservices (PCI DSS Req 6.2.4, 6.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Ingests Git platform webhooks to verify merges to payment repositories include required reviewers, supporting secure software engineering practices for custom payment software.
- **App/TA:** Custom HEC `sourcetype="github:pull_request"` or Splunk Add-on for Bitbucket if used
- **Data Sources:** `index=devsecops` `sourcetype="github:pull_request"` — `repo`, `merged`, `review_count`, `labels`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=devsecops sourcetype="github:pull_request" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_payment_repos.csv repo OUTPUT pci_repo
| where pci_repo="true" AND merged="true"
| eval review_ok=if(review_count>=1 AND match(labels,"(?i)security-reviewed"),1,0)
| where review_ok=0
| table _time, repo, user, review_count, labels, url
| sort _time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Configure org-wide webhooks with HEC token. (2) Align label policy with secure SDLC. (3) Block deploy pipeline on Splunk summary alert if desired. (4) Monthly export for PCI evidence binder.
- **Visualization:** Table (exceptions), pie (review_ok ratio overall), timeline (merge times).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.11.37 · Change Control Completeness for Production Payment Releases (PCI DSS Req 6.5.1, 6.5.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Change
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Joins deployment pipeline events to approved change tickets so every production change affecting payment software follows formal change control as PCI expects for secure systems.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) if available
- **Data Sources:** `index=ci` `sourcetype="jenkins:build"` or `sourcetype="azuredevops:release"`; `index=itsm` `snow:change_request`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ci sourcetype="jenkins:build" earliest=-7d
| where match(job,"(?i)payment|checkout|token")
| where result="SUCCESS" AND like(build_url,"%prod%")
| eval chg_hint=replace(build_parameters,".*?(CHG\\d{7}).*","\\1")
| join type=left max=1 chg_hint [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-14d
    | rename number as chg_hint
    | fields chg_hint, state
  ]
| where isnull(state) OR NOT match(state,"(?i)closed|implemented")
| table _time, job, build_url, chg_hint, state
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require pipeline to inject `CHG` token parameter. (2) Add Git branch allow-list. (3) Alert on null `state`. (4) Retain build logs 13 months.
- **Visualization:** Table (unlinked deploys), column chart (violations by week), single value (count).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

---

### UC-22.11.38 · DAST and SAST Finding Density Before Payment Service Releases (PCI DSS Req 6.3.1, 6.3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks high-severity static and dynamic scan findings per build of payment APIs so security testing is evidenced prior to promoting code that could affect account data.
- **App/TA:** Custom `sourcetype="veracode:scan"` / `checkmarx:scan"` HEC normalizers
- **Data Sources:** `index=devsecops` `sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan")` — `app_name`, `build_id`, `severity`, `status`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=devsecops sourcetype IN ("veracode:scan","checkmarx:scan") earliest=-30d
| lookup pci_payment_apps.csv app_name OUTPUT pci_app
| where pci_app="true"
| where severity IN ("High","Very High","Critical")
| where match(status,"(?i)open|new)")
| stats count by app_name, build_id, scan_type
| where count>0
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map scanner project names to internal `app_name`. (2) Gate release on `count=0` for Critical if policy mandates. (3) Correlate with UC-22.11.37 for same `build_id`. (4) Quarterly trending for PCI interview.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (open highs by app), table (build_id), line (count over time).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.11.39 · Public-Facing Payment Web Tier Patch and Library Drift (PCI DSS Req 6.3.3, 6.2.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces outdated frameworks (Log4j, OpenSSL) reported by container image scanners for checkout services, tying technical debt to protection of public-facing web applications.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` `qualys:container` or `tenable:sc:vuln` container plugins
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:container" earliest=-7d
| where match(image_repo,"(?i)checkout|payment|gw|token")
| where match(_raw,"(?i)log4j|openssl|struts|spring4shell)")
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen by image_repo, image_tag, dq
| sort image_repo, image_tag
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest CI-generated image tags with digest. (2) Map repo names to PCI asset IDs. (3) Auto-create patch stories under SLA. (4) Document accepted risk for deferred base image updates.
- **Visualization:** Table (image_repo, tag, dq), single value (images with findings), timeline (last_seen).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Qualys](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2964), [Splunk Add-on for Tenable](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.11.40 · Role-Based Group Membership Drift for Active Directory CDE OU (PCI DSS Req 7.2.1, 7.2.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Detects additions to privileged groups tied to the CDE organizational unit so access restrictions remain aligned with documented roles and least privilege between quarterly access reviews.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 4728,4732,4756; `sourcetype="msad:change"` if Splunk AD TA
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4728,4732,4756) earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_cde_ad_groups.csv GroupName OUTPUT cde_group
| where cde_group="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as added_time, values(MemberName) as members by GroupName, SubjectUserName
| sort - added_time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `pci_cde_ad_groups.csv` for Domain Admins subset used in CDE. (2) Correlate `SubjectUserName` with privileged account inventory. (3) Immediate alert to IAM. (4) Snapshot daily to summary index for reviews.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (4728 events), table (GroupName, members), single value (daily adds).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.41 · Excessive Database Grants on Schemas Storing Tokenized PAN (PCI DSS Req 7.2.1, 7.2.5)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Highlights service accounts or users with `db_owner` or `SELECT` on token vault schemas beyond the documented roster, evidencing least privilege for access to systems with account data.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect, `sourcetype="mssql:audit"` or scripted `sql:grants` HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=db` `sourcetype="sql:grants"` — `principal`, `permission`, `schema_name`, `host`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=db sourcetype="sql:grants" earliest=-24h
| where match(schema_name,"(?i)token|vault|payment|cardholder)")
| lookup pci_db_allowed_principals.csv principal OUTPUT allowed
| where isnull(allowed) OR allowed="false"
| stats values(permission) as perms by host, principal, schema_name
| sort host, schema_name
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Export grants nightly via SQL job to HEC. (2) Refresh allowed principal list after each access review. (3) Auto-ticket violations. (4) Store weekly CSV for PCI Req 7 workpapers.
- **Visualization:** Table (violations), heatmap (principal x schema), single value (distinct excessive grants).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

### UC-22.11.42 · Access Request and Approval Completeness for CDE VPN Accounts (PCI DSS Req 7.2.3, 8.2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Verifies each new VPN entitlement for CDE users has a matching approved access request record so access is granted only with documented business justification.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` GlobalProtect or `sourcetype="pan:globalprotect"`; `index=itsm` `snow:sc_req_item`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" subtype="gateway-connected" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_cde_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT cde_profile
| where cde_profile="true"
| eval user=lower(user)
| join type=left max=1 user [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" earliest=-30d short_description="*VPN*CDE*"
    | eval user=lower(caller_id)
    | stats latest(state) as req_state by user
  ]
| where isnull(req_state) OR NOT match(req_state,"(?i)closed|fulfilled")
| stats dc(_time) as connect_events by user, source
| sort - connect_events
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize `user` to corporate UPN. (2) Align RITM naming with join filter. (3) Alert IAM on missing ticket within 24h of first connect. (4) Monthly reconciliation report.
- **Visualization:** Table (users without tickets), column chart (connect_events), pie (fulfilled vs missing).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.43 · Least-Privilege Validation — Interactive Logons to Database Tier from Workstations (PCI DSS Req 7.2.5, 8.2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces database interactive logins from non-administrator workstations that may violate least privilege for personnel access to cardholder data stores.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server (2648) if audit ingested
- **Data Sources:** `index=db` `mssql:audit` action="AUDIT SESSION" or `sourcetype="oracle:audit"`; `lookup pci_jump_hosts.csv` client IP
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=db sourcetype="mssql:audit" earliest=-24h
| where match(action,"(?i)login|authentication")
| lookup pci_jump_hosts.csv ip AS client_ip OUTPUT is_jump
| where isnull(is_jump) OR is_jump="false"
| lookup pci_db_tier.csv server_name OUTPUT tier
| where tier="carddata_db"
| stats count by client_ip, server_name, database_principal_name
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate jump host egress IPs. (2) Exclude application service accounts via lookup. (3) Pair with bastion session logs for false positives. (4) Quarterly access review input.
- **Visualization:** Table (client_ip, principal), geomap, bar (count by server_name).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2648), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.44 · Privilege Escalation Chains on CDE Windows Servers (PCI DSS Req 7.2.5, 10.2.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Correlates token elevation, service installation, and logon type sequences on payment servers so unauthorized privilege escalation is detected and investigated under access restriction obligations.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 4672,4698,7045; `lookup pci_payment_servers.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4672,4698,7045) earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_payment_servers.csv ComputerName OUTPUT pci_host
| where pci_host="true"
| transaction ComputerName maxspan=300m maxevents=20 startswith=EventCode=4672 endswith=EventCode=7045
| where eventcount>=3
| table duration, ComputerName, EventCode, Security_ID, Account_Name
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune `maxspan` to environment. (2) Add known patch service names to allow list. (3) Create ES correlation search from this pattern. (4) Retain transactions in summary index with redaction.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (transaction), table (suspicious hosts), single value (transaction count).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.45 · Shared and Break-Glass Account Usage on Payment Infrastructure (PCI DSS Req 8.2.6, 8.6.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks authentications to shared admin or break-glass accounts on in-scope systems so unique identification requirements are enforced except for documented emergency scenarios.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` `linux:secure`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=linux sourcetype="linux:secure" earliest=-24h)
| eval acct=coalesce(TargetUserName,user)
| lookup pci_shared_accounts.csv account AS acct OUTPUT shared_flag
| where shared_flag="true"
| lookup pci_in_scope_hosts.csv host OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true"
| stats values(src_ip) as src_ips, count by acct, host
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Inventory all shared accounts with approvers. (2) Require ticket number in logon script where possible. (3) Alert on concurrent src_ips. (4) Quarterly review of `src_ips` diversity.
- **Visualization:** Table (acct, host, src_ips), chord or link chart (acct to src_ip), single value (distinct shared logons).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.46 · Vendor Remote Access Sessions into CDE Jump Hosts (PCI DSS Req 7.2.5, 12.8.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Lists vendor identities and source IPs using remote access paths into the CDE so third-party access is monitored consistently with service provider due diligence expectations.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` EventCode 4624 LogonType 10; `index=netfw` GlobalProtect logs with vendor realm
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 LogonType=10 earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_jump_hosts.csv ComputerName OUTPUT jump_host
| where jump_host="true"
| lookup pci_vendor_accounts.csv TargetUserName OUTPUT vendor_name
| where isnotnull(vendor_name)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by TargetUserName, vendor_name, WorkstationName, IpAddress
| sort vendor_name
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map vendor accounts to contracts in GRC. (2) Cross-check active maintenance windows. (3) Alert outside window. (4) Include in third-party access quarterly attestations.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (sessions), table (vendor_name, user), map (IpAddress).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.11.47 · MFA Gap Detection for CDE Interactive and Remote Access (PCI DSS Req 8.4.1, 8.4.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces successful logons to CDE systems where MFA claim or step-up evidence is absent, supporting multi-factor authentication for all access into the cardholder environment.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` EventCode 4624 with `AuthenticationPackageName`; Azure AD sign-in logs if hybrid `sourcetype="azure:aad:signin"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_cde_servers.csv ComputerName OUTPUT in_cde
| where in_cde="true"
| where LogonType IN (10,3)
| eval mfa_ok=if(match(AuthenticationPackageName,"(?i)Negotiate") AND isnotnull(TransmittedServices),1,0)
| where mfa_ok=0
| stats count by ComputerName, TargetUserName, LogonType, WorkstationName
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enrich with ADFS/Azure token claims via forwarder if available—replace `mfa_ok` logic accordingly. (2) Exclude machine accounts. (3) High-fidelity alert for human users only. (4) Document compensating VPN MFA at perimeter if host MFA absent.
- **Visualization:** Table (gaps), pie (MFA_ok vs not), single value (distinct users affected).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.48 · Domain Password Policy Compliance via Resultant Set of Policy Events (PCI DSS Req 8.3.6, 8.3.7)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Ingests periodic exports of password policy settings to confirm minimum length, complexity, and history requirements for accounts with access to system components in scope for PCI.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` `sourcetype="ad:password_policy"` from scripted LDAP dump to HEC
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="ad:password_policy" earliest=-7d
| eval meets_pci=if(minPwdLength>=12 AND pwdHistoryLength>=4 AND complexityRequired="TRUE",1,0)
| where meets_pci=0
| stats latest(_time) as last_report by domain_dn, minPwdLength, pwdHistoryLength, complexityRequired
| sort domain_dn
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Schedule PowerShell `Get-ADDefaultDomainPasswordPolicy` hourly to HEC. (2) Map fine-grained password policies per CDE OU if used. (3) Alert GPO team on failure. (4) Store compliant snapshots quarterly.
- **Visualization:** Table (non-compliant domains), gauge (meets_pci %), timeline (policy changes).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### UC-22.11.49 · Failed Authentication Burst Detection on Payment Gateway Accounts (PCI DSS Req 8.3.4, 10.2.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Trends failed logon attempts against payment application service accounts and operator IDs so brute-force activity is visible and aligned with monitoring of invalid access attempts.

- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` EventCode 4625; `index=linux` `linux:secure` `failed password`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4625 earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=linux sourcetype="linux:secure" "failed password" earliest=-24h)
| eval acct=coalesce(TargetUserName,user)
| lookup pci_payment_accounts.csv account AS acct OUTPUT gateway_acct
| where gateway_acct="true"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count by _time, acct, src_ip
| where count>25
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate payment service account list. (2) Whitelist vulnerability scanner IPs. (3) Auto-block at firewall for sustained bursts. (4) Daily SOC review metrics.
- **Visualization:** Timechart (failed logons), table (acct, src_ip, count), heatmap (acct x hour).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl

```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

```spl
| transaction TargetUserName maxspan=60m startswith=EventCode=4720 endswith=EventCode=4728
| lookup pci_cde_ad_groups.csv GroupName OUTPUT cde_group
| where cde_group="true"
| table duration, TargetUserName, GroupName, SubjectUserName
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map `TargetUserName` alignment between events—may require `rename` on 4720 field `TargetUserName` as new user and 4728 `MemberName`. (2) Adjust `transaction` keys per environment testing. (3) Alert IAM for out-of-band adds. (4) Evidence for user identification procedures.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (transactions), table (new users to CDE groups), single value (count per day).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

### UC-22.11.51 · Inactive Human Accounts Still Entitled to CDE Groups (PCI DSS Req 8.2.5, 8.2.6)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Joins last successful logon timestamps to current CDE group membership so dormant identities are removed before they become an easy target for misuse of credentials.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** Daily `sourcetype="ad:user_snapshot"` HEC; `lookup pci_cde_ad_groups.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="ad:user_snapshot" earliest=-2d
| lookup pci_cde_ad_groups.csv memberOf OUTPUT in_cde_group
| where in_cde_group="true"
| eval idle_days=round((now()-lastLogonTimestamp)/86400,1)
| where idle_days>90 AND NOT match(samAccountName,"(?i)^svc-")
| table samAccountName, idle_days, lastLogonTimestamp, department, manager
| sort - idle_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build LDAP snapshot forwarder with `lastLogonTimestamp`. (2) Align 90-day threshold to corporate policy. (3) Auto-disable workflow in IAM integration. (4) Monthly PCI access review attachment.
- **Visualization:** Table (idle users), bar (idle_days), single value (count stale CDE users).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### UC-22.11.52 · Generic Account Prohibition — `admin` / `root` Interactive Success on CDE (PCI DSS Req 8.2.6, 2.2.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Alerts on successful interactive use of generic superuser accounts on in-scope Linux payment hosts, enforcing unique IDs and eliminating shared root for routine administration.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=linux` `sourcetype="linux:secure"` or `auditd` — `user`, `tty`, `src_ip`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=linux sourcetype="linux:secure" earliest=-24h
| where match(_raw,"Accepted password|Accepted publickey")
| rex "for (?<acct>\\S+) from (?<src_ip>\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+)"
| where acct IN ("root","admin","Administrator")
| lookup pci_linux_payment_hosts.csv host OUTPUT pci_host
| where pci_host="true"
| stats count by host, acct, src_ip
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Prefer `auditd` TYPE=USER_LOGIN for richer fields if available. (2) Allow break-glass from bastion subnet only via `where`. (3) Immediate SOC page. (4) Quarterly attestation of zero violations goal.
- **Visualization:** Table (violations), map (src_ip), single value (weekly violation count).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.53 · Remote Access MFA Evidence Correlation — VPN Success Without Step-Up Token (PCI DSS Req 8.4.2, 8.5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Joins VPN authentication logs with identity provider MFA success events to prove remote entry into the CDE used multi-factor authentication for each session where policy requires it.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055) for AAD sign-ins
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `pan:globalprotect`; `index=o365` `azure:aad:signin`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" subtype="gateway-connected" earliest=-24h
| eval session=coalesce(session_id,gp_session_id)
| eval user=lower(user)
| join type=left max=1 user [
    search index=o365 sourcetype="azure:aad:signin" earliest=-24h
    | eval user=lower(userPrincipalName)
    | where authenticationRequirement="multiFactorAuthentication" OR mfaDetail!="[]"
    | stats latest(_time) as mfa_time by user
  ]
| where isnull(mfa_time) OR mfa_time > _time + 300
| stats count by user, src, portal
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Clock-skew normalize `_time` fields across sources. (2) Tune join window; `mfa_time` should precede VPN connect. (3) Replace logic if Okta/other IdP. (4) Document compensating SAML MFA at IdP only.
- **Visualization:** Table (sessions missing MFA join), timeline, single value (violations).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.54 · Service Account Inventory Reconciliation — Unexpected SPN or Delegation Changes (PCI DSS Req 8.2.1, 8.6.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks modifications to Kerberos SPNs and constrained delegation on payment-related service accounts so non-human identities stay inventoried and cannot silently gain broader authentication rights.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` EventCode 4742,4738,5136 (Directory Service Changes for `servicePrincipalName`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4742,4738) earliest=-7d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)servicePrincipalName|TrustedToAuthForDelegation|msDS-AllowedToDelegateTo)")
| lookup pci_service_accounts.csv SamAccountName OUTPUT pci_svc
| where pci_svc="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_change, values(EventCode) as codes by SamAccountName, SubjectUserName
| sort - first_change
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `pci_service_accounts.csv` from CMDB. (2) Map EventCode descriptions for auditors. (3) Require CAB for SPN edits. (4) Daily diff email to identity team.
- **Visualization:** Table (changes), timeline, single value (weekly change count).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.11.55 · Session Timeout Enforcement on Payment Web Admin Consoles (PCI DSS Req 8.2.8)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Measures idle session duration before logout on administrative payment portals using HTTP session cookies or app logs so inactive sessions re-authenticate within policy limits.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Stream (1809), `index=web` `access_combined`
- **Data Sources:** `index=web` `sourcetype="access_combined"` for `/admin` URIs with `session_id`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=web sourcetype="access_combined" earliest=-24h
| where match(uri,"(?i)/admin|/portal/manage")
| transaction session_id maxpause=30m maxspan=8h
| eval idle_mins=round(duration/60,2)
| where idle_mins>15
| stats max(idle_mins) as max_idle by session_id, clientip
| sort - max_idle
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align `maxpause` with configured server session timeout. (2) Mask `session_id` in dashboards. (3) Tune for long-running report downloads. (4) Pair with WAF session fixation checks.
- **Visualization:** Histogram (idle_mins), table (longest sessions), single value (sessions exceeding policy).
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809), [CIM: Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Web)

### UC-22.11.56 · Physical Badge Access to Data Center Containing Cardholder Systems (PCI DSS Req 9.2.1, 9.4.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Physical Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks granted and denied door reads for suites housing payment hardware so physical entry to sensitive areas is logged and reviewable for onsite PCI assessments.
- **App/TA:** Custom `sourcetype="pacs:ccure"` or `lenel:access"` CSV/HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=physical` `sourcetype="pacs:door"` — `reader_id`, `badge_id`, `result`, `site`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=physical sourcetype="pacs:door" earliest=-7d
| lookup pci_dc_readers.csv reader_id OUTPUT cde_dc_zone
| where cde_dc_zone="true"
| eval denied=if(match(result,"(?i)deny|reject|unknown"),1,0)
| timechart span=1d sum(denied) as denied_reads, count as total_reads
| eval deny_rate=if(total_reads>0, round(100*denied_reads/total_reads,2), null())
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map reader IDs to PCI zones from facility diagrams. (2) De-identify `badge_id` in dashboards if required. (3) Alert on sustained deny spikes (tailgating). (4) Quarterly reviewer export.
- **Visualization:** Line (deny_rate), column (denied_reads), table (top denied badges hashed).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.11.57 · Visitor Log Completeness for Data Center Escorted Access (PCI DSS Req 9.4.1, 9.4.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Compares visitor check-in events from front desk systems to escorted badge-in events inside the CDE suite so visitor management procedures are complete and auditable.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect or `sourcetype="visitor:checkin"` HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=physical` `visitor:checkin`; `pacs:door` escort reader pairings
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=physical sourcetype="visitor:checkin" earliest=-7d
| eval visit_key=md5(concat(visitor_id,date_hour,site))
| join type=left max=0 visit_key [
    search index=physical sourcetype="pacs:door" earliest=-7d escort_flag="true"
    | eval visit_key=md5(concat(visitor_id,date_hour,site))
    | stats count as escort_swipes by visit_key
  ]
| where isnull(escort_swipes) OR escort_swipes=0
| table _time, visitor_id, site, host_escort_expected
| sort _time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align `visitor_id` keys between systems. (2) Define mandatory escort readers. (3) Daily exception report to facilities. (4) Store signed visitor logs for assessor samples.
- **Visualization:** Table (missing escorts), single value (open exceptions), column chart (by site).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

### UC-22.11.58 · Secure Media Destruction Workflow Completion for Backup Tapes with CHD (PCI DSS Req 9.5.1, 3.2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks chain-of-custody and destruction certificates for media classified as containing account data so classified media is destroyed when no longer needed for business or legal reasons.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) or `sourcetype="ironmountain:destruction"`
- **Data Sources:** `index=logistics` `sourcetype="media:chain"` — `barcode`, `status`, `destroyed_time`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=logistics sourcetype="media:chain" earliest=-90d
| where classification="PCI_CHD"
| where match(status,"(?i)in_transit|awaiting_destroy|stored")
| eval age_days=round((now()-created_time)/86400,1)
| where age_days>120
| stats max(age_days) as oldest_days by barcode, status, vault_site
| sort - oldest_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest vendor destruction webhook as status `destroyed`. (2) Align retention to legal hold exceptions via lookup. (3) Escalate aged media weekly. (4) Attach certificates to GRC record.
- **Visualization:** Table (oldest media), gauge (avg age_days), timeline (status transitions).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)

```spl
| sort first_alert
```

### UC-22.11.60 · Quarterly Physical Access List Review Exception Tracking (PCI DSS Req 9.2.4, 12.1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks completion of quarterly access roster reviews for personnel with keys or badges to CDE facilities, tying physical security policy to measurable workflow deadlines.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), `sourcetype="grc:control_test"`
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:control_test"` — `control_id`, `test_status`, `due_date`, `owner`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:control_test" earliest=-120d
| where control_id IN ("PCI-9.2.4-PHYS-REVIEW","PCI-9.2.4")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_date AND NOT match(test_status,"(?i)pass|complete"),1,0)
| stats latest(test_status) as status, latest(due_date) as due by owner
| where overdue=1 OR isnull(status)
| sort due
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Create recurring GRC control with quarterly `due_date`. (2) Push completion events from ServiceNow attestation. (3) Escalate to facility security lead. (4) Export closed tests for PCI policy evidence (Req 12 linkage).
- **Visualization:** Table (overdue owners), timeline (due_date), single value (overdue count).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.11.61 · Audit Log Source Completeness — Missing Windows Security Events per CDE Host (PCI DSS Req 10.2.1, 10.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Identifies in-scope Windows servers that stopped sending Security event logs so audit trails required for reconstructing access to system components remain complete and continuous.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` `WinEventLog:Security`; `lookup pci_windows_cde_assets.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| tstats prestats=t count WHERE index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" earliest=-25h latest=-1h BY host
| stats count by host
| inputlookup pci_windows_cde_assets.csv
| fields hostname
| rename hostname as host
| join type=left host [
    search | tstats prestats=t count WHERE index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" earliest=-25h latest=-1h BY host
    | stats count by host
  ]
| eval has_logs=if(isnotnull(count) AND count>0,1,0)
| where has_logs=0
| table host
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Validate `tstats` summaries are enabled for `windows` index or use metadata `| metadata type=hosts` alternative if needed. (2) Page if any CDE host missing. (3) Check forwarder connectivity. (4) Daily compliance health dashboard.
- **Visualization:** Table (missing hosts), single value (gap count), choropleth by data center if tagged.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.11.62 · Log Ingestion Pipeline Lag and Parser Error Rate for PCI Indexes (PCI DSS Req 10.2.1, 10.5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Operations, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Monitors indexing latency and `date_parser` failures on indexes holding CDE evidence so log pipeline health does not silently break forensic reconstruction timelines.
- **App/TA:** Splunk internal monitoring, `_internal` sourcetypes
- **Data Sources:** `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd` / `metrics.log`; `index=_introspection`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=TailReader OR component=AggregatorMiningProcessor earliest=-24h
| eval pci_idx=if(match(log_message,"(?i)pci|cde|payment"),1,0)
| where pci_idx=1 OR index IN ("pci","cde","payment")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count by _time, component
| sort _time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag forwarders with `pci_index` in `serverclass.conf`. (2) Pair with `index=_internal` `log_index_processor` errors filtered by dest index. (3) Alert on sustained zero events for high-volume sources. (4) SLO dashboard for SOC manager sign-off.
- **Visualization:** Line (events per 15m), overlay parser error series, single value (error count).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.11.63 · Daily Log Review Workflow — PCI Queue Ticket Closure SLA (PCI DSS Req 10.4.1, 10.4.1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks time from automated PCI log review finding to analyst disposition so periodic log reviews are not only scheduled but completed with auditable closure records.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `` `notable` `` filtered by `rule_name` like `PCI*` or `tag="pci"`; fields `status`, `closed_time`, `_time`
- **SPL:**
```spl
`notable` (tag="pci" OR like(rule_name,"%PCI%")) earliest=-7d
| eval age_h=if(isnotnull(closed_time), (closed_time-_time)/3600, (now()-_time)/3600)
| eval breached=if(isnull(closed_time) AND age_h>24,1,0)
| stats sum(breached) as open_breach, count as total by rule_name
| sort - open_breach
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag PCI-related correlation searches consistently. (2) Align 24h SLA to policy. (3) Supervisor dashboard for aging. (4) Export weekly metrics for PCI log review evidence.
- **Visualization:** Table (rule_name, open_breach), column chart (aging buckets), single value (% closed within SLA).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

```spl
| where match(_raw,"(?i)ntp|clock|stratum|unsync|time\\s+set)")
| lookup pci_time_critical_devices.csv host OUTPUT requires_ntp
| where requires_ntp="true"
| stats count by host, sourcetype
| sort - count
```

### UC-22.11.66 · Critical System Clock Skew Between Database and Application Tier (PCI DSS Req 10.6.1, 10.6.1.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Operations
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Compares `_time` deltas between paired application and database events sharing a `correlation_id` so forensic timelines for CHD access stay coherent across tiers.
- **App/TA:** Custom application JSON logs with `correlation_id`
- **Data Sources:** `index=app` `sourcetype="payment:api:json"`; `index=db` `oracle:audit` with same `correlation_id` if injected
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=app sourcetype="payment:api:json" earliest=-1h) OR (index=db sourcetype="oracle:audit" earliest=-1h)
| where isnotnull(correlation_id)
| eval src=if(index="app","app","db")
| stats earliest(eval(if(src="app",_time,null()))) as t_app, earliest(eval(if(src="db",_time,null()))) as t_db by correlation_id
| eval skew_sec=abs(t_app-t_db)
| where skew_sec>5 AND isnotnull(t_app) AND isnotnull(t_db)
| stats perc95(skew_sec) as p95_skew, count by correlation_id
| where p95_skew>5
| sort - p95_skew
| head 100
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require apps to propagate `correlation_id` into DB audit. (2) Tune threshold per transaction latency SLA. (3) Pair with NTP health UC. (4) Weekly ops review.
- **Visualization:** Histogram (skew_sec), table (worst correlation_id), single value (count high skew).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

---

### UC-22.11.67 · Comprehensive Audit Trail for Successful CDE Administrator Logons (PCI DSS Req 10.2.1, 10.2.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0

- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` `auditd` type=USER_LOGIN
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h)
 OR (index=linux sourcetype="linux:audit" type="USER_LOGIN" earliest=-24h)
| lookup pci_cde_servers.csv coalesce(ComputerName,host) as host OUTPUT in_cde
| where in_cde="true"
| eval user_id=coalesce(TargetUserName,acct)
| eval src=coalesce(IpAddress,addr)
| table _time, host, user_id, LogonType, src, TerminalSessionId
| sort - _time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure 4624 includes workstation name and IP fields populated. (2) Mask sensitive columns in shared dashboards. (3) Long retention index or archive for 12+ months. (4) Map to quarterly access review samples.
- **Visualization:** Table (sortable audit view), timeline, pivot by user_id.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.11.68 · Log Retention Index Frozen Bucket Age Compliance (PCI DSS Req 10.5.1, 10.5.1.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Capacity
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Reports oldest searchable bucket age for indexes storing PCI audit evidence so at least twelve months of immediately available history exists before cold or frozen archives.
- **App/TA:** `rest` search against Splunk cluster manager or monitoring console
- **Data Sources:** `| rest /services/data/indexes` — `title`, `frozenTimePeriodInSecs`, `totalEventCount`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| where match(title,"^(pci|cde|payment)_")
| eval retain_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,2)
| where retain_days<395
| table title, retain_days, totalEventCount, disabled
| sort retain_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Adjust `frozenTimePeriodInSecs` to ≥395 days per policy (allow leap buffer). (2) Document frozen/archive retrieval for older periods. (3) Alert if `disabled=1` on PCI indexes. (4) Quarterly compliance sign-off screenshot.
- **Visualization:** Table (index, retain_days), gauge (min retain_days), single value (indexes under policy).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Planned maintenance, backups, or batch jobs can drive metrics outside normal bands — correlate with change management windows.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

---

### UC-22.11.69 · Automated Log Review — Correlation of Firewall Deny Bursts with IDS Signatures (PCI DSS Req 10.4.1, 11.4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Joins short-window firewall denies with IDS alerts touching the same `src_ip` so automated reviews surface coordinated attack patterns without waiting for manual cross-index pivoting.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `pan:traffic`; `index=ids` `sourcetype="suricata:alert"` or `pan:threat`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" action=deny earliest=-1h
| stats count by src, dest
| where count>500
| join type=left max=0 src [
    search index=ids (sourcetype="suricata:alert" OR sourcetype="pan:threat") earliest=-1h
    | rename src_ip as src
    | stats dc(signature) as sigs by src
  ]
| where isnotnull(sigs) AND sigs>0
| table src, dest, count, sigs
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune `map` limits for performance; consider `join` with subsearch capped. (2) Replace with ES notable linking if preferred. (3) Document tuning for false positive rates. (4) Daily automated report to SOC leads.
- **Visualization:** Table (correlated src), bubble chart (count vs sigs), timeline.
- **CIM Models:** Intrusion_Detection
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(IDS_Attacks.signature) as agg_value from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.src, IDS_Attacks.dest | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Intrusion_Detection](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Intrusion_Detection)
