### UC-22.11.70 · File Integrity Monitoring Alerts on Payment Web Roots (PCI DSS Req 10.5.2, 11.5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces Splunk Universal Forwarder or OSSEC/AIDE change alerts on directories serving checkout static content so unauthorized modification of critical files is detected promptly.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=os` `sourcetype="ossec"` or `auditd` paths; `sourcetype="stash"` from `monitor://` with `hash` in `change_management`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os (sourcetype="linux:audit" OR sourcetype="ossec") earliest=-24h
| where match(name,"(?i)/var/www/payment|/opt/checkout/dist)")
| where match(key,"(?i)write|attrib|chmod|unlink|create)")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_change, values(key) as ops by host, name
| sort - first_change
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Scope FIM to PCI web roots only to reduce noise. (2) Correlate with deploy pipeline user. (3) Page on off-hours changes. (4) Store weekly digest for change-detection evidence (Req 11 overlap).
- **Visualization:** Table (file paths), timeline (first_change), heatmap (host x hour).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.11.71 · Security Event Correlation — Payment API Errors with Concurrent Admin Logons (PCI DSS Req 10.4.1, 10.2.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Correlates spikes in payment API `5xx` responses with privileged session starts in the same window so automated reviews flag potential tampering or misconfiguration during maintenance abuse.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=app` `payment:api:json`; `index=windows` EventCode 4624 LogonType 10
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=app sourcetype="payment:api:json" status>=500 earliest=-4h
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as err5xx by _time, host
| where err5xx>100
| join type=inner _time [
    search index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 LogonType=10 earliest=-4h
    | lookup pci_privileged_users.csv TargetUserName OUTPUT priv
    | where priv="true"
    | bin _time span=5m
    | stats dc(TargetUserName) as priv_logons by _time
  ]
| table _time, host, err5xx, priv_logons
| sort _time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align `span` to traffic profile. (2) Maintain privileged user list from IAM. (3) Tune err5xx threshold per service. (4) Store notable-worthy rows in summary index.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (err5xx vs priv_logons), dual-axis line, table.
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.dest span=5m | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Web)

### UC-22.11.72 · Log Source Coverage Gaps — Expected Sourcetypes with Zero Events (PCI DSS Req 10.2.1, 12.10.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Operations
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Compares a reference list of required sourcetypes for PCI monitoring to last-hour ingestion so missing log categories are detected before an audit sample fails completeness tests.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), metadata searches
- **Data Sources:** `| metadata type=sourcetypes index=pci OR index=cde`; lookup `pci_required_sourcetypes.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup pci_required_sourcetypes.csv
| fields required_sourcetype, required_index
| join type=left required_sourcetype [
    | metadata type=sourcetypes index=pci index=cde
    | rename sourcetype as required_sourcetype
    | eval seen_recent=if(recentTime>relative_time(now(),"-2h"),1,0)
    | fields required_sourcetype, seen_recent, totalCount
  ]
| where isnull(seen_recent) OR seen_recent=0
| table required_index, required_sourcetype, seen_recent, totalCount
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Curate lookup with owner contact per sourcetype. (2) Run hourly; exclude maintenance windows via secondary lookup. (3) Auto-ticket to platform ops. (4) Attach zero-gap monthly PDF for PCI evidence.
- **Visualization:** Table (missing sources), single value (missing count), heatmap (by required_index).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.11.73 · ASV External Scan Failure and Non-Compliant Finding Trend (PCI DSS Req 11.3.1, 11.3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks approved scanning vendor run outcomes and residual high-risk findings on Internet-facing payment hosts so quarterly external vulnerability scanning evidence stays current and remediated.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` `qualys:was` or `tenable:io:vm` with `scan_type="ASV"` if tagged
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:was" earliest=-120d
| where match(scan_name,"(?i)ASV|PCI\\s*External)")
| eval failed_scan=if(match(status,"(?i)error|failed|aborted"),1,0)
| timechart span=30d sum(failed_scan) as failed_runs, count as total_runs
| eval fail_pct=if(total_runs>0, round(100*failed_runs/total_runs,2), null())
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag ASV scans distinctly from internal VM. (2) Join open findings `severity>=3` on same `fqdn`. (3) Alert if `fail_pct>0` two quarters in a row. (4) Store PDF reports in GRC with Splunk deep link.
- **Visualization:** Line (`fail_pct`), column (failed_runs), table (last scan status by fqdn).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest span=30d | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Qualys](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2964), [Splunk Add-on for Tenable](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.11.74 · Internal Authenticated Vulnerability Scan Coverage by CDE Subnet (PCI DSS Req 11.3.1, 11.3.1.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Verifies each CDE /24 received at least one credentialed scan in the rolling quarter so internal vulnerability scanning frequency and scope requirements are met with machine evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` `tenable:sc:vuln` with `scan_name`, `host_ip`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype="tenable:sc:vuln" earliest=-90d
| where match(scan_name,"(?i)internal|credentialed|CDE)")
| eval subnet=replace(host_ip,"^(\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+)\\.\\d+$","\\1.0/24")
| lookup pci_cde_subnets_summary.csv subnet OUTPUT required_scan
| where required_scan="true"
| stats latest(_time) as last_scan by subnet
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_scan)/86400,1)
| where days_since>90 OR isnull(last_scan)
| sort days_since
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize IP field names from Tenable TA. (2) Adjust `subnet` extraction for IPv6 if in scope. (3) Alert subnets over 90 days. (4) Map exceptions to documented outage windows.
- **Visualization:** Table (subnet, days_since), choropleth if subnets mapped to sites, gauge (worst gap).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Tenable](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

```spl
| lookup pci_approved_bssids.csv bssid OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved="false"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen, values(classification) as cls by bssid, ssid, site_id
| sort first_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Import approved corporate BSSIDs after each AP refresh. (2) Correlate with technician work orders. (3) Physical inspection workflow for persistent rogues. (4) Quarterly export for onsite assessor.

### UC-22.11.76 · Penetration Test Finding Severity and Re-test Status (PCI DSS Req 11.4.1, 11.4.5)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks open penetration test findings and re-validation dates so critical issues from annual or incremental tests are remediated within agreed timelines and evidence is ready for PCI validation.
- **App/TA:** `sourcetype="grc:penetration_test"` HEC from reporting portal
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:penetration_test"` — `finding_id`, `severity`, `status`, `retest_date`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:penetration_test" earliest=-365d
| where match(engagement_scope,"(?i)CDE|payment|PCI)")
| where match(status,"(?i)open|accepted_risk")
| eval overdue_retest=if(isnotnull(retest_date) AND now()>retest_date,1,0)
| stats values(severity) as sev, max(overdue_retest) as overdue by finding_id, title
| where match(mvjoin(sev,","),"(?i)critical|high") OR overdue=1
| sort finding_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize severity strings. (2) Link `finding_id` to Jira epics. (3) Monthly steering committee view. (4) Store signed pentest report hash in GRC with Splunk snapshot.
- **Visualization:** Kanban-style table (status), timeline (retest_date), single value (open critical count).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.11.77 · IDS and IPS Alert Volume Baseline and Spike Detection (PCI DSS Req 11.4.1, 11.4.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Applies moving baselines to IDS/IPS alert rates on CDE perimeter sensors so intrusion-detection failures or sudden drops in alerting—often a sign of sensor issues—are visible alongside spikes.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ids` `pan:threat`; `suricata:alert`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ids sourcetype IN ("pan:threat","suricata:alert") earliest=-30d
| lookup pci_sensor_zones.csv sensor OUTPUT cde_adjacent
| where cde_adjacent="true"
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time, signature
| eventstats median(count) as med by signature
| eval spike=if(count>med*4 AND med>10,1,0)
| where spike=1
| table _time, signature, count, med
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune `med>10` for low-noise signatures. (2) Pair drops with health checks. (3) Feed spikes to SOC shift lead. (4) Include in monthly IDS tuning minutes.
- **Visualization:** Line (count by signature top-N), table (spike rows), single value (spike days).
- **CIM Models:** Intrusion_Detection
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.signature span=1d | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Intrusion_Detection](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Intrusion_Detection)

### UC-22.11.78 · Segmentation Control Validation — Scanner IP Blocked at CDE Border as Expected (PCI DSS Req 11.4.1, 1.3.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Confirms that active segmentation tests from non-CDE scanner IPs are denied at the NSC while still logged, producing evidence that untrusted networks cannot reach CHD without passing controls.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` `pan:traffic` with `src` in `pci_segmentation_scanner_ips.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup pci_segmentation_scanner_ips.csv ip AS src OUTPUT is_scanner
| lookup pci_cde_subnets.csv subnet AS dest OUTPUT in_cde
| where is_scanner="true" AND in_cde="true"
| stats count by action, rule, src, dest
| eval expected_deny=if(action IN ("deny","drop","reset-client"),1,0)
| stats sum(expected_deny) as deny_hits, count as total by src
| eval deny_ratio=if(total>0, round(100*deny_hits/total,2), null())
| where deny_ratio<95
| sort src
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Coordinate scanner IP list with annual segmentation test vendor. (2) Alert if `deny_ratio` drops (misconfiguration). (3) Attach results to PCI evidence library. (4) Run after major firewall pushes.
- **Visualization:** Table (src, deny_ratio), bar (action mix), single value (unexpected allows).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.11.79 · Quarterly Internal Scan Remediation Aging Buckets (PCI DSS Req 11.3.1.1, 6.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Buckets open internal scan findings by age so quarterly rescan and fix cycles for high-risk issues remain on schedule and visible to management dashboards.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` `qualys:host` with `FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME`, `STATUS`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:host" earliest=-120d
| where match(TAGS,"(?i)PCI|CDE)")
| where match(STATUS,"(?i)active|new|reopened)")
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(FIRST_FOUND_DATETIME,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"))/86400,1)
| eval bucket=case(age_days<=30,"0-30d", age_days<=60,"31-60d", age_days<=90,"61-90d",1=1,"90+d")
| stats count by bucket, SEVERITY_LEVEL
| sort bucket
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm Qualys timestamp format for `strptime`. (2) Map `SEVERITY_LEVEL` to PCI risk. (3) Executive stack chart weekly. (4) Align `90+d` alerts with remediation charter.
- **Visualization:** Stacked column (bucket x severity), pie (90+d share), table (raw host list drilldown).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Qualys](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2964), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.11.80 · External Network Scan Job Failures and Timeout Trending (PCI DSS Req 11.3.2, 6.3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Operations
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Trends scan job timeouts and authentication failures so external vulnerability scanning remains reliable and gaps do not cause missed quarterly compliance windows.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Qualys (2964)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` `qualys:scan_summary` or vendor API log `qualys:scan_target`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype="qualys:scan_summary" earliest=-180d
| where match(scan_title,"(?i)external|perimeter|PCI)")
| eval failed=if(match(status,"(?i)error|cancel|timeout|auth\\s*fail"),1,0)
| timechart span=30d sum(failed) as failed_jobs, count as jobs
| eval fail_rate=if(jobs>0, round(100*failed_jobs/jobs,2), null())
| trendline sma2(fail_rate) as fail_trend
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Deduplicate rescheduled runs by `scan_id`. (2) Correlate auth fails with credential vault rotations. (3) Alert when `fail_rate` exceeds 10%. (4) Document compensating manual scans if needed.
- **Visualization:** Line (`fail_rate`, `fail_trend`), column (`failed_jobs`), table (last error text truncated).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(Vulnerabilities.cvss) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest span=30d | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Qualys](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2964), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.11.81 · Critical Payment Application Binary and Config File Integrity Alerts (PCI DSS Req 11.5.1, 11.5.1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Monitors change-detection alerts on payment service binaries and configuration files so unauthorized modifications to critical systems are caught in line with intrusion-detection expectations for file integrity.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082)
- **Data Sources:** `index=os` `sourcetype="linux:audit"` paths `/opt/payment/bin`, `crowdstrike:filevantage` if available
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os sourcetype="linux:audit" type="PATH" earliest=-24h
| where match(name,"(?i)/opt/payment/bin/|/etc/payment/[^/]+\\.conf$)")
| where match(nametype,"(?i)create|delete|normal") AND NOT match(tostring(auid),"(?i)unset|^\\s*$") AND auid!="4294967295"
| stats earliest(_time) as t0, values(comm) as process by host, name, auid
| join type=left max=0 host [
    search index=ci sourcetype="jenkins:build" earliest=-26h result="SUCCESS"
    | rename NODE_NAME as host
    | stats latest(_time) as deploy_t by host
  ]
| eval near_deploy=if(isnotnull(deploy_t) AND abs(t0-deploy_t)<=600,1,0)
| where near_deploy=0
| table t0, host, name, process, auid, deploy_t
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune `near_deploy` window to pipeline length. (2) Require signed deploy artifacts with known hashes. (3) Page SOC on `near_deploy=0`. (4) Map to Req 10 FIM evidence where overlapping.
- **Visualization:** Table (unexpected changes), timeline (t0), single value (off-deploy changes per week).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.dest | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5994), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

---

### UC-22.11.82 · Network Topology Drift — New L3 Adjacency or BGP Peer on CDE Perimeter (PCI DSS Req 11.4.1, 1.2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Detects new BGP neighbors or unexpected VLAN routing adjacencies on routers bordering the CDE so network diagrams and segmentation assumptions stay accurate after infrastructure changes.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Cisco IOS syslog
- **Data Sources:** `index=netops` `syslog:cisco` — `%BGP-5-ADJCHANGE`; `pan:system` interface/route events
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netops sourcetype="syslog:cisco" earliest=-7d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)BGP-5-ADJCHANGE|neighbor\\s+[0-9.]+")
| lookup pci_perimeter_devices.csv host OUTPUT pci_border
| where pci_border="true"
| rex "neighbor (?<nbr>\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+)"
| lookup pci_approved_bgp_peers.csv nbr OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved="false"
| stats earliest(_time) as seen by host, nbr
| sort seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain approved peer list per POP. (2) Include maintenance windows in lookup. (3) Immediate network architecture review. (4) Update diagram repository on each approved add.
- **Visualization:** Table (new neighbors), network diagram export link, timeline (seen).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

---

### UC-22.11.83 · Security Awareness Training Completion for Personnel with CDE Access (PCI DSS Req 12.6.1, 12.6.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Measures annual or periodic training completion for users who can access the cardholder environment so the information security awareness program is evidenced by actual completion rates, not policy alone.
- **App/TA:** LMS HEC `sourcetype="knowbe4:training"` or Workday `workday:learning`
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` `sourcetype="knowbe4:training"` — `user`, `course`, `completed_date`, `status`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype="knowbe4:training" earliest=-400d
| lookup pci_cde_users.csv email AS user OUTPUT has_cde_access
| where has_cde_access="true"
| where match(course,"(?i)PCI|security\\s*awareness|phishing)")
| eval completed=if(isnotnull(completed_date) OR match(status,"(?i)completed"),1,0)
| stats sum(completed) as done, count as assigned by user
| eval pct=if(assigned>0, round(100*done/assigned,2), null())
| where pct<100
| sort pct, user
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Sync HR email with IAM quarterly. (2) Auto-remind via SOAR when `pct<100`. (3) Store annual completion CSV for PCI. (4) Exclude contractors with separate policy if applicable via lookup flag.
- **Visualization:** Table (incomplete users), gauge (org completion %), bar (by department from lookup join).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

---

### UC-22.11.84 · Incident Response Plan Tabletop and Live Test Execution Logging (PCI DSS Req 12.10.1, 12.10.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Indexes IR test outcomes and scenario coverage so annual (or more frequent) response exercises for payment breaches are tracked with timestamps, participants, and lessons learned for PCI readiness reviews.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"`
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:ir_test"` — `test_id`, `scenario`, `executed_date`, `result`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:ir_test" earliest=-400d
| where match(scenario,"(?i)payment|breach|PAN|CDE|PCI)")
| stats latest(executed_date) as last_run, latest(result) as outcome by test_id
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_run,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,1)
| where days_since>365 OR isnull(last_run)
| table test_id, last_run, days_since, outcome
| sort days_since
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Push test completion from GRC after each tabletop. (2) Adjust `days_since` threshold if policy requires semi-annual tests. (3) Link `test_id` to remediation tickets. (4) Board briefing attachment optional.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (last_run), table (overdue tests), single value (tests past due).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)

---

### UC-22.11.85 · Formal Risk Assessment Evidence and Residual Risk Score Trend (PCI DSS Req 12.3.1, 12.3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks annual enterprise risk assessment submissions including PCI-scoped risk items and residual scores so targeted risk analyses required by the standard are current and leadership-reviewed.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect or `sourcetype="grc:risk_register"`
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:risk_register"` — `risk_id`, `pci_relevant`, `residual_score`, `review_date`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:risk_register" earliest=-730d
| where pci_relevant="true"
| eval overdue=if(now()>strptime(review_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),1,0)
| stats latest(residual_score) as score, max(overdue) as past_due by risk_id, title
| where past_due=1 OR score>15
| sort score
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align `review_date` to annual cycle. (2) Map `score` scale 1–25 consistently. (3) Escalate `past_due` to risk committee. (4) Export closed reviews for PCI policy pack.
- **Visualization:** Line (residual_score trend in summary index), table (past_due risks), heatmap (domain x score).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

### UC-22.11.86 · Third-Party Service Provider Compliance Scorecard Ingest (PCI DSS Req 12.8.1, 12.8.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Centralizes SOC reports and PCI AOC freshness dates for processors and cloud providers that affect account data so service-provider due diligence and monitoring obligations are continuously visible.
- **App/TA:** `sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard"` HEC from TPRM platform
- **Data Sources:** `index=vendor` `sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard"` — `vendor`, `pci_aoc_expiry`, `soc2_expiry`, `open_findings`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vendor sourcetype="vendor:compliance_scorecard" earliest=-1d
| eval pci_exp_epoch=strptime(pci_aoc_expiry,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_to_pci=round((pci_exp_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| where days_to_pci<60 OR open_findings>0
| table vendor, pci_aoc_expiry, days_to_pci, soc2_expiry, open_findings
| sort days_to_pci
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Automate monthly vendor portal scrape to HEC. (2) Map vendors to in-scope services in lookup. (3) Legal review when `days_to_pci<30`. (4) Attach artifacts in document store with Splunk link.
- **Visualization:** Table (vendors at risk), timeline (expiry dates), single value (count expiring <60d).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.11.87 · Acceptable Use Policy Annual Attestation Completion (PCI DSS Req 12.1, 12.6.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks which employees have electronically signed the acceptable use policy each year so security policies acknowledged by users are provable for HR and PCI policy interviews.
- **App/TA:** Workday / ServiceNow attestation HEC `sourcetype="hr:policy_attestation"`
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` `sourcetype="hr:policy_attestation"` — `policy_code`, `user`, `signed_date`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype="hr:policy_attestation" policy_code="AUP_V2026" earliest=-400d
| stats latest(signed_date) as last_signed by user
| eval signed_epoch=strptime(last_signed,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval stale=if(signed_epoch<relative_time(now(),"-365d@d"),1,0)
| where stale=1 OR isnull(last_signed)
| table user, last_signed, stale
| sort user
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Version `policy_code` each revision. (2) Join workforce roster to find never-signed. (3) Manager digest weekly until 100%. (4) Export completion CSV for PCI annual evidence.
- **Visualization:** Gauge (% current), table (stale users), bar (by department).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.11.88 · Security Roles and Responsibilities Assignment Completeness (PCI DSS Req 12.1.3, 12.5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Verifies each PCI-defined security role in the responsibility matrix has a named primary and backup assignee in the HR or IAM feed so accountability for security activities is not ambiguous during audits.
- **App/TA:** `sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment"` HEC from IAM
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` `sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment"` — `role_name`, `primary_user`, `backup_user`, `effective_date`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype="iam:pci_role_assignment" earliest=-1d
| where match(role_name,"(?i)CISO|PCI\\s*Lead|IR\\s*Lead|NetSec|DBA\\s*CHD)")
| eval missing_primary=isnull(primary_user) OR primary_user=""
| eval missing_backup=isnull(backup_user) OR backup_user=""
| where missing_primary=1 OR missing_backup=1
| table role_name, primary_user, backup_user, effective_date
| sort role_name
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align `role_name` strings with PCI responsibility matrix. (2) Daily sync from Workday/Azure AD groups. (3) Page HRIS on gaps. (4) Quarterly executive sign-off stored in GRC.
- **Visualization:** Table (gaps), matrix visual (role x assignee), single value (open gaps).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.11.89 · Technology Acceptable Use — USB Mass Storage on CDE Workstations (PCI DSS Req 12.3.2, 2.2.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Surfaces device plug events for removable media on in-scope workstations so technology usage policies restricting untrusted storage near account data are enforced and auditable.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` `Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration` or `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 6416
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration" earliest=-7d
| where match(ClassName,"(?i)USBSTOR|diskdrive)")
| lookup pci_cde_workstations.csv ComputerName OUTPUT in_cde_ws
| where in_cde_ws="true"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_insert, values(DeviceDescription) as dev by ComputerName, InstanceId
| sort - first_insert
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable PnP operational log forwarding on CDE laptops if any exist. (2) Block at GPO where possible; use this as detective control. (3) Investigate each event. (4) Map to acceptable use training module completion.
- **Visualization:** Table (devices), timeline (first_insert), single value (weekly USB attempts).
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.11.90 · Annual Information Security Policy Review and Approval Workflow (PCI DSS Req 12.1.1, 12.1.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI DSS v4.0
- **Value:** Tracks policy document versions, review due dates, and approver signatures so the security policy is reviewed at least annually and management acknowledges changes affecting the CDE.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928) or `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"`
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype="grc:policy_review"` — `policy_id`, `version`, `next_review_date`, `approver`, `status`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:policy_review" earliest=-730d
| where match(policy_id,"(?i)PCI|INFOSEC|ACCESS|NETWORK)")
| eval due_epoch=strptime(next_review_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()>due_epoch AND NOT match(status,"(?i)approved|published"),1,0)
| stats latest(version) as ver, latest(status) as st, max(overdue) as od by policy_id
| where od=1
| table policy_id, ver, st, next_review_date
| sort policy_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Push policy metadata whenever GRC workflow updates. (2) Link to document repository deep links. (3) Alert compliance manager 60 days before `next_review_date`. (4) Archive approved PDF hash in same event.
- **Visualization:** Table (overdue policies), timeline (next_review_date), gauge (% policies current).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### 22.10 HIPAA

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** EHR/EMR audit logs (Epic, Cerner/Oracle Health), Windows Security Event Logs, Active Directory, Microsoft 365 audit logs, VPN/remote access logs, database audit trails (MSSQL, Oracle), endpoint protection logs, email DLP events, physical access (badge) systems, medical device integration logs, backup system logs, HR/identity management feeds.

### UC-22.10.1 · HIPAA Risk Analysis Evidence — Asset & ePHI System Inventory (§164.308(a)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Correlates systems that store or transmit ePHI with vulnerability and patch posture so security leaders can evidence an ongoing, documented risk analysis aligned with the Security Rule.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=assets` (CMDB/ES assets), `index=vulnerability` (Tenable), `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:integration` (interface engine hosts), Windows Security 4688/5156 for clinical app servers
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=assets sourcetype IN ("cmdb:server","es:assets")) OR (index=vulnerability sourcetype="tenable:vuln")
| eval ephi_system=if(match(lower(_raw),"epic|cerner|clarity|caboodle|emr|ehr|phi|hipaa"),1,0)
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen values(sourcetype) as sources dc(host) as hosts by system_name, ip
| lookup ephi_system_register.csv ip OUTPUT contains_ephi, data_owner, baa_id
| where contains_ephi="true" OR ephi_system=1
| sort - last_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `ephi_system_register.csv` with IP/CIDR, owner, and BAA ID; (2) normalize host/IP fields across CMDB and vuln feeds; (3) schedule weekly and route gaps to GRC for formal risk register updates; (4) attach CVSS and compensating controls in ticketing.
- **Visualization:** Table (system, owner, last vuln scan), Bar chart (critical findings by ePHI tier), Single value (unscanned ePHI hosts).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities (when Tenable is CIM-tagged), N/A for mixed CMDB
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Vulnerabilities.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - agg_value
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

---

### UC-22.10.2 · HIPAA Risk Management — Control Deficiency Tracking (§164.308(a)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Tracks open HIPAA control gaps from assessments and pen tests through remediation so risk is reduced to reasonable and appropriate levels, not just documented once.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `` `notable` `` or `index=grc` HEC JSON (`control_id`, `framework`, `status`, `due_date`, `owner`) from GRC exports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="grc:hipaa_control" earliest=-90d framework="HIPAA_Security_Rule"
| eval overdue=if(status!="Closed" AND now()>strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),1,0)
| stats count as open_controls sum(overdue) as overdue_controls by owner, control_family
| sort - overdue_controls
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define control_family values mapping to 164.308/310/312 families; (2) ingest weekly GRC CSV/HEC; (3) alert on `overdue_controls>0`; (4) require closure notes referencing implemented safeguard.
- **Visualization:** Table (owner, open, overdue), Column chart (by control family), Timeline (median days to close).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.10.3 · Information System Activity Review — Cross-Source ePHI Access Summary (§164.308(a)(1)(ii)(D))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Produces a daily leadership-ready rollup of who touched ePHI across EHR, VPN, and databases so workforce activity review obligations are operationally met, not ad hoc.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, `index=vpn` `sourcetype=paloalto:globalprotect`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart") OR (index=cerner sourcetype="cerner:audit" Action="PatientChartView") OR (index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" Application="*Hyperspace*") OR (index=vpn sourcetype="paloalto:globalprotect" status="success")
| eval user=coalesce(USER_ID, PractitionerId, user, src_user)
| eval PAT_ID=coalesce(PAT_ID, PatientId)
| bin _time span=1d
| stats dc(PAT_ID) as patients_touched dc(host) as systems by _time, user
| sort - patients_touched
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align user ID formats via `identity_lookup.csv`; (2) restrict index ACLs to privacy/security; (3) schedule daily PDF/dashboard to HIPAA committee; (4) retain per legal record retention policy.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (users x day), Table (top reviewers), Single value (distinct users accessing charts).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication (VPN when CIM-tagged), N/A for Epic native
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Authentication.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user span=1d | sort - agg_value
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [Splunk Add-on for Citrix](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.10.4 · Workforce Clearance — HR Hire vs AD Account Creation (§164.308(a)(3))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Flags Active Directory accounts created or enabled before HR clearance effective date, supporting workforce clearance procedures for roles with ePHI access.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode IN (4720,4722,4738), `index=hr` `sourcetype=workday:worker` or CSV HEC (`employee_id`, `clearance_status`, `effective_date`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=windows EventCode IN ("4720","4722") TargetUserName="*") OR (index=hr sourcetype="workday:worker")
| eval emp_id=coalesce(employee_id, TargetUserName)
| eval event_type=if(EventCode="4720","AccountCreated",if(EventCode="4722","AccountEnabled","HRRecord"))
| stats earliest(_time) as first_ad latest(_time) as last_hr by emp_id, event_type
| join type=outer emp_id [
    search index=hr sourcetype="workday:worker" earliest=-180d
    | eval clearance_epoch=strptime(effective_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
    | table employee_id, clearance_status, clearance_epoch
    | rename employee_id as emp_id ]
| eval violation=if(first_ad < clearance_epoch AND clearance_status="Cleared","PreClearance_AD",null())
| where isnotnull(violation)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize timestamps to UTC; (2) map service accounts to exclusion lookup; (3) route violations to IAM and HR; (4) quarterly attestation report.
- **Visualization:** Table (account, violation, timestamps), Single value (violations 30d).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication (optional for 4624 correlation)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.10.5 · Termination — Access Revocation Within Policy Window (§164.308(a)(3)(ii)(C))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Detects logons, VPN sessions, or EHR access after HR termination timestamp so access revocation SLAs are provably enforced.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4624, `index=citrix`, `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `index=hr` terminations (`term_date`, `samaccountname`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype="workday:termination" earliest=-30d
| eval term_epoch=strptime(term_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| table samaccountname, term_epoch
| rename samaccountname as user
| join type=inner user [
    search index=windows EventCode="4624" earliest=-30d
    | eval user=mvindex(Security_ID,0)
    | rex field=user "\\\\(?<sam>[^\\\\]+)$"
    | eval user=coalesce(sam,user)
    | table _time, user, WorkstationName, Logon_Type ]
| where _time > term_epoch
| eval hours_after_term=round((_time-term_epoch)/3600,2)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest authoritative termination feed within 15 minutes SLA; (2) tune out break-glass shared accounts; (3) auto-disable via SOAR optional; (4) weekly HIPAA access committee digest.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, hours_after_term, workstation), Timeline (violations), Single value (count).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Citrix](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.10.6 · Security Awareness & Training — Phish Simulation Failure to ePHI Risk (§164.308(a)(5))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Links repeated phishing test failures to subsequent risky ePHI handling patterns so awareness gaps become targeted coaching, not checkbox training.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055)
- **Data Sources:** `index=security_awareness` HEC (`campaign_id`, `user`, `result`), `index=o365` `sourcetype=ms:o365:management` (DLP/alerts), `index=epic` chart exports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=security_awareness sourcetype="phish:sim" result="Clicked" earliest=-90d
| stats count as fail_clicks by user
| where fail_clicks>=3
| map maxsearches=50 search="index=epic sourcetype=\"epic:audit\" USER_ID=\"$user$\" AccessType IN (\"Report\",\"Export\") earliest=-90d | stats count as risky_epic_events by USER_ID"
| sort - risky_epic_events
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Prefer `lookup` + subsearch limits for scale; (2) de-identify in dashboards; (3) HR/privacy joint review; (4) document coaching in LMS export.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, fails, risky events), Bar chart (failures by department from HR lookup).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)

### UC-22.10.7 · Login Monitoring & Security Incident Procedures — Brute Force to Clinical SSO (§164.308(a)(6))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Surfaces credential stuffing and password spray against Hyperspace/SSO endpoints so incidents are detected and handled under HIPAA security incident procedures.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ad` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security` EventCode=4625, `index=okta` `sourcetype=OktaIM2:log` (if used), `index=citrix` AAA failures
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ad sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode="4625" earliest=-24h
| eval user=TargetUserName
| eval src=src_ip
| stats count values(EventCode) as codes dc(ComputerName) as dc_used by user, src
| where count>=15 AND dc_used>=3
| lookup hipaa_clinical_apps.csv src OUTPUT app_name
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Baseline legitimate auth patterns during go-live; (2) integrate with ES notable or SOAR playbooks; (3) document IR steps for suspected credential compromise affecting ePHI.
- **Visualization:** Choropleth (src geo if iplocation enabled), Table (user, src, count), Single value (distinct locked accounts).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.10.8 · Contingency Plan — Backup Job Success for ePHI Databases (§164.308(a)(7))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Monitors backup completion for Clarity/Caboodle and clinical data stores so availability safeguards in the contingency plan are continuously evidenced.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=backup` `sourcetype=commvault:job` or `rubrik:events`, SQL `msdb.dbo.backupset` via DBX, Epic Clarity backup logs if forwarded
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=backup sourcetype="commvault:job" earliest=-48h (database="CLARITY" OR database="CABOODLE" OR app="Epic*")
| eval success=if(match(lower(status),"success|completed"),1,0)
| stats sum(success) as ok_jobs count as total_jobs latest(_time) as last_job by database, client_name
| eval health=if(ok_jobs=total_jobs,"OK","FAIL")
| where health!="OK"
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag databases containing ePHI in backup catalog; (2) page DBAs on failure; (3) quarterly restore test outcomes ingested as separate sourcetype for correlation.
- **Visualization:** Single value (failed jobs), Table (DB, last success), Timeline (job duration anomalies).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

### UC-22.10.9 · Periodic Evaluation — Control Test Evidence Ingest (§164.308(a)(8))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Centralizes periodic technical test results (MFA coverage, encryption checks) with timestamps for HIPAA periodic technical and non-technical evaluation evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (HEC), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=hipaa:periodic_test` JSON lines from Qualys/Azure Policy/AWS Config exporters
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="hipaa:periodic_test" earliest=-365d test_family="HIPAA_Technical"
| eval period_month=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m")
| stats latest(result) as last_result latest(_time) as last_run by control_id, period_month
| where last_result!="Pass"
| sort period_month, control_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Standardize `result` enum Pass/Fail/Partial; (2) join to asset inventory; (3) annual HIPAA evaluation deck exports from this dataset.
- **Visualization:** Matrix (control x month), Table (failing controls), Column chart (pass rate trend).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

### UC-22.10.10 · Business Associate Contracts — BAA Coverage for Connected Systems (§164.308(a)(1)(ii)(B))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Highlights new interfaces or SaaS tenants talking to ePHI systems without a registered BAA so legal can close contract gaps before production traffic carries PHI.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:interface` (remote host, interface name), `lookup baa_register.csv` (`vendor`, `hostname`, `baa_status`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:interface" earliest=-7d
| stats latest(_time) as last_seen values(interface_name) as ifaces by remote_host
| lookup baa_register.csv hostname as remote_host OUTPUT vendor, baa_status, baa_expiry
| where isnull(baa_status) OR baa_status!="Active"
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_seen)/86400,1)
| sort - last_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate `baa_register.csv` from contract repository; (2) integrate ServiceNow vendor risk tasks; (3) block firewall exceptions until BAA recorded (process outside Splunk).
- **Visualization:** Table (host, vendor, BAA gap), Bar chart (new hosts per week without BAA).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

### UC-22.10.11 · Business Associate Agreements — Expiry & Auto-Renewal Monitoring (§164.308(b)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Audit
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Prevents lapsed BAAs for vendors that process ePHI by tracking expiry, auto-renew flags, and required amendment workflows.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud (CSV/HEC)
- **Data Sources:** `index=legal` `sourcetype=ironclad:contract` or scheduled CSV `baa_master.csv` (`vendor_id`, `baa_end`, `auto_renew`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup baa_master.csv
| eval days_to_end=round((strptime(baa_end,"%Y-%m-%d")-now())/86400,0)
| eval alert_tier=case(days_to_end<0,"EXPIRED",days_to_end<=30,"CRITICAL",days_to_end<=90,"WARN",1=1,"OK")
| where alert_tier!="OK"
| table vendor_id, vendor_name, baa_end, auto_renew, alert_tier, data_categories
| sort days_to_end
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Nightly refresh from CLM API; (2) route CRITICAL to legal ops; (3) map vendor_id to technical assets for coverage attestation.
- **Visualization:** Single value (expired count), Table (upcoming renewals), Timeline (signature dates).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.10.12 · Sanction Policy — Privileged Abuse on EHR Audit Logs (§164.308(a)(1)(ii)(C))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Detects workforce sanctions triggers such as repeated self-family lookups or audit log tampering attempts by IT admins supporting HIPAA sanction policy enforcement.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` (`USER_ID`, `PAT_ID`, `AccessType`), HR relationship file `employee_dependents.csv` (hashed IDs)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-30d
| lookup employee_dependents.csv employee_id as USER_ID OUTPUT dependent_patient_hash
| eval pat_hash=sha256(PAT_ID)
| where pat_hash=dependent_patient_hash
| stats count by USER_ID, PAT_ID
| where count>=1
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Use salted hashes only; (2) legal/privacy approval for dependent mapping; (3) integrate HR sanctions workflow; (4) false positive review for legitimate care team overlap.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, event count), Link analysis graph (user-patient edges).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### UC-22.10.13 · Unique User Identification — Shared or Generic EHR Accounts (§164.312(a)(2)(i))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Finds authentication and EHR events tied to shared kiosk or interface accounts so every action can be attributed to an identified user as required for ePHI access.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, `lookup forbidden_shared_accounts.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit") OR (index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session")
| eval account=upper(coalesce(USER_ID, UserName))
| lookup forbidden_shared_accounts.csv account OUTPUT policy
| where isnotnull(policy)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats dc(PAT_ID) as patients dc(client_ip) as src_ips count as events by account, _time
| where events>10
| sort - events
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain list of prohibited generic accounts; (2) push exceptions only via CAB-approved break-glass IDs; (3) correlate Citrix `ClientName` for kiosk attribution where possible.
- **Visualization:** Table (account, events, patients), Timeline (shared account spikes).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication (Citrix/AD when CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Authentication.src) as agg_value from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - agg_value
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Citrix](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.10.14 · Emergency Access Procedure — Downtime / Break-Glass Account Usage (§164.312(a)(2)(ii))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Audits emergency and downtime accounts touching production ePHI so procedural safeguards around emergency access are reviewable and time-bound.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` 4624 for `svc_epic_emergency*`, `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` `AccessContext="Downtime"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=windows EventCode="4622" OR EventCode="4624") Account_Name="svc_epic_emergency*" earliest=-30d
| append [
    search index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessContext="*" earliest=-30d
    | rex field=AccessContext "(?<ctx>Downtime|Emergency)"
    | where ctx IN ("Downtime","Emergency") ]
| eval principal=coalesce(Account_Name, USER_ID)
| stats earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last dc(host) as systems by principal, ctx
| eval duration_h=round((last-first)/3600,2)
| sort - last
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require post-event attestation form ID in separate ticketing sourcetype and join; (2) auto-expire passwords via IAM; (3) weekly privacy review of all uses.
- **Visualization:** Table (principal, duration, systems), Single value (uses per month).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Authentication.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.10.15 · Automatic Logoff — Stale Clinical Workstation Sessions (§164.312(a)(2)(iii))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Identifies unusually long Citrix/VDI sessions and unlocked Windows consoles in patient care areas where automatic logoff may be misconfigured.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=citrix` session start/end, `WinEventLog:Security` 4800/4801 (workstation lock)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d
| eval start=strptime(SessionStart,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end=if(isnotnull(SessionEnd), strptime(SessionEnd,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), now())
| eval session_hours=round((end-start)/3600,2)
| where session_hours>12 AND match(Application,"Hyperspace|Epic")
| stats max(session_hours) as max_hrs by UserName, client_ip, PublishedApplication
| sort - max_hrs
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align with published idle timeout policy (e.g., 30 min); (2) tune for legitimate long shifts using department lookup; (3) feed findings to desktop engineering for GPO fixes.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (max session by dept), Table (user, app, hours).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Citrix](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

---

### UC-22.10.16 · Encryption of ePHI at Rest — TDE / BitLocker Status for Clinical Datastores (§164.312(a)(2)(iv))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Surfaces SQL Server instances hosting Clarity that lack Transparent Data Encryption or hosts missing full-disk encryption so at-rest ePHI protections meet the addressable implementation specification evidence bar.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server (Splunkbase 2648), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `mssql:audit` or scripted input `sqlserver:tde_status`, `WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-BitLocker/Operational`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=database sourcetype="sqlserver:tde_status" earliest=-1d
| eval encrypted=if(match(lower(encryption_state),"encrypted|on"),1,0)
| lookup ephi_sql_instances.csv instance_name OUTPUT contains_phi, owner_team
| where contains_phi="true" AND encrypted=0
| stats latest(_time) as last_check by instance_name, database_name, owner_team
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Collect TDE via DB Connect scheduled statement against `sys.databases`; (2) ingest BitLocker compliance from SCCM/Intune if available; (3) track exceptions with approved risk acceptance IDs.
- **Visualization:** Table (instance, DB, encryption state), Single value (non-compliant count).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2648), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### UC-22.10.17 · Audit Controls — High-Volume ePHI Read Baseline & Anomaly (§164.312(b))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Uses statistical baselines on audited ePHI reads to detect abnormal access volumes indicative of snooping or compromised credentials while proving audit control operation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` (`USER_ID`, `PAT_ID`, `AccessType`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-90d
| bin _time span=1d
| stats dc(PAT_ID) as daily_patients by USER_ID, _time
| eventstats median(daily_patients) as med by USER_ID
| eval z=(daily_patients-med)/sqrt(med+0.001)
| where z>4 AND daily_patients>30
| sort - z
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Use `anomalydetection` command alternatively for streaming; (2) exclude legitimate roles via `hipaa_role_exclusions.csv`; (3) route to privacy investigations queue.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (daily patients by user), Table (spikes), Scatter (median vs spike).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)

---

### UC-22.10.18 · Integrity — Unexpected UPDATE/DELETE on PHI Tables (Clarity/Caboodle) (§164.312(c)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Alerts on bulk or off-hours SQL changes to tables holding patient demographics or clinical facts so improper alteration of ePHI is detected and investigated.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server (2648)
- **Data Sources:** `index=mssql` `sourcetype=mssql:audit` (`statement`, `schema_name`, `object_name`, `application_name`, `session_server_principal_name`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=mssql sourcetype="mssql:audit" earliest=-24h action_class="DML"
| where match(lower(object_name),"pat|patient|phi|epic") AND match(lower(statement),"update|delete")
| eval is_bulk=if(match(statement,"(?i)where\s+1=1|truncate|delete\s+from"),1,0)
| where is_bulk=1 OR (match(lower(application_name),"microsoft sql server management studio") AND _time < relative_time(now(),"@d+18h"))
| stats count by session_server_principal_name, object_name, application_name
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable SQL Server Audit to SIEM; (2) map ETL service accounts to allow list; (3) pair with change tickets from ServiceNow; (4) retain immutable copy to WORM storage if required.
- **Visualization:** Table (principal, object, count), Timeline (bulk ops).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2648)

---

### UC-22.10.19 · Entity Authentication — Smart Card / Certificate Logon Failures (§164.312(d))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Tracks failed certificate-based logons to clinical workstations and VDI pools so weak or expired credentials cannot silently undermine strong authentication for ePHI.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode IN (4625,4776) with `Authentication_Package=Negotiate` and `Key_Length>0`, PKI OCSP events if available
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode="4625" earliest=-24h
| search Logon_Process="Kerberos" OR Authentication_Package="Negotiate"
| eval fail_reason=Status, Sub_Status
| stats count values(WorkstationName) as workstations by TargetUserName, fail_reason, src_ip
| where count>=5
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enrich with AD `userCertificate` expiry via scripted lookup; (2) alert on clusters before smart card lockouts impact patient care; (3) document PKI renewal procedures.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, reason, count), Choropleth (src_ip).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.10.20 · Transmission Security — TLS 1.0/1.1 Deprecation for EHR Integrations (§164.312(e)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Highlights legacy TLS usage on connections carrying HL7/HTTPS to integration engines so transmission safeguards for ePHI meet current encryption standards.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Stream (1809) or Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk (`Splunk_TA_paloalto`), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:decrypted` OR `stream:tls` fields (`ssl_version`, `dest_ip`, `app`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network (sourcetype="pan:decrypted" OR sourcetype="stream:tls") earliest=-7d
| eval tls=coalesce(ssl_version, tls_version)
| where tls IN ("TLSv1","TLSv1.0","TLSv1.1","SSLv3")
| lookup ephi_integration_subnets.csv dest_ip OUTPUT integration_name, contains_ephi
| where contains_ephi="true"
| stats count by dest_ip, integration_name, tls, app
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate integration subnet lookup from interface inventory; (2) partner with integration team on phased TLS upgrades; (3) verify after change with same search zero results.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (legacy TLS hits by integration), Table (src/dest/app).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic (when mapped)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.app | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [Splunk_TA_paloalto](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)
