### UC-22.10.21 · Access Control — Role-Based Violations (Coder Accessing Medication Admin) (§164.312(a)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Detects EHR workspace or activity codes inconsistent with HR job role, supporting least-privilege access control for ePHI workflows.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` (`Activity`, `USER_ID`), `lookup epic_user_roles.csv` (`USER_ID`, `hr_role`, `allowed_activities`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" earliest=-24h
| lookup epic_user_roles.csv USER_ID OUTPUT hr_role, allowed_activities mvsep="|"
| eval allowed=if(isnull(allowed_activities), "*", allowed_activities)
| where isnotnull(hr_role) AND NOT match(Activity, allowed)
| stats count values(Activity) as bad_activities by USER_ID, hr_role
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Refresh role mapping nightly from IAM; (2) tune with clinical float pools; (3) integrate with provisioning QA before go-live.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, role, violations), Heatmap (activity vs role).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---

### UC-22.10.22 · Remote ePHI Access — MFA Gap for VPN + O365 Clinical Mail (§164.312(e)(1) / §164.308(a)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Surfaces successful VPN or Exchange Online logons without MFA claim for users in ePHI-privileged groups so remote access meets organizational authentication policy.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=o365` `sourcetype=ms:o365:reporting` or `Audit.Exchange` / `AzureActiveDirectory` JSON, `index=vpn` GlobalProtect logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload="AzureActiveDirectory" Operation="UserLoggedIn" earliest=-24h
| eval mfa=if(match(ExtendedProperties,"MFA\s*completed|multi-factor"),1,0)
| lookup hipaa_ephi_users.csv user OUTPUT in_ephi_group
| where in_ephi_group="true" AND mfa=0
| stats count by user, ClientIP, ApplicationId
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm `ExtendedProperties` field names for your tenant; (2) include Conditional Access exclusions report; (3) feed to IAM for forced enrollment.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, IP, count), Map (ClientIP), Single value (non-MFA logons).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication (if O365 CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.10.23 · FHIR / SMART on FHIR App Access to ePHI Scopes (§164.312(d))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Monitors OAuth token grants and FHIR resource access so only authorized applications and scopes can read or write clinical data exposed via interoperability APIs.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), HTTP Event Collector (HEC)
- **Data Sources:** `index=interop` `sourcetype=epic:fhir_audit` or Azure API Management / Apigee logs (`client_id`, `scope`, `patient`, `status_code`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=interop sourcetype="epic:fhir_audit" earliest=-24h
| eval scopes=split(scope," ")
| mvexpand scopes
| lookup fhir_app_register.csv client_id OUTPUT approved_scopes_mv
| eval approved=if(isnotnull(mvfind(approved_scopes_mv,scopes)),1,0)
| where approved=0 AND status_code IN ("200","201")
| stats count by client_id, scopes, user_id, patient_id
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest authorization server logs with scope strings; (2) maintain app registry with approved scopes; (3) block or revoke tokens via IAM integration for critical hits.
- **Visualization:** Table (client, scope, hits), Sankey (client → resource type).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

---

### UC-22.10.24 · Medical Device Integration — Unapproved HL7 Feeds to EMPI (§164.312(a)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Detects new sending facilities or IP addresses pushing ADT/ORM traffic into the integration engine without change control, protecting access control around device-mediated ePHI flows.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Stream (1809) or `Splunk_TA_f5` / load balancer logs, Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=hl7` `sourcetype=hl7:receive` (`MSH-4`, `MSH-3`, `sending_ip`), `lookup approved_hl7_senders.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hl7 sourcetype="hl7:receive" earliest=-7d
| rex field=_raw "^MSH\\|[^|]*\\|[^|]*\\|(?<sending_app>[^|]*)\\|(?<sending_facility>[^|]*)"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen dc(PID) as patients by sending_ip, sending_facility, sending_app
| lookup approved_hl7_senders.csv sending_ip sending_facility OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved="false"
| sort - patients
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize HL7 with TA or `rex` consistently; (2) integrate Mirth/Interface engine connection table; (3) auto-ticket network security for rogue feeds.
- **Visualization:** Table (IP, facility, patients), Node graph (sender → engine).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.10.25 · Endpoint Controls — ePHI Clipboard/Print from VDI (§164.312(a)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Tracks clipboard redirection and client drive mapping events on clinical VDI where policies should block exfiltration paths for ePHI.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082)
- **Data Sources:** `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session` ICA channel flags, `index=endpoint` `sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts` or `ProcessRollup2` with clipboard tools
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d ClientDrive="On" OR Clipboard="ClientToServer"
| lookup hipaa_epic_users.csv UserName OUTPUT is_clinical_user
| where is_clinical_user="true"
| stats count by UserName, client_ip, PublishedApplication, ClientDrive, Clipboard
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align Citrix policy names with log fields; (2) validate expected exceptions (transcription vendors); (3) remediate via Citrix policy sets.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, flags, count), Bar chart (policy violations by site).
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint (CrowdStrike when CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.user | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Citrix](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5994), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.10.26 · Transmission Security — Unencrypted SMTP with PHI Patterns (§164.312(e)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Alerts when internal mail relays accept messages on port 25 without TLS that contain medical record number or DOB patterns, evidencing safeguards for ePHI in motion.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for Stream (1809)
- **Data Sources:** `index=mail` `sourcetype=proofpoint:mta` or `ms:o365:reporting` MessageTrace, `stream:smtp` metadata
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=mail sourcetype="proofpoint:mta" earliest=-24h
| eval tls_used=if(match(lower(smtp_tls_status),"none|no|plaintext"),0,1)
| regex subject="(?i)\b(MRN|medical record|DOB|date of birth)\b"
| where tls_used=0
| stats count values(rcpt) as recipients by sender, subject, client_ip
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Prefer DLP classification fields when available instead of regex on body; (2) route to messaging team for forced TLS connectors; (3) document exceptions for legacy lab devices with compensating controls.
- **Visualization:** Table (sender, subject snippet, recipients), Single value (plaintext PHI candidates).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055), [Splunk Add-on for Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809)

### UC-22.10.27 · Integrity — Caboodle / Clarity ETL Job Failures & Partial Loads (§164.312(c)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Detects failed or partial analytics warehouse loads that could silently skew quality reporting built on ePHI, supporting integrity of data used for care and compliance analytics.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=etl` `sourcetype=sqlserver:agent_job` or Informatica/SSIS logs, `index=epic` Caboodle job status HEC
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=etl sourcetype="sqlserver:agent_job" earliest=-48h (job_name="*CABOODLE*" OR job_name="*CLARITY*")
| eval ok=if(match(lower(run_status),"succeeded|success"),1,0)
| stats latest(run_status) as last_status latest(_time) as last_run sum(ok) as ok_runs count as runs by job_name, server_name
| eval health=if(ok_runs=runs,"OK","DEGRADED")
| where health!="OK"
| sort last_run
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map job names per environment; (2) create ITSI KPI for critical ETL health; (3) correlate with downstream dashboard freshness checks.
- **Visualization:** Single value (failed jobs), Table (job, server, status), Timeline (duration).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

---

### UC-22.10.28 · Workstation Security — Unattended Unlocked Sessions in Clinical Pods (§164.310(b))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Correlates long gaps between workstation unlock events in nursing stations with active Hyperspace sessions to reduce shoulder-surfing and unauthorized viewing risks.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` 4801 (unlock), 4800 (lock), `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit` from same `Workstation_ID`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN ("4800","4801") earliest=-7d
| eval action=if(EventCode="4800","lock","unlock")
| transaction WorkstationName startswith=eval(action="unlock") endswith=eval(action="lock") maxspan=8h
| eval idle_during=if(duration>3600 AND eventcount<3,1,0)
| where idle_during=1
| table WorkstationName, duration, user, _time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Deploy only on designated clinical OU to limit noise; (2) pair with physical security rounds checklist; (3) educate on clean desk + lock policy.
- **Visualization:** Histogram (session durations), Table (worst workstations).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

---

### UC-22.10.29 · Device & Media Controls — USB Mass Storage on ePHI Workstations (§164.310(d)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Captures removable media mount attempts on endpoints that access Hyperspace so device and media control policies for ePHI workstations are enforceable and auditable.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration` 400, `index=endpoint` `sourcetype=crowdstrike:usb` if available
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Kernel-PnP/Configuration" EventCode="400" earliest=-7d
| where match(ClassName,"USBSTOR|Disk&Ven_")
| lookup clinical_workstations.csv ComputerName OUTPUT contains_ephi_workstation
| where contains_ephi_workstation="true"
| stats count by ComputerName, ClassName, DeviceID
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce BitLocker + DLP on exceptions; (2) require temporary USB grants via ServiceNow with ticket ID in log annotation optional; (3) quarterly exception review.
- **Visualization:** Table (host, device class, count), Single value (violations).
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5994), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.10.30 · Media Controls — Large PHI Print Jobs to Non-Secure Printers (§164.310(d)(2))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Monitors unusually large chart print jobs routed to non-secure print queues so physical disclosure risks from uncontrolled hardcopy are reduced.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational` 307 (`Document`, `Pages`, `PrinterName`, `UserName`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype="WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-PrintService/Operational" EventCode="307" earliest=-7d
| eval pages=tonumber(Pages)
| lookup secure_print_queues.csv PrinterName OUTPUT secure_queue
| where (isnull(secure_queue) OR secure_queue="false") AND pages>=50
| stats sum(pages) as total_pages count as jobs by UserName, PrinterName
| sort - total_pages
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Classify printers by site/zone; (2) integrate Pharos/Equitrac if used; (3) educate on minimum necessary printing.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, printer, pages), Bar chart (jobs by location).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### UC-22.10.31 · Facility vs Logical Access — Badge-In Without VPN/SSO for Remote Roles (§164.310(a)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Correlates physical badge events with absence of corresponding VPN or SSO session for users tagged as onsite-only, highlighting impossible travel or credential sharing.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), HTTP Event Collector (HEC)
- **Data Sources:** `index=pacs` or `index=physical` `sourcetype=genetec:access` (`badge_id`, `reader`, `result`), `index=vpn` GlobalProtect, `lookup badge_to_user.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=physical sourcetype="genetec:access" result="Granted" earliest=-1d
| lookup badge_to_user.csv badge_id OUTPUT samaccountname, work_mode
| where work_mode="OnsiteOnly"
| eval day=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=left samaccountname, day [
    search index=vpn sourcetype="paloalto:globalprotect" earliest=-1d
    | eval day=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d")
    | stats earliest(_time) as vpn_first by src_user, day
    | rename src_user as samaccountname ]
| where isnull(vpn_first)
| stats count by samaccountname, reader, day
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize time zones for multi-campus; (2) tune for vendor remote workers; (3) integrate HR work location attribute.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, reader, days), Map (reader sites).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---

### UC-22.10.32 · Workstation Use — After-Hours Login from Non-Clinical IP Space (§164.310(b) / §164.310(c))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Flags Citrix/VDI launches for inpatient CPOE roles originating from residential ISP ASNs outside hospital guest WiFi, supporting workstation use policies in shared clinical environments.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, MaxMind `iplocation`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H")
| where (hour<6 OR hour>22)
| iplocation client_ip
| lookup clinical_role_users.csv UserName OUTPUT role
| where role IN ("RN_INPATIENT","MD_INPATIENT") AND NOT match(Org,"*Health System*")
| stats count by UserName, client_ip, Country, City, Org
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Replace `Org` heuristic with ASN lookup table; (2) allow VPN concentrator ranges; (3) route to privacy for workforce policy review.
- **Visualization:** Map (client_ip), Table (user, ASN), Timeline (after-hours spikes).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic (optional enrichment)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Citrix](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

| lookup epic_active_careteam.csv PAT_ID USER_ID as member_id OUTPUT relation

| stats count dc(PAT_ID) as patients by USER_ID
| where count>=5
| sort - count

### UC-22.10.34 · Break-Glass — Emergency Access Reason Codes & Post-Review (§164.502(a)(2)(ii) / policy)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Ensures every break-glass chart override includes a reason code and is followed by supervisor attestation within policy timelines.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:breakglass` (`USER_ID`, `PAT_ID`, `reason_code`, `session_id`), `index=itsm` ServiceNow tasks (`short_description`, `parent_incident`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:breakglass" earliest=-30d
| eval has_reason=if(isnotnull(reason_code) AND reason_code!="","OK","MISSING")
| join type=left session_id [
    search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d short_description="*break*glass*"
    | rename number as attestation_ticket, sys_id as session_id ]
| eval reviewed=if(isnotnull(attestation_ticket),"Yes","No")
| where has_reason!="OK" OR reviewed="No"
| table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, reason_code, has_reason, reviewed
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map `session_id` join keys to your ticketing design; (2) 24h alert on missing reason; (3) 72h alert on missing attestation; (4) quarterly privacy committee rollup.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (open attestations), Table (violations), Single value (% reviewed).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---

### UC-22.10.35 · Non-Treating Provider — Specialty Mismatch Chart Access (§164.502(a)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Highlights when users in non-clinical specialties repeatedly open inpatient charts outside their documented specialty cohort, a common snooping pattern.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup provider_specialty.csv` (`USER_ID`, `specialty_group`), Clarity-derived `patient_unit_map.csv` (`PAT_ID`, `unit_service_line`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-30d
| lookup provider_specialty.csv USER_ID OUTPUT specialty_group
| lookup patient_unit_map.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT unit_service_line
| eval mismatch=if(isnotnull(specialty_group) AND NOT match(unit_service_line, specialty_group),1,0)
| where mismatch=1 AND specialty_group IN ("BILLING","CODING","IT","HR")
| stats dc(PAT_ID) as patients by USER_ID, specialty_group
| where patients>=10
| sort - patients
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Refresh patient service line nightly; (2) tune for legitimate cross-coverage; (3) route to privacy investigations with manager context.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, specialty, patients), Bar chart (violations by department).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---

### UC-22.10.36 · Bulk ePHI Export — Clarity SQL / Caboodle Extract Volume Spike (§164.502(b) / §164.312(b))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Detects unusually large SELECT/BCP operations against patient fact tables that may indicate unauthorized bulk download of ePHI for exfiltration.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server (2648)
- **Data Sources:** `index=mssql` `sourcetype=mssql:audit` (`statement`, `rows`, `session_server_principal_name`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=mssql sourcetype="mssql:audit" earliest=-24h action_class="SELECT"
| where match(lower(statement),"from\s+clarity\.(pat|patient|episodes)") OR match(lower(statement),"from\s+caboodle")
| eval row_estimate=coalesce(rows,0)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(row_estimate) as est_rows by session_server_principal_name, _time, client_ip
| eventstats median(est_rows) as med by session_server_principal_name
| where est_rows > med*20 AND est_rows>100000
| sort - est_rows
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map ETL service accounts to allow list; (2) require data request tickets with correlation ID in SQL comment where feasible; (3) integrate DLP for flat file exports.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (rows/hour by principal), Table (top spikes).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft SQL Server](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2648)

### UC-22.10.37 · After-Hours ePHI Access — Billing Users on Inpatient Charts (§164.502(b))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Surfaces patient chart access during overnight windows by revenue cycle roles where business need is uncommon, supporting workforce training and sanctions workflows.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup hipaa_department_roles.csv` (`USER_ID`, `dept`, `after_hours_allowed`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-30d
| eval hour=tonumber(strftime(_time,"%H"))
| where hour>=22 OR hour<=5
| lookup hipaa_department_roles.csv USER_ID OUTPUT dept, after_hours_allowed
| where dept="REVENUE_CYCLE" AND after_hours_allowed!="true"
| stats dc(PAT_ID) as patients by USER_ID, dept
| sort - patients
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align dept codes with HR feed; (2) add on-call exceptions; (3) integrate with privacy investigation templates.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (hour x user group), Table (users, patients).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.10.38 · Deceased Patient Records — Access After Death Date (§164.502(f) / policy)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Monitors chart access after documented death timestamps for users not on approved post-mortem workflows (HIM, organ donation, medical examiner).
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup patient_death_index.csv` (`PAT_ID`, `death_dttm`, `restricted_flag`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-90d
| lookup patient_death_index.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT death_dttm, restricted_flag
| where isnotnull(death_dttm) AND _time > strptime(death_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| lookup post_mortem_allowed_roles.csv USER_ID OUTPUT allowed_role
| where isnull(allowed_role)
| stats count by USER_ID, PAT_ID
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Refresh death index from ADT feed within SLA; (2) legal guidance on state law vs HIPAA; (3) sensitive dashboard ACLs.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, patient, events), Single value (restricted accesses).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

### UC-22.10.39 · VIP / High-Profile Patient — Elevated Access Monitoring (§164.502(a) / policy)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Applies extra scrutiny to any user accessing patients on the VIP/high-profile flag list to deter and detect snooping.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup vip_patients.csv` (`PAT_ID`, `vip_level`, `start_date`, `end_date`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" AccessType="PatientChart" earliest=-30d
| lookup vip_patients.csv PAT_ID OUTPUT vip_level, start_date, end_date
| where isnotnull(vip_level) AND _time>=strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d") AND _time<=strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| lookup vip_care_team.csv PAT_ID USER_ID OUTPUT is_authorized
| where isnull(is_authorized)
| table _time, USER_ID, PAT_ID, vip_level, Activity
```
- **Implementation:** (1) VIP list restricted to small privacy group managing lookup; (2) real-time alert to privacy on-call; (3) never place plaintext celebrity names in Splunk—use surrogate IDs only.

### UC-22.10.40 · Research Access — Chart Views Without Active IRB Consent Flag (§164.502(a)(1) / §164.512(i))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Correlates research coordinator EHR access with IRB-approved patient rosters to catch out-of-protocol chart reviews.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:audit`, `lookup irb_active_subjects.csv` (`STUDY_ID`, `PAT_ID`, `consent_status`, `window_end`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=epic sourcetype="epic:audit" Activity="*Research*" OR Context="*Study*" earliest=-30d
| rex field=Context "STUDY:(?<STUDY_ID>[^|]+)"
| lookup irb_active_subjects.csv STUDY_ID PAT_ID OUTPUT consent_status, window_end
| eval in_window=if(_time<strptime(window_end,"%Y-%m-%d"),1,0)
| where consent_status!="Active" OR in_window=0 OR isnull(consent_status)
| stats values(STUDY_ID) as studies dc(PAT_ID) as patients by USER_ID
| sort - patients
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Standardize study context logging in Hyperspace build; (2) IRB office owns roster refresh; (3) integrate with Office for Human Research Protections policies.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, studies, patients), Bar chart (violations by study).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.10.41 · Accounting of Disclosures — Registry vs EHR-Logged Disclosures (§164.528)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced

- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Reconciles disclosure events recorded in the accounting of disclosures repository with interface and manual disclosure logs so patients receive accurate histories.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / HEC, Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:disclosure_log` (`disclosure_id`, `PAT_ID`, `recipient_class`), `index=epic` `sourcetype=epic:disclosure_export`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:disclosure_log" earliest=-30d
| stats count by disclosure_id, PAT_ID, recipient_class
| join type=left PAT_ID [
    search index=epic sourcetype="epic:disclosure_export" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(_time) as epic_export by PAT_ID ]
| where isnull(epic_export) AND recipient_class IN ("LawEnforcement","Insurance","Employer")
| table disclosure_id, PAT_ID, recipient_class, count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define authoritative system of record; (2) fix integration gaps when join misses; (3) legal review before patient-facing export.
- **Visualization:** Table (missing reconciliations), Column chart (disclosures by class).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.10.42 · Patient Portal — Suspicious MyChart Password Reset & MFA Changes (§164.312(d) / §164.530(c))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Detects takeover patterns on patient portals that could expose ePHI and undermine patient trust and access controls.
- **App/TA:** HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055)
- **Data Sources:** `index=mychart` `sourcetype=epic:mychart_audit` (`action`, `user_mrn`, `src_ip`), optional Okta/Azure AD B2C logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=mychart sourcetype="epic:mychart_audit" earliest=-7d (action="PasswordReset" OR action="MFAEnrollmentChanged" OR action="EmailChanged")
| stats earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last dc(src_ip) as src_dc values(action) as actions by user_mrn
| where src_dc>=3 OR (last-first)<300 AND mvcount(actions)>=2
| sort - src_dc
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Mask MRN in non-production; (2) integrate fraud scoring from vendor if available; (3) patient outreach playbook for suspected takeover.
- **Visualization:** Table (MRN hash, actions, IPs), Timeline (burst resets).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication (if federated IdP logs are CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.10.43 · Breach Discovery — Time-to-Detect from First PHI Indicator (§164.404)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Measures elapsed time between earliest technical indicator of a potential PHI incident and formal incident creation so discovery timelines support Breach Notification Rule evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `` `notable` `` (`_time`, `rule_name`, `status`), `index=dlp` `sourcetype=ms:o365:management` or `ms:o365:dlp` (`IncidentId`, `CreatedTime`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
`notable` HIPAA_Breach_Candidate=1 earliest=-90d
| eval detect_time=_time
| join IncidentId type=left [
    search index=dlp sourcetype="o365:dlp" earliest=-90d
    | eval first_indicator=strptime(CreatedTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    | stats min(first_indicator) as first_dlp by IncidentId ]
| eval hours_to_detect=round((detect_time-first_dlp)/3600,2)
| table IncidentId, first_dlp, detect_time, hours_to_detect, rule_name, status
| sort - hours_to_detect
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag correlation searches that create HIPAA candidate notables; (2) normalize UTC; (3) executive dashboard for legal discovery SLA.
- **Visualization:** Histogram (hours_to_detect), Table (incidents), Single value (median detect hours).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---

### UC-22.10.44 · Breach Risk Assessment — Four-Factor Documentation Tracking (§164.402 / §164.404)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Audit
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Stores structured answers for nature/extent of PHI, unauthorized recipient, actual acquisition, and mitigation so breach vs non-breach determinations are auditable.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_ra` JSON (`incident_id`, `factor1`, `factor2`, `factor3`, `factor4`, `preliminary_result`, `reviewer`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_ra" earliest=-365d
| eval complete=if(isnotnull(factor1) AND isnotnull(factor2) AND isnotnull(factor3) AND isnotnull(factor4),"Yes","No")
| where complete="No" OR isnull(preliminary_result)
| eval days_open=round((now()-_time)/86400,1)
| table incident_id, days_open, reviewer, factor1, factor2, factor3, factor4
| sort - days_open
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Emit JSON from GRC web form via HEC; (2) retain immutable snapshots on decision changes; (3) legal owns field definitions.
- **Visualization:** Table (open assessments), Single value (incomplete RA count).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.10.45 · Individual Notification — Letter Generation & Mailing Evidence (§164.404(b), (d)(1))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Audit
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Tracks generation, print, and certified mail tracking numbers for breach letters within the 60-day window to support individual notification compliance.
- **App/TA:** HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928) if tickets drive mailhouse
- **Data Sources:** `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_notice` (`incident_id`, `patient_token`, `letter_status`, `mail_tracking_id`, `mailed_dttm`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_notice" earliest=-120d
| eval mailed_epoch=strptime(mailed_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval breach_discovered=strptime(breach_discovered_dttm,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval days_to_mail=round((mailed_epoch-breach_discovered)/86400,1)
| where letter_status!="Mailed" OR days_to_mail>60 OR isnull(mail_tracking_id)
| stats count by incident_id, letter_status
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Integrate print vendor webhooks; (2) never index full SSN—use tokenized IDs; (3) legal sign-off field in same event.
- **Visualization:** Gantt-style timeline (discovered → mailed), Table (exceptions).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)

### UC-22.10.46 · HHS Secretary Notification — 500+ Individuals Threshold Watch (§164.408)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Audit
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Rolling count of affected individuals per incident versus the 500-person statutory threshold for timely HHS portal submission.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_subject` (`incident_id`, `subject_token`, `included_in_count`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_subject" earliest=-365d
| where included_in_count="true"
| stats dc(subject_token) as affected by incident_id
| eval hhs_required=if(affected>=500,"Yes","No")
| join incident_id [
    search index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:hhs_filing" earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(filed_dttm) as hhs_filed by incident_id ]
| where hhs_required="Yes" AND isnull(hhs_filed)
| table incident_id, affected, hhs_required
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Dedupe subjects carefully per legal guidance; (2) separate incidents vs linked incidents; (3) alert at 450 rolling to create runway.
- **Visualization:** Single value (open >500 without filing), Table (incident, affected).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.10.47 · Media Notification — Large-State Resident Threshold Tracking (§164.406(c))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Audit
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Computes per-state affected resident counts to trigger prominent media notice obligations when thresholds in a jurisdiction or overall breach size demand public notice.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_subject` (`incident_id`, `state`, `media_required`), `lookup state_media_threshold.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_subject" earliest=-365d included_in_count="true"
| stats dc(subject_token) as affected by incident_id, state
| lookup state_media_threshold.csv state OUTPUT threshold
| eval media_needed=if(affected>=threshold,"Yes","No")
| where media_needed="Yes"
| join incident_id [
    search index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:media_notice" earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(published_dttm) as published by incident_id, state ]
| where isnull(published)
| table incident_id, state, affected, threshold
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Legal provides threshold table including state mini-HIPAA laws; (2) do not store full addresses in Splunk; (3) coordinate with comms team workflow IDs.
- **Visualization:** Choropleth (affected by state), Table (missing media publishes).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.10.48 · Breach Log / Incident Register — Immutable Chronological Record (§164.402 / policy)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Audit
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Maintains a searchable chronological register of all breach-suspected and confirmed incidents with status transitions for OCR-style examinations.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_register` (`register_id`, `incident_title`, `status`, `severity`, `owner`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_register" earliest=-365d
| stats latest(status) as last_status latest(_time) as last_update earliest(_time) as opened latest(incident_title) as incident_title values(owner) as owner by register_id
| eval days_open=round((now()-opened)/86400,0)
| where last_status IN ("Draft","Investigating") AND days_open>30
| table register_id, incident_title, last_status, owner, days_open, last_update
| sort - days_open
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Append-only sourcetype from GRC; (2) restrict to privacy/legal roles; (3) export annually to offline counsel archive.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (status transitions), Table (stale investigations), Single value (open incidents >30d).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.10.49 · Breach Remediation — Control Implementation Evidence Post-Incident (§164.308(a)(1)(ii)(A))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Links post-breach corrective actions (MFA rollout, DLP rules) to measurable control telemetry so remediation is demonstrable, not narrative-only.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055)
- **Data Sources:** `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:remediation_task` (`incident_id`, `control_id`, `target_date`), `index=o365` DLP rule hits, `index=ad` MFA coverage scripted metrics
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:remediation_task" earliest=-180d status!="Closed"
| eval due=strptime(target_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join control_id [
    search index=metrics sourcetype="hipaa:control_metric" earliest=-1d
    | stats latest(metric_value) as current_value by control_id ]
| eval behind=if(now()>due AND current_value<100,1,0)
| where behind=1
| table incident_id, control_id, target_date, current_value, owner
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define `metric_value` as percent complete (e.g., MFA enrolled / total); (2) nightly scripted input; (3) board-level dashboard after major breaches.
- **Visualization:** Bullet chart (current vs target), Table (behind tasks).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)

### UC-22.10.50 · Annual Breach Reporting — Trend of Affected Individuals & Root Cause (§164.408 / OCR reporting)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Audit
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Aggregates year-over-year breach statistics and root causes for leadership and regulatory annual summaries.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:breach_closeout` (`close_date`, `affected_count`, `root_cause`, `vector`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:breach_closeout" earliest=-800d confirmed_breach="true"
| eval year=strftime(strptime(close_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),"%Y")
| stats sum(affected_count) as individuals count as incidents by year, root_cause, vector
| sort year, - individuals
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Standardize `root_cause` taxonomy (misdelivery, theft, hacking); (2) align with OCR data request fields; (3) scrub narrative PHI from free-text fields.
- **Visualization:** Stacked column (individuals by cause per year), Line chart (incident count trend).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.10.51 · Business Associate Access — VPN/SSO Sessions Originating from BA Address Space (§164.308(b) / §164.502(e))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Surfaces new source ASNs or IP ranges used by business associates connecting into hosted EHR jump boxes so BA access stays within contracted technical boundaries.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Citrix (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=citrix` `sourcetype=citrix:session`, CIDR lookup `ba_ip_ranges` in `transforms.conf` (`vendor_name`, `baa_id`) or `ba_ip_ranges.csv` with one row per approved egress IP
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=citrix sourcetype="citrix:session" earliest=-7d PublishedApplication="*Epic*BAA*"
| lookup ba_ip_ranges client_ip OUTPUT vendor_name, baa_id
| where isnull(vendor_name)
| iplocation client_ip
| stats count dc(UserName) as distinct_users by client_ip, Country, Org, PublishedApplication
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define `ba_ip_ranges` in `transforms.conf` as a CIDR lookup on `client_ip`, or maintain one row per approved egress IP in `ba_ip_ranges.csv`; (2) refresh when BA contracts change VPN concentrators; (3) alert on first-seen IP before allowlisting; (4) optional threat intel enrichment on `client_ip`.
- **Visualization:** Table (client IP, users, sessions), Map (geo), Single value (sessions from non-allowlisted BA egress).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Citrix](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.10.52 · BAA Compliance Evidence — Control Attestations vs Technical Telemetry (§164.308(b)(3) / §164.502(e))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Audit
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Joins annual BA questionnaire answers (encryption, logging) with Splunk-ingested technical proof points so subcontractor compliance is evidence-based, not checkbox-only.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / HEC, Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=privacy` `sourcetype=hipaa:baa_attestation` (`vendor_id`, `control_id`, `answer`), `index=metrics` `sourcetype=hipaa:ba_control_metric` (`vendor_id`, `control_id`, `measured_value`)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="hipaa:baa_attestation" earliest=-365d
| where answer IN ("Yes","FullyImplemented")
| join vendor_id, control_id [
    search index=metrics sourcetype="hipaa:ba_control_metric" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(measured_value) as measured by vendor_id, control_id ]
| eval gap=if(isnull(measured) OR measured<95,"Evidence_Gap","OK")
| where gap!="OK"
| table vendor_id, control_id, answer, measured, gap
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map each attestation row to an automated metric (for example TLS coverage percent from vulnerability scans); (2) legal sets materiality thresholds; (3) feed gaps into vendor risk reviews.
- **Visualization:** Matrix (vendor x control), Table (gaps only), Column chart (percent evidence coverage).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---

### UC-22.10.53 · Subcontractor Access — Downstream API Keys Touching ePHI Interfaces (§164.502(e) / BAA chain)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Monitors first-seen API keys or OAuth clients used by BA subcontractors against an approved key registry so the extended BAA chain does not introduce unreviewed ePHI paths.
- **App/TA:** HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=interop` `sourcetype=apigee:proxy` or AWS API Gateway logs (`client_id`, `api_key`, `consumer`), `lookup ba_subcontractor_clients.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=interop sourcetype="apigee:proxy" uri_path="/fhir/*" earliest=-30d
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen dc(status) as status_codes by client_id, api_key
| eval key_or_client=coalesce(client_id, api_key)
| lookup ba_subcontractor_clients.csv key_or_client OUTPUT approved_vendor, subcontractor_name
| where isnull(approved_vendor)
| sort first_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Hash static API keys at ingest with `sha256(api_key)` for display fields; (2) require procurement to register subcontractor keys before production; (3) auto-revoke via API management integration on critical alerts.
- **Visualization:** Table (first_seen, key hash, path), Timeline (new clients per week).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.10.54 · Third-Party Data Sharing — O365 Sharing Links to External Domains on PHI Libraries (§164.502(b) / §164.514(e))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Surfaces SharePoint or OneDrive anonymous or company links created in sites labeled for clinical operations that resolve to recipients outside the covered entity tenant.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055)
- **Data Sources:** `index=o365` `sourcetype=ms:o365:management` SharePointFileOperation or SharingSet operations, Purview sensitivity labels when present
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" Workload=SharePoint earliest=-7d Operation IN ("SharingSet","AnonymousLinkCreated","CompanyLinkCreated")
| eval site_label=coalesce(SensitivityLabel, SiteSensitivity)
| where match(lower(site_label),"clinical|phi|hipaa|restricted")
| eval sharee_lower=lower(Sharee)
| eval external=if(match(sharee_lower,"@yourhealthorg\.com$"),0,1)
| where external=1
| stats count values(Sharee) as sharees by UserId, ObjectId, Operation, site_label
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Replace `@yourhealthorg\.com` with your primary tenant SMTP domain; (2) align `site_label` with Purview labels actually deployed; (3) tune for legitimate research collaborators via domain allow list; (4) integrate with insider risk workflows.
- **Visualization:** Table (user, object, sharees), Bar chart (external shares by label), Single value (anonymous links).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)

### UC-22.10.55 · Cloud Service Provider — ePHI Hosting Admin Actions in AWS or Azure Audit (§164.308(a)(1) / §164.502(e))
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA
- **Value:** Tracks CSP administrator and break-glass activity against accounts or subscriptions tagged as hosting replicated ePHI backups or analytics, supporting BA and cloud subcontractor oversight.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Amazon Web Services (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=aws` `sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail` (`userIdentity.arn`, `eventName`, `requestParameters`), Azure Activity Log (`azure:monitor`), CMDB lookup `aws_account_ephi_tags.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=aws sourcetype="aws:cloudtrail" earliest=-7d
| rename userIdentity.arn as user_arn
| eval account=coalesce(recipientAccountId, aws_account_id)
| lookup aws_account_ephi_tags.csv account OUTPUT contains_ephi_backup
| where contains_ephi_backup="true"
| search eventName IN ("AssumeRole","GetObject","PutObject","DeleteObject","CreateSnapshot","RunInstances","ModifyDBInstance")
| eval principal=mvindex(split(user_arn,"/"),-1)
| stats count values(eventName) as actions by principal, account, sourceIPAddress
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag AWS accounts in `aws_account_ephi_tags.csv` from enterprise architecture; (2) use organization CloudTrail with integrity validation; (3) route root and break-glass usage to SOC and privacy; (4) map ARNs to named individuals via IAM Identity Center export.
- **Visualization:** Table (principal, actions, account), Timeline (snapshot or delete spikes), Single value (break-glass events).
- **CIM Models:** Change (CloudTrail when CIM-tagged), N/A for raw CloudTrail
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Amazon Web Services](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

---

### 22.13 NERC CIP

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, syslog from ESP firewalls and EACMS, CyberArk PAM sessions, physical access control (PACS badging), ICS/SCADA historian exports, BES Cyber System inventory (CSV/lookup), patch management system exports, backup system logs, NTP/PTP sources, Splunk audit logs.

### UC-22.13.1 · BES Cyber Asset Inventory Reconciliation Against Telemetry (CIP-002-6 R1 Part 1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We find assets appearing in security telemetry that are missing from the BES Cyber System inventory before auditors do, closing registration gaps that undermine categorization.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=wineventlog` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, lookup `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4624,4768) earliest=-24h
| eval asset=lower(coalesce(dest_nt_host, WorkstationName))
| stats dc(asset) as hits by asset
| append [
    search index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic bes_zone="ESP" earliest=-24h action=allowed
    | eval asset=lower(dest)
    | stats count by asset
  ]
| stats sum(count) as pan_hits max(hits) as win_hits by asset
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv asset OUTPUT bes_system impact_rating
| where isnull(bes_system)
| eval evidence=coalesce(win_hits, pan_hits)
| sort - evidence
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv` from the official BES Cyber System list. (2) Normalize hostnames and IP keys consistently. (3) Schedule weekly and route gaps to asset governance. (4) Close tickets by CMDB update or correct Splunk tagging.
- **Visualization:** Table (unregistered assets), Bar chart (hits by index), Single value (gap count)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Network_Traffic (when CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

---


### UC-22.13.2 · BES Cyber System Impact Rating Drift Detection (CIP-002-6 R2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We highlight when operational tags on hosts disagree with the registered high or medium impact rating so impact determinations stay defensible.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_security` asset records, `bes_cyber_system_categorization.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup bes_cyber_system_categorization.csv
| eval key=lower(bes_asset_hostname)
| join type=left key [
    search index=ot_security sourcetype="ot:asset" earliest=-24h
    | eval key=lower(hostname)
    | stats latest(impact_tag) as observed_impact by key
  ]
| where lower(observed_impact)!=lower(impact_rating)
| table bes_system, impact_rating, observed_impact, key
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate `impact_tag` from OT Security Add-on or CMDB sync. (2) Tune mapping for staging assets. (3) Monthly governance report. (4) Drive formal CIP-002 review updates when drift persists.
- **Visualization:** Table (mismatches), Single value (drift count), Column chart (by site)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151)

---


### UC-22.13.3 · ESP Boundary Change Detection via Firewall Configuration Events (CIP-002-6 R1 Part 1.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We tie ESP security rule, zone, and interface edits to change records so boundary changes to BES Cyber Systems are visible and attributable.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:config` OR `sourcetype=pan:system`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype=chg:firewall`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network (sourcetype=pan:config OR sourcetype=pan:system) earliest=-7d
| where match(object,"(?i)security|zone|interface") OR match(cmd,"(?i)commit")
| lookup esp_object_inventory object OUTPUT esp_segment_id
| join type=left max=0 object [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(approved) as approved latest(change_id) as change_id by firewall_object
    | rename firewall_object as object
  ]
| where approved!="true" OR isnull(change_id)
| table _time, admin, object, esp_segment_id, change_id, approved
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Forward Panorama or device config logs. (2) Map changed objects to ESP segments in `esp_object_inventory`. (3) Require ITSM linkage for production commits. (4) Weekly compliance dashboard export.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (changes), Table (unauthorized edits), Bar chart (by ESP segment)
- **CIM Models:** Change (when CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.13.4 · Annual CIP-002 Categorization Review Evidence Package (CIP-002-6 R4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We timestamp inventory validation searches and signed review workflows so annual categorization review evidence is searchable and complete.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=itsm` `sourcetype=cip:review_ticket`, `index=cip_evidence` `sourcetype=cip:search_evidence`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=cip:review_ticket review_type="CIP-002_Categorization" earliest=-400d
| eval signed_epoch=strptime(signed_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval annual_due=relative_time(now(),"-1y@d")
| eval signed_ok=if(signed_epoch>=annual_due,1,0)
| append [
    search index=cip_evidence sourcetype=cip:search_evidence search_name="CIP002_inventory_recon" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(_time) as last_proof by search_name
  ]
| table review_type, owner, signed_at, signed_ok, last_proof
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Emit HEC when the responsible manager signs the annual review. (2) Schedule the inventory recon search to write `cip:search_evidence` events. (3) Alert if `signed_ok=0` approaching the compliance date. (4) PDF the dashboard for the audit binder.
- **Visualization:** Single value (review current), Timeline (signatures vs proof runs), Table (owners)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---


### UC-22.13.5 · Security Policy Exception Register with Expiration Tracking (CIP-003-8 R1 Part 1.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We prevent policy exceptions from silently exceeding approved dates so security management controls stay within delegated risk acceptance.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip:policy_exception` (exception_id, control_ref, expires_on, status)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip:policy_exception status="active" earliest=-120d
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(expires_on,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval expired=if(now()>exp_epoch,1,0)
| eval days_left=round((exp_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| where expired=1 OR days_left<14
| table exception_id, control_ref, owner, expires_on, expired, days_left
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest the exception register via HEC or DB Connect on change. (2) Map `control_ref` to CIP-003 policies. (3) Alert at fourteen days and on expiration. (4) Require closure events to flip status.
- **Visualization:** Table (near-expired), Single value (expired count), Time chart (open exceptions)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---


### UC-22.13.6 · Cyber Security Plan Technical Control Attestation — MFA for Privileged Access (CIP-003-8 R2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We compare multifactor authentication signals for privileged BES accounts against the cyber security plan so documented controls match authentication reality.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=wineventlog` `EventCode=4624`, lookup `bes_privileged_users.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h
| lookup bes_privileged_users.csv Account_Name AS user OUTPUT requires_mfa bes_system
| where requires_mfa="true"
| eval strong_pkg=if(match(AuthenticationPackageName,"(?i)Negotiate|Kerberos") AND LogonType IN ("10","3"),1,0)
| stats count as logons sum(strong_pkg) as strong_signals by user, bes_system
| eval ratio=round(strong_signals/logons,3)
| where ratio < 0.9
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enrich with IdP-specific fields if forwarded via HEC. (2) Tune `strong_pkg` logic with your MFA architecture. (3) Monthly report to the security working group. (4) Track remediation in ITSM.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (ratio by user), Table (exceptions), Single value (users below threshold)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.13.7 · Delegated Authority Register Change History (CIP-003-8 R3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We retain an auditable history when delegated authority for cyber security duties is granted or revoked, supporting management control evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip:delegation_register`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip:delegation_register earliest=-730d
| eval eff=strptime(effective_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval rev=if(isnotnull(revoked_date),strptime(revoked_date,"%Y-%m-%d"),null())
| eval active=if(isnull(rev) OR rev>now(),1,0)
| stats latest(scope) as scope latest(effective_date) as effective latest(revoked_date) as revoked by delegate
| eval status=if(isnull(revoked) OR revoked="","active","revoked")
| sort delegate
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Push delegation CSV rows append-only through a scripted input or HEC. (2) Align fields with the official register. (3) Quarterly PDF export for legal retention. (4) Correlate revocations with HR events.
- **Visualization:** Table (delegates), Timeline (effective dates), Single value (active delegates)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.8 · Low-Impact BES Cyber System Electronic Access Path Enforcement (CIP-003-8 R4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We catch remote access sessions to low-impact assets that bypass approved intermediaries or applications, reducing undocumented electronic access paths.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, `bes_low_impact_hosts.csv`, `cip003_approved_remote_paths.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic action=allowed earliest=-24h
| lookup bes_low_impact_hosts.csv ip AS dest OUTPUT asset_id impact
| where impact="low"
| lookup cip003_approved_remote_paths.csv app src_zone dest_zone OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved)
| stats sum(bytes_out) as bytes_out count by dest, app, rule, user
| sort - bytes_out
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain low-impact asset IP list from CIP-002 outputs. (2) Define approved app and zone tuples. (3) Daily alert on violations. (4) Feed confirmed exceptions to the CIP-003 exception workflow.
- **Visualization:** Table (violations), Bar chart (disallowed apps), Single value (distinct assets)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.app | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.13.9 · Security Awareness Training Completion for BES Personnel (CIP-004-6 R1 Part 1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We list personnel with active BES access who have not completed the current awareness module so training evidence is current before audits.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` `sourcetype=lms:completion`, `bes_privileged_users.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup bes_privileged_users.csv
| join type=left employee_id [
    search index=hr sourcetype=lms:completion course_id="CIP_SECURITY_AWARENESS" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(completed_at) as completed_at by employee_id
  ]
| eval completed_epoch=strptime(completed_at,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval stale=if(isnull(completed_epoch) OR completed_epoch<relative_time(now(),"-350d@d"),1,0)
| where stale=1
| table employee_id, role, completed_at
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Sync LMS completions nightly. (2) Align `employee_id` with directory accounts. (3) Alert managers fourteen days before the annual due wave. (4) Export CSV for the training coordinator.
- **Visualization:** Table (incomplete), Single value (percent complete), Column chart (by department)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.10 · Personnel Risk Assessment Due-Date Monitoring (CIP-004-6 R2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We highlight access holders whose personnel risk assessment is overdue while they still touch BES systems, reducing stale risk decisions.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:pra`, `index=wineventlog` privileged logons
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype=hr:pra earliest=-730d
| eval next_epoch=strptime(next_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| rename employee_id as user
| join type=left user [
    search index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 earliest=-30d
    | lookup bes_privileged_users.csv Account_Name AS user OUTPUT employee_id
    | stats dc(ComputerName) as systems by user
  ]
| where next_epoch < now() OR isnull(next_due)
| table user, assessment_date, next_due, systems
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest PRA due dates from HR or GRC. (2) Correlate with active privileged users only. (3) Auto-ticket overdue users with recent BES logons. (4) Quarterly leadership rollup.
- **Visualization:** Table (overdue), Timeline (assessment dates), Single value (overdue count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.11 · Electronic Access Authorization Record Coverage for PAM Sessions (CIP-004-6 R3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We verify CyberArk sessions target systems that have a valid, unexpired access authorization on file for the user.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295)
- **Data Sources:** `index=pam` `sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog`, `cip004_access_authorizations.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog action="Logon" earliest=-24h
| lookup cip004_access_authorizations.csv user, address OUTPUT auth_id expires
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(expires,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where isnull(auth_id) OR now()>exp_epoch
| stats count by user, address, safe
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize `address` to the same key as the authorization register. (2) Nightly reconciliation job. (3) Feed hits to access governance. (4) Attach export to quarterly access review.
- **Visualization:** Table (missing or expired auth), Bar chart (by safe), Single value (sessions)
- **CIM Models:** N/A
