### UC-22.13.12 · Post-Termination Access Revocation within 24 Hours (CIP-004-6 R4 Part 4.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We measure hours from HR termination timestamp to Active Directory account disable for BES personnel, evidencing the one-day removal expectation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:termination`, `index=wineventlog` `EventCode=4726`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype=hr:termination earliest=-14d
| eval term_epoch=strptime(term_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| rename employee_id as user
| join type=left user [
    search index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4726 earliest=-14d
    | stats earliest(_time) as disable_epoch by TargetUserName
    | rename TargetUserName as user
  ]
| eval delta_h=round((disable_epoch-term_epoch)/3600,2)
| where isnull(disable_epoch) OR delta_h>24
| table user, term_time, disable_epoch, delta_h
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure 4726 from all in-scope domains. (2) Normalize time zones to UTC. (3) Page IAM when SLA missed. (4) Optionally join CyberArk disable events as supplemental proof.
- **Visualization:** Table (SLA misses), Column chart (delta hours), Single value (miss count)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)


### UC-22.13.14 · Background Investigation Recency for ESP and PAM Users (CIP-004-6 R6)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We flag active PAM users whose background investigation date exceeds policy limits so personnel suitability evidence stays current.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295)
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` `sourcetype=hr:background`, `index=pam` CyberArk logons
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup esp_privileged_personnel.csv
| lookup hr_background employee_id OUTPUT investigation_date
| eval inv_epoch=strptime(investigation_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval age_days=round((now()-inv_epoch)/86400,0)
| join type=left employee_id [
    search index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog action="Logon" earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(_time) as last_pam by user
    | rename user as employee_id
  ]
| where age_days>2555 AND isnotnull(last_pam)
| table employee_id, investigation_date, age_days, last_pam
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Replace `2555` with your policy threshold lookup. (2) Weekly sync from HR. (3) Suspend access per procedure when triggered. (4) Document waivers in CIP-003 exceptions.
- **Visualization:** Table (stale investigations), Single value (count), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295)

### UC-22.13.15 · ESP Inbound First-Seen External Source Detection (CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We detect new external sources allowed through ESP entry rules so undocumented inbound paths are reviewed before they become normal traffic.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, lookup `esp_inbound_baseline.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic dest_zone="ESP_DMZ" action=allowed earliest=-24h
| eval ext_src=if(cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8",src) OR cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12",src) OR cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16",src),null(),src)
| where isnotnull(ext_src)
| lookup esp_inbound_baseline.csv ext_src AS ext_src OUTPUT approved_vendor
| where isnull(approved_vendor)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen values(dest) as targets by ext_src, rule
| sort - first_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Seed `esp_inbound_baseline.csv` with known vendor ranges. (2) Promote reviewed sources into the baseline. (3) Create ES notable for first-seen. (4) Map rules to EAP register fields.
- **Visualization:** Table (new sources), Map (geo), Time chart (first-seen rate)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.13.16 · ESP Outbound Bytes Spike versus Rolling Baseline (CIP-005-6 R2 Part 2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We surface unusual outbound volume from ESP-internal segments that may indicate data exfiltration or misconfigured services.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic` (bytes_out, src, dest, src_zone)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic src_zone="ESP_INTERNAL" action=allowed earliest=-30d
| eval internal_dest=cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8",dest) OR cidrmatch("172.16.0.0/12",dest) OR cidrmatch("192.168.0.0/16",dest)
| where internal_dest=0
| bin _time span=1d
| stats sum(bytes_out) as b_out by src, dest, _time
| eventstats median(b_out) as med by src, dest
| eval spike=if(b_out>med*8 AND med>0,1,0)
| where spike=1
| table _time, src, dest, b_out, med
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune multiplier and internal CIDRs. (2) Exclude CDN and update destinations via lookup. (3) Tag BES Cyber Assets on `src`. (4) Pair with PCAP workflow if available.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (bytes), Table (spikes), Single value (spike count)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_out) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest span=1d | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.13.17 · Denied ESP Traversal Bursts Followed by Allowed Sessions (CIP-005-6 R2 Part 2.3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We correlate dense deny activity at the ESP with subsequent allows to prioritize perimeter probing that may indicate misconfiguration or attack.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic dest_zone="ESP_PERIMETER" earliest=-4h
| eval outcome=if(action="deny","deny","allow")
| bin _time span=5m
| stats sum(eval(if(outcome=="deny",1,0))) as denies sum(eval(if(outcome=="allow",1,0))) as allows by src, _time
| where denies>40 AND allows>0
| sort - denies
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Calibrate deny thresholds per ESP. (2) Enrich with threat log if separate sourcetype. (3) Feed ES correlation. (4) Document benign scanner ranges in lookup.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (deny vs allow), Table (src clusters), Single value (burst windows)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src span=5m | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

---


### UC-22.13.18 · Interactive Remote Access Session Duration on Jump Hosts (CIP-005-6 R3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We track RDP or vendor remote sessions through registered jump hosts so interactive remote access is visible for ESP monitoring.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295)
- **Data Sources:** `index=wineventlog` `EventCode` 4624/4634, `bes_jump_hosts.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4624,4634) LogonType=10 earliest=-24h
| lookup bes_jump_hosts.csv ComputerName OUTPUT jump_id esp_segment
| where isnotnull(jump_id)
| transaction Account_Name maxspan=7200 startswith=(EventCode=4624) endswith=(EventCode=4634)
| eval duration_min=round(duration/60,2)
| table _time, Account_Name, ComputerName, esp_segment, duration_min
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain jump host list aligned with CIP-005 EAP documentation. (2) Ensure logoff events are audited. (3) Cross-check CyberArk session metadata. (4) Alert on sessions exceeding policy without active work order.
- **Visualization:** Histogram (duration), Table (sessions), Single value (sessions over threshold)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.13.19 · EACMS and IRA Authentication Failure Concentration (CIP-005-6 R4 Part 4.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We detect brute-force patterns against Electronic Access Control and Monitoring Systems that gate ESP remote access.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=eacms:auth`, `index=os` `sourcetype=linux:secure`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=network sourcetype=eacms:auth result="failure") OR (index=os sourcetype=linux:secure "Failed password")
earliest=-24h
| eval user=coalesce(user, account)
| lookup eacms_asset_map host OUTPUT eacms_id
| stats count by user, src_ip, host, eacms_id
| where count>25
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize vendor syslog into `eacms:auth`. (2) Distinguish IRA accounts from vendors. (3) Integrate with ES adaptive response. (4) Retain raw events per records retention.
- **Visualization:** Table (top sources), Time chart (failures), Single value (locked-out systems)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.src, Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.13.20 · ESP Security Rule Commit Without Approved Change Record (CIP-005-6 R1 Part 1.4)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We flag firewall commits affecting ESP rules when no linked change ticket exists, supporting controlled configuration of the security perimeter.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:config`, `index=itsm` `sourcetype=chg:firewall`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:config earliest=-7d
| where match(cmd,"(?i)commit")
| join type=left max=0 admin [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:firewall earliest=-30d
    | stats latest(change_id) as change_id by submitter
    | rename submitter as admin
  ]
| where isnull(change_id)
| table _time, admin, object, cmd, change_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map Panorama admin IDs to ITSM submitter IDs. (2) Require change_id in commit messages where supported. (3) Weekly governance report. (4) Escalate repeat offenders.
- **Visualization:** Table (unapproved commits), Timeline, Bar chart (by admin)
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.13.21 · Vendor Interactive Remote Access — Encryption and Session Recording Evidence (CIP-005-6 R3 Part 3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We verify vendor interactive remote access uses approved encryption (for example TLS versions on VPN or SSH) and that PAM session recording identifiers exist for the same sessions.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect` or SSL VPN logs, `index=pam` `sourcetype=cyberark:ptalog`, `sourcetype=cyberark:session_recording`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:globalprotect earliest=-24h
| eval tls_version=coalesce(tls_version,ssl_tls_version)
| eval tls_ok=if(match(tls_version,"TLSv1\.[23]"),1,0)
| where tls_ok=0 AND isnotnull(user)
| stats count by user, public_ip, tls_version, gateway
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map GlobalProtect or SSL VPN field names to `tls_version`. (2) Run a companion saved search on `index=pam` for vendor sessions missing `recording_id` in `cyberark:session_recording`. (3) Alert on weak TLS or missing recording for vendor users. (4) Document compensating controls for legacy jump hosts.
- **Visualization:** Table (encryption and recording gaps), Timeline, Single value (violation count)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High

- **Data Sources:** `index=os` `sourcetype=linux:secure`, `sourcetype=serial_concentrator`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os sourcetype=linux:secure earliest=-14d (pppd OR ttyS OR ttyUSB OR "serial console")
| lookup bes_network_segments.csv host OUTPUT segment
| where segment="ESP_adjacent"
| stats count by host, process, user
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Inventory any remaining dial or OOB paths. (2) Forward concentrator logs. (3) Alert on any production hit unless maintenance window lookup matches. (4) Document removal in CIP-010.
- **Visualization:** Table (events), Single value (distinct hosts), Time chart
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.13.23 · Physical Access Outside Approved Maintenance Window at PSP (CIP-006-6 R1 Part 1.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We alert on badge-granted entries at Physical Security Perimeters outside documented maintenance windows for follow-up with operations.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151)
- **Data Sources:** `index=physical` `sourcetype=pacs:access`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access result="granted" earliest=-7d
| lookup cip_psp_register reader_id OUTPUT psp_name maint_cron_ok
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H")
| eval dow=strftime(_time,"%w")
| lookup psp_maint_windows.csv psp_name dow hour OUTPUT in_window
| where in_window!="true"
| table _time, employee_id, psp_name, reader_id, in_window
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Encode maintenance windows in `psp_maint_windows.csv`. (2) Add holiday exceptions. (3) Route alerts to physical security. (4) Correlate with work orders.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (after-hours), Table (events), Single value (count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151)

### UC-22.13.24 · Unauthorized Physical Access Attempts at PSP (CIP-006-6 R2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We aggregate denied, forced, and tailgate physical access events for rapid triage and evidence of physical perimeter monitoring.

- **Data Sources:** `index=physical` `sourcetype=pacs:access`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access earliest=-24h
    (result="denied" OR event_type IN ("forced","tailgate","passback"))
| lookup cip_psp_register reader_id OUTPUT psp_name bes_site
| stats count by event_type, psp_name, bes_site
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map readers to PSP inventory. (2) Push high severity to SOC bridge. (3) Optional join to VMS syslog. (4) Weekly trending for reliability.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (event types), Table (raw), Single value (tailgates)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate

- **Data Sources:** `index=physical` `sourcetype=pacs:access`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access badge_type="visitor" earliest=-7d
| where isnull(escort_badge_id) OR escort_present="false"
| lookup cip_psp_register reader_id OUTPUT psp_name
| stats count by visitor_id, reader_id, psp_name
| where count>=1
| table visitor_id, reader_id, psp_name, count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Fix join logic per PACS export (example simplified). (2) Use `transaction` on reader_id if vendor provides visit_id. (3) Daily report to reception. (4) Tune window to site policy.
- **Visualization:** Table (violations), Single value (rate), Time chart

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.13.26 · Physical Access Log Ingest Continuity for Retention Evidence (CIP-006-6 R4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We detect multi-day gaps in PACS indexing that could undermine evidence of physical access monitoring and review.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** `index=physical` `sourcetype=pacs:access`, `_internal`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access earliest=-60d
| timechart span=1d count as events
| streamstats window=7 avg(events) as baseline
| eval gap=if(events<baseline*0.15 OR events=0,1,0)
| where gap=1
| table _time, events, baseline
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Exclude planned outages via lookup. (2) Alert physical security and Splunk admins. (3) Track forwarder uptime. (4) Export gap analysis for audits.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (daily volume), Table (gap days), Single value (max gap hours)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

---


### UC-22.13.27 · PSP Forced Door with Concurrent ESP Interactive Logon (CIP-006-6 R3 Part 3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We time-align severe physical alarms with interactive logons at the same site to support combined cyber-physical incident analysis.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=physical` forced events, `index=wineventlog` RDP logons
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=physical sourcetype=pacs:access event_type="forced" earliest=-6h
| eval ptime=_time, site=bes_site
| join type=left site [
    search index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4624 LogonType=10 earliest=-6h
    | lookup bes_site_map.csv ComputerName OUTPUT bes_site as site
    | stats count by Account_Name, ComputerName, site
  ]
| where isnotnull(Account_Name)
| table ptime, site, employee_id, Account_Name, ComputerName, count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `bes_site_map.csv` from facility records. (2) Tune time bucketing (example uses join on site). (3) Prefer summary indexing for scale. (4) Attach results to CIP-008 preliminary review.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (overlay), Table (correlated pairs), Single value (pair count)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.13.28 · Listening Port and Service Baseline Deviation on BES Servers (CIP-007-6 R1 Part 1.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We compare live listening ports from host telemetry to an approved baseline so unauthorized services on BES Cyber Assets are detected quickly.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=os` `sourcetype=netstat` or scripted listening ports, `bes_port_baseline.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os (sourcetype=netstat OR sourcetype=listening_ports) earliest=-24h
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats latest(port) as port latest(proto) as proto by host, port
| lookup bes_port_baseline.csv host, port OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved)
| table host, port, proto, bes_system
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Collect listening port data via UF scripted input. (2) Build baseline from golden images. (3) Alert on new ports for seven days. (4) Feed changes into CIP-010 change tickets.
- **Visualization:** Table (new ports), Bar chart (by host), Single value (distinct deviations)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### UC-22.13.29 · Security Patch Evaluation Completion Tracking (CIP-007-6 R2 Part 2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We track that each published critical patch for BES assets received a documented evaluation record within policy time.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=patch` `sourcetype=wsus:summary` or `sourcetype=tenable:plugin`, `patch_evaluation_register.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=patch sourcetype=wsus:summary earliest=-35d classification="Critical"
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| join type=left kb_id [
    search index=grc sourcetype=cip:patch_evaluation kb_id=* earliest=-35d
    | stats latest(evaluated_at) as evaluated_at by kb_id
  ]
| where isnull(evaluated_at)
| stats dc(host) as hosts_missing_eval by kb_id, title
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest patch catalog with release dates. (2) Require evaluation HEC event per KB. (3) Alert before internal evaluation SLA. (4) Export for CIP-007 evidence binders.
- **Visualization:** Table (unevaluated KBs), Single value (count), Time chart (evaluations)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.30 · Patch Installation or Mitigation within 35-Day Window (CIP-007-6 R2 Part 2.3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We highlight applicable patches past thirty-five days from completed evaluation without apply or approved mitigation plan.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=patch` `sourcetype=patch:status`, `cip007_mitigation_plans.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=patch sourcetype=patch:status bes_asset=1 earliest=-120d
| eval eval_epoch=strptime(eval_completed,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval due_epoch=eval_epoch+(35*86400)
| eval applied=if(match(status,"(?i)installed"),1,0)
| lookup cip007_mitigation_plans.csv patch_id OUTPUT plan_id approved_until
| eval mitigated=if(isnotnull(plan_id),1,0)
| where applied=0 AND mitigated=0 AND now()>due_epoch
| table host, patch_id, eval_completed, due_epoch, status
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align `eval_completed` with official evaluation logs. (2) Import mitigation plans from GRC. (3) Daily alert to server owners. (4) Ticket exceptions through CIP-003.
- **Visualization:** Table (overdue patches), Column chart (by team), Single value (asset count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---


### UC-22.13.31 · Malware Prevention Agent Coverage on BES Cyber Assets (CIP-007-6 R3 Part 3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We list in-scope hosts that have not reported antivirus or EDR health within the required interval.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=endpoint` `sourcetype=WinDefender:Status` or `sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv
| rename hostname as host
| join type=left host [
    search index=endpoint (sourcetype=WinDefender:Status OR sourcetype=crowdstrike:hosts) earliest=-48h
    | stats latest(_time) as last_report by host
  ]
| eval stale=if(isnull(last_report) OR (now()-last_report)>172800,1,0)
| where stale=1
| table host, bes_system, last_report
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize host naming between inventory and endpoint tools. (2) Set stale threshold per vendor guidance. (3) Weekly report to operations. (4) Track remediation tickets.
- **Visualization:** Table (uncovered hosts), Single value (percent reporting), Map (optional site)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

```spl
index=endpoint (sourcetype=WinDefender:Threat OR sourcetype="ms:defender:alert") earliest=-7d
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT bes_system impact_rating
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last by host, threat_name, action
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Fix sourcetype list to your EDR. (2) Map actions to blocked vs quarantined. (3) Create ES notable with CIP-008 link. (4) Retain chain-of-custody fields if exported.
- **Visualization:** Table (threats), Time chart (detections), Single value (distinct hosts)
- **CIM Models:** Malware (when CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl

```

### UC-22.13.33 · Security Event Log Generation Validation for Windows BES Hosts (CIP-007-6 R4 Part 4.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We detect Windows systems where the Security channel stopped forwarding while the host remains online, risking missing auditable events.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=wineventlog` `sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security`, `_internal` forwarder metrics
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" earliest=-48h
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats latest(_time) as last_sec by host
| eval gap_h=round((now()-last_sec)/3600,2)
| where gap_h>24
| table host, bes_system, last_sec, gap_h
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune gap threshold per risk. (2) Exclude maintenance windows. (3) Alert server owners and Splunk admins. (4) Track ticket closure with log resume proof.
- **Visualization:** Table (stale log sources), Single value (host count), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

```spl
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv ComputerName AS host OUTPUT bes_system

| eval outcome=if(EventCode==4625,"fail","success")
| stats count by Account_Name, ComputerName, outcome, bes_system
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure 4625 auditing enabled. (2) Add threshold alerts for failures. (3) Correlate with password spray use cases. (4) Retain per entity log policy.

- **CIM Models:** Authentication

```spl

| lookup shared_accounts_register.csv Account_Name OUTPUT shared_owner review_due
| where isnotnull(shared_owner)
| eval due_epoch=strptime(review_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where due_epoch < now() OR isnull(review_due)
| stats dc(ComputerName) as systems values(bes_system) as sites by Account_Name, shared_owner, review_due
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain register with review due dates. (2) Quarterly export for access governance. (3) Require re-approval tickets. (4) Remove retired shared accounts from register.

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

```spl
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv dest as asset OUTPUT bes_system

| sort - detected_time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag ES notables with BES context at correlation time. (2) Define which rules imply CIP-008 review. (3) Integrate with incident command workflow. (4) Preserve search artifacts per policy.

- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical

- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We correlate OT historian or EMS quality flags with cyber alerts to help classify incidents that may affect BES reliability or operations.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_hist` `sourcetype=scada:point`, `index=ot_security` security events
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_hist sourcetype=scada:point earliest=-24h (quality="BAD" OR quality="UNCERTAIN")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count by substation, point_name, _time
| join type=left substation [
    search index=ot_security sourcetype="ot:alert" earliest=-24h
    | stats latest(description) as cyber_alert by substation
  ]
| where isnotnull(cyber_alert)
| table _time, substation, point_name, count, cyber_alert
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize substation keys between historians and security tools. (2) Train analysts on EOP impact criteria. (3) Document classification decisions in ITSM. (4) Retain historian extracts per legal hold guidance.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (quality vs alerts), Table (joined events), Single value (windows)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151)

### UC-22.13.40 · Incident Response Timeline Milestone Tracking (CIP-008-6 R3 Part 3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We track detection, containment, eradication, and recovery timestamps from ITSM to evidence structured incident response progression.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=itsm` `sourcetype=incident:milestone` or ServiceNow custom sourcetype
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=incident:milestone category="CIP008" earliest=-90d
| eval det=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval con=strptime(contained_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval rec=strptime(recovered_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval contain_h=round((con-det)/3600,2)
| eval recover_h=round((rec-con)/3600,2)
| table incident_id, detected_at, contained_at, recovered_at, contain_h, recover_h
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require milestone fields on CIP-tagged incidents. (2) Validate timestamps in UTC. (3) Dashboard for leadership. (4) Export for after-action reviews.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (milestones), Table (SLAs), Column chart (mean contain hours)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---


### UC-22.13.41 · NERC Filing Deadline Compliance Countdown (CIP-008-6 R4 Part 4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We compute hours remaining until regulatory filing deadlines from the official incident discovery time to reduce missed submissions.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=itsm` `sourcetype=cip008_incident` (discovered_at, filing_deadline, filed_at)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=cip008_incident earliest=-60d
| eval disc=strptime(discovered_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval due=strptime(filing_deadline,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval hours_left=round((due-now())/3600,2)
| eval filed=if(isnotnull(filed_at),1,0)
| where filed=0
| table incident_id, discovered_at, filing_deadline, hours_left, owner
| sort hours_left
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate deadlines per NERC filing category from legal. (2) Alert at seventy-two, twenty-four, and six hours. (3) Attach filing confirmation artifact. (4) Restrict access to sensitive fields.
- **Visualization:** Single value (min hours left), Table (open filings), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.42 · Incident Evidence Preservation — Splunk Search Artifact Export Audit (CIP-008-6 R5 Part 5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We audit exports of saved searches and large result downloads during active incidents to support evidence preservation and chain-of-custody discipline.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** `_audit` index `action=search` `info=granted`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=_audit action=search info=granted earliest=-30d total_count>50000
| lookup cip008_active_incidents.csv search_name OUTPUT incident_id
| where isnotnull(incident_id)
| stats sum(total_count) as rows values(user) as users by incident_id, search_name
| sort - rows
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain active incident lookup updated by IR. (2) Tune `total_count` threshold. (3) Pair with proxy DLP if used. (4) Retain audit logs per records program.
- **Visualization:** Table (large searches), Bar chart (by user), Single value (export events)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.13.43 · Post-Incident Lessons Learned Action Item Tracking (CIP-008-6 R6)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We track closure of lessons-learned tasks after CIP-008 incidents so corrective actions do not stall after the initial response.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=itsm` `sourcetype=post_incident_action`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=post_incident_action cip008=1 earliest=-365d
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval open=if(match(status,"(?i)closed"),0,1)
| where open=1 AND due_epoch < now()
| table action_id, incident_id, owner, due_date, status, description
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Create ticket template for post-incident actions. (2) Weekly reminder digest. (3) Escalate overdue items to CISO office. (4) Link evidence in Splunk dashboards.
- **Visualization:** Table (overdue actions), Single value (count), Column chart (by owner)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.44 · Backup Job Success and Failure for BES Databases and Configuration Stores (CIP-009-6 R1 Part 1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We verify backup jobs for systems supporting BES recovery completed successfully so recovery planning rests on reliable restore points.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=backup` `sourcetype=veeam:job` or `commvault:job` or `rubrik:events`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=backup (sourcetype=veeam:job OR sourcetype=commvault:job) earliest=-48h
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv job_target AS host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats latest(result) as last_result latest(_time) as last_run by host, job_name
| where last_result!="Success"
| table host, job_name, last_result, last_run, bes_system
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map backup job targets to BES inventory. (2) Alert on failures and missed schedules. (3) Pair with ticket automation. (4) Retain success summaries for auditors.
- **Visualization:** Table (failed jobs), Single value (failure count), Time chart (success rate)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.45 · Backup Media Integrity Test Results (CIP-009-6 R2 Part 2.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We track restore-test and checksum results for backup media containing BES configuration or security data.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=backup` `sourcetype=backup:integrity_test`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=backup sourcetype=backup:integrity_test bes_scope=1 earliest=-180d
| eval test_epoch=strptime(test_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval age_days=round((now()-test_epoch)/86400,0)
| where match(result,"(?i)fail") OR age_days>120
| table media_id, test_date, result, age_days, vault
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Emit HEC when integrity tests complete. (2) Define maximum age without re-test. (3) Alert storage team. (4) Document failures in CIP-008 if data loss risk.
- **Visualization:** Table (stale or failed tests), Timeline, Single value (failed)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.46 · Recovery Plan Exercise Attendance and Scenario Evidence (CIP-009-6 R3 Part 3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We log who ran tabletop or functional recovery exercises and which BES systems were exercised, supporting recovery plan testing evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip009_exercise`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip009_exercise earliest=-400d
| eval ex_epoch=strptime(exercise_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval annual_cutoff=relative_time(now(),"-1y@d")
| stats latest(exercise_type) as last_type latest(exercise_date) as last_date values(participant) as people by bes_system
| eval compliant=if(strptime(last_date,"%Y-%m-%d")>=annual_cutoff,1,0)
| where compliant=0
| table bes_system, last_date, last_type, people
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Push exercise completion events from GRC. (2) Align `bes_system` with CIP-002 list. (3) Alert before annual due. (4) Attach scenario documents out-of-band with links in events.
- **Visualization:** Table (systems needing exercise), Single value (non-compliant count), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.47 · Data Preservation During Recovery Activities (CIP-009-6 R4 Part 4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We monitor mass deletes or index resets during declared recovery windows that could destroy forensic or compliance evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** `_audit` index destructive actions, `index=itsm` maintenance windows
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=_audit (action=delete OR match(action,"(?i)clean")) earliest=-7d
| lookup bes_splunk_indexes.csv index OUTPUT bes_evidence_index
| where bes_evidence_index="true"
| join type=left user [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=maint_window earliest=-14d
    | eval mw=1
    | fields user mw
  ]
| where isnull(mw)
| table _time, user, action, index, object
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag evidence-bearing indexes in lookup. (2) Require maintenance window tickets for bulk deletes. (3) Page IR and compliance on hits. (4) Pair with bucket freeze procedures.
- **Visualization:** Table (events), Timeline, Single value (violations)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.13.48 · BES Cyber System Restore Drill — RTO Measurement (CIP-009-6 R5 Part 5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We compute restore-time metrics from drill tickets and application availability pings to evidence recovery capability testing.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=itsm` `sourcetype=restore_drill`, `index=apps` `sourcetype=http:ping`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=restore_drill bes_system=* earliest=-400d
| eval start=strptime(started_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end=strptime(restored_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval rto_min=round((end-start)/60,2)
| lookup cip009_rto_targets.csv bes_system OUTPUT target_min
| where rto_min > target_min
| table drill_id, bes_system, rto_min, target_min, restored_at
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate RTO targets from recovery plans. (2) Validate timestamps from orchestration tools. (3) Track remediation for misses. (4) Annual leadership briefing export.
- **Visualization:** Column chart (RTO vs target), Table (misses), Single value (worst RTO)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.49 · Baseline Configuration Deviation — Key OS and Application Settings (CIP-010-4 R1 Part 1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We compare configuration snapshots from servers and appliances to approved baselines so unauthorized drift on BES Cyber Assets is visible.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=config` `sourcetype=config:snapshot`, `bes_config_baseline.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=config sourcetype=config:snapshot earliest=-24h
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| lookup bes_config_baseline.csv host, setting_name OUTPUT expected_value
| where expected_value!=current_value
| table host, setting_name, expected_value, current_value, bes_system
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Collect snapshots via scripted input or Chef/Ansible exports. (2) Version baselines with change tickets. (3) Alert on critical settings only first. (4) Feed drift into change review board.
- **Visualization:** Table (drift rows), Bar chart (by setting), Single value (host count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### UC-22.13.50 · Unauthorized Software Installation on BES Windows Servers (CIP-010-4 R2 Part 2.1)

- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security

- **Data Sources:** `index=endpoint` `sourcetype=inventory:software`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype=inventory:software earliest=-7d
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| lookup approved_software_catalog.csv product_name OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen by host, product_name, version
| sort - first_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate approved catalog from change management. (2) Deduplicate version strings. (3) Weekly review with server owners. (4) Auto-ticket unapproved installs.
- **Visualization:** Table (new software), Bar chart (by product), Single value (distinct hosts)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

### UC-22.13.51 · Configuration Change Authorization Linkage for Network Devices (CIP-010-4 R3 Part 3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We require network configuration commits on ESP devices to reference an approved change identifier in syslog or commit comments.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:config`, `index=itsm`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:config earliest=-7d
| rex field=cmd "(?i)CHG(?<chg>[0-9]{6,10})"
| lookup esp_device_inventory.csv hostname AS host OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true"
| join type=left chg [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=chg:network earliest=-30d
    | rename number as chg
    | stats latest(state) as ticket_state by chg
  ]
| where isnull(ticket_state) OR ticket_state!="Closed"
| table _time, host, cmd, chg, ticket_state
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Standardize change number format in commit templates. (2) Map devices to ESP scope. (3) Daily alert on missing tickets. (4) Escalate repeat violators.
- **Visualization:** Table (missing linkage), Bar chart (by device), Single value (count)
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

---


### UC-22.13.52 · Vulnerability Assessment After Material Configuration Changes (CIP-010-4 R4 Part 4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We find material firewall or OS changes without a subsequent vulnerability scan within the required follow-up window.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=itsm` material changes, `index=vuln` Tenable or Qualys
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=chg:network material=1 earliest=-35d
| eval chg_time=strptime(closed_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| rename number as change_id
| join type=left change_id [
    search index=vuln sourcetype=tenable:scans earliest=-35d
    | rename change_ticket as change_id
    | stats latest(finished_at) as scan_done by change_id
  ]
| eval scan_epoch=strptime(scan_done,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| where isnull(scan_epoch) OR (scan_epoch-chg_time)>1209600
| table change_id, closed_at, scan_done
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Flag material changes in ITSM. (2) Require scanner to tag `change_ticket`. (3) Alert at fourteen days without scan. (4) Document risk acceptance if delayed.
- **Visualization:** Table (missing scans), Timeline, Single value (open gaps)
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities (when CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

---


### UC-22.13.53 · Transient Cyber Asset Connection Logging to ESP Jump Zones (CIP-010-4 R5 Part 5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We log and summarize laptops or vendor devices connecting transiently inside ESP jump zones for transient cyber asset visibility.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=pan:traffic`, DHCP logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype=pan:traffic dest_zone="ESP_JUMP" earliest=-24h
| eval tca=if(match(src,"dhcp_pool"),1,0)
| lookup dhcp_leases.csv mac AS mac OUTPUT hostname
| stats earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last sum(bytes) as bytes by src, hostname, user
| sort - last
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Correlate DHCP MAC to user if available. (2) Maintain TCA register with expected devices. (3) Alert on unknown TCA over one hour. (4) Scan-before-connect workflow tracked separately.
- **Visualization:** Table (TCA sessions), Timeline, Map (optional)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=6416 earliest=-7d
| lookup bes_engineering_hosts.csv ComputerName OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| stats count by ComputerName, DeviceName, ClassName, bes_system
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable device installation auditing. (2) Optionally deploy Sysmon for richer context. (3) Weekly review with OT engineering. (4) Document approved maintenance media in lookup.
- **Visualization:** Table (mounts), Bar chart (by host), Single value (distinct devices)
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint (when CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

### UC-22.13.55 · TCA Scan-Before-Connect Compliance from NAC or Agent Logs (CIP-010-4 R5 Part 5.3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We verify transient cyber assets received a successful posture or malware scan before being granted ESP network access.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=network` `sourcetype=cisco:ise:radius` or `sourcetype=pan:globalprotect`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype=cisco:ise:radius earliest=-24h Acct-Status-Type=Start
| lookup tca_device_registry.csv calling_station_id OUTPUT asset_tag
| where isnotnull(asset_tag)
| join type=left calling_station_id [
    search index=endpoint sourcetype=defender:scan earliest=-24h
    | rename device_id as calling_station_id
    | stats latest(scan_result) as scan by calling_station_id
  ]
| where scan!="clean" OR isnull(scan)
| table _time, calling_station_id, user, scan, asset_tag
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align RADIUS calling-station IDs with endpoint inventory keys. (2) Tune for your NAC vendor sourcetype. (3) Block or quarantine integration outside Splunk. (4) Retain pass/fail for audits.
- **Visualization:** Table (non-compliant TCA), Single value (count), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.13.56 · Baseline Update Documentation — Config Baseline Version Changes (CIP-010-4 R1 Part 1.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We audit when official configuration baseline versions change and require an authorizing record for each update.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip010_baseline_version earliest=-365d
| sort 0 - _time
| streamstats current=f latest(baseline_version) as prev_version by platform
| where baseline_version!=prev_version
| eval has_auth=if(isnotnull(auth_record_id),1,0)
| where has_auth=0
| table _time, platform, baseline_version, prev_version, owner, auth_record_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Emit version bump events from CMDB or GitOps. (2) Require `auth_record_id` from CAB approval. (3) Alert compliance on missing linkage. (4) Archive prior baselines offline.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (version bumps), Table (missing auth), Single value (violations)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.57 · BES Cyber System Information Access via Share and Web Downloads (CIP-011-3 R1 Part 1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We monitor access to file shares and web paths classified as BES Cyber System Information for inappropriate bulk retrieval.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=wineventlog` `EventCode=5145` or proxy logs, `bcsi_path_tags.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=5145 earliest=-24h
| lookup bcsi_path_tags.csv ShareName, RelativeTargetName OUTPUT classification
| where classification="BCSI"
| stats sum(TransferredBytes) as bytes by Account_Name, ShareName, ComputerName
| where bytes > 104857600
| sort - bytes
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag BCSI paths in lookup from information protection register. (2) Enable detailed file share auditing. (3) Alert on high-byte transfers. (4) Integrate with insider threat program.
- **Visualization:** Table (large transfers), Bar chart (by user), Single value (events)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### UC-22.13.58 · BCSI Storage Location Inventory vs. Observed Disk Paths (CIP-011-3 R2 Part 2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We compare discovered storage paths containing BCSI keywords against the approved storage location inventory.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=inventory` `sourcetype=filesystem:scan`, `bcsi_approved_locations.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=inventory sourcetype=filesystem:scan earliest=-7d match(path,"(?i)one-line|relay|settings|cyber")
| lookup bes_cyber_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT bes_system
| where isnotnull(bes_system)
| lookup bcsi_approved_locations.csv host, path_prefix OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved)
| stats values(path) as paths by host, bes_system
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Run constrained filesystem scans with legal approval. (2) Tune regex to reduce false positives. (3) Investigate unknown paths quickly. (4) Update inventory when validated.
- **Visualization:** Table (unapproved paths), Single value (host count), Bar chart (by site)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

```spl
| stats count by action, sender, recipient
| sort - count
```

### UC-22.13.60 · BCSI Destruction and Sanitization Evidence from ITAD Tickets (CIP-011-3 R4 Part 4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We correlate IT asset disposal tickets with wipe certificates indexed as structured events for sanitization evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=itsm` `sourcetype=itad:disposal`, `sourcetype=itad:wipe_cert`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=itad:disposal status="completed" earliest=-365d
| rename asset_serial as serial
| join type=left serial [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=itad:wipe_cert earliest=-365d
    | stats latest(wipe_method) as method latest(cert_id) as cert by serial
  ]
| where bcsi_stored="true" AND isnull(cert)
| table disposal_id, serial, asset_tag, completed_at, cert
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require vendors to upload certificate metadata to ITSM. (2) Alert when disposal closes without cert. (3) Store PDF hashes out-of-band if needed. (4) Annual sampling by internal audit.
- **Visualization:** Table (missing certs), Single value (gap count), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.61 · Control Center Real-Time Assessment TLS Cipher and Certificate Health (CIP-012-1 R1 Part 1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We monitor TLS handshakes or certificate metadata for inter-control-center assessment links to catch weak ciphers or expiring certificates.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Stream (optional) or certificate inventory feeds, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=cert` `sourcetype=cert:inventory`, `control_center_links.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cert sourcetype=cert:inventory earliest=-7d
| lookup control_center_links.csv fqdn OUTPUT link_name cip012_scope
| where cip012_scope="true"
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(not_after,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_left=round((exp_epoch-now())/86400,0)
| where days_left < 45 OR match(cipher_suite,"(?i)RC4|DES")
| table link_name, fqdn, days_left, cipher_suite, issuer
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate inventory from scanners or ACME logs. (2) Alert at forty-five and fourteen days to expiry. (3) Track cipher suite deprecations. (4) Pair with firewall allowlist verification.
- **Visualization:** Table (at-risk links), Single value (expiring count), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** Certificates (when CIM-tagged)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Certificates.All_Certificates by All_Certificates.ssl_subject_common_name, All_Certificates.dest | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Certificates](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Certificates)

### UC-22.13.62 · Inter-Control-Center Link Availability from Synthetic Tests (CIP-012-1 R2 Part 2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We measure synthetic ping or application handshake success rates between control centers to evidence communications availability.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=synthetic` `sourcetype=http:ping` or `sourcetype=tcp:check`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=synthetic (sourcetype=http:ping OR sourcetype=tcp:check) cip012_link=1 earliest=-24h
| stats count(eval(match(status,"(?i)success"))) as ok count as total by link_id
| eval pct=round(100*ok/total,2)
| where pct < 99.5
| lookup control_center_links.csv link_id OUTPUT src_cc dest_cc
| table link_id, src_cc, dest_cc, pct, ok, total
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Deploy lightweight synthetic from each center. (2) Tune SLA percentage. (3) Page network operations on breach. (4) Retain raw samples for monthly report.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (availability), Single value (worst link), Table (breaches)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.13.63 · Communication Path Integrity — Unexpected Route or ASN Changes (CIP-012-1 R3 Part 3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We detect when BGP or path monitoring shows control center interconnects traversing unexpected autonomous systems or countries.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netflow` `sourcetype=flow:internet` or `sourcetype=thousandeyes:tests`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netflow sourcetype=flow:internet cip012_path=1 earliest=-24h
| stats values(dest_asn) as asn_list by src_cc, dest_cc, hour
| lookup cip012_approved_asn.csv src_cc, dest_cc, dest_asn OUTPUT approved
| mvexpand asn_list
| rename asn_list as dest_asn
| lookup cip012_approved_asn.csv src_cc, dest_cc, dest_asn OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved)
| table src_cc, dest_cc, dest_asn, hour
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build approved ASN matrix with carriers. (2) Enrich flows with GeoIP cautiously. (3) Alert NOC on drift. (4) Document maintenance reroutes in lookup.
- **Visualization:** Table (unexpected ASNs), Map (optional), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.13.64 · Vendor Risk Assessment Due Dates for Critical Suppliers (CIP-013-1 R1 Part 1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We track vendor security risk assessments for suppliers touching BES Cyber Systems and alert before assessments expire.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=vendor:risk`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=vendor:risk critical_vendor=1 earliest=-730d
| eval due=strptime(assessment_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval days_left=round((due-now())/86400,0)
| where days_left < 30 OR isnull(assessment_completed)
| table vendor_id, vendor_name, assessment_due, days_left, owner
| sort days_left
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Sync vendor register from TPRM tool. (2) Alert at ninety, thirty, and seven days. (3) Block new access for expired high-risk vendors per policy. (4) Export for supply chain committee.
- **Visualization:** Table (due soon), Single value (expired count), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype=package:verify bes_scope=1 earliest=-30d
| where match(result,"(?i)fail|invalid|unsigned")
| stats count by package_name, host, signer, result
| sort - count
```

- **Visualization:** Table (failures), Bar chart (by package), Single value (hosts)
- **CIM Models:** Malware (when CIM-tagged)

```spl

```

### UC-22.13.66 · Vendor Security Incident Notification Receipt Tracking (CIP-013-1 R3 Part 3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We log inbound vendor security bulletins and track acknowledgement SLAs for incidents that may affect BES software or services.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=email` `sourcetype=vendor:bulletin` or HEC JSON
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=email sourcetype=vendor:bulletin earliest=-90d severity IN ("high","critical")
| eval recv=strptime(received_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval ack_epoch=strptime(acknowledged_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval ack_h=round((ack_epoch-recv)/3600,2)
| where isnull(acknowledged_at) OR ack_h>72
| table bulletin_id, vendor, received_at, acknowledged_at, ack_h
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Parse vendor mailing lists into structured events. (2) Define acknowledgement workflow in ITSM. (3) Alert procurement and security. (4) Link to CIP-008 if operational impact.
- **Visualization:** Table (late acks), Single value (open critical), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

- **Regulations:** NERC CIP

- **Data Sources:** `index=itsm` `sourcetype=supply_chain_incident`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=supply_chain_incident earliest=-180d status!="Closed"
| eval due=strptime(remediation_due,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where due < now()
| stats values(task) as open_tasks by incident_id, vendor, component
| sort - incident_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Create ticket type for supply chain cases. (2) Link tasks to asset inventory rows. (3) Daily stand-up dashboard. (4) Close with compensating control evidence if needed.
- **Visualization:** Table (overdue incidents), Column chart (by vendor), Single value (open)

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We index completed threat assessment metadata for transmission stations so evidence dates and reviewers are searchable for CIP-014 reviews.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment earliest=-730d
| eval completed=strptime(completed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval due_review=relative_time(completed,"+3y@d")
| where now()>due_review
| table station_id, completed_date, reviewer, due_review, next_assessment_scheduled
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Push assessment completions via HEC when signed. (2) Set review cadence per program (example three years). (3) Alert owners before due. (4) Attach PDF hash externally if required.
- **Visualization:** Table (stations due), Timeline, Single value (count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.13.69 · Transmission Physical Security Plan Control Checklist Status (CIP-014-3 R2 Part 2.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NERC CIP
- **Value:** We track periodic checklist completions for transmission station physical security plan controls such as lighting, fencing, and cameras.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip014_control_check`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip014_control_check earliest=-400d
| eval performed=strptime(performed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval overdue=if(now()-performed>7776000,1,0)
| where overdue=1 AND result!="pass"
| table station_id, control_id, performed_date, result, owner
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Encode ninety-day inspection default (tune per plan). (2) Mobile form integration to Splunk via HEC. (3) Escalate failed controls to field ops. (4) Photo evidence stored outside Splunk with URL field.
- **Visualization:** Table (failed/overdue), Column chart (by control), Single value (stations)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151)

```spl
| lookup cip014_station_register station_id OUTPUT criticality
| stats count earliest(_time) as first latest(_time) as last by station_id, alarm_type, criticality
| sort - count
```
