### 22.14 NIST 800-53 Rev. 5

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833), Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for Google Workspace (Splunkbase 5556), Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620), Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 2757), Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060), Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk App for Lookup File Editing (Splunkbase 1724)

**Data Sources:** Windows Security / Sysmon (`WinEventLog:Security`, `XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational`), Linux auditd (`linux:audit`), AWS CloudTrail (`aws:cloudtrail`), Microsoft 365 unified audit (`ms:o365:management`), Google Workspace Reports (`google:workspace:admin`), firewall and IDS/IPS (`pan:threat`, `cisco:asa`, `suricata`), VPN and remote access logs, Splunk `_audit` and `_internal`, vulnerability indexes (`tenable:sc` / `tenable:vuln`), CMDB/inventory lookups, certificate and TLS metadata, database audit trails, ticketing (`snow:incident`, `snow:change_request`), CIM data models (Authentication, Change, Endpoint, Network_Traffic, Intrusion_Detection, Malware, Vulnerabilities, Certificates)

### UC-22.14.1 · Centralized Audit Event Logging Policy Coverage (AU-2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Shows which systems and sourcetypes are forwarding security-relevant events so auditors can evidence that logging policy scope matches AU-2 event types.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=sec` OR `index=os` — Windows Security, Linux auditd, cloud control planes (sourcetypes vary by TA)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index IN (sec, os, cloud) earliest=-24h
| stats dc(host) as hosts, dc(sourcetype) as sourcetypes, count as events by index, sourcetype
| lookup nist_au2_event_coverage.csv sourcetype OUTPUT au2_event_family, required
| eval gap=if(required="true" AND events=0, "No_Data", "OK")
| sort index, sourcetype
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `nist_au2_event_coverage.csv` mapping sourcetypes to AU-2 event families; (2) schedule daily and alert on sourcetypes with `required=true` and zero volume; (3) onboard missing forwarders or enable missing channels; (4) attach evidence export for assessors.
- **Visualization:** Table (coverage by sourcetype), Single value (percent required sources reporting), Bar chart (event volume by family)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Endpoint, Change — where CIM-tagged
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.14.2 · Audit Record Content Completeness for Privileged Actions (AU-3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Validates that privileged account activity records include who, what, when, where, and outcome fields required for AU-3 content of audit records.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` OR CIM `Authentication` — EventCode 4672, 4728, 4732, 4756, etc.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-24h EventCode IN (4672,4728,4732,4756,4767,4720,4722,4725)
| eval missing_user=if(isnull(user) OR user="",1,0)
| eval missing_dest=if(isnull(dest) OR dest="",1,0)
| eval missing_outcome=if(isnull(signature) OR signature="",1,0)
| eval au3_gap=missing_user+missing_dest+missing_outcome
| stats count as events, sum(au3_gap) as incomplete by EventCode, host
| where incomplete>0
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize Windows events into CIM Authentication fields via props/transforms; (2) tune extractions for workgroup vs domain naming; (3) alert when `incomplete>0` for Tier0 hosts; (4) pair with AD schema docs for field mapping evidence.
- **Visualization:** Table (EventCode, host, incomplete counts), Bar chart (gaps by host), Single value (incomplete rate)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.14.3 · Audit Storage Capacity and Index Growth Guardrails (AU-4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Detects indexes approaching disk or license limits that could cause loss of audit evidence, supporting AU-4 audit storage capacity management.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform (REST, DMC)
- **Data Sources:** `_internal` (metrics.log license), `/services/data/indexes` (index size, frozenTimePeriodInSecs)
- **SPL:**
```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0 NOT title IN ("_*")
| eval size_gb=currentDBSizeMB/1024
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,1)
| table title, size_gb, maxTotalDataSizeMB, retention_days, frozenTimePeriodInSecs
| sort - size_gb
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Run from a scheduled search with `rest` rights; (2) compare `size_gb` to business thresholds by index class; (3) integrate license usage from `_internal` for combined risk scoring; (4) open change requests before forced rolling.
- **Visualization:** Single value (indexes over threshold), Table (top consumers), Column chart (size by index)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.14.4 · Response to Audit Logging Failures and Forwarder Gaps (AU-5)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Correlates forwarder offline events, TCP routing failures, and HEC 4xx/5xx spikes so operators can respond before AU-5 audit logging failures silently drop evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Universal Forwarder / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** `_internal` sourcetype=`splunkd` OR `http_event_collector_metrics`, deployment server phonehome metrics
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=_internal earliest=-4h (sourcetype=splunkd OR sourcetype=http_event_collector_metrics)
| bin _time span=15m
| stats count(eval(match(_raw,"(?i)blocked|pause|queue|full|failure"))) as failure_signals,
        dc(host) as hosts by _time, sourcetype
| where failure_signals>0
| sort - failure_signals
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable verbose forwarder logging only on troubleshooting windows to avoid noise; (2) route `_internal` to a security operations index with RBAC; (3) alert on sustained `failure_signals`; (4) document compensating controls when maintenance pauses logging.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (failure signals), Table (top hosts), Alert list (spikes)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

```spl
| stats count by user, action, dest, app
| sort - count
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define `privileged=1` via `identities.csv` or ES asset priority; (2) replace macro with your CIM index macro; (3) schedule weekly PDF/CSV to GRC; (4) store signed exports for assessor sampling.
- **Visualization:** Table (top privileged actions), Bar chart (by user), Sankey or pivot (optional ES)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication

```spl

```

### UC-22.14.6 · Audit Reduction and Report Generation Integrity (AU-7)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Tracks scheduled searches that materialize audit reports to ensure AU-7 reduction and aggregation does not remove required contextual fields.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** `_audit` (search, savedsearch_name, info), summary indexes `index=summary`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=_audit action=search info=completed earliest=-24h
| search savedsearch_name="*AU7*" OR savedsearch_name="*audit_report*"
| stats avg(total_run_time) as avg_runtime, max(total_run_time) as max_runtime, count by savedsearch_name, user
| eval sla_breach=if(avg_runtime>300,1,0)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Prefix audit-export saved searches with a standard name; (2) validate fields in summary vs raw sample monthly; (3) alert on `sla_breach`; (4) document field retention in the audit reduction procedure.
- **Visualization:** Table (saved search performance), Single value (breach count), Time chart (runtime trend)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.14.7 · Time Synchronization and Clock Skew for Audit Timestamps (AU-8)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Surfaces hosts whose event `_time` diverges from indexer time beyond tolerance, evidencing AU-8 time stamp synchronization.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Universal Forwarder, Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:System` (W32Time), `linux:secure` / `ntp` logs, `_indextime` vs `_time` delta
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os earliest=-24h
| eval skew_sec=abs(_indextime-_time)
| stats perc95(skew_sec) as p95_skew, max(skew_sec) as max_skew by host, sourcetype
| where p95_skew>120 OR max_skew>300
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Standardize NTP/chrony on all forwarders; (2) tune thresholds per sourcetype (batch logs vs real-time); (3) alert Tier0 assets on breach; (4) attach remediation runbook for clock drift.
- **Visualization:** Table (worst skew by host), Single value (hosts breaching threshold), Map (optional geo context)
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

```spl
| sort - count
```

- **Visualization:** Table (object, actions), Timeline (admin changes), Single value (critical changes per day)

### UC-22.14.9 · Non-Repudiation Evidence for Sensitive Transactions (AU-10)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Chains application user actions to authentication events and source IPs to support AU-10 non-repudiation for high-risk transactions.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (Splunkbase 2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742)
- **Data Sources:** Application DB audit tables (via DBX), `WinEventLog:Security` logons
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=app sourcetype=app:txn_audit earliest=-24h status=success risk_tier="HIGH"
| eval join_key=md5(user.src_ip)
| join type=left join_key [
    search index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4624 earliest=-24h
    | eval join_key=md5(user.src_ip)
    | stats latest(_time) as last_logon by join_key, user, src_ip
  ]
| where isnull(last_logon)
| table _time, user, src_ip, txn_id, action
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Prefer `transaction` or `stats` over `join` at scale — prototype first; (2) hash PII keys consistently; (3) validate clock sync (AU-8); (4) document limitations where shared accounts break non-repudiation.
- **Visualization:** Table (orphaned high-risk txns), Timeline (txn vs logon), Stats (match rate)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication (for Windows side)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.14.10 · Audit Record Retention Compliance vs Policy (AU-11)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Compares configured frozen retention to policy targets for systems of record logs supporting AU-11 audit record retention.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** `/services/data/indexes`, lookup `nist_retention_policy.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0
| search disabled=0
| eval retention_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,1)
| lookup nist_retention_policy.csv title OUTPUT required_min_days, system_category
| eval compliant=if(retention_days+0>=required_min_days+0,"Yes","No")
| where compliant="No" OR isnull(required_min_days)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build `nist_retention_policy.csv` with index or prefix to required days; (2) reconcile with offline archive retention; (3) alert on non-compliance; (4) export for POA&M tracking.
- **Visualization:** Table (index, retention, required), Bar chart (gap days), Single value (non-compliant count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.14.11 · Audit Generation Coverage for Critical Network Controls (AU-12)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Verifies that boundary devices continue generating audit records for allow/deny decisions, evidencing AU-12 audit generation for network events.
- **App/TA:** Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621)
- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic`, `pan:threat` or CIM Network_Traffic / Intrusion_Detection sources
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=net (sourcetype=pan:traffic OR sourcetype=pan:threat) earliest=-24h
| stats earliest(_time) as first, latest(_time) as last, count by host, sourcetype
| eval gap_minutes=round((now()-strptime(last,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S %z"))/60,1)
| eval gap_minutes=if(isnull(gap_minutes), round((now()-last)/60,1), gap_minutes)
| where gap_minutes>30
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Fix timestamp parsing per TA — sample `last` field; (2) tune gap threshold per HA pair; (3) alert if firewall stops logging; (4) map to firewall inventory CM-8.
- **Visualization:** Table (firewall, gap), Single value (devices silent), Time chart (EPS trend)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic, Intrusion_Detection
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High

```spl
index=os earliest=-24h (sourcetype=linux:secure OR sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security)
| search process=sshd OR EventCode IN (4624,4647,4778)
| eval session_actor=coalesce(user, Account_Name)
| stats earliest(_time) as start, latest(_time) as end, values(process) as processes by session_actor, src_ip, dest
| eval duration_sec=end-start
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable verbose sshd logging per policy; (2) map EventCode meanings in a lookup; (3) alert on long sessions to sensitive assets; (4) redact passwords never present in logs.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (session spans), Table (duration by admin), Bar chart (sessions by jump host)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.src, Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

### UC-22.14.14 · Alternate Audit Capability During Control Outages (AU-15)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Detects when primary logging pipelines fail and compensating syslog or packet capture taps increase, evidencing AU-15 alternate audit capability activation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Stream (Splunkbase 1809), Splunk Connect for Syslog
- **Data Sources:** `_internal` pipeline errors, `index=net` tap sourcetypes
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=_internal OR index=net earliest=-24h
| eval channel=case(match(sourcetype,"stream"),"PCAP_TAP", match(sourcetype,"syslog"),"SYSLOG_ALT",1=1,"PRIMARY")
| bin _time span=15m
| stats sum(eval(if(channel="PRIMARY",1,0))) as primary_hits,
        sum(eval(if(channel!="PRIMARY",1,0))) as alt_hits by _time, host
| eval alt_ratio=round(alt_hits/(primary_hits+alt_hits+0.001),3)
| where alt_ratio>0.3
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Label alternate paths consistently; (2) tune ratio for your architecture; (3) require ticket linkage when alt_ratio spikes; (4) review monthly with contingency tests (CP-10).
- **Visualization:** Time chart (alt_ratio), Table (hosts), Single value (minutes in compensating mode)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic (Stream)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest span=15m | sort - agg_value
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Stream](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1809), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

---

### UC-22.14.15 · Cross-Organizational Audit Forwarding Health to SIEM (AU-16)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Validates receipt rates from partner or subsidiary syslog streams against SLAs for AU-16 cross-organizational auditing agreements.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform, Splunk Connect for Syslog
- **Data Sources:** `index=partner*` OR `host=partner_*` sourcetype=syslog or HEC tokens per org
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=partner earliest=-24h
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by _time, org_id, sourcetype
| eventstats median(count) as med by org_id, sourcetype
| eval drop=if(count < med*0.5, 1, 0)
| where drop=1
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag events with `org_id` at ingest; (2) baseline median EPS per hour/day; (3) alert on sustained drops; (4) document contractual EPS and retention in the interconnection agreement (CA-9).
- **Visualization:** Time chart (EPS by org), Table (drop windows), Single value (orgs in breach)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

```spl
| outputlookup ad_account_last_change.csv
```

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

### UC-22.14.17 · Access Enforcement — Unauthorized Access Attempts to Sensitive Shares (AC-3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Surfaces repeated failed object access attempts that indicate AC-3 access enforcement gaps or misconfigured ACLs.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` EventCode 4656/4663 (object access) when auditing enabled
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4656,4663) earliest=-24h
| search Object_Type="File" ShareName="\\*SECRET*"
| stats count by user, ShareName, ObjectName, host
| where count>25
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable granular SACLs on crown-jewel shares; (2) normalize `ShareName` fields; (3) tune threshold per sensitivity; (4) integrate with case management for access reviews.
- **Visualization:** Table (top requesters), Bar chart (by share), Heatmap (hour x user optional)
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.14.18 · Separation of Duties Violations in Change Tickets (AC-5)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Detects the same identity approving and implementing changes for AC-5 separation of duties.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
- **Data Sources:** `snow:change_request` with requested_by, assigned_to, closed_by
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request earliest=-30d
| eval same_actor=if(requested_by=assigned_to OR requested_by=closed_by,1,0)
| where same_actor=1 AND priority IN ("1","2")
| table number, short_description, requested_by, assigned_to, closed_by, priority, _time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field names match your SNOW transform; (2) exclude emergency changes via `chg_model` lookup; (3) alert weekly summary to ITGC; (4) document approved break-glass codes.
- **Visualization:** Table (violations), Pie chart (SoD pass/fail), Single value (count open)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)

### UC-22.14.19 · Least Privilege — Excessive Cloud IAM Permissions (AC-6)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Reviews AWS CloudTrail `AttachUserPolicy` events for high-risk managed policies supporting AC-6 least privilege.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876)
- **Data Sources:** `aws:cloudtrail` management events
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-7d eventName IN ("AttachUserPolicy","PutUserPolicy","AttachRolePolicy")
| spath path=eventSource output=eventSource
| spath path=requestParameters output=requestParameters
| search requestParameters="*AdministratorAccess*" OR requestParameters="*IAMFullAccess*"
| stats count by userName, eventName, requestParameters, aws_account_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Use `spath` or JSON KV mode consistently; (2) maintain deny-list of risky policy ARNs; (3) auto-ticket attachments; (4) quarterly access review export.
- **Visualization:** Table (who attached what), Time chart (high-risk grants), Bar chart (by account)
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.14.21 · System Use Notification Banner Acceptance in SSH Sessions (AC-8)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Verifies SSH banner or interactive MOTD markers appear in session logs for AC-8 system use notification where technically feasible.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (Splunkbase 833)
- **Data Sources:** `linux:secure` sshd session lines
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os sourcetype=linux:secure earliest=-7d process=sshd
| search "Accepted" OR "session opened"
| rex "(?i)banner=(?<banner_ack>yes|no)"
| stats count by banner_ack, dest
| where banner_ack="no" OR isnull(banner_ack)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Standardize sshd_config `Banner` path; (2) optionally emit structured JSON from bastion; (3) do not log full banner text if classified; (4) report exceptions monthly.
- **Visualization:** Table (hosts missing ack), Bar chart (by datacenter), Single value (percent compliant)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833)

### UC-22.14.22 · Session Lock Events for Workstation Inactivity Policy (AC-11)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Monitors workstation lock (4800) and unlock (4801) ratio for AC-11 session lock enforcement.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` 4800/4801
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4800,4801) earliest=-7d
| eval evt=if(EventCode=4800,"lock","unlock")
| stats count by evt, ComputerName
| xyseries ComputerName evt count
| eval lock_ratio=round(lock/(lock+unlock+0.001),3)
| where lock_ratio<0.2
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Validate ComputerName field extraction; (2) exclude kiosk OU via lookup; (3) sample manual validation on outliers; (4) integrate with MDM compliance scores if available.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (lock_ratio by OU via lookup), Table (lowest hosts)
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Monitors workstation lock](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4800), [and unlock](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4801), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.14.23 · Session Termination on Logoff and VPN Disconnect (AC-12)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Correlates VPN connect/disconnect with OS logoff events to evidence AC-12 session termination when sessions end.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742)
- **Data Sources:** `cisco:asa` VPN teardown, `WinEventLog:Security` 4634
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=net OR index=sec earliest=-24h (sourcetype=cisco:asa OR sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security)
| eval et=case(EventCode=4634,"win_logoff", match(_raw,"Teardown"),"vpn_teardown",1=1,null())
| stats count by et, user, src_ip
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize VPN user field to match AD `user`; (2) alert on long VPN sessions without logoff (heuristic); (3) document remote access policy mapping to AC-17; (4) tune for split tunnel false positives.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (VPN vs logoff), Table (long sessions), Single value (disconnect anomalies)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic, Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1620), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.14.24 · Remote Access Anomalies — Geo-Velocity and Off-Hours VPN (AC-17)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Detects impossible travel and off-hours remote access for AC-17 remote access monitoring.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA (Splunkbase 1620)
- **Data Sources:** `ms:o365:management` SignIns, `cisco:asa` VPN logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management Workload=AzureActiveDirectory Operation=UserLoggedIn earliest=-24h
| iplocation extended_field=ClientIP
| stats earliest(_time) as t1, latest(_time) as t2, values(Country) as countries by UserId, ClientIP
| eval delta_h=round((t2-t1)/3600,2)
| where mvcount(countries)>1 AND delta_h<2
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Validate `UserLoggedIn` operation naming for tenant; (2) pair with MFA status field if present; (3) reduce noise with corporate travel calendar lookup optional; (4) document tuning in SSP.
- **Visualization:** Map (country hops), Table (impossible travel), Time chart (after-hours VPN)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055), [Splunk Add-on for Cisco ASA](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1620), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-30d Operation="Consent to application"
| stats values(UserId) as users, latest(_time) as last_seen by ObjectId
| lookup risky_oauth_apps.csv ObjectId OUTPUT app_risk
| where app_risk="high" OR match(ObjectId,"unknown")
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build `risky_oauth_apps.csv` from security reviews; (2) integrate CASB signals if forwarded; (3) quarterly user attestation; (4) auto-revoke workflow outside Splunk with ticket link.

### UC-22.14.26 · Multifactor Authentication Gaps for Interactive Sign-Ins (IA-2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Surfaces cloud sign-ins that succeed without strong authentication methods for IA-2 identification and authentication (MFA).
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055), Splunk Add-on for Google Workspace (Splunkbase 5556)
- **Data Sources:** `ms:o365:management` SignIn logs, `google:workspace:login`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management Workload=AzureActiveDirectory earliest=-24h
| search Operation="UserLoggedIn" OR Operation="Sign-in activity*"
| eval mfa=if(match(AuthenticationMethod,"*MFA*") OR match(AuthenticationMethod,"*multifactor*"),1,0)
| where mfa=0 AND ResultStatus="Success"
| stats count by UserId, ClientIP, AuthenticationMethod, ApplicationId
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize `AuthenticationMethod` values for your tenant; (2) exclude break-glass accounts via lookup; (3) alert on admin roles without MFA; (4) map to Conditional Access rollout milestones.
- **Visualization:** Table (users without MFA), Bar chart (by app), Time chart (trend)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055), [Splunk Add-on for Google Workspace](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5556), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53

```spl
| stats latest(OperatingSystem) as os, dc(UserId) as users by DeviceId

| where compliance_state!="compliant" OR isnull(compliance_state)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build `corp_managed_devices.csv` from Intune/JAMF export; (2) refresh nightly; (3) alert on unknown DeviceId with privileged users; (4) document attribute mapping in SSP.
- **Visualization:** Table (non-compliant devices), Pie chart (compliance mix), Single value (unknown devices)
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

### UC-22.14.29 · Authenticator Management — Password Age and Rotation Anomalies (IA-5)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Detects accounts with old passwords or sudden mass resets indicative of IA-5 authenticator management issues or attacks.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` 4723/4724 (password reset/change)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=sec sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4723,4724) earliest=-7d
| stats count by Account_Name, EventCode, src
| eventstats sum(count) as resets by Account_Name
| where resets>3
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enrich with last password set from AD export join; (2) alert on helpdesk spikes; (3) correlate with impossible travel; (4) document self-service vs admin resets.
- **Visualization:** Table (burst resets), Time chart (4723 volume), Single value (unique accounts)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.14.32 · Re-Authentication for Sensitive Application Roles (IA-11)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High

- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53

- **Data Sources:** `ms:o365:management` sign-ins with `AuthenticationRequirement` / `SessionId` if present
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-24h
| search ApplicationId IN ("*high_risk_app_guid_1*","*high_risk_app_guid_2*")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by UserId, SessionId
| eval session_age_min=round((now()-last_seen)/60,1)
| where session_age_min>480
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Replace ApplicationId tokens with production GUIDs; (2) align session length to policy; (3) pair with CASB step-up events if available; (4) document exceptions for legacy apps.
- **Visualization:** Table (long sessions), Single value (policy breaches), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.14.33 · Identity Proofing Evidence for HR Onboarding Events (IA-12)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Tracks completion timestamps of identity proofing checkpoints from HR systems for IA-12 identity evidence (process control, not PII in Splunk).
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), HTTP Event Collector (HEC)
- **Data Sources:** `snow:sc_req_item` onboarding catalog, or HEC JSON `id_proof_status` without government ID numbers
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item earliest=-90d short_description="*Onboard*"
| eval proof_complete=if(state="Closed" AND match(short_description,"(?i)id proof"),1,0)
| stats latest(_time) as closed_time, values(state) as states by number, opened_at
| where proof_complete=0 AND relative_time(now(),opened_at) < 2592000
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Never index raw ID document numbers — use status flags only; (2) align `short_description` filters with catalog; (3) alert HR on overdue proofing; (4) map retention to AU-11.
- **Visualization:** Table (open onboarding), Time chart (cycle time), Single value (SLA breaches)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)

### UC-22.14.34 · Flaw Remediation SLA Tracking from Vulnerability Scans (SI-2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Measures age of open critical vulnerabilities for SI-2 flaw remediation timeliness.
- **App/TA:** Tenable Add-On for Splunk (Splunkbase 4060), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263)
- **Data Sources:** `tenable:sc` or `tenable:vuln` plugin outputs, ES `index=vulnerabilities` if normalized
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vulns sourcetype=tenable:vuln earliest=-7d severity IN ("Critical","High")
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(first_found,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,1)
| where age_days>30 AND state!="Fixed"
| stats max(age_days) as oldest_open, values(plugin_id) as plugins by host, severity
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize `first_found` timestamp format from your TA; (2) join CMDB owner; (3) alert on SLA breach; (4) feed POA&M (CA-5).
- **Visualization:** Table (oldest vulns), Bar chart (by owner via lookup), Single value (count past SLA)
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest, Vulnerabilities.severity | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Tenable Add-On for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.14.35 · Malicious Code Protection — EDR / AV Detections Volume and Gaps (SI-3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Monitors endpoint malware detection pipelines for silence or surges for SI-3 malicious code protection effectiveness.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Sysmon (community/TA), CrowdStrike Falcon Event Streams (TA), or CIM Malware sources
- **Data Sources:** `XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational` EventCode=1 with AV vendor logs, `malware` CIM-tagged data
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=endpoint earliest=-24h (sourcetype=XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational OR tag=malware)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count by _time, host
| eventstats median(count) as med by host
| eval silent=if(count < med*0.1 AND med>10,1,0)
| where silent=1
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Baseline per host class; (2) exclude offline laptops with lookup; (3) alert on sustained silence; (4) cross-check agent health from MDM.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (detections per host), Table (silent hosts), Single value (agents anomalous)
- **CIM Models:** Malware
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Malware.Malware_Attacks by Malware_Attacks.dest span=1h | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Malware](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Malware)

### UC-22.14.36 · System Monitoring — Host Instrumentation Coverage vs Inventory (SI-4)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Compares active forwarders to CMDB inventory for SI-4 system monitoring coverage.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Deployment Server / Splunk Cloud Monitoring Console
- **Data Sources:** `_internal` `splunkd` connection metrics, lookup `cmdb_hosts.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=Metrics group=tcp, connections earliest=-4h
| stats latest(kb) as kb, dc(source) as peers by hostname
| rename hostname as host
| inputlookup append=t cmdb_hosts.csv
| stats values(in_scope) as in_scope, max(peers) as reporting by host
| where in_scope="true" AND isnull(reporting)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Replace internal metric approach with `| metadata type=hosts` nightly if simpler; (2) reconcile host naming; (3) alert missing Tier0; (4) document agent deployment standard (CM-7).
- **Visualization:** Table (missing monitoring), Single value (coverage %), Choropleth (by site from lookup)
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.14.39 · Software and Firmware Integrity — Unexpected Driver Loads (SI-7)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Flags rare Sysmon driver loads and kernel module events for SI-7 software and firmware integrity monitoring.

- **Data Sources:** `XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational` EventCode 6
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=endpoint sourcetype=XmlWinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational EventCode=6 earliest=-7d
| stats count by ImageLoaded, host
| eventstats median(count) as med global=t
| where count < med*0.01 OR match(ImageLoaded,"(?i)temp|users\\public")
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enrich with driver signer if captured; (2) allowlist known AV drivers via lookup; (3) alert on temp-path loads; (4) integrate with binary reputation service.
- **Visualization:** Table (rare drivers), Bar chart (by host), Scatter (prevalence vs first_seen)
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

```spl
index=app earliest=-24h log_level IN ("ERROR","FATAL")

| sort - count
```

### UC-22.14.42 · Information Management — Sensitive Fields in Unapproved Indexes (SI-12)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53

```spl
index=app sourcetype=app:json earliest=-24h
| regex _raw="(?i)\b(?:4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?)\b"
| stats count by index, sourcetype, host
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Run from isolated role with limited indexes; (2) mask at ingest after discovery; (3) never broadly expose results; (4) document data handling per SI-12 procedure.
- **Visualization:** Table (hits by index), Single value (unique hosts)

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.14.45 · Incident Handling Stage Timestamps from Case Management (IR-4)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Measures time between detection, containment, and eradication milestones for IR-4 incident handling.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
- **Data Sources:** `` `notable` `` or `snow:incident` with custom fields for IR stages
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident category="Security" earliest=-90d
| eval detect=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval contain=strptime(u_contained_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval eradicate=strptime(u_eradicated_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval contain_h=round((contain-detect)/3600,2)
| where contain_h>24 OR isnull(contain)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Add custom fields in SNOW for stage timestamps; (2) validate `strptime` format; (3) alert leadership on SLA misses; (4) post-incident review linkage IR-8.
- **Visualization:** Table (aging incidents), Time chart (mean time to contain), Single value (breach count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928)

- **Value:** Compares endpoint configuration snapshots to approved baselines for CM-2 baseline configuration.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), custom scripted baseline forwarder
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-GroupPolicy/Operational` or inventory JSON
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os sourcetype=win_build_inventory earliest=-24h
| lookup gold_windows_build.csv build_number OUTPUT approved_patch_level
| eval drift=if(patch_level<approved_patch_level,"behind","ok")
| where drift!="ok"
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Create periodic inventory scripted input; (2) refresh gold CSV from SOE team; (3) alert on drift; (4) map to CM-6 parameters.
- **Visualization:** Table (non-compliant hosts), Bar chart (by build), Single value (drift %)
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.14.53 · Configuration Change Control — Unauthorized Firewall Rule Adds (CM-3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Detects firewall commits outside maintenance windows without change tickets for CM-3 configuration change control.
- **App/TA:** Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk (Splunkbase 2757), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
- **Data Sources:** `pan:system` or `pan:config` logs, `snow:change_request`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=net sourcetype=pan:system earliest=-7d (status="commit" OR match(_raw,"(?i)commit"))
| eval change_window=if(_time % 86400 > 72000 OR _time % 86400 < 18000,1,0)
| lookup recent_changes.csv device commit_id OUTPUT ticket
| where isnull(ticket) AND change_window=0
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize PAN commit events; (2) build `recent_changes.csv` from SNOW scheduled output; (3) alert on orphan commits; (4) emergency change tagging.
- **Visualization:** Table (orphan commits), Timeline, Single value (count)
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Palo Alto Networks Add-on for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.14.54 · Security Impact Analysis Signals for Emergency Changes (CM-4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Flags emergency changes with subsequent security alerts for CM-4 security impact analysis feedback.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928), Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263)
- **Data Sources:** `snow:change_request` type=emergency, `` `notable` ``
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request type=emergency earliest=-30d
| eval cid=number
| join type=left cid [
    search `notable` earliest=-30d
    | rex field=annotations "(?i)chg:(?<cid>CHG[0-9]+)"
    | stats count by cid
  ]
| where count>0
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require CHG id in ES annotations via workflow; (2) tune join window; (3) monthly emergency change review deck; (4) document false positive handling.
- **Visualization:** Table (changes with notables), Bar chart (alert types)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.14.55 · Access Restrictions for Change — Privileged Route Changes (CM-5)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Monitors BGP or routing table changes from privileged accounts for CM-5 access restrictions for change.
- **App/TA:** Network device TAs (Cisco IOS, Juniper), syslog
- **Data Sources:** `cisco:ios` routing syslog messages
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=net sourcetype=cisco:ios earliest=-7d
| search "%BGP-5-ADJCHANGE" OR "%SYS-5-CONFIG_I" OR "neighbor"
| stats count by user, host, command
| where count>0 AND NOT user IN ("svc_net_automation")
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable AAA accounting to syslog; (2) parse `user` field; (3) alert on human-initiated changes without ticket keyword in command comment if used; (4) integrate RANCID/Oxidized diffs optional.
- **Visualization:** Table (commands), Timeline, Single value (human changes)
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)


```spl
| where approved!="true" OR isnull(approved)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Refresh approved ports CSV by environment; (2) exclude ephemeral ranges with care; (3) alert on new listeners on servers; (4) pair with CM-8 inventory.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl

```

```spl
index=net sourcetype=infoblox:dhcp earliest=-24h
| stats latest(mac) as mac, latest(fingerprint) as fp by hostname, ip
| lookup cmdb_hosts.csv hostname OUTPUT owner, classification
| where isnull(owner) AND match(classification,"unknown")
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize hostname case; (2) dedupe NAT; (3) feed discovery to CMDB; (4) monthly rogue asset report.
- **Visualization:** Table (unknown assets), Map (subnet), Single value (count)

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.14.60 · Control Assessment Findings Ingest and Aging (CA-2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Tracks open assessment findings for CA-2 control assessments and independent reviews.
- **App/TA:** HTTP Event Collector (HEC) from GRC tool
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=assessment:finding`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=assessment:finding status="open" earliest=-365d
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(opened_date,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,1)
| where age_days>90
| stats max(age_days) as oldest by control_set, finding_id, owner
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Standardize control_set to NIST families; (2) alert owners; (3) export for assessor closeout; (4) map to CA-7 dashboards.
- **Visualization:** Table (stale findings), Bar chart (by family), Single value (open >90d)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.14.61 · Information Exchange Agreements — Data Share Volume Anomalies (CA-3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Monitors API traffic to partner enclaves for CA-3 information exchange agreement compliance.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876) API Gateway, or reverse proxy logs
- **Data Sources:** `aws:apigateway` or `sourcetype=nginx:plus:access` with partner route prefixes
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cloud sourcetype=aws:apigateway earliest=-24h resourcePath="/partner/*"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by _time, partner_id
| eventstats median(bytes) as med by partner_id
| eval spike=if(bytes > med*5,1,0)
| where spike=1
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag `partner_id` at API GW stage; (2) baseline weekly seasonality; (3) alert on spikes; (4) document agreement limits.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (bytes by partner), Table (spike windows)
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest span=1h | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Web)

- **Data Sources:** `inputlookup poam_open.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup poam_open.csv
| eval due_epoch=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where due_epoch < now() AND status!="Closed"
| table poam_id, control, owner, due_date, status, risk_rating
| sort due_date
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Automate CSV refresh from authoritative GRC; (2) RBAC the lookup; (3) weekly leadership digest; (4) integrate CAP tracking.
- **Visualization:** Table (overdue POA&M), Single value (count), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.14.63 · Continuous Monitoring Control Health Scores (CA-7)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Rolls up data source freshness, detection coverage, and vuln SLA into a CA-7 continuous monitoring scorecard.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263)
- **Data Sources:** Multiple summary indexes: `summary:ingest_health`, `summary:vuln_sla`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=summary sourcetype=ca7:kpi earliest=-1d
| stats latest(ingest_score) as ingest, latest(detection_score) as det, latest(vuln_score) as vuln by _time env
| eval overall=round((ingest+det+vuln)/3,1)
| where overall < 85
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate `ca7:kpi` via scheduled searches; (2) weight factors by risk; (3) monthly AO briefing; (4) tie to CA-8 test windows.
- **Visualization:** Single value (overall score), Radial gauge, Table (by env)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High

```spl
index=sec (sourcetype=suricata OR sourcetype=pan:threat) earliest=-30d
| lookup pentest_windows.csv src_ip OUTPUT engagement_id, tester
| where isnotnull(engagement_id)
| stats count by engagement_id, signature, dest
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `pentest_windows.csv` with IPs and dates; (2) reduce false IR-4 by tagging engagements; (3) store ROE in GRC; (4) after-action detection gap review.
- **Visualization:** Table (detections during tests), Time chart (activity), Single value (coverage)
- **CIM Models:** Intrusion_Detection
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.signature, IDS_Attacks.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.14.65 · Internal System Connections — East-West New Service Relationships (CA-9)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Discovers new internal connection pairs not seen in baseline for CA-9 internal system connections authorization.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (Splunkbase 1621)
- **Data Sources:** CIM `Network_Traffic` accelerated
- **SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic where earliest=-7d latest=now by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port
| rename All_Traffic.* as *
| eval pair=src."->".dest.":".dest_port
| inputlookup append=t internal_connection_allowlist.csv
| stats first(allowed) as allowed, sum(count) as hits by pair, src, dest, dest_port
| where allowed!="true" OR isnull(allowed)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build allowlist from CMDB application dependency matrix; (2) tune minimum hit threshold; (3) alert on new database ports; (4) document approval workflow.
- **Visualization:** Table (new pairs), Sankey (optional), Graph (ES)
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

```spl
| where count>1000
```

```spl
| sort - clients
```

- **Visualization:** Table (weak TLS services), Bar chart (by cipher), Single value (affected IPs)
- **CIM Models:** Certificates
- **CIM SPL:**

### UC-22.14.69 · Network Disconnect for Inactive Sessions on Admin Services (SC-10)

- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53

- **App/TA:** F5 BIG-IP APM or Zscaler logs via TA
- **Data Sources:** `sourcetype=zscaler:web` or `f5:apm:session`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=proxy sourcetype=zscaler:web earliest=-24h app_class="Admin"
| transaction user maxpause=30m maxspan=24h
| eval idle_mins=round(duration/60,1)
| where idle_mins>240
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Validate `transaction` keys; (2) align idle timeout to policy minutes; (3) alert on excessive admin idle tunnels; (4) document VPN vs ZTNA differences.
- **Visualization:** Table (long idle admin sessions), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.14.70 · Cryptographic Key Management Events from Cloud KMS (SC-12)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Audits KMS key disable/delete operations for SC-12 cryptographic key establishment and management.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876), Splunk Add-on for GCP (TA)
- **Data Sources:** `aws:cloudtrail` KMS events
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-30d eventSource="kms.amazonaws.com" eventName IN ("DisableKey","ScheduleKeyDeletion","DeleteImportedKeyMaterial")
| stats values(eventName) as actions, latest(_time) as last by userName, requestParameters
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Parse `requestParameters` for key ARNs into fields; (2) alert outside maintenance CAB; (3) dual-control evidence from SNOW comments optional join; (4) map to SC-13.
- **Visualization:** Table (KMS changes), Timeline, Single value (destructive ops)
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.14.71 · Cryptographic Protection — BitLocker or Disk Encryption Status Drops (SC-13)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Tracks endpoint encryption compliance for SC-13 cryptographic protection.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (Splunkbase 742), Intune HEC
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-BitLocker/Operational` or inventory JSON
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os sourcetype=win:bitlocker_status earliest=-24h protection_status="Off"
| stats count by host, volume, reason
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize status strings; (2) exclude expected lab hosts via lookup; (3) auto-quarantine workflow optional; (4) map to SC-28 overlap for at-rest.
- **Visualization:** Table (unencrypted volumes), Single value (non-compliant hosts)
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.14.72 · Session Authenticity — Token Replay Across Geographies (SC-23)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Detects same refresh token used from distant geolocations within short windows for SC-23 session authenticity.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (Splunkbase 4055)
- **Data Sources:** `ms:o365:management` token events if available, or proxy OAuth logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:o365:management earliest=-24d Operation="Token issued*"
| iplocation ClientIP
| stats earliest(_time) as t1, latest(_time) as t2, values(Country) as countries, values(ClientIP) as ips by UserId, SessionId
| eval dt=t2-t1
| where mvcount(countries)>1 AND dt < 7200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm operation strings; (2) reduce noise with corporate travel; (3) integrate with IR-4; (4) force re-auth playbooks.
- **Visualization:** Table (suspected replay), Map (IPs), Timeline
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.14.73 · Protection of Information at Rest — Storage Encryption Misconfigurations (SC-28)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Finds newly created volumes or buckets without encryption-at-rest enabled for SC-28 protection of information at rest.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for AWS (Splunkbase 1876)
- **Data Sources:** `aws:cloudtrail` `CreateVolume` `CreateBucket`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cloud sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-7d eventName IN ("CreateVolume","CreateBucket")
| spath path=responseElements output=responseElements
| regex responseElements="(?i)encrypted.?false|Encryption:?None|null"
| stats count by userName, eventName, aws_account_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tune regex to your JSON shape; (2) auto-open CM-3 change if intentional; (3) integrate AWS Config rules for continuous check; (4) evidence for assessor sampling.
- **Visualization:** Table (non-encrypted creates), Time chart, Single value (count)
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.14.74 · Risk Assessment Inputs — Control Deficiency Hotspots (RA-3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Aggregates multiple weak signals (failed controls, vulns, policy violations) by system for RA-3 risk assessment prioritization.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance`, `index=vulns`, `index=sec` — use summary joins
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=compliance sourcetype=cis:assessment control_result="FAIL" earliest=-30d
| stats count as ctrl_fails by hostname
| join type=left hostname [
    search index=vulns sourcetype=tenable:vuln severity="Critical" earliest=-30d
    | stats count as crit_vulns by host
    | rename host as hostname
  ]
| eval risk_score=ctrl_fails*2 + crit_vulns*5
| sort - risk_score
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Replace `join` with `lookup` at scale; (2) calibrate weights annually; (3) export to enterprise risk register; (4) document assumptions.
- **Visualization:** Table (top systems), Bubble chart (optional), Single value (mean score)
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.14.75 · Vulnerability Monitoring — Exploitable in the Wild Prioritization (RA-5)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Joins vuln scan data with threat intel tags like KEV for RA-5 vulnerability monitoring.

- **Data Sources:** `tenable:vuln` with `plugin_id`, `kev_catalog.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vulns sourcetype=tenable:vuln earliest=-7d state!="Fixed"
| lookup kev_catalog.csv plugin_id OUTPUT in_kev, due_date
| where in_kev="true"
| stats max(patch_due_sla_days) as sla by host, plugin_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Sync KEV catalog regularly; (2) align SLA to policy; (3) alert owners; (4) integrate patch change windows CM-3.
- **Visualization:** Table (KEV findings), Single value (count), Time chart (mean age)
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Tenable Add-On for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4060), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.14.76 · Risk Response Effectiveness After Control Changes (RA-7)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Compares alert rates before and after control deployments for RA-7 risk response monitoring.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263)
- **Data Sources:** `` `notable` `` with `rule_name` tags for control initiatives
- **SPL:**
```spl
`notable` earliest=-60d tag="control_initiative_X"
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count by _time
| eventstats first(count) as baseline, latest(count) as latest
| eval reduction_pct=round(100*(baseline-latest)/baseline,1)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag rules consistently during pilots; (2) seasonally adjust baselines; (3) leadership narrative in monthly report; (4) document residual risk.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (notables per week), Single value (reduction %)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.14.77 · Threat Hunting Outcomes Logged for Repeatable Hunts (RA-10)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Structures hunt hypothesis, queries, and findings for RA-10 threat hunting (as organizational capability evidence).
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (Splunkbase 263) — Enterprise Security Content Update or custom KVStore
- **Data Sources:** `index=thunt` `sourcetype=hunt:record` HEC JSON
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=thunt sourcetype=hunt:record earliest=-90d
| stats values(hypothesis) as hyps, latest(outcome) as outcome, max(duration_min) as duration by hunt_id, hunter
| where outcome="inconclusive" OR duration>480
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require hunt closure fields; (2) link to IR tickets when escalated; (3) quarterly metrics for leadership; (4) library of proven SPL in repo outside Splunk.
- **Visualization:** Table (hunt backlog), Bar chart (outcomes), Single value (inconclusive %)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.14.78 · Contingency Plan Tabletop and Activation Logging (CP-2)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Tracks formal contingency plan activations and tests for CP-2 contingency plan maintenance.
- **App/TA:** HTTP Event Collector (HEC)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=cp:activation`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cp:activation earliest=-365d
| stats latest(activation_time) as last_act, count by plan_name, scenario_type
| eval months_since=round((now()-strptime(last_act,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/2628000,2)
| where months_since>12
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Log both tests and real activations distinctly; (2) alert owners; (3) align with BIA refresh cycles; (4) map dependencies CA-9.
- **Visualization:** Table (stale plans), Timeline, Single value (plans overdue)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)
