### UC-22.14.79 · System Backup Success and RPO Violations (CP-9)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Monitors backup job completions and anomalies for CP-9 system backup.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for NetBackup / Commvault / Veeam TAs or HEC from backup software
- **Data Sources:** `index=backup` `sourcetype=veeam:job`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=backup sourcetype=veeam:job earliest=-7d
| stats latest(job_end) as last_end, latest(job_result) as result, latest(data_read_gb) as gb by job_name, host
| eval hours_since=round((now()-strptime(last_end,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/3600,1)
| where result!="Success" OR hours_since>26
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Adjust `strptime` to vendor format; (2) define RPO per tier in lookup; (3) alert backup admins + IR-4 if ransomware context; (4) test restores log separately CP-10.
- **Visualization:** Table (failed/stale jobs), Single value (SLA breaches), Time chart (duration)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.14.80 · System Recovery Time Objective Tracking from DR Drills (CP-10)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Captures measured recovery time metrics from DR drills for CP-10 system recovery and reconstitution.
- **App/TA:** HTTP Event Collector (HEC), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (Splunkbase 1928)
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` `sourcetype=dr:drill` or `snow:change_request` DR template
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=dr:drill earliest=-365d
| eval rto_min=recovery_end_epoch - failover_start_epoch
| lookup app_tiers.csv application OUTPUT rto_target_min
| where rto_min > rto_target_min
| table drill_id, application, rto_min, rto_target_min, notes
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure epoch fields populated at drill completion; (2) store raw drill packets in secure share; (3) feed POA&M if missed; (4) integrate with IR-8 updates.
- **Visualization:** Table (RTO breaches), Bar chart (by app), Single value (worst RTO)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928), [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### 22.15 IEC 62443

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Nozomi Networks TA for Splunk (6905), Claroty xDome TA for Splunk, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** OT firewall logs (Palo Alto, Fortinet), industrial IDS/IPS (Nozomi Guardian, Claroty, Dragos), DCS/SCADA historian exports, PLC/RTU event logs, HMI audit trails, PAM/jump host sessions, engineering workstation logs, industrial protocol captures (Modbus, OPC-UA, DNP3, IEC 61850), asset inventory feeds, patch management databases, network flow data from OT segments.

### UC-22.15.1 · OT Security Policy Control Evidence from Log Review (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.2.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Correlates policy-required controls (access, change, remote sessions) to indexed evidence so the IACS security program can demonstrate ongoing compliance with documented requirements.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_sec` — `sourcetype="nozomi:alerts"`, `sourcetype="claroty:alert"`, `sourcetype="pan:system"`; lookup `iec62443_policy_controls.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_sec earliest=-30d (sourcetype="nozomi:alerts" OR sourcetype="claroty:alert" OR sourcetype="pan:system")
| eval control_family=case(match(_raw,"(?i)remote"),"Remote_Access", match(_raw,"(?i)change|config"),"Change_Management", true(),"General_Security_Event")
| lookup iec62443_policy_controls.csv control_family OUTPUT policy_clause
| stats count by control_family, policy_clause, sourcetype
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain lookup mapping control families to 2-1 policy clauses; (2) normalize sourcetypes with CIM tags where applicable; (3) schedule weekly PDF for program reviews; (4) route uncategorized families to GRC.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (events by control family), Table (clause coverage), Single value (distinct clauses with hits).
- **CIM Models:** Alerts (when CIM-tagged), N/A otherwise
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Alerts.Alerts by Alerts.severity, Alerts.signature, Alerts.app | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [CIM: Alerts](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Alerts)

---

### UC-22.15.2 · IACS Cyber Risk Assessment — Critical OT Asset Exposure (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.2.3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Surfaces internet-facing or high-CVSS OT assets to inform documented risk treatment and residual risk acceptance for the IACS.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk DB Connect (2686) for CMMS
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_assets`, `index=ot_vuln`, optional `ot_dmz_exposure.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_assets OR index=ot_vuln earliest=-7d
| eval asset_id=coalesce(device_id, asset_id, host)
| stats values(cve) as cves, max(cvss_base_score) as max_cvss, latest(criticality) as tier by asset_id
| lookup ot_dmz_exposure.csv asset_id OUTPUT exposed_to_untrusted
| where max_cvss>=7.0 OR exposed_to_untrusted="true"
| eval risk_note=case(isnotnull(cves) AND exposed_to_untrusted="true","Treat first: exposure + CVE", exposed_to_untrusted="true","Network exposure", true(),"Critical vulnerability concentration")
| table asset_id, tier, max_cvss, exposed_to_untrusted, risk_note, cves
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest vulnerability exports with OT asset keys; (2) refresh exposure lookup from Claroty/Nozomi topology; (3) align tiers with SL-T; (4) attach results to formal RA register.
- **Visualization:** Scatter (CVSS vs count), Table (top assets), Single value (dual-risk count).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities (when mapped), N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

---

### UC-22.15.3 · OT Security Awareness Training Completion (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.3.2.6)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Measures completion of OT-specific training by role so awareness obligations for personnel working on IACS are auditable.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686) or HEC from LMS
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr_lms` `sourcetype="lms:ot_training"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr_lms sourcetype="lms:ot_training" earliest=-400d
| where like(lower(course_code),"%otsec%") OR like(lower(course_code),"%ics%")
| eval done_epoch=strptime(completed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| stats latest(done_epoch) as last_done by employee_id, role
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_done)/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_done) OR days_since>365
| sort - days_since
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest LMS extracts with privacy minimization; (2) map mandatory courses per role in policy; (3) join to AD only on approved fields; (4) alert managers for overdue learners.
- **Visualization:** Table (overdue), Bar (completions/month), Single value (% current).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

### UC-22.15.4 · Privileged OT Account Changes vs Personnel Screening (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.3.3.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Flags new or elevated OT privileged accounts that lack matching screening status, supporting personnel security for IACS access paths.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` 4720/4728/4732; `index=cyberark` PTA/CPM
- **SPL:**
```spl
((index=windows EventCode IN ("4720","4728","4732") Message="*OT_*") OR (index=cyberark sourcetype IN ("cyberark:pta","cyberark:cpm")))
| where index="windows" OR match(_raw,"(?i)OT_|PLC_|SCADA")
| eval account=coalesce(Account_Name, user, dest_user)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_evt, latest(_time) as last_evt by account, EventCode, host
| join type=left account [ search index=hr sourcetype="hr:screening" | stats latest(status) as screening by samaccountname | rename samaccountname as account ]
| where screening!="Cleared" OR isnull(screening)
| table first_evt, last_evt, account, EventCode, screening, host
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable AD auditing for sensitive OT groups; (2) ingest CyberArk for break-glass and vendor IDs; (3) HR feed with legal basis documented; (4) remediate within SLA.
- **Visualization:** Timeline, Table (exceptions), Single value (open items).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

---

### UC-22.15.5 · IACS Incident Response Containment Interval (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.3.4.5)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Quantifies detection-to-containment for OT cyber incidents and separates drills from production to evidence an exercised IR plan.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_incidents` ServiceNow `snow:incident` or SOAR with `contained_at`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_incidents sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-180d
| where category="OT/Cyber" OR match(short_description,"(?i)ics|scada|plc")
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), contained=strptime(contained_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval is_drill=if(match(tag,"drill") OR match(short_description,"(?i)drill|tabletop"),1,0)
| eval contain_min=round((contained-opened)/60,1)
| where isnotnull(contain_min)
| stats median(contain_min) as p50, perc95(contain_min) as p95, count by is_drill
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require containment milestone in workflow; (2) align timestamps to OT SOC clock; (3) compare drill vs live separately; (4) feed p95 into program KPIs.
- **Visualization:** Box plot, Table (recent), Single value (p95 live).
- **CIM Models:** Ticket_Management
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management by All_Ticket_Management.status, All_Ticket_Management.priority, All_Ticket_Management.category | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928), [CIM: Ticket Management](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Ticket_Management)

### UC-22.15.6 · Control System Continuity — Historian Export and OT Backup Health (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.3.4.3)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Detects missed historian snapshots or failed OT-tier backups that undermine recovery time objectives for control systems.
- **App/TA:** Universal Forwarder on backup/historian bridge hosts
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_hist` export status; `index=backup` Commvault/Veeam
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_hist sourcetype="hist:export_status" earliest=-72h
| stats latest(_time) as last_ok, latest(status) as st by plant, historian_id
| eval hours_since=round((now()-last_ok)/3600,1)
| where st!="SUCCESS" OR hours_since>26
| append [ search index=backup sourcetype="veeam:JobSession" OR sourcetype="commvault:job" earliest=-72h | where match(JobName,"(?i)OT|Historian|VMware_OT") | stats latest(Result) as st by JobName | where st!="Success" ]
| sort - hours_since
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Emit structured export rows from each historian interface; (2) tag backup jobs covering OT VMs; (3) tune thresholds to RPO; (4) link runbook for cold standby DCS.
- **Visualization:** Table (gaps), Single value (failed jobs), Time chart (export lag).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.15.7 · OT Security Audit Population Sampling Dashboard (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.4.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Delivers repeatable audit samples of OT security-relevant events across zones for internal and external assessments of the security program.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), CIM Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_audit` unified; `ot_zones.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_audit earliest=-90d
| lookup ot_zones.csv cidr OUTPUT zone, sl_t
| stats count as evt, dc(user) as users, dc(dest_ip) as peers by zone, sourcetype
| sort zone, - evt
| head 300
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Land firewall, HMI, PAM, IDS into one auditable index with RBAC; (2) maintain CIDR→zone lookup; (3) export monthly CSV to auditors; (4) document sampling method in scope letter.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (zone × sourcetype), Table, Bar (evt by zone).
- **CIM Models:** Change, Authentication when tagged
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(All_Changes.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.15.8 · OT Change Management — PLC Download Without CM Ticket (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.3.4.4)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Correlates controller downloads or online edits with change tickets to enforce controlled maintenance for IACS devices.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), ServiceNow TA (1928)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_eng` vendor PLC audit; `snow:change_request`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:download" OR sourcetype="siemens:audit" OR sourcetype="ab:audit" earliest=-14d
| eval ck=coalesce(project_id, program_name, concat("PLC-",controller))
| eval t0=_time
| join type=left ck [ search index=itsm sourcetype="snow:change_request" earliest=-30d | eval ck=coalesce(u_ot_project, number) | where state IN ("Closed Complete","Implement") | fields number, ck, opened_at ]
| where isnull(number)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_dl, latest(user) as users, values(src_ip) as src by ck, controller
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize vendor fields to shared `ck`; (2) mandate CM tickets carry project keys; (3) tune time join if needed via subsearch; (4) weekly engineering review of unmatched.
- **Visualization:** Table (unmatched), Timeline, Single value (count/day).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [ServiceNow TA](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1928), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.15.9 · Physical Access vs OT Network First-Seen Asset (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.3.3.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Relates physical access events to new or unknown OT assets appearing on protected VLANs to strengthen environmental controls narrative.
- **App/TA:** Nozomi Networks TA (6905), HEC from PACS
- **Data Sources:** `index=pacs` `sourcetype="pacs:door"`; `index=ot_net` asset updates
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=pacs sourcetype="pacs:door" door_id IN ("OT-MCC-A","OT-SERVER-1") earliest=-24h
| eval t_phys=_time, who=badge_id
| join max=0 type=inner who [
  search index=ot_net sourcetype="nozomi:assets" earliest=-24h
  | where first_seen_time>_time-86400
  | eval who=owner
  | stats min(first_seen_time) as fs by asset_name, vlan, who
]
| eval delta_sec=fs-t_phys
| where delta_sec>120 OR isnull(who)
| table _time, door_id, badge_id, asset_name, vlan, delta_sec
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Sync clocks between PACS and NMS; (2) enrich asset owner field; (3) filter tailgating test doors; (4) integrate physical security escalation path.
- **Visualization:** Table, Map (optional), Time chart.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Nozomi Networks TA](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/6905)

### UC-22.15.10 · Vendor Remote Maintenance Window and Dual-Control Evidence (IEC 62443-2-1 / 4.4.3.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Monitors vendor VPN and PSM sessions against contracted hours and approval records to evidence supplier risk management for OT maintenance.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295)
- **Data Sources:** `pan:globalprotect` or `pan:sslvpn`; `cyberark:psm`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-14d
| lookup vendor_access.csv user OUTPUT vendor, window
| eval hod=strftime(_time,"%H")
| eval off_window=case(window="business" AND (hod<7 OR hod>18),1, window="24x7",0, true(),0)
| join type=left user [ search index=cyberark sourcetype="cyberark:psm" earliest=-14d | stats count as psm_hits by user ]
| where vendor!="Internal" AND (off_window=1 OR psm_hits=0)
| stats count by vendor, user, off_window
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Populate vendor contract lookup; (2) force vendor IDs through PSM; (3) alert security + procurement on violations; (4) quarterly supplier review export.
- **Visualization:** Bar (by vendor), Table, Single value (weekly violations).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.15.11 · HMI and Jump Host Human User Identification Gaps (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Detects interactive OT logons missing identifiable human user mapping to support unique identification of people accessing IACS UIs.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Windows TA (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=pam` CyberArk PSM; `WinEventLog:Security` 4624 on jump hosts
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=pam sourcetype="cyberark:psm" earliest=-7d
| eval human=coalesce(suser, user, src_user)
| where isnull(human) OR human="-" OR match(human,"^svc_")
| stats count by dest_host, application, protocol
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce personal accounts on PSM; (2) block shared HMI logins at source; (3) integrate LDAP attribute for service vs human; (4) remediate top dest_hosts first.
- **Visualization:** Table (top gaps), Single value (events with no human id).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [Windows TA](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.15.12 · Non-Interactive Industrial Protocol Clients Without Device Identity (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Finds Modbus/OPC-UA sessions where client identity cannot be tied to an approved automation asset, weakening software process and device identification.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_protocol` Edge Hub `edge_hub_modbus`, `edge_hub_opcua`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_protocol (sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" OR sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua") earliest=-24h
| eval client_key=coalesce(client_ip, opcua_client_appuri, modbus_unit_id)
| lookup ot_approved_clients.csv client_key OUTPUT asset_name, owner
| where isnull(asset_name)
| stats dc(function_code) as fc_dc, dc(node_id) as nodes, count by client_key, sourcetype
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build authoritative client inventory from engineering CMDB; (2) enrich Edge Hub events with IP→asset; (3) alert on new clients; (4) certificate-pin OPC-UA where possible.
- **Visualization:** Table (unknown clients), Map (optional), Time chart (first seen).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151)

### UC-22.15.13 · Stale or Shared OT Service Accounts (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Surfaces OT accounts with weak rotation, excessive group breadth, or shared naming patterns inconsistent with IACS account management practice.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` password changes 4723/4724; AD snapshot via scripted input
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows EventCode IN ("4723","4724") earliest=-180d
| eval account=Target_User_Name
| where match(account,"(?i)scada|plc|hmi|svc_ot|batch")
| stats latest(_time) as last_pw_change by account
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_pw_change)/86400,0)
| where days_since>90 OR isnull(last_pw_change)
| join type=left account [ search index=ad_snapshot sourcetype="ad:user" | fields account, memberOf | eval grp_count=mvcount(memberOf) ]
| table account, days_since, grp_count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest periodic LDAP snapshots to CSV; (2) define password rotation SLAs by tier; (3) eliminate shared accounts; (4) track exceptions with expiry dates.
- **Visualization:** Table, Bar (aging buckets), Single value (stale count).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

---

### UC-22.15.14 · Weak OTP or Missing MFA Step for OT VPN (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.5)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Identifies VPN authentications that skip second factor or reuse legacy token methods for paths into OT jump zones.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `pan:globalprotect` or `pan:auth`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| eval mfa=coalesce(factor_type, auth_method, mfa_mechanism)
| where stage="login" AND (isnull(mfa) OR mfa="none" OR mfa="password-only")
| lookup ot_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT requires_mfa
| where requires_mfa="true"
| stats count by user, portal, public_ip, host
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Map portals to MFA policy; (2) upgrade GP app versions enforcing MFA; (3) block legacy profiles; (4) weekly exception report to IAM.
- **Visualization:** Table, Single value (violations), Time chart.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.15.15 · Short or Default Password Patterns in OT Authentication Logs (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.7)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Uses policy checks on authentication metadata (where available) and heuristics on failed logon bursts to catch weak password hygiene in OT accounts.
- **App/TA:** Windows TA (742), FortiGate TA
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` 4625; `fortigate_traffic` or `sslvpn` auth failures
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=windows EventCode="4625" Status!="0x0") OR (index=fw sourcetype="fortigate_event" subtype="vpn")
| eval user=coalesce(Account_Name, user, vpnuser)
| where match(user,"(?i)hmi|operator|ot_")
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as fails by _time, user, src_ip
| where fails>30
| sort - fails
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Never log cleartext passwords—use failure reason codes only; (2) pair with AD password policy audit offline; (3) enforce lockout thresholds on jump hosts; (4) reset compromised accounts via PAM.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (fail spikes), Table (user, src), Single value (distinct users).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.src span=1h | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Windows TA](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

```spl
| search status!="Good" OR match(_raw,"(?i)BadCertificate|issuer|untrusted")
| eval server=coalesce(opcua_server_endpoint, dest_host)
| stats count by server, status, opcua_security_mode, opcua_user_token_type
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Deploy UA GDS or centralized PKI; (2) map status codes to runbooks; (3) alert on spike per server; (4) rotate OPC-UA app certs on schedule.

### UC-22.15.17 · OT Access from Untrusted Networks Without Split Tunnel Block (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.13)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Flags VPN or agent sessions originating from high-risk geos or residential ASNs while accessing OT jump portals.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `pan:globalprotect` with `public_ip`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| iplocation public_ip
| lookup ot_trusted_country.csv Country OUTPUT trust
| where trust!="trusted" OR like(ASN,"*RESIDENTIAL*")
| lookup ot_jump_portals.csv portal OUTPUT sl_t
| stats earliest(_time) as first, latest(_time) as last, values(public_ip) as ips by user, portal, Country
| sort - last
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain country allowlist aligned to business; (2) require hardware token from untrusted regions; (3) integrate threat intel ASN feed; (4) auto-disable accounts on repeat hits.
- **Visualization:** Choropleth, Table, Single value (sessions).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic when joined to firewall
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic when joined to firewall](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic_when_joined_to_firewall)

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" earliest=-24h
| where function_code IN (5,6,15,16,22,23)
| lookup modbus_write_acl.csv src_ip dest_plc OUTPUT role
| where role!="engineer_approved" OR isnull(role)
| stats values(register_start) as regs, count by src_ip, dest_plc, user
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build ACL from approved EWS IPs; (2) segment read-only analytics clients; (3) test with HIL bench; (4) integrate with SOAR for host isolation.

### UC-22.15.19 · Unsigned Macros or Scripts Executed on Engineering Workstation (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.4)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Monitors execution of script engines and office macros from OT engineering hosts where mobile code should be restricted.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Microsoft-Windows-Sysmon/Operational` EventCode 1
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=sysmon EventCode=1 host IN ("OT-EWS-*") earliest=-7d
| where (Image LIKE "%\powershell.exe" OR Image LIKE "%\mshta.exe" OR Image LIKE "%\cscript.exe")
    AND NOT(CommandLine LIKE "%-nop -enc%")
| lookup ews_signed_scripts.csv CommandLine OUTPUT approved
| where approved!="true"
| stats count by host, Image, CommandLine
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Deploy Sysmon with tuned config for EWS; (2) Approve known vendor installers via hash lookup; (3) block VBA macros at policy layer; (4) review weekly top commands.
- **Visualization:** Table, Sankey (parent→child optional), Time chart.
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint (Sysmon when mapped)
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.15.20 · HMI Session Lock Bypass — Long Idle Operator Consoles (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.5)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Measures continuous HMI operator sessions without screen-lock events to evidence session lock policy effectiveness.
- **App/TA:** Windows TA or HMI vendor audit forwarder
- **Data Sources:** `index=hmi_audit` `sourcetype="winlogon"` on HMI thin clients
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hmi_audit (sourcetype="winlogon" OR sourcetype="hmi:session") earliest=-7d
| eval action=coalesce(EventCode, event_type)
| transaction user host maxspan=8h maxpause=15m
| eval duration_min=round(duration/60,1)
| where duration_min>240 AND mvcount(action)=1
| table starttime, endtime, user, host, duration_min
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce GPO screen lock on HMI OS; (2) reduce `maxpause` per plant standard; (3) alert on transactions > policy; (4) physical walk-through for exceptions.
- **Visualization:** Timeline, Table, Single value (long sessions).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.15.21 · Forced Remote Engineering Session Termination After Hours (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.6)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Ensures vendor and internal remote sessions to OT are closed at shift end by correlating still-active tunnels with schedule.
- **App/TA:** Palo Alto (2757), CyberArk (4295)
- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic` app=ssl,sslvpn; `cyberark:psm` session end
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-2d
| where app IN ("ssl","sslvpn","gp-gateway") AND dest_zone="OT-Jump"
| eval sess_key=concat(src,"-",dest_ip)
| stats min(_time) as start, max(_time) as end by sess_key, user
| eval span_h=(end-start)/3600
| eval ended_after_shift=if((strftime(end,"%H")>"22" OR strftime(end,"%H")<"05") AND span_h>4,1,0)
| where ended_after_shift=1
| table start, end, span_h, sess_key, user
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Configure firewall idle timeouts; (2) CyberArk max session duration; (3) validate OT maintenance windows; (4) alert if sessions exceed 8h.
- **Visualization:** Timeline, Table, Bar (duration buckets).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Palo Alto](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.15.22 · OT Auditable Event Coverage by Zone and Source (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.8)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Compares expected auditable sources per IEC 62443 zone to actual indexed event rates to find blind spots.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151)
- **Data Sources:** `metadata` sources; `index=ot_audit`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| tstats count where index=ot_audit by sourcetype, host prefix=ot
| lookup ot_expected_audit_sources.csv sourcetype OUTPUT zone, required
| eval gap=if(required="true" AND count<100,"low_volume","ok")
| where gap!="ok"
| table sourcetype, host, count, zone
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define required sourcetypes per Purdue level; (2) onboard missing forwarders; (3) validate license throttling not dropping; (4) quarterly coverage attestation.
- **Visualization:** Bar (actual vs expected), Table, Single value (gaps).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151)

### UC-22.15.23 · Audit Index Growth vs Licensed Retention (Capacity Risk) (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.9)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Forecasts `ot_audit` disk usage to avoid silent loss of security audit records before policy retention is met.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Cloud REST
- **Data Sources:** `/services/data/indexes` for `ot_audit`
- **SPL:**
```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes splunk_server=local count=0 title=ot_audit
| eval gb=currentDBSizeMB/1024, days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,1)
| eval daily_gb=gb/30
| eval days_to_full=if(daily_gb>0, round((maxTotalDataSizeMB/1024)/daily_gb,1), null())
| table title, gb, days, daily_gb, days_to_full
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Set `maxTotalDataSizeMB` with headroom; (2) route verbose protocols to warm/cold tier; (3) expand frozen path; (4) alert if `days_to_full`<90.
- **Visualization:** Single value, Gauge, Table.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate

- **Data Sources:** `_internal` metrics.log, `splunkd` health
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=_internal source=*metrics.log group=tcpout_connections earliest=-4h
| stats latest(kb) as kb by host
| join type=left host [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h "Closing tcp" OR "ERROR TcpOutputFd" | stats count as errs by host ]
| where isnull(kb) OR errs>10
| table host, kb, errs
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable MC alerts; (2) map hosts to OT sites; (3) redundant intermediate HF; (4) local buffering on UF until restored.
- **Visualization:** Table (affected sites), Single value (hosts down), Time chart.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.15.25 · Clock Skew on OT Hosts vs NTP Stratum (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 2.11)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Surfaces OT servers and RTUs with large `_indextime` vs `_time` deltas undermining trustworthy audit timestamps.
- **App/TA:** Universal Forwarder time debug or vendor syslog
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_infra` with `_time` and `orig_time` if extracted
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_infra earliest=-24h
| eval skew=abs(_indextime-_time)
| where skew>120
| stats max(skew) as max_skew, perc95(skew) as p95_skew by host, sourcetype
| sort - p95_skew
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce PTP/NTP on all IEDs; (2) fix dual-homed syslog paths; (3) alert >120s; (4) document stratum hierarchy per site.
- **Visualization:** Table, Single value (worst skew), Bar by site.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Looks for PLC program uploads/downloads signed by only one engineering identity when policy requires dual control.
- **App/TA:** Vendor PLC audit, ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_eng` `sourcetype="plc:audit"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:audit" action="PROGRAM_CHANGE" earliest=-30d
| eval change_id=coalesce(change_ticket, work_order)
| stats values(user) as signers, dc(user) as signer_count by change_id, controller
| where signer_count<2 AND len(change_id)>4
| table change_id, controller, signers, signer_count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require second approval in CM tool; (2) map PLC audit user fields; (3) integrate e-signature if available; (4) weekly compliance report.
- **Visualization:** Table, Timeline, Single value (violations).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(All_Changes.user) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.15.27 · OPC-UA Message Tampering Indicators via Signature Failures (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 3.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Raises alerts when OPC-UA signing/encryption modes downgrade or signature checks fail, indicating integrity issues on industrial sessions.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Edge Hub
- **Data Sources:** `edge_hub_opcua` security headers
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua" earliest=-24h
| eval mode=coalesce(opcua_security_mode,"None")
| stats count by opcua_server_endpoint, mode, opcua_security_policy_uri
| eventstats sum(count) as tot by opcua_server_endpoint
| eval pct=round(100*count/tot,2)
| where mode="None" AND pct>1
| sort - pct
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Disable None except lab VLANs; (2) baseline each server; (3) alert on new None percentage; (4) correlate with firewall TLS inspection if used.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (mode mix), Table, Single value (servers with None).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.15.28 · PLC Firmware Verification Job Failures (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 3.3)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Tracks scheduled integrity or attestation jobs on controllers that fail or never run, weakening verification of security functionality.
- **App/TA:** Asset intelligence CSV or vendor API HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_integrity` vendor attestation logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_integrity sourcetype="plc:integrity_scan" earliest=-30d
| stats latest(result) as last_res, latest(_time) as last_run by controller, firmware_version
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_run)/86400,0)
| where last_res!="PASS" OR days_since>30
| table controller, firmware_version, last_res, days_since
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Schedule vendor integrity checks post-patch; (2) ingest pass/fail JSON; (3) integrate with patch CMDB; (4) block production promotion on FAIL.
- **Visualization:** Table, Single value (controllers overdue), Time chart.

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.15.29 · Unexpected PLC Logic CRC Change (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 3.4)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Compares reported logic checksums or project CRCs to last approved baseline to detect unauthorized software changes.
- **App/TA:** Historian or engineering export
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_eng` CRC events; `approved_plc_crc.csv`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:crc" earliest=-7d
| lookup approved_plc_crc.csv controller program_name OUTPUT expected_crc
| where isnotnull(expected_crc) AND crc!=expected_crc
| stats earliest(_time) as first_bad by controller, program_name, crc, expected_crc, user
| sort - first_bad
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Export CRC after each validated download; (2) store gold images securely; (3) auto-ticket on mismatch; (4) offline forensic for first event.
- **Visualization:** Table, Timeline, Single value (mismatches/day).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.15.30 · Out-of-Range SCADA Setpoints and Invalid Control Commands (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 3.5)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Validates control writes against engineering limits and recipe tables to catch dangerous setpoint or mode commands.
- **App/TA:** Historian HEC, DCS export
- **Data Sources:** `index=scada` `sourcetype="hist:control"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=scada sourcetype="hist:control" earliest=-24h
| lookup setpoint_limits.csv tag OUTPUT min_sp, max_sp, allowed_modes
| eval bad=if(value<min_sp OR value>max_sp OR !mvfind(allowed_modes, mode),1,0)
| where bad=1
| table _time, tag, value, min_sp, max_sp, mode, operator
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Publish limits from P&ID digital twin; (2) test alarms in shadow mode; (3) integrate with MES recipes; (4) alert operations supervisor.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value (bad commands).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.15.31 · Cleartext Industrial Credentials in PCAP-Derived Metadata (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Scans IDS metadata for username or password fields in cleartext industrial protocols where confidentiality must be preserved.
- **App/TA:** Claroty xDome TA, Nozomi TA
- **Data Sources:** `claroty:alert` or `nozomi:packets_meta` (example sourcetype)
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_sec (sourcetype="claroty:alert" OR sourcetype="nozomi:alerts") earliest=-7d
| where match(_raw,"(?i)password=|passwd=|user=.*pass") AND NOT match(_raw,"(?i)\\*\\*\\*")
| rex field=_raw "(?i)(?<proto>modbus|opcua|dnp3).*"
| stats count by proto, src_ip, dest_ip, signature
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Never index full payload in prod—use IDS summaries; (2) rotate exposed creds; (3) enable TLS where vendor supports; (4) segment sniffers.
- **Visualization:** Table, Single value (hits), Bar by protocol.
- **CIM Models:** Intrusion_Detection when CIM-tagged
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Intrusion_Detection.IDS_Attacks by IDS_Attacks.src, IDS_Attacks.dest, IDS_Attacks.signature | sort - count
```
- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Intrusion_Detection when CIM-tagged](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Intrusion_Detection_when_CIM-tagged)

```spl
| where cipher IN ("RC4","3DES","DES") OR tls_version="TLSv1.0" OR tls_version="TLSv1.1"
| stats count by dest_ip, cipher, tls_version, app
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Disable legacy TLS on servers; (2) upgrade PanOS profiles; (3) exception process for legacy PLC web UIs; (4) monthly compliance scan.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.app | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.15.33 · East-West OT Traffic Crossing Intended Purdue Levels (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced

- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| lookup ot_zone_pairs.csv src_zone dest_zone OUTPUT allowed_conduit
| where isnull(allowed_conduit) OR allowed_conduit="false"
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes by src_zone, dest_zone, app
| sort - bytes
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain zone pair matrix from ISA-62443 drawings; (2) log intra-zone noise baseline; (3) alert new apps; (4) feed exceptions with expiry.
- **Visualization:** Sankey (zone flows), Table, Single value (new pairs/week).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.app | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

```spl
| eval severity=coalesce(severity, priority, level)

| stats count by signature, src_ip, dest_zone, sourcetype
| sort - count
```

- **CIM SPL:**


```spl
| eval vlan=vlan_id
| join type=left name [ search index=ot_assets | rename asset_name as name | fields name, safety_related, vlan_expected ]
| where safety_related="true" AND vlan!=vlan_expected
| stats values(vlan) as vlans_seen by name, safety_related
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag SIS vs BPCS in CMDB; (2) enforce separate VLANs; (3) migration plan for mixed assets; (4) document compensating controls if any.

```spl
| sort - count
```

- **Value:** Detects firmware or OS image transfer sessions to RTUs from non-engineering hosts, testing authorization enforcement on components.
- **App/TA:** Dragos/Claroty alerts + firewall
- **Data Sources:** `claroty:alert` category=firmware; `pan:traffic` ftp/scp
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_sec sourcetype="claroty:alert" category="firmware" earliest=-14d
| append [ search index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" app IN ("ftp","scp","sftp") dest_zone="OT-RTU" earliest=-14d
    | lookup ot_engineering_nets.csv src OUTPUT role | where role!="ews" ]
| stats earliest(_time) as t0, values(src) as sources by dest, signature, sourcetype
| sort - t0
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Block firmware protocols except via jump host; (2) require signed images; (3) alert any ftp to field; (4) integrate vendor secure boot logs if available.
- **Visualization:** Timeline, Table, Single value (events).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.15.38 · Embedded PLC Audit Log Forwarding Gaps (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 2.8)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Identifies PLCs that stopped emitting security audit events to Splunk compared to a seven-day baseline.
- **App/TA:** `metadata` or custom heartbeat
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_eng` per-controller heartbeat
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_eng sourcetype="plc:audit" earliest=-14d
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count by _time, controller
| eventstats avg(count) as baseline by controller
| where _time>relative_time(now(),"-3d@d") AND count < baseline*0.2
| table _time, controller, count, baseline
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable syslog/SNMP trap from PLC if supported; (2) UF on gateway concentrator; (3) alert on 24h silence; (4) spares swap procedure.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (count by PLC), Table, Single value (silent PLCs).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

---

### UC-22.15.39 · Secure Boot or Image Hash Mismatch on Industrial Appliance (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 3.4)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Correlates component attestation failures with subsequent process alarms to prioritize firmware integrity incidents.
- **App/TA:** HEC from appliance vendor or Edge Hub
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_edge` `sourcetype="edge:attest"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_edge sourcetype="edge:attest" earliest=-7d
| where result!="OK" OR match(_raw,"(?i)hash mismatch|secure boot fail")
| join type=left device_id [ search index=ot_hist sourcetype="hist:alarm" earliest=-7d | stats count as alarms by device_id ]
| table _time, device_id, result, alarms
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Collect TPM/UEFI events from industrial gateways; (2) isolate device on mismatch; (3) compare to gold hash registry; (4) vendor RMA workflow.
- **Visualization:** Table, Timeline, Single value (failed devices).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.15.40 · Modbus Serial Gateway Exposing Registers to Wrong TCP Subnet (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 4.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Flags TCP-side Modbus requests from subnets that should not see serial-segment registers, supporting confidentiality on component channels.
- **App/TA:** Edge Hub Modbus TCP capture
- **Data Sources:** `edge_hub_modbus`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" transport="tcp" earliest=-24h
| lookup serial_gateway_allowlist.csv dest_ip src_subnet OUTPUT permitted
| where permitted!="true"
| stats dc(function_code) as fcs, sum(register_count) as regs by src_ip, dest_ip
| sort - regs
```
- **Implementation:** (1) ACL on gateway Ethernet port; (2) VPN overlay for remote; (3) alert new src subnets; (4) document data diodes where used.
- **Visualization:** Table, Heatmap, Time chart.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.15.41 · Duplicate IP or MAC on OT Switch — Segmentation Violation (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 5.1)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Surfaces ARP/ND anomalies that indicate bridging or misconfiguration undermining component-level network segmentation.
- **App/TA:** Switch syslog, Claroty
- **Data Sources:** `cisco:ios` ARP, `claroty:alert`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=net sourcetype="cisco:ios" "%Duplicate address%" OR "%ARP collision%" earliest=-7d
| append [ search index=ot_sec sourcetype="claroty:alert" signature="*ARP*" earliest=-7d ]
| stats count by host, vlan, signature, src_ip
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enable DHCP snooping; (2) BPDU guard on access ports; (3) correlate with change tickets; (4) physical inspection for rogue bridges.
- **Visualization:** Table, Topology panel (if ITSI), Time chart.
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" isnotnull(exception_code) earliest=-4h
| bin _time span=1m
| stats count by _time, dest_plc, src_ip, exception_code
| where count>500
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Rate-limit at gateway; (2) ACL offending IP; (3) correlate with IDS; (4) verify PLC CPU load via vendor API.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value (peaks/min).

### UC-22.15.43 · PLC CPU Load and Scan Cycle Degradation (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 6.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Monitors embedded controller resource metrics to catch resource exhaustion from misconfiguration or attack precursors.
- **App/TA:** SNMP from OT NMS or vendor API HEC
- **Data Sources:** `index=ot_perf` `sourcetype="snmp:plc"`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_perf sourcetype="snmp:plc" earliest=-24h metric_name IN ("cpuUtil","scanCycleMs","taskOverload")
| timechart span=15m perc95(metric_value) by host, metric_name
| untable _time metric_series metric_value
| where metric_value>85 AND like(metric_series,"%cpuUtil%")
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Poll at safe intervals; (2) baseline per process; (3) alert above threshold; (4) tie to recent downloads.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table (worst hosts), Single value (breach minutes).
- **CIM Models:** Performance
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Performance.CPU by Performance.host span=15m | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Performance](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Performance)

### UC-22.15.44 · Redundant Controller Failover During Attack Window (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 7.1)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Correlates IDS alerts with DCS redundancy state changes to evidence availability protections via backup paths.
- **App/TA:** DCS alarm export, IDS
- **Data Sources:** `index=hist_alarms` redundancy; `nozomi:alerts`
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=hist_alarms sourcetype="dcs:redundancy" message="*failover*") OR (index=ot_sec sourcetype="nozomi:alerts" (severity="high" OR severity="critical" OR match(severity,"(?i)^high")))
| eval key=coalesce(controller_pair, dest_ip)
| transaction key maxspan=10m
| where mvcount(sourcetype)>1
| table _time, key, duration, sourcetype
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize timestamps; (2) validate expected failover tests; (3) exclude maintenance windows; (4) post-incident review template.
- **Visualization:** Timeline, Table, Single value (correlated events).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.15.45 · Historian Query Saturation During Incident (IEC 62443-4-2 / CR 7.2)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Tracks historian and OPC query rates to ensure analytical consumers do not starve operators during sustained attacks or mis-tuned reports.
- **App/TA:** Historian ODBC/API logs
- **Data Sources:** `index=hist_queries`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hist_queries sourcetype="hist:sql" earliest=-24h
| bin _time span=5m
| stats count as qps by _time, client_app, historian_cluster
| eventstats perc95(qps) as p95_global
| where qps > p95_global*3
| sort - qps
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Separate reporting VLAN; (2) cap concurrent ODBC; (3) throttle BI tools; (4) burst alerts to ops.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value (saturated windows).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

### UC-22.15.46 · Zone Boundary Traffic Monitoring — Anomalous Inter-Zone Volume (IEC 62443-3-2 / Zone boundary traffic monitoring)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Baselines permitted bytes and sessions between OT zones and flags spikes that may indicate policy drift, covert channels, or misrouted traffic at conduits.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=fw` `sourcetype="pan:traffic"` with `src_zone`, `dest_zone`, `bytes`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-14d
| eval zone_pair=concat(src_zone,"->",dest_zone)
| bin _time span=1h
| stats sum(bytes) as b, dc(dest_port) as ports by _time, zone_pair, app
| eventstats median(b) as med by zone_pair, app
| eval ratio=if(med>0, round(b/med,2), null())
| where ratio>8 AND b>10485760
| sort - b
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Build `ot_zone_pairs_expected.csv` for pairs that should exist; (2) exclude known maintenance windows; (3) alert on new `app` on sensitive pairs; (4) correlate with change records.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (bytes by zone_pair), Heatmap (zone_pair × hour), Table (top spikes).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(All_Traffic.dest_port) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.app span=1h | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.15.47 · Conduit Traffic Allowlist Enforcement — Unexpected DNP3 Functions (IEC 62443-3-2 / Conduit traffic allowlist enforcement)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Detects DNP3 function codes on encrypted OT tunnels that are not in the approved conduit function matrix.
- **App/TA:** Edge Hub or serial concentrator logs
- **Data Sources:** `edge_hub_dnp3` or `dnp3:frame`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_protocol sourcetype="edge_hub_dnp3" conduit_id="C-DMZ-OT1" earliest=-24h
| lookup dnp3_conduit_matrix.csv conduit_id function_code OUTPUT allowed
| where allowed!="true"
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, function_code, conduit_id
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Document matrix per conduit; (2) test in lab; (3) alert and optional block at gateway; (4) annual matrix review with engineering.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (function×conduit), Table, Time chart.
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Lantern — use case library](https://lantern.splunk.com/)

```spl
index=ot_sub sourcetype="iec61850:goose" earliest=-24h
| stats count by app_id, src_mac, vlan, bay
| eventstats median(count) as med by app_id
| eval ratio=round(count/med,2)
| where ratio>5 OR count>10000
| sort - ratio
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Baseline per bay during commissioning; (2) segregate process bus; (3) integrate relay vendor validation; (4) NERC/IEC dual use where applicable.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value (anomalous APPIDs).

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical

```spl
index=ot_sec sourcetype="claroty:session" earliest=-7d
| lookup asset_safety_class.csv asset_id OUTPUT safety_class
| lookup vlan_role.csv vlan OUTPUT vlan_role
| where vlan_role="SIS" AND safety_class!="SIS"
| stats dc(asset_id) as assets, count by vlan, protocol
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align CMDB safety flags; (2) re-cable or re-ACL; (3) monthly scan; (4) document compensating monitoring.

### UC-22.15.50 · DMZ Integrity Between IT and OT — Corporate Browser to OT HMI (IEC 62443-3-2 / DMZ integrity between IT and OT)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Finds IT-originated web traffic reaching OT HMIs that should only be reachable via bastion or reverse proxy in the DMZ.
- **App/TA:** Palo Alto (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic` url_category, zones
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-7d
| where dest_zone="OT-Web-HMI" AND src_zone="Corporate-Users" AND app="web-browsing"
| lookup ot_hmi_exposure.csv dest_ip OUTPUT exposure
| where exposure!="via_dmz_proxy"
| stats count by src_ip, dest_ip, url_category, user
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce explicit proxy paths; (2) split DNS views; (3) microsegment HMI; (4) user awareness for bookmarked IPs.
- **Visualization:** Table, Map (src), Time chart.
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.src, Web.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.15.51 · Engineering Workstation Access Control — RDP Skips Jump Tier (IEC 62443-3-2 / Engineering workstation access control)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Alerts when RDP/WinRM sessions skip the jump tier and land directly on low-Purdue controllers or Windows I/O servers.
- **App/TA:** Windows + firewall
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security` 4624 type=10; `pan:traffic` rdp
- **SPL:**
```spl
(index=windows EventCode=4624 Logon_Type=10 host="PLC-WIN-IO-*") OR (index=fw sourcetype="pan:traffic" app="rdp" dest_zone="OT-L1")
| eval session_key=coalesce(src_ip, src)
| lookup ot_jump_required.csv dest_ip OUTPUT jump_required
| where jump_required="true"
| stats count by user, dest_ip, host
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Disable RDP on controllers; (2) enforce PSM-only path; (3) GPO firewall; (4) weekly exception report.
- **Visualization:** Table, Timeline, Single value (violations).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security

- **App/TA:** Historian vendor logs
- **Data Sources:** `hist:sql` or `pi:audit`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hist_queries sourcetype="hist:sql" earliest=-7d
| eval bytes_mb=round(bytes/1048576,2)
| where bytes_mb>500 AND match(client_ip,"^10\.12\.") AND isnull(service_principal)
| stats sum(bytes_mb) as total_mb by client_app, client_ip, historian_cluster
| sort - total_mb
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require Kerberos or cert auth for service accounts; (2) throttle BI extracts; (3) DLP review for tag exports; (4) document data classification.
- **Visualization:** Bar (apps), Table, Single value (GB moved).

```spl
index=wireless sourcetype="meraki:association" OR sourcetype="cisco:wlc" ssid="OT-PROD-*" earliest=-24h
| iplocation src_ip
| lookup ot_wireless_allowlist.csv client_mac OUTPUT asset_class
| where asset_class!="instrumentation"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen, latest(_time) as last_seen by client_mac, ssid, ap_name
| sort - last_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) WPA3-Enterprise with cert; (2) dynamic PSK per device class; (3) block consumer OUIs; (4) RF site survey after changes.
- **Visualization:** Table, Map, Time chart.

- **CIM SPL:**

```spl
index=vpn sourcetype="pan:globalprotect" earliest=-7d
| eval split=coalesce(split_tunnel,"unknown")
| lookup ot_vpn_profiles.csv portal OUTPUT tunnel_mode_required
| where tunnel_mode_required="full" AND split!="disabled"
| stats dc(public_ip) as distinct_exit, count by user, portal, split
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Push portal config from Panorama; (2) block local subnet access lists; (3) monthly config export diff; (4) user re-education.

- **CIM SPL:**

### UC-22.15.55 · Zone Trust Level Verification — SL-T vs Observed Purdue Layer (IEC 62443-3-2 / Zone trust level verification)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IEC 62443
- **Value:** Joins SL-T/Criticality from inventory to observed Purdue level to find assets operating below required trust level for their criticality.
- **App/TA:** Industrial Asset Intelligence
- **Data Sources:** `ot_assets` with `sl_t`; `ot_topology` observed layer
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot_assets OR index=ot_topology earliest=-1d
| eval id=coalesce(asset_id, device_id)
| stats latest(sl_t) as required_tier, latest(observed_purdue_level) as observed by id
| eval mismatch=case(required_tier="SL-3" AND observed>2,1, required_tier="SL-2" AND observed>3,1, true(),0)
| where mismatch=1
| table id, required_tier, observed
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Refresh topology weekly from IDS; (2) reconcile moves with CMDB; (3) migration plan to correct zone; (4) risk acceptance workflow for exceptions.
- **Visualization:** Table, Scatter (tier vs level), Single value (count).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.12.1 · User provisioning evidence tied to financial application accounts (SOX §404 / COSO)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "User provisioning evidence tied to financial application accounts" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX §404 / COSO attestation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-24h EventCode=4720
| eval uc_tag="22.12.1"
| rex field=Message "(?i)Account Name:\\s+(?<acct>\\S+)"
| join type=left acct [ search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item earliest=-30d short_description="*provision*" | rex field=short_description "(?i)user\\s+(?<acct>\\S+)" | stats values(number) as ritm by acct ]
| where isnull(ritm)
| stats count by acct, host
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Change, Ticket_Management, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3207), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)
