### UC-22.12.2 · Privileged access review completion and aging for financial systems (SOX §404)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Privileged access review completion and aging for financial systems" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX §404 attestation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item earliest=-120d (short_description="*access review*" OR cat_item="*certification*")
| eval uc_tag="22.12.2"
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed=if(isnotnull(closed_at),strptime(closed_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"),null())
| eval age_days=round((now()-opened)/86400,1)
| where isnull(closed) AND age_days>45
| stats max(age_days) as max_age, values(number) as tickets by assignment_group, opened_by
| sort - max_age
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Change, Ticket_Management, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3207), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced

- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Segregation of duties conflicts across SAP / Oracle financial roles" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX §404 attestation.

- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=erp sourcetype=sap:audit earliest=-30d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.3"
| stats values(agr_name) as roles by uname
| eval role_count=mvcount(roles)
| lookup sap_sod_matrix.csv uname OUTPUT conflict_flag
| where conflict_flag="true" OR role_count>6
| table uname, roles, conflict_flag
```

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-7d EventCode IN (4672,4648)
| eval uc_tag="22.12.4"
| lookup sox_finance_prod_hosts.csv host OUTPUT finance_tier
| where finance_tier="PROD_GL"
| rex field=Message "(?i)Account Name:\\s+(?<priv_user>\\S+)"

| sort - count
```

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Authentication.dest) as agg_value from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - agg_value
```

### UC-22.12.5 · Terminated-user authentication after HR termination date (SOX §404)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Terminated-user authentication after HR termination date" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX §404 attestation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-14d EventCode IN (4624,4768)
| eval uc_tag="22.12.5"
| lookup hr_terminated_users.csv sam_account_name OUTPUT term_date
| where isnotnull(term_date)
| eval term_epoch=strptime(term_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where _time > term_epoch + 3600
| stats min(_time) as first_post_term by sam_account_name, ComputerName
| sort first_post_term
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Change, Ticket_Management, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item earliest=-180d short_description="*access certification*"
| eval uc_tag="22.12.6"
```

- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Emergency change retrospective documentation completeness" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX ITGC attestation.

- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request earliest=-180d type="emergency"
| eval uc_tag="22.12.8"
| eval start_epoch=strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval end_epoch=strptime(end_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval pi_ok=if(match(work_notes,"(?i)post.?implementation") OR match(close_notes,"(?i)PIR"),1,0)
| where pi_ok=0 AND state="Closed"
| table number, u_service_ci, start_date, state, short_description
```

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

```spl
| eval uc_tag="22.12.11"
| eval cab_evidence=if(match(work_notes,"(?i)CAB") OR match(comments,"(?i)minutes"),1,0)
| where cab_evidence=0
| stats values(number) as changes by u_service_ci, assignment_group
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request earliest=-365d state IN ("4","-1")
| eval uc_tag="22.12.13"
| lookup sox_in_scope_ci.csv cmdb_ci OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true"
| eval rollback_ok=if(match(close_notes,"(?i)rollback|backout"),1,0)
```

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

### UC-22.12.14 · Changes executed outside approved maintenance windows (SOX ITGC)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Changes executed outside approved maintenance windows" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX ITGC attestation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:change_request earliest=-90d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.14"
| eval start_epoch=strptime(start_date,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval dow=strftime(start_epoch,"%u"), hr=strftime(start_epoch,"%H")
| lookup maintenance_windows.csv assignment_group OUTPUT allowed_dow, start_hr, end_hr
| eval in_window=if(dow=allowed_dow AND hr>=start_hr AND hr<=end_hr,1,0)
| where in_window=0 AND u_priority="1"
| table number, assignment_group, start_date, cmdb_ci
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Change, Ticket_Management, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.12.15 · Financial close batch job failures and runtime SLA breaches (SOX close)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Financial close batch job failures and runtime SLA breaches" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX close attestation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=scheduler sourcetype=control-m:job job_name="*FIN_CLOSE*" earliest=-14d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.15"
| eval end_epoch=strptime(end_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval start_epoch=strptime(start_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval runtime_min=round((end_epoch-start_epoch)/60,1)
| where status!="SUCCESS" OR runtime_min>180
| stats count by job_name, order_id, status
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value, Bar chart
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Change, Ticket_Management, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Close-processing cluster CPU saturation during peak windows" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX performance attestation.

- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os (sourcetype=perfmon:cpu OR sourcetype=perfmon:mem) host="FIN-CLOSE-*" earliest=-14d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.19"
| eval util=if(sourcetype="perfmon:cpu",100-PercentIdleTime,PercentCommittedBytesInUse)
| bin span=5m _time
| stats perc95(util) as p95 by host, sourcetype
| where p95>92
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.12.21 · Priority incident aging for finance-critical configuration items (SOX operations)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Priority incident aging for finance-critical configuration items" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX operations attestation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident priority IN ("1","2") earliest=-90d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.21"
| lookup sox_in_scope_ci.csv cmdb_ci OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true"
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), resolved=strptime(resolved_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval hours=round((resolved-opened)/3600,2)
| where (priority="1" AND hours>4) OR (priority="2" AND hours>24)
| stats count by assignment_group, category
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=erp sourcetype=sap:close_task earliest=-60d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.22"
| stats sum(eval(status="C")) as done, count as total by task_group, responsible_user
| eval pct=round(100*done/total,1)
| where pct<100
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced

- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=erp sourcetype=sap:bseg earliest=-30d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.23"
| eval hr=strftime(_time,"%H"), amt=abs(dmbtr)

| sort - dollars
```

### UC-22.12.24 · Sequential ERP document number gap detection (SOX audit trail)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Sequential ERP document number gap detection" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX audit trail attestation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=erp sourcetype=sap:bkpf earliest=-7d bukrs="1000"
| eval uc_tag="22.12.24"
| eval docn=tonumber(belnr)
| sort docn
| streamstats window=1 current=f last(docn) as prev
| eval gap=docn-prev
| where gap>1
```

### UC-22.12.27 · Subledger-to-general-ledger reconciliation variance monitoring (SOX reconciliation)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "Subledger-to-general-ledger reconciliation variance monitoring" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX reconciliation attestation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=erp sourcetype=sap:faglflext racct="0000110000" earliest=-1d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.27"
| stats sum(hslvt) as gl by rbukrs
| append [ search index=erp sourcetype=sap:bsid earliest=-1d | stats sum(wrbtr) as ar by bukrs | rename bukrs as rbukrs ]
| stats values(gl) as gl, values(ar) as ar by rbukrs
| eval variance=abs(mvindex(gl,0)-mvindex(ar,0))
| where variance>1000
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert

- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC

- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:problem category="Control Exception" earliest=-365d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.30"
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval age=round((now()-opened)/86400,0)
| where state!="Closed" AND age>60
| stats max(age) as max_age by assignment_group
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

### UC-22.12.33 · IT control self-assessment questionnaire completion rates (SOX CSA)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOX / ITGC
- **Value:** Supports SOX IT general and application controls evidence for "IT control self-assessment questionnaire completion rates" by correlating security, change, ERP, and ITSM telemetry with control ownership records, strengthening SOX CSA attestation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Active Directory (3207), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow (1928), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, Active Directory replication/audit, `snow:*`, SAP/Oracle audit, `mssql:audit`, PAM, backup jobs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:sc_req_item cat_item="*IT Control Self-Assessment*" earliest=-120d
| eval uc_tag="22.12.33"
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval q=strftime(opened,"%Y-Q%q")
| stats count as issued, sum(eval(state="Closed")) as done by q, opened_by
| eval rate=round(100*done/issued,1)
```

### UC-22.16.1 · IT/OT boundary deny vs allow ratio by zone pair (TSA Pipeline Security)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** TSA Pipeline Security Directive
- **Value:** Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "IT/OT boundary deny vs allow ratio by zone pair".
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot sourcetype=pan:traffic earliest=-24h zone_dest="OT-L2*"
| eval uc_tag="22.16.1"
| stats sum(eval(action=="allow")) as allows, sum(eval(action=="deny")) as denies by zone_src, app
| eval deny_pct=round(100*denies/(allows+denies+0.001),2)
| sort - allows
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic, Authentication, Vulnerabilities, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.app | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

### UC-22.16.2 · Cross-zone traffic volume spike vs baseline (TSA segmentation)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** TSA Pipeline Security Directive
- **Value:** Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Cross-zone traffic volume spike vs baseline".
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot sourcetype=pan:traffic earliest=-30d dest_port IN (502,20000,44818)
| eval uc_tag="22.16.2"
| bin span=6h _time
| stats sum(bytes) as b by _time, src_ip, dest_ip
| eventstats median(b) as p50 by dest_ip
| where b > p50*4
| table _time, src_ip, dest_ip, b, p50
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Choropleth or site bar, Table, Single value
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic, Authentication, Vulnerabilities, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2757), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-24h EventCode=4624 Logon_Type=3
| eval uc_tag="22.16.3"

| where isnotnull(ot_zone)
| transaction src_ip maxspan=300s maxpause=30s
| eval hops=eventcount
| where hops>12
| table src_ip, hops, duration, ot_zone
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **SPL:**
```spl
index=linux sourcetype=linux_secure host="jmp-pipeline-dmz-*" earliest=-7d
| eval uc_tag="22.16.5"

| eval multi_host=if(mvcount(mvdedup(host))>1,1,0)
| where multi_host=1
```

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** TSA Pipeline Security Directive
- **Value:** Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Contractor MFA gaps to OT bastion and VPN portals".
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=o365 sourcetype=ms:aad:signin earliest=-24h
| eval uc_tag="22.16.9"
| where appDisplayName="Pipeline OT Bastion" AND authenticationRequirement!="multiFactorAuthentication" AND resultType="Success"
| stats count by userPrincipalName, ipAddress
```

- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate

- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** TSA Pipeline Security Directive

- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:pta earliest=-90d reason="*break*glass*"
| eval uc_tag="22.16.12"
| join type=left user [
    search index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident priority="1" earliest=-90d | eval inc_start=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S") | fields number user inc_start
  ]
| eval aligned=if(_time>=relative_time(inc_start,"-15m") AND _time<=relative_time(inc_start,"+8h"),1,0)
| where aligned=0
```

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert

- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** TSA Pipeline Security Directive
- **Value:** Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Cybersecurity plan milestone on-time completion".

- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=summary sourcetype=tsa:milestone earliest=-730d
| eval uc_tag="22.16.14"
| eval due=strptime(due_date,"%Y-%m-%d"), done=strptime(completed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval on_time=if(done<=due,1,0)
| stats sum(on_time) as hits, count by workstream
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t sum(All_Traffic.bytes_in) as agg_value from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.16.20 · Documented subnets vs observed NetFlow peers (TSA architecture)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** TSA Pipeline Security Directive
- **Value:** Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Documented subnets vs observed NetFlow peers".
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netflow sourcetype=flow:ipfix earliest=-7d
| eval uc_tag="22.16.20"
| eval dst_subnet=mvindex(split(dst,"."),0).".".mvindex(split(dst,"."),1).".".mvindex(split(dst,"."),2)".0/24"
| stats sum(bytes) as b by dst_subnet
| lookup pipeline_documented_subnets.csv dst_subnet OUTPUT zone
| where isnull(zone)
| sort - b
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

### UC-22.16.21 · Control-loop latency before vs after hardening window (TSA effectiveness)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** TSA Pipeline Security Directive
- **Value:** Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "Control-loop latency before vs after hardening window".
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot sourcetype=edge_hub:opcua metric_name="*LoopLatency*" earliest=-30d
| eval uc_tag="22.16.21"
| eval phase=if(_time<relative_time(now(),"-14d@d"),"before","after")
| stats perc95(metric_value) as p95 by device_id, phase
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic, Authentication, Vulnerabilities, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance

### UC-22.16.29 · First-seen OT patch-repository binary hashes (TSA supply chain)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** TSA Pipeline Security Directive
- **Value:** Supports pipeline cybersecurity program oversight under TSA Pipeline Security Directive by correlating operational telemetry with governance records for "First-seen OT patch-repository binary hashes".
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** `pan:traffic`, SCADA/HMI audit, PAM, `nozomi:alert`, NetFlow, ITSM, passive OT asset discovery
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=os sourcetype=stash:file_integrity path="*OT_Patch_Repo*" earliest=-90d
| eval uc_tag="22.16.29"
| stats earliest(_time) as first_seen by sha256, path
| eventstats earliest(first_seen) as g by sha256
| where first_seen=g AND first_seen > relative_time(now(),"-30d@d")
```

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

### UC-22.18.1 · FactoryTalk excessive operator login sessions (API RP 1164)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** API RP 1164
- **Value:** Evidences API RP 1164 cybersecurity practices for pipeline SCADA using OT and engineering audit sources.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Edge Hub, Nozomi Networks TA for Splunk (6905), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** RTU/PLC, DNP3, Modbus OPC-UA, Wonderware/Ignition, historian, OT firewall, wireless sensor feeds
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=scada sourcetype=factorytalk:audit earliest=-7d event="Login"
| eval uc_tag="22.18.1"
| stats dc(session_id) as sessions by operator_id, client_host
| where sessions>20
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic, Endpoint, Operational Telemetry, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner

```spl
index=scada sourcetype=factorytalk:audit earliest=-24h
| eval uc_tag="22.18.5"
```

- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert

- **Regulations:** API RP 1164

- **Data Sources:** RTU/PLC, DNP3, Modbus OPC-UA, Wonderware/Ignition, historian, OT firewall, wireless sensor feeds
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot sourcetype=nozomi:asset earliest=-14d
| eval uc_tag="22.18.22"
| stats latest(firmware_version) as fw by name, vendor
| join type=left name [
    search index=ot sourcetype=ics_cert_advisory earliest=-30d | stats latest(required_fw) as req by model | rename model as name
  ]
| where fw!=req
```


### UC-22.18.23 · Schneider PLC logic changes by user span (API RP 1164)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** API RP 1164
- **Value:** Evidences API RP 1164 cybersecurity practices for pipeline SCADA using OT and engineering audit sources.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Edge Hub, Nozomi Networks TA for Splunk (6905), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** RTU/PLC, DNP3, Modbus OPC-UA, Wonderware/Ignition, historian, OT firewall, wireless sensor feeds
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot sourcetype=schneider:ecostruxure:audit earliest=-30d
| eval uc_tag="22.18.23"
| where match(change_type,"(?i)logic|program")
| stats earliest(_time) as first_change, latest(_time) as last_change by plc_name, user
| eval span_h=round((last_change-first_change)/3600,2)
| where span_h<1
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

### UC-22.18.25 · Wonderware flow/pressure tag jumps over 50 percent (API RP 1164)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** API RP 1164
- **Value:** Evidences API RP 1164 cybersecurity practices for pipeline SCADA using OT and engineering audit sources.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Edge Hub, Nozomi Networks TA for Splunk (6905), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** RTU/PLC, DNP3, Modbus OPC-UA, Wonderware/Ignition, historian, OT firewall, wireless sensor feeds
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=scada sourcetype=wonderware:hist earliest=-1d
| eval uc_tag="22.18.25"
| where match(tagname,"(?i)flow|pressure")
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=f last(value) as prev
| eval jump=abs(value-prev)
| where jump > prev*0.5
| table _time, tagname, value, prev
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

### UC-22.18.27 · DNP3 sequence-number gaps (API RP 1164)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** API RP 1164
- **Value:** Evidences API RP 1164 cybersecurity practices for pipeline SCADA using OT and engineering audit sources.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Edge Hub, Nozomi Networks TA for Splunk (6905), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** RTU/PLC, DNP3, Modbus OPC-UA, Wonderware/Ignition, historian, OT firewall, wireless sensor feeds
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot sourcetype=dnp3:traffic earliest=-24h
| eval uc_tag="22.18.27"
| eval seq_gap=if(seq_num!=prev_seq+1 AND prev_seq>0,1,0)
| sort 0 dest_ip, _time
| streamstats window=2 global=f current=f last(seq_num) as prev_seq
| where seq_gap=1
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic, Endpoint, Operational Telemetry, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.18.29 · Pipeline cyber incident MTTR tracking (API RP 1164)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** API RP 1164
- **Value:** Evidences API RP 1164 cybersecurity practices for pipeline SCADA using OT and engineering audit sources.
- **App/TA:** Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk Industrial Asset Intelligence, Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Edge Hub, Nozomi Networks TA for Splunk (6905), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** RTU/PLC, DNP3, Modbus OPC-UA, Wonderware/Ignition, historian, OT firewall, wireless sensor feeds
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=itsm sourcetype=snow:incident category="Pipeline Cyber" earliest=-365d
| eval uc_tag="22.18.29"
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval resolved=strptime(resolved_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval mttr_h=round((resolved-opened)/3600,2)
| stats median(mttr_h) as p50_mttr by assignment_group
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

### UC-22.17.1 · LIMS audit entries missing reason codes (21 CFR Part 11)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** FDA 21 CFR Part 11
- **Value:** Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=gxp sourcetype=lims:audit earliest=-24h action IN ("INSERT","UPDATE","DELETE")
| eval uc_tag="22.17.1"
| eval gap=if(isnull(reason_code) AND action!="INSERT",1,0)
| stats sum(gap) as missing_reason, count by instrument_id, user_id
| where missing_reason>0
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value
- **CIM Models:** Change, Authentication, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.17.3 · MES batch clock-skew vs generated timestamp (21 CFR Part 11)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** FDA 21 CFR Part 11
- **Value:** Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=gxp sourcetype=mes:batch earliest=-7d
| eval uc_tag="22.17.3"
| eval clock_ok=if(strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")==generated_ts,1,0)
| where clock_ok=0
| stats count by batch_id, equipment_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.17.5 · LIMS records past retention without disposition (21 CFR Part 11)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** FDA 21 CFR Part 11
- **Value:** Supports Part 11 technical controls evidence by correlating validated system audit trails with identity and change records.

- **Data Sources:** LIMS, MES, ELN, CDS, QMS/ERP validation logs, Windows security for GxP workstations
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=gxp sourcetype=lims:audit earliest=-400d
| eval uc_tag="22.17.5"
| eval retain_until=strptime(retention_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where retain_until < now() AND disposition!="destroyed"
| stats count by study_id, cabinet
```


- **Regulations:** FDA 21 CFR Part 11

- **SPL:**
```spl
index=gxp sourcetype=eln:signature earliest=-30d
| eval uc_tag="22.17.10"

```

### 22.19 FISMA / FedRAMP

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** AWS CloudTrail, Azure Activity Logs, GovCloud audit logs, vulnerability scan results (Nessus/Tenable), STIG compliance scans, system inventory databases, POA&M databases, continuous monitoring feeds, security assessment reports.

---

### UC-22.19.1 · CloudTrail high-volume mutating actions (FISMA / FedRAMP)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** FISMA / FedRAMP
- **Value:** Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).
- **Data Sources:** CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, Tenable/STIG scans, POA&M exports, AAD/O365 sign-in, inventory
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=aws sourcetype=aws:cloudtrail earliest=-24h readOnly=false
| eval uc_tag="22.19.1"
| stats dc(userIdentity.arn) as actors by eventName
| where actors>5 AND match(eventName,"(?i)Delete|Put|Create")
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.
- **Visualization:** Time chart, Table, Single value, Heat map
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Change, Vulnerabilities, or N/A
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t dc(Authentication.user) as agg_value from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3110), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.19.3 · STIG file-integrity hash mismatch (FISMA / FedRAMP)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** FISMA / FedRAMP
- **Value:** Strengthens agency or cloud service continuous authorization evidence under FISMA / FedRAMP across cloud, endpoint, and GRC feeds.

```spl
index=os sourcetype=stash:file_integrity earliest=-7d
| eval uc_tag="22.19.3"
```

```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security earliest=-30d EventCode IN (4720,4726)
```

### UC-22.20.1 · CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 1 (CMMC 2.0 Level 2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** CMMC 2.0
- **Value:** Demonstrates contractor cybersecurity hygiene for "CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 1" using CUI-scoped telemetry mapped to CMMC 2.0 Level 2 expectations.

- **Data Sources:** `WinEventLog:Security`, CrowdStrike FDR, Defender for Office 365, NetFlow, encryption posture exports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cmdb sourcetype=snow:cmdb_ci_server earliest=-1d
| eval uc_tag="22.20.1"
| where match(classification,"(?i)CUI") AND isnull(marking_label)
| stats count by name, owner
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

### UC-22.20.2 · CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 2 (CMMC 2.0 Level 2)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** CMMC 2.0
- **Value:** Demonstrates contractor cybersecurity hygiene for "CMMC Level 2 practice evidence — CUI control area 2" using CUI-scoped telemetry mapped to CMMC 2.0 Level 2 expectations.

- **SPL:**
```spl

| eval uc_tag="22.20.2"

| where cui_flag="true" AND Logon_Type IN ("10","2")
| stats count by Account_Name, IpAddress
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Confirm field extractions and CIM tags for the sourcetypes in scope; (2) Load or maintain the referenced lookups/KVStore collections with owner attestation dates; (3) Schedule the search at an interval aligned to the control’s materiality; (4) Route positive findings to the compliance ticketing queue with required evidence fields; (5) Retain scheduled search exports per records management and legal hold procedures.

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### 22.21 EU AI Act

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** AI/ML model serving logs, training pipeline audit trails, model registry events, automated decision system outputs, human oversight action logs, conformity assessment records, model performance metrics, bias detection outputs, incident reports.

---

### UC-22.21.1 · Automatic Recording Events for High-Risk AI (Art. 12) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Demonstrates automatic recording of operation events for high-risk AI systems under Art. 12(1), including retention-ready correlation of model_id, decision_id, and subject_reference hash.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_ops` `sourcetype=mlflow:audit` OR `sourcetype=vertex:prediction` OR HEC `sourcetype=ai:high_risk:record` — fields: model_id, decision_id, subject_ref_hash, risk_class
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_ops (sourcetype="mlflow:audit" OR sourcetype="vertex:prediction" OR sourcetype="ai:high_risk:record") earliest=-24h risk_class="high"
| where isnotnull(model_id) AND isnotnull(decision_id)
| eval record_complete=if(match(_raw,"subject_ref_hash"),1,0)
| stats count as events, sum(record_complete) as with_subject_hash, dc(model_id) as models by sourcetype, host
| eval coverage_pct=round(100*with_subject_hash/events,2)
| sort - events
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize high-risk serving logs to include model_id, decision_id, and hashed subject reference; (2) tag sourcetype with risk_class; (3) schedule hourly and alert when coverage_pct < 99.5; (4) route breaches to compliance queue in ES.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by sourcetype), Single value (coverage_pct), Table (models with gaps)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)

---
### UC-22.21.2 · Model Input/Output Logging Integrity (Art. 12) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Verifies that each inference event carries paired input feature hash and output label/probability for Art. 12 traceability and post-incident reconstruction.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_serving` HEC JSON — input_feature_hash, output_label, output_prob, model_version
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_serving sourcetype="ai:inference:json" earliest=-4h
| eval has_in=if(isnotnull(input_feature_hash) AND len(input_feature_hash)>=16,1,0)
| eval has_out=if(isnotnull(output_label) OR isnotnull(output_prob),1,0)
| eval pair_ok=if(has_in=1 AND has_out=1,1,0)
| stats count as n, sum(pair_ok) as paired by model_version, deployment
| eval pair_rate=round(100*paired/n,2)
| where pair_rate < 100 OR n>0
| sort + pair_rate
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require inference middleware to emit paired fields; (2) add props.conf FIELDALIAS if vendor names differ; (3) alert on pair_rate drops > 0.1% in 15m; (4) retain raw JSON in restricted index.
- **Visualization:** Line chart (pair_rate over time), Table (worst deployments), Single value (global pair_rate)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2890), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)

---
### UC-22.21.3 · Automated Decision Audit Trail Reconstruction (Art. 12) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Chains decision_id across orchestrator, model gateway, and business rules engine to evidence end-to-end decision audit trail required for high-risk ADM logging.
- **App/TA:** Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_decision` sourcetypes: `airflow:task`, `ai:gateway`, `rules:engine` — shared decision_id
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_decision earliest=-24h decision_id=*
| eval hop=case(sourcetype="airflow:task",1,sourcetype="ai:gateway",2,sourcetype="rules:engine",3,true(),0)
| stats values(sourcetype) as path, min(_time) as t0, max(_time) as t1, range(_time) as span_sec by decision_id
| eval complete=if(mvcount(path)>=3,1,0)
| where complete=0
| eval span_min=round(span_sec/60,2)
| table decision_id, path, span_min, t0, t1
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Propagate decision_id across pipeline stages; (2) create macro `ai_decision_index`; (3) schedule every 30m for incomplete chains; (4) attach ITSI episode template for cross-team triage.
- **Visualization:** Sankey or sequence table (path), Histogram (span_sec), Table (incomplete decision_id)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---
### UC-22.21.4 · Model Performance Degradation Detection (Art. 12) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Detects sudden drops in accuracy/auroc vs baseline to support operational logging and risk management for high-risk AI performance stability.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_metrics` `sourcetype=ai:model:kpi` — model_id, metric_name, metric_value, window
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_metrics sourcetype="ai:model:kpi" metric_name="accuracy" earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1h perc95(metric_value) as p95_acc by model_id
| untable _time model_id p95_acc
| eventstats median(p95_acc) as baseline by model_id
| eval delta=p95_acc-baseline
| where delta < -0.02
| table _time, model_id, p95_acc, baseline, delta
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest batch evaluation KPIs hourly; (2) tune -0.02 threshold per model risk file; (3) create ITSI KPI thresholding; (4) document rollback criteria in QMS.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (p95_acc vs baseline), Table (negative deltas), Single value (models in alert)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2890), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

---
### UC-22.21.5 · Bias Drift Monitoring Across Protected Attributes (Art. 12) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Tracks disparate impact ratio across demographic buckets over sliding windows to evidence ongoing bias monitoring aligned with Art. 12 and Annex IV risk management.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_fairness` `sourcetype=ai:bias:eval` — attribute, bucket, selection_rate, approval_rate
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_fairness sourcetype="ai:bias:eval" earliest=-48h
| stats latest(selection_rate) as sr by attribute, bucket, model_id
| eventstats max(sr) as sr_max, min(sr) as sr_min by attribute, model_id
| eval dipr=round(sr_max/nullif(sr_min,0),3)
| where dipr > 1.25 OR sr_min < 0.01
| sort attribute, -dipr
| table model_id, attribute, bucket, sr, dipr
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure fairness jobs emit non-identifying buckets; (2) join reference rates via DB Connect if needed; (3) alert when DIPR exceeds policy; (4) store model card link in lookup.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (bucket vs selection_rate), Bar chart (dipr by attribute), Table (violations)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2890), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---
### UC-22.21.6 · Transparency — Explainability Payload Presence (Art. 13) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Confirms user-facing responses include required explanation fields (summary, confidence, limitations) for transparency obligations under Art. 13.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_ux` `sourcetype=ai:chat:response` — fields: explanation_summary, confidence, limitations
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_ux sourcetype="ai:chat:response" earliest=-24h
| eval has_sum=if(len(explanation_summary)>20,1,0)
| eval has_conf=if(isnum(confidence),1,0)
| eval has_lim=if(len(limitations)>10,1,0)
| eval art13_ok=if(has_sum+has_conf+has_lim=3,1,0)
| stats count as n, sum(art13_ok) as ok by product, model_id
| eval ok_pct=round(100*ok/n,2)
| where ok_pct < 100
| sort + ok_pct
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Instrument API gateway to log structured explainability fields; (2) redact PII in explanations; (3) weekly report to product/legal; (4) tie to conformity documentation version.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (ok vs n), Table (failing product/model_id), Single value (ok_pct)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)

---
### UC-22.21.7 · Model Version Tracking and Immutable Version IDs (Art. 13) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Detects production traffic served from non-semver or missing model_version to support traceability and change control evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_serving` — model_version, deployment, traffic_pct
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_serving sourcetype="ai:gateway:access" earliest=-4h
| eval semver_ok=if(match(model_version,"^v?\d+\.\d+\.\d+"),1,0)
| stats count as reqs, sum(semver_ok) as semver_hits by deployment, model_version
| eval semver_pct=round(100*semver_hits/reqs,2)
| where isnull(model_version) OR model_version="" OR semver_ok=0
| sort - reqs
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce semver in model registry webhook; (2) block deploy if version missing; (3) alert on null model_version > 10/min; (4) map deployment to ITSI entity.
- **Visualization:** Pie chart (semver_ok), Table (bad versions), Column chart (reqs by deployment)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)

### UC-22.21.8 · Training Data Lineage Event Completeness (Art. 13) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Validates dataset_id, snapshot_hash, and license_tag on each training run completion for dataset lineage traceability.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ml_train` `sourcetype=kubeflow:run` OR `sourcetype=mlflow:run`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ml_train (sourcetype="kubeflow:run" OR sourcetype="mlflow:run") event_type="completed" earliest=-7d
| eval lineage_ok=if(isnotnull(dataset_id) AND isnotnull(snapshot_hash) AND isnotnull(license_tag),1,0)
| stats count as runs, sum(lineage_ok) as ok by experiment, pipeline
| eval ok_pct=round(100*ok/runs,2)
| where ok_pct < 100
| sort + ok_pct
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Add lineage fields at pipeline DSL; (2) join catalog table via DB Connect for license_tag validation; (3) block promotion if lineage_ok=0; (4) export monthly compliance CSV.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (ok_pct by pipeline), Table (failed runs), Single value (runs missing license_tag)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2890)

### UC-22.21.9 · Feature Importance Audit Snapshot Correlation (Art. 13) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Correlates SHAP export job completion with model_version to retain feature importance audit snapshots for transparency reviews.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ml_explain` `sourcetype=ai:shap:export` — job_id, model_version, artifact_uri
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ml_explain sourcetype="ai:shap:export" earliest=-30d
| stats latest(_time) as last_export by model_version
| join type=left model_version [
    search index=ai_serving sourcetype="ai:gateway:access" earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(model_version) as versions_seen by model_version
  ]
| eval stale=if(isnull(last_export) OR (now()-last_export)>2592000,1,0)
| where stale=1
| table model_version, last_export, versions_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Schedule SHAP export on each release; (2) store artifact_uri in object storage with WORM; (3) alert if model_version in prod without export in 7d; (4) document in technical file.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (exports), Table (stale models), Single value (stale count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2890), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)

### UC-22.21.10 · Algorithm Change Documentation Linkage (Art. 13) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Ensures each production model_version has linked change_ticket and approver for algorithm change documentation.

- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_governance` HEC — model_version, change_ticket, approver, deployed_at
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_governance sourcetype="ai:deploy:gov" earliest=-90d
| stats latest(deployed_at) as deployed_at, values(approver) as approvers by model_version, change_ticket
| eval ticket_ok=if(match(change_ticket,"^(CHG|CR)-\d+"),1,0)
| where ticket_ok=0 OR mvcount(approvers)<1
| sort - deployed_at
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Require CI/CD to emit governance event; (2) validate ticket regex against ITSM; (3) create lookup fallback from DB Connect ITSM export; (4) quarterly audit sample.
- **Visualization:** Table (violations), Bar chart (ticket_ok rate by month), Single value (open violations)

### UC-22.21.11 · Human Override Action Logging Coverage (Art. 14) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Measures fraction of automated decisions overridden by human operators with mandatory reason codes for Art. 14 human oversight.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_hil` `sourcetype=ai:human:override` — decision_id, operator_id, reason_code
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_hil sourcetype="ai:human:override" earliest=-24h
| eval reason_ok=if(match(reason_code,"^[A-Z]{2,4}_\d{2,4}$"),1,0)
| stats count as overrides, sum(reason_ok) as with_valid_reason by application
| eval reason_pct=round(100*with_valid_reason/overrides,2)
| where overrides>0 AND reason_pct < 100
| sort + reason_pct
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce reason_code enum in UI; (2) map operator_id to HR directory via lookup; (3) alert on missing reason_code; (4) retain 24-month archive per policy.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (overrides by application), Table (invalid reason), Single value (reason_pct)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621)
