### UC-22.21.12 · Escalation to Human Review Queue Latency (Art. 14) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Tracks SLA from model_low_confidence event to human_assigned for timely human review per oversight procedures.
- **App/TA:** Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_hil` — event=low_confidence | event=human_assigned — decision_id
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_hil sourcetype="ai:human:queue" earliest=-7d (event="low_confidence" OR event="human_assigned")
| eval t=if(event="low_confidence",_time,null())
| eval t2=if(event="human_assigned",_time,null())
| stats min(t) as t_lc, min(t2) as t_ha by decision_id
| eval sla_sec=t_ha-t_lc
| where isnotnull(t_lc) AND (isnull(t_ha) OR sla_sec>900)
| eval breach=if(isnull(t_ha),"unassigned",if(sla_sec>900,"late","ok"))
| table decision_id, t_lc, t_ha, sla_sec, breach
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Unify timestamps to UTC; (2) set SLA based on risk class; (3) ITSI threshold on median sla_sec; (4) playbook for reassignment.
- **Visualization:** Histogram (sla_sec), Table (breaches), Single value (pct within SLA)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

### UC-22.21.13 · Autonomous Decision Threshold Breach Monitoring (Art. 14) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Alerts when decisions are auto-approved above configured risk_score without human gate, evidencing threshold enforcement.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_policy` `sourcetype=ai:decision:policy` — risk_score, auto_approve, human_gate
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_policy sourcetype="ai:decision:policy" earliest=-24h auto_approve=true
| lookup ai_risk_thresholds.csv model_id OUTPUT max_auto_risk
| eval breach=if(risk_score > max_auto_risk,1,0)
| where breach=1 OR (auto_approve=true AND human_gate=false AND risk_score>0.6)
| stats count by model_id, user_segment, breach
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain thresholds lookup by jurisdiction; (2) correlate with credit/product policies; (3) notable in ES on breach; (4) monthly threshold review attestation.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (breach count), Table (top model_id), Single value (breaches/1k decisions)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2890)

### UC-22.21.14 · Human-in-the-Loop Intervention Effectiveness (Art. 14) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Compares downstream outcomes after override vs baseline auto path to evidence intervention effectiveness for governance reviews.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_outcomes` — decision_id, path (override|auto), outcome_score, days_to_outcome
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_outcomes sourcetype="ai:outcome:track" earliest=-90d
| stats median(outcome_score) as med_score, median(days_to_outcome) as med_days by path, product_line
| eval uplift=if(path="override", med_score, null())
| eventstats max(eval(if(path="auto", med_score, null()))) as base by product_line
| eval delta=if(path="override", med_score-base, null())
| where path="override"
| table product_line, med_score, base, delta, med_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Align outcome_score definition with business KPI; (2) ensure ethical review sign-off on metrics; (3) quarterly dashboard to board risk committee; (4) document limitations.
- **Visualization:** Box plot (simulated via table), Bar chart (delta by product_line), Table (medians)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2890), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

```spl
index=ai_console sourcetype="ai:admin:session" earliest=-24h
| bin _time span=5m
| stats dc(session_id) as concurrent_sessions by _time, operator_id

| timechart span=1h max(concurrent_sessions) by operator_id

```

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High

- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Monitors last_review date for each QMS control_id to ensure Art. 17 quality management system remains current.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=qms` CSV via DB Connect or `sourcetype=qms:control:event` — control_id, last_review, owner
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=qms sourcetype="qms:control:event" earliest=-400d
| stats latest(last_review) as lr_epoch by control_id, owner
| eval days_since=round((now()-lr_epoch)/86400,1)
| where days_since > 365 OR isnull(lr_epoch)
| sort - days_since
| table control_id, owner, days_since
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest QMS export nightly; (2) map owners to RACI; (3) alert >365d; (4) attach evidence artifact hash field when available.
- **Visualization:** Table (stale controls), Bar chart (days_since distribution), Single value (stale count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

```spl
| stats count as calls, sum(eval(http_status>=400)) as errors by operation, registration_id
```

### UC-22.21.19 · CE Marking Evidence — Test Report Coverage (Art. 48) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Joins product_sku to test_report_id coverage from test lab feeds for CE marking evidentiary chain.
- **App/TA:** Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=product` `sourcetype=plm:sku` joined with `index=testlab` `sourcetype=lab:report`
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=plm sourcetype="plm:sku" earliest=-1d
| fields product_sku, model_family, hw_rev
| join type=left product_sku [
    search index=testlab sourcetype="lab:report" earliest=-400d
    | stats latest(report_id) as test_report_id by product_sku
  ]
| eval covered=if(isnotnull(test_report_id),1,0)
| stats sum(covered) as ok, count as total
| eval coverage_pct=round(100*ok/total,2)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize SKU keys; (2) nightly DB Connect sync; (3) alert coverage_pct < 100 for EU SKUs; (4) map to notified body references outside Splunk.
- **Visualization:** Single value (coverage_pct), Table (uncovered SKU), Pie chart (covered vs not)

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.21.20 · Risk Management System — Hazard Log Update SLA (Art. 9/Annex IV) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Measures time from new_hazard to hazard_reviewed in risk register stream for RMS operational effectiveness.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_risk` `sourcetype=rms:hazard` — hazard_id, status, transition
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_risk sourcetype="rms:hazard" earliest=-90d
| eval opened=if(status="new",_time,null())
| eval reviewed=if(status="reviewed",_time,null())
| stats min(opened) as t_open, max(reviewed) as t_rev by hazard_id
| eval review_hours=round((t_rev-t_open)/3600,2)
| where isnull(t_rev) OR review_hours>168
| sort - review_hours
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure hazard_id stable; (2) escalate unreviewed >7d; (3) link to incident index; (4) quarterly RMS committee export.
- **Visualization:** Histogram (review_hours), Table (open hazards), Single value (mean review_hours)

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.21.21 · Post-Market Surveillance Signal Detection (Art. 72) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Uses z-score on daily serious_complaint signals per model_id for early post-market surveillance escalation.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit (2890), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_pms` `sourcetype=ai:complaint:serious` — model_id, region
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_pms sourcetype="ai:complaint:serious" earliest=-120d
| timechart span=1d count by model_id
| untable _time model_id count
| eventstats avg(count) as mu, stdev(count) as sd by model_id
| eval z=(count-mu)/nullif(sd,0)
| where z>3
| table _time, model_id, count, mu, z
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Define serious_complaint taxonomy; (2) dedupe by ticket_id; (3) alert z>3 for 2 consecutive days; (4) feed pharmacovigilance-style review where applicable.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (count with z overlay), Table (spikes), Single value (models in spike)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Machine Learning Toolkit](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2890), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

### UC-22.21.22 · Serious Incident Report Pack Timeliness (Art. 73) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Tracks time from serious_incident_detected to regulator_packet_submitted for serious incident reporting workflows.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_incident` — incident_id, milestone, _time
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_incident sourcetype="ai:serious:incident" earliest=-180d
| eval m_detect=if(milestone="detected",_time,null())
| eval m_submit=if(milestone="regulator_submitted",_time,null())
| stats min(m_detect) as t0, min(m_submit) as t1 by incident_id
| eval hours_to_submit=round((t1-t0)/3600,2)
| where isnull(t1) OR hours_to_submit>72
| table incident_id, t0, t1, hours_to_submit
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Legal defines milestones; (2) immutable append-only index ACL; (3) alert at 60h; (4) attach packet hash in external DMS.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (milestones), Table (late incidents), Single value (open past 72h)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.21.23 · Model Withdrawal / Recall Execution Audit (Art. 12/72) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Verifies recall_command acknowledged by each edge node within SLA for model withdrawal evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Enterprise Security (263)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ai_edge` `sourcetype=ai:recall:ack` — recall_id, node_id, ack_time
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ai_edge sourcetype="ai:recall:ack" earliest=-30d
| stats min(ack_time) as first_ack, dc(node_id) as nodes_acked by recall_id
| join type=left recall_id [
    search index=ai_edge sourcetype="ai:recall:target" earliest=-30d
    | stats dc(node_id) as nodes_total by recall_id
  ]
| eval pct=round(100*nodes_acked/nodes_total,2)
| where pct < 100
| table recall_id, nodes_acked, nodes_total, pct, first_ack
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain authoritative node inventory; (2) alert pct < 100 after 4h; (3) ITSI episode per recall_id; (4) store signed ack externally.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (pct by recall_id), Table (lagging nodes), Single value (min pct)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263)

---
### UC-22.21.24 · User Feedback Collection Pipeline Health (Art. 72) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Monitors volume and error rate of in-app feedback submissions for post-market user feedback collection integrity.
- **App/TA:** Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=app` `sourcetype=app:feedback` — http_status, session_id
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=app sourcetype="app:feedback" earliest=-7d
| eval ok=if(http_status>=200 AND http_status<300,1,0)
| timechart span=1h sum(ok) as ok_ct, count as total
| eval ok_rate=round(100*ok_ct/total,2)
| where ok_rate < 95
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Add HEC token per environment; (2) dedupe by feedback_id; (3) alert drops >20% vs baseline; (4) map to product owner.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (ok_rate), Single value (7d avg ok_rate), Table (hours below 95%)
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest span=1h | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Web)

---
### UC-22.21.25 · Regulatory Authority Notification Delivery Confirmation (Art. 73) (EU AI Act)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AI Act
- **Value:** Confirms secure notification channel message_id acknowledged by authority mailbox/API within policy window.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055) or generic mail gateway logs
- **Data Sources:** `index=mail` `sourcetype=o365:messaging` OR `sourcetype=sendgrid:event` — message_id, event, recipient_domain
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=mail (sourcetype="o365:messaging" OR sourcetype="sendgrid:event") earliest=-30d recipient_domain="*authority*"
| stats earliest(eval(if(event="queued",_time,null()))) as t_q,
        earliest(eval(if(event="delivered",_time,null()))) as t_d,
        earliest(eval(if(match(event,"bounce|dropped"),_time,null()))) as t_fail
    by message_id
| eval deliver_h=round((t_d-t_q)/3600,3)
| where isnull(t_d) OR isnotnull(t_fail)
| table message_id, t_q, t_d, t_fail, deliver_h
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag regulatory messages with dedicated header; (2) alert on bounce; (3) retain DKIM pass evidence; (4) legal owns recipient allowlist.
- **Visualization:** Table (failed deliveries), Time chart (deliver_h), Single value (undelivered count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

### 22.22 PSD2 / Payment Services

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** Payment gateway transaction logs, SCA authentication events, Open Banking API logs, fraud detection system outputs, PIS/AIS provider access logs, card scheme transaction data, 3DS authentication logs, bank API gateway logs, chargeback/dispute records.

### UC-22.22.1 · SCA Challenge Rate Monitoring (RTS on SCA & CSC) (PSD2 RTS on SCA & CSC)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Monitors PSD2 strong customer authentication telemetry against documented baselines to evidence RTS compliance and proportionate exemption usage.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** `index=payments` `sourcetype=psd2:sca` — aspsp_id, psu_id, challenge, sca_exemption, outcome, authentication_method
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=payments sourcetype="psd2:sca" earliest=-24h
| eval sca_dim="challenge"
| eval challenge_flag=if(challenge="true" OR challenge="1",1,0)
| stats count as authn, sum(eval(outcome="success")) as ok, sum(challenge_flag) as ch, dc(psu_id) as psu by aspsp_id, sca_dim
| lookup psd2_sca_baseline.csv aspsp_id OUTPUT challenge_rate_baseline AS baseline
| eval challenge_rate=round(100*ch/authn,3)
| eval delta=round(challenge_rate-baseline,3)
| eval uc_id="22.22.1"
| where authn>100 AND (delta>1 OR delta<-1)
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, sca_dim, authn, challenge_rate, baseline, delta
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (challenge_rate), Bar chart (delta by aspsp_id), Table (top outliers)
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Common Information Model Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1621), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.22.7 · Transaction Fraud Detection — Score and Rule Hits (PSD2 / EBA fraud guidelines)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Supports PSD2 security objectives and EBA fraud monitoring expectations by correlating payment, authentication, and fraud-engine telemetry for typology coverage.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** index=fraud `fdm:score` — user_id, amount_eur, score, outcome, merchant_id, corridor
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=fraud sourcetype="fdm:score" earliest=-24h
| eval psd2_fraud_tag="fraud"
| eval high_value=if(amount_eur>5000 OR amount>5000,1,0)
| stats count as events, dc(user_id) as users, sum(high_value) as hv, median(eval(if(isnum(score),score,null()))) as med_score by psd2_fraud_tag, bin(_time,15m)
| eval uc_id="22.22.7"
| where events > 55
| sort - events
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (events by time bin), Table (top corridors/merchants), Single value (hv count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

- **Data Sources:** index=swift `cbpr:mt103` — user_id, amount_eur, score, outcome, merchant_id, corridor
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=swift sourcetype="cbpr:mt103" earliest=-24h
| eval psd2_fraud_tag="xb"
| eval high_value=if(amount_eur>5000 OR amount>5000,1,0)
| stats count as events, dc(user_id) as users, sum(high_value) as hv, median(eval(if(isnum(score),score,null()))) as med_score by psd2_fraud_tag, bin(_time,15m)
| eval uc_id="22.22.11"
| where events > 75
| sort - events
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (events by time bin), Table (top corridors/merchants), Single value (hv count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

### UC-22.22.12 · Account Takeover Signals Linked to Payment Instrument Changes (PSD2 / EBA fraud guidelines)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Supports PSD2 security objectives and EBA fraud monitoring expectations by correlating payment, authentication, and fraud-engine telemetry for typology coverage.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** index=auth `login:risk` — user_id, amount_eur, score, outcome, merchant_id, corridor
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=auth sourcetype="login:risk" earliest=-24h
| eval psd2_fraud_tag="ato"
| eval high_value=if(amount_eur>5000 OR amount>5000,1,0)
| stats count as events, dc(user_id) as users, sum(high_value) as hv, median(eval(if(isnum(score),score,null()))) as med_score by psd2_fraud_tag, bin(_time,15m)
| eval uc_id="22.22.12"
| where events > 80
| sort - events
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap (events by time bin), Table (top corridors/merchants), Single value (hv count)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

### UC-22.22.13 · TPP Access Monitoring for AIS/PIS Traffic (RTS APIs) (PSD2 / RTS on SCA & CSC)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Monitors third-party provider access patterns, consent alignment, and API security controls required for PSD2 open banking operational resilience.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876)
- **Data Sources:** `index=openbanking` — tpp_id, consent_id, consent_scope, accessed_scope, http_status, environment, bytes_out
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=openbanking sourcetype="ob:api:access" earliest=-24h
| eval api_tag="tpp"
| eval is_prod=if(match(environment,"(?i)prod"),1,0)
| eval scope_mismatch=if(isnotnull(consent_scope) AND isnotnull(accessed_scope) AND consent_scope!=accessed_scope,1,0)
| stats count as calls, sum(eval(http_status>=400)) as err, sum(scope_mismatch) as sm, dc(tpp_id) as tpp by aspsp_id, api_tag, is_prod
| eval uc_id="22.22.13"
| eval err_rate=round(100*err/calls,2)
| where err_rate>2 OR sm>0 OR (api_tag="env" AND is_prod=0 AND tpp>50)
| sort - err_rate
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (err_rate), Bar chart (calls by tpp_id), Table (scope_mismatch samples)
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Web.Web by Web.status, Web.http_method, Web.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Web)

### UC-22.22.19 · Payment Processing Integrity — Duplicate Authorization IDs (PSD2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Provides transaction-level integrity and monitoring evidence aligned with PSD2 operational and security expectations for ASPSPs and payment processors.
- **App/TA:** Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=payments` `paygw:txn` — authorization_id, merchant_id, amount_eur, fx_rate, reversal_flag
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=payments sourcetype="paygw:txn" earliest=-24h
| eval txn_tag="dup"
| eval eur_equiv=coalesce(amount_eur, amount*fx_rate)
| stats count as txns, dc(authorization_id) as auth_dc, sum(eval(eur_equiv>100000)) as very_high by merchant_id, txn_tag, bin(_time,1h)
| eval dup_suspect=if(txns>auth_dc AND txn_tag="dup", txns-auth_dc, 0)
| eval uc_id="22.22.19"
| where very_high>0 OR dup_suspect>5 OR txn_tag="refund"
| sort - very_high
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Column chart (very_high), Table (merchant_id), Single value (dup_suspect)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---

- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Column chart (very_high), Table (merchant_id), Single value (dup_suspect)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---
### UC-22.22.25 · Major Incident Notification Readiness to NCA (Payment Service) (PSD2 / NCA operational expectations)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=payments` `pay:incident:milestone` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:incident:milestone" earliest=-90d
| eval inc_tag="nca"
| stats earliest(eval(if(milestone=="detected",_time,null()))) as t0,
        earliest(eval(if(milestone=="nca_notified",_time,null()))) as t1,
        dc(incident_id) as incidents by inc_tag, severity
| eval hours_to_nca=round((t1-t0)/3600,2)
| eval uc_id="22.22.25"
| where inc_tag="nca" AND (isnull(t1) OR hours_to_nca>24)
| sort - hours_to_nca
| table uc_id, severity, incidents, t0, t1, hours_to_nca
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

---
### UC-22.22.26 · Operational vs Security Incident Classification Consistency (PSD2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=payments` `pay:incident:milestone` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:incident:milestone" earliest=-90d
| eval inc_tag="class"
| where isnotnull(security_class) AND isnotnull(ops_class)
| eval class_mismatch=if(security_class!=ops_class,1,0)
| stats sum(class_mismatch) as mismatches, count as events by aspsp_id, severity
| eval uc_id="22.22.26"
| where mismatches>0
| sort - mismatches
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, severity, mismatches, events
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

### UC-22.22.27 · Customer Impact Assessment Coverage for Incidents (PSD2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=payments` `pay:incident:milestone` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:incident:milestone" earliest=-90d
| eval inc_tag="cust"
| where milestone="impact_assessed"
| eval impact_missing=if(isnull(impacted_psu_estimate) OR impacted_psu_estimate<0,1,0)
| stats sum(impact_missing) as missing, dc(incident_id) as incidents by aspsp_id, severity
| eval uc_id="22.22.27"
| where missing>0
| sort - missing
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, severity, missing, incidents
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

### UC-22.22.28 · Payment Service Availability SLO Breach Tracking (PSD2)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=payments` `pay:health:probe` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:health:probe" earliest=-7d
| eval inc_tag="slo"
| bin _time span=5m
| stats avg(availability_pct) as avgp, min(availability_pct) as min_av by aspsp_id, payment_scheme, _time
| eval uc_id="22.22.28"
| where avgp < 99.5 OR min_av < 99.0
| sort _time
| table uc_id, _time, aspsp_id, payment_scheme, avgp, min_av
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

### UC-22.22.29 · Fraud Loss Reporting Aggregation by Product Line (PSD2 / EBA fraud reporting)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=payments` `pay:fraud:loss` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:fraud:loss" earliest=-400d
| eval inc_tag="loss"
| stats sum(loss_eur) as total_loss, dc(case_id) as cases by product_line, reporting_quarter
| eventstats median(total_loss) as med_loss by reporting_quarter
| eval uc_id="22.22.29"
| eval uplift=round(total_loss-med_loss,2)
| where uplift>10000 OR total_loss>250000
| sort - total_loss
| table uc_id, reporting_quarter, product_line, cases, total_loss, med_loss, uplift
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841)

### UC-22.22.30 · Quarterly Statistical Reporting Dataset Reconciliation (PSD2 / EBA reporting)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PSD2
- **Value:** Supports incident governance, customer communications evidence, and supervisory reporting workflows associated with payment service continuity and fraud statistics.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841)
- **Data Sources:** `index=payments` `pay:reg:stats` — incident_id, milestone, severity, impacted_psu_estimate
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=payments sourcetype="pay:reg:stats" earliest=-400d
| eval inc_tag="stats"
| stats sum(eval(mismatch="true")) as mismatches, count as rows by aspsp_id, reporting_quarter
| eval uc_id="22.22.30"
| eval mismatch_rate=round(100*mismatches/rows,2)
| where mismatches>0 OR mismatch_rate>0.5
| sort - mismatches
| table uc_id, aspsp_id, reporting_quarter, mismatches, rows, mismatch_rate
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Timeline (milestones), Table (late NCA notifications), Single value (mismatches)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Add-on for GitHub](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5765)

### 22.23 EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA)

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (5765), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** Product security event logs, vulnerability disclosure platforms, SBOM repositories, CI/CD pipeline logs, security testing outputs, incident response records, firmware update deployment logs, customer security advisory feeds.

### UC-22.23.1 · Security-by-Default Configuration Evidence (CRA Art. 10(2)) (EU CRA)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA)
- **Value:** Supports CRA product security, vulnerability handling, and transparency obligations with auditable telemetry from engineering and incident processes.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (5765)
- **Data Sources:** `index=product_security` `product:telemetry:security` — product_id, release_id, cve_id, status, result, enisa_ticket_id
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=product_security (sourcetype="product:telemetry:security" OR sourcetype="github:actions") earliest=-30d
| eval cra_tag="default_cfg"
| eval uc_id="22.23.1"
| stats count as events, sum(eval(status="fail" OR result="fail")) as fails, dc(release_id) as releases by product_id, cra_tag
| where fails>0
| sort - fails
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (fails), Table (product_id), Single value (releases with fails)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for GitHub](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5765)

### UC-22.23.15 · Actively Exploited Vulnerability Notification Window (24h evidence) (EU CRA)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU Cyber Resilience Act (CRA)
- **Value:** Supports CRA product security, vulnerability handling, and transparency obligations with auditable telemetry from engineering and incident processes.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for GitHub (5765)
- **Data Sources:** `index=product_security` `sec:incident:notify` — product_id, release_id, cve_id, status, result, enisa_ticket_id
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=product_security sourcetype="sec:incident:notify" actively_exploited=true earliest=-30d
| eval cra_tag="active24"
| eval uc_id="22.23.15"
| stats earliest(_time) as t_detect, latest(notification_sent_epoch) as t_sent by product_id, cve_id
| eval hours_to_notify=if(isnotnull(t_sent), round((t_sent-t_detect)/3600,2), null())
| where isnull(t_sent) OR hours_to_notify>24
| sort - hours_to_notify
| table uc_id, product_id, cve_id, t_detect, t_sent, hours_to_notify
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (fails), Table (product_id), Single value (releases with fails)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for GitHub](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5765)

### 22.24 eIDAS 2.0 / Trust Services

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** Qualified trust service provider (QTSP) logs, certificate lifecycle events, timestamping authority logs, e-signature platform audit trails, EU Digital Identity Wallet logs, PKI infrastructure logs, HSM audit trails, conformity assessment evidence.

---

### UC-22.24.1 · Qualified Certificate Issuance and Revocation Audit Trail (eIDAS / ETSI)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** eIDAS 2.0 / EU trust services
- **Value:** Supports qualified trust service auditing, wallet security monitoring, and PKI integrity evidence expected under eIDAS trust frameworks.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=trust` `qtsp:cert:event` — trust_service, serial, not_after, ocsp_url, http_status, wallet_id
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=trust sourcetype="qtsp:cert:event" earliest=-7d
| eval eidas_tag="iss"
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(not_after,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval days_to_exp=round((exp_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| stats count as events, min(days_to_exp) as min_days, sum(eval(http_status>=400)) as http_err by trust_service, eidas_tag
| eval uc_id="22.24.1"
| where min_days < 30 OR http_err>0 OR eidas_tag="susp"
| sort min_days
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Single value (min_days), Table (trust_service), Time chart (http_err)
- **CIM Models:** Certificates
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Certificates.All_Certificates by All_Certificates.ssl_subject_common_name, All_Certificates.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [CIM: Certificates](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Certificates)

---

- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=trust` `eudi:wallet:issuance` — trust_service, serial, not_after, ocsp_url, http_status, wallet_id
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=trust sourcetype="eudi:wallet:issuance" earliest=-7d
| eval eidas_tag="wiss"
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(not_after,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval days_to_exp=round((exp_epoch-now())/86400,1)
| stats count as events, min(days_to_exp) as min_days, sum(eval(http_status>=400)) as http_err by trust_service, eidas_tag
| eval uc_id="22.24.5"
| where min_days < 30 OR http_err>0 OR eidas_tag="susp"
| sort min_days
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Single value (min_days), Table (trust_service), Time chart (http_err)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742)

### 22.25 AML / CFT (Anti-Money Laundering)

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk UBA (Splunkbase 2941), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** Core banking transaction logs, wire transfer records, trade finance systems, customer onboarding/KYC platforms, sanctions screening outputs, PEP database feeds, suspicious activity report (SAR) systems, correspondent banking logs, trade surveillance platforms, currency transaction reports.

---

### UC-22.25.1 · Structuring and Smurfing Pattern Detection (Just Below Thresholds) (EU AMLD / national law)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU AML/CFT framework
- **Value:** Strengthens anti-money laundering and counter-terrorist financing monitoring, investigations, and supervisory examination readiness across transactions, customers, and list screening.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk UBA (2941), Splunk DB Connect (2686)
- **Data Sources:** `index=aml` `core:wire:txn` — customer_id, amount_eur, screening_result, screening_latency_ms, list_version
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=aml sourcetype="core:wire:txn" earliest=-7d
| eval aml_tag="struct"
| eval uc_id="22.25.1"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as events, sum(eval(screening_result="hit")) as hits, median(screening_latency_ms) as lat by customer_id, aml_tag, _time
| eventstats perc95(amount_eur) as p95_amt by aml_tag
| where (aml_tag="struct" AND amount_eur>9000 AND amount_eur<10000) OR hits>0 OR lat>800
| sort - events
| head 300
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (hits/lat), Histogram (amount_eur), Table (customer_id)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk UBA](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2941), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

---

```spl
| eval aml_tag="pkr"
| eval uc_id="22.25.17"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as events, sum(eval(screening_result="hit")) as hits, median(screening_latency_ms) as lat by customer_id, aml_tag, _time
| eventstats perc95(amount_eur) as p95_amt by aml_tag
| where (aml_tag="struct" AND amount_eur>9000 AND amount_eur<10000) OR hits>0 OR lat>800
| sort - events
| head 300
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (hits/lat), Histogram (amount_eur), Table (customer_id)

- **Data Sources:** `index=aml` `aml:product:risk` — customer_id, amount_eur, screening_result, screening_latency_ms, list_version
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=aml sourcetype="aml:product:risk" earliest=-7d
| eval aml_tag="prod_r"
| eval uc_id="22.25.31"
| bin _time span=1h
| stats count as events, sum(eval(screening_result="hit")) as hits, median(screening_latency_ms) as lat by customer_id, aml_tag, _time
| eventstats perc95(amount_eur) as p95_amt by aml_tag
| where (aml_tag="struct" AND amount_eur>9000 AND amount_eur<10000) OR hits>0 OR lat>800
| sort - events
| head 300
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize field extractions and CIM tags; (2) schedule hourly or near-real-time; (3) route alerts to SOC/compliance queues; (4) export evidence packs for supervisory review.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (hits/lat), Histogram (amount_eur), Table (customer_id)
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk UBA](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2941), [Splunk DB Connect](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/2686)

### 22.26 Norwegian Regulatory Framework

**Primary App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621).

**Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports.

---

### UC-22.26.1 · Classified information system monitoring (NSM RUT)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Sikkerhetsloven; NSM veiledning
- **Value:** Detects privileged logons and policy changes on hosts designated for classified processing to evidence continuous monitoring aligned with national security system expectations.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" earliest=-24h EventCode IN (4624,4625,4672)
| search ComputerName="GOV-CL-*" OR host="GOV-CL-*"
| eval logon_type=coalesce(Logon_Type, LogonType)
| stats count, values(IpAddress) as src_ips by user, ComputerName, logon_type
| where count>50 OR logon_type IN ("10","3")
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

---

### UC-22.26.2 · Security clearance tracking (Sikkerhetsloven)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Sikkerhetsloven; NSM veiledning
- **Value:** Joins authentication success events to HR clearance attributes to highlight accounts active after clearance expiry or role change.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr_security OR index=identity sourcetype IN ("clearance:hr","okta:user_lifecycle") earliest=-7d
| eval upn=lower(coalesce(user, profile_login))
| stats latest(clearance_level) as clr, latest(_time) as hr_ts by upn
| join type=left upn [| search index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4624,4634) earliest=-7d | eval upn=lower(user) | stats latest(_time) as last_auth by upn]
| where clr IN ("None","Revoked") AND last_auth>hr_ts
| table upn, clr, hr_ts, last_auth
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

---

### UC-22.26.3 · NSM reporting compliance (NSM IKT)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Sikkerhetsloven; NSM veiledning
- **Value:** Measures incident queue aging against internal NSM-aligned reporting deadlines including evidence attachments.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
`notable` (tag="nsm_reportable" OR rule_name="*national*security*") earliest=-30d
| eval detected=_time
| eval reported=strptime(reported_authority_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval hours=round((reported-detected)/3600,2)
| eval breach=if(isnull(reported) AND (now()-detected)>86400,1,if(hours>24,1,0))
| table _time, rule_name, owner, status, hours, breach
| sort - breach, - hours
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

---

### UC-22.26.4 · Protective security measures (NSM beskyttelse)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Sikkerhetsloven; NSM veiledning
- **Value:** Detects hardening drift (new services, weak protocols) on systems under protective security baselines.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4768,4769,4776) earliest=-24h
| stats dc(ComputerName) as hosts, count as krb by user
| join type=left user [| inputlookup mfa_required_nsm_users.csv | fields user mfa_enforced]
| where (isnull(mfa_enforced) OR mfa_enforced="false") AND krb>200
| sort - krb
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.26.5 · Information system accreditation evidence (NSM akkreditering)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Sikkerhetsloven; NSM veiledning
- **Value:** Aggregates control tests, exceptions, and approvals into a quarterly accreditation evidence view.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=change sourcetype IN ("snow:chg","servicenow:change") earliest=-30d (short_description="*accred*" OR cmdb_ci="*CL-*")
| stats values(state) as states, min(_time) as opened, max(_time) as updated by number, cmdb_ci
| eval reopened=if(mvcount(mvfilter(match(states,"(Implement|Reopened)"))) > 0, 1, 0)
| table number, cmdb_ci, opened, updated, reopened
| sort - updated
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.26.6 · Power system availability monitoring (Kraftberedskap)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Kraftberedskapsforskriften; NVE
- **Value:** Correlates SCADA-linked outages and restorations to evidence availability monitoring for electricity preparedness.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ot sourcetype IN ("opcua:session","scada:hmi:login") asset="GRID-*" earliest=-24h
| bin span=15m _time
| stats dc(session_id) as sessions by _time, asset, user
| eventstats median(sessions) as med by asset
| where sessions > med*3 AND sessions>4
| sort - sessions
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.26.7 · Grid SCADA access control (NVE)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Kraftberedskapsforskriften; NVE
- **Value:** Tracks jump-host and VPN sessions targeting SCADA enclaves for least-privilege and session integrity evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h
| eval src_zone=coalesce(src_zone,"IT-Unclassified"), dest_zone=coalesce(dest_zone,"Unknown")
| search src_zone="IT-Corporate" AND dest_zone="OT-SCADA" AND action="allow"
| lookup ot_segmentation_allowlist.csv src dest dest_port OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved="false"
| stats sum(bytes) as bytes, values(app) as apps by src, dest, dest_port
| sort - bytes
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.src, Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.26.8 · Emergency preparedness evidence (Kraftberedskap)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Kraftberedskapsforskriften; NVE
- **Value:** Ingests drill timelines, inject acknowledgements, and recovery checkpoints as auditable preparedness records.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=comms sourcetype IN ("drill:inject","drill:ack") earliest=-120d
| stats earliest(eval(if(match(_raw,"inject"),_time,null()))) as t_inject, earliest(eval(if(match(_raw,"ack"),_time,null()))) as t_ack by drill_id
| eval ack_min=round((t_ack-t_inject)/60,1)
| where isnull(t_ack) OR ack_min>15
| table drill_id, t_inject, t_ack, ack_min
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

```spl
index=os sourcetype IN ("drilling:app:status","WinEventLog:Application") host="DRILL-*" earliest=-24h
| rex field=_raw "Version=(?<app_ver>\d+\.\d+\.\d+)"
| stats latest(app_ver) as observed by host
| lookup drilling_control_baseline.csv host OUTPUT expected_ver
| where isnotnull(expected_ver) AND observed!=expected_ver
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. ```spl


- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.26.15 · Petroleum facility access control (Petroleumsforskriften)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Petroleumsforskriften; PSA
- **Value:** Correlates physical badge events with logical sessions into engineering workstations for facility access control.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=pacs sourcetype="badge:swipe" site="NOR-OIL-*" earliest=-24h
| eval day=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d"), user=lower(user)
| stats earliest(_time) as first_in, latest(_time) as last_in by user, day, site
| join type=left user, day [| search index=vpn sourcetype="paloalto:globalprotect" log_type="connect" earliest=-2d
    | eval day=strftime(_time,"%Y-%m-%d"), user=lower(user)
    | stats dc(public_ip) as vpn_ips by user, day]
| where isnull(vpn_ips) AND (last_in-first_in)>3600
| table user, day, site, first_in, last_in, vpn_ips
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Personopplysningsloven; Datatilsynet

- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="breach:ticket" jurisdiction="NO" earliest=-180d
| eval confirmed=strptime(confirmed_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval filed=strptime(datatilsynet_filed_at,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| eval hours=round((filed-confirmed)/3600,2)
| eval breach_sla=if(isnull(filed) OR hours>72,1,0)
| table breach_id, confirmed_at, datatilsynet_filed_at, hours, breach_sla
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count

- **Regulations:** Personopplysningsloven; Datatilsynet
- **Value:** Validates transfer tickets include documented safeguards before data leaves the EEA boundary.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, OT/ICS system logs, power grid SCADA, petroleum control systems, government system audit trails, identity management systems, security incident reports
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=proxy sourcetype="zscaler:web" earliest=-24h
| iplocation dest_host
| lookup eea_countries.csv Country OUTPUT in_eea
| where in_eea!="true"
| lookup transfer_register.csv dest_host OUTPUT mechanism
| where isnull(mechanism) OR mechanism="pending"
| stats count by dest_host, Country, mechanism
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain supporting lookups (classified hosts, sectors, transfers) aligned to Norwegian regulatory language. (2) Schedule searches with Europe/Oslo time semantics for reporting cutoffs. (3) Route ES notables to the correct national security, OT, or privacy queues. (4) Quarterly false-positive tuning with HR, operations, and legal stakeholders.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (events by host), Table (top users), Single value (policy violations).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/833), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" earliest=-34h EventCode IN (4624,4625,4648)
| lookup uk_oes_assets.csv host OUTPUT oes_tier
| where isnotnull(oes_tier)
```

### UC-22.27.4 · Network and information systems incident reporting (UK NIS Regulations)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIS Regulations (UK)

- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails (AWS, Azure), network infrastructure logs, financial application logs, identity management systems, vulnerability scan outputs, incident response logs, operational resilience evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
`notable` (rule_name="*NIS*" OR tag="uk_nis_incident") earliest=-30d
| eval hours=round((now()-_time)/3600,2)
| eval overdue=if(status IN ("New","In Progress") AND hours>12,1,0)
| table _time, rule_name, owner, status, hours, overdue
| sort - overdue, - hours
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `uk_oes_inventory.csv` with OES identifiers, essential services, and CAF mappings. (2) Tag correlation searches with `nis_relevant` or `fca_relevant` for workflow routing. (3) Integrate IR ticketing milestones for competent authority and FCA clocks. (4) Monthly review with compliance, technology risk, and resilience forums.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (event volume), Heatmap (service x control), Table (top risk drivers).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
