### UC-22.27.9 · Competent authority notification completeness (UK NIS Regulations)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIS Regulations (UK)
- **Value:** Supports UK UK NIS Regulations supervisory expectations by correlating technical telemetry with governance records for competent authority notification completeness.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails (AWS, Azure), network infrastructure logs, financial application logs, identity management systems, vulnerability scan outputs, incident response logs, operational resilience evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="nis:notification" earliest=-365d
| eval filed=strptime(filed_competent_authority_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval delta_h=round((filed-detected)/3600,2)
| where isnull(filed) OR delta_h>72
| table incident_ref, detected_at, filed_competent_authority_at, delta_h
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `uk_oes_inventory.csv` with OES identifiers, essential services, and CAF mappings. (2) Tag correlation searches with `nis_relevant` or `fca_relevant` for workflow routing. (3) Integrate IR ticketing milestones for competent authority and FCA clocks. (4) Monthly review with compliance, technology risk, and resilience forums.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (event volume), Heatmap (service x control), Table (top risk drivers).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.27.12 · Impact tolerance testing evidence (FCA operational resilience)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** FCA SS1/21 operational resilience
- **Value:** Supports UK FCA operational resilience supervisory expectations by correlating technical telemetry with governance records for impact tolerance testing evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails (AWS, Azure), network infrastructure logs, financial application logs, identity management systems, vulnerability scan outputs, incident response logs, operational resilience evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=test sourcetype="resilience:scenario" earliest=-365d
| eval tol=strptime(impact_tolerance_breach_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| stats count(eval(breach="true")) as breaches, count as runs by ibs_name
| eval breach_rate=round(100*breaches/runs,2)
| where breach_rate>0
| sort - breach_rate
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `uk_oes_inventory.csv` with OES identifiers, essential services, and CAF mappings. (2) Tag correlation searches with `nis_relevant` or `fca_relevant` for workflow routing. (3) Integrate IR ticketing milestones for competent authority and FCA clocks. (4) Monthly review with compliance, technology risk, and resilience forums.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (event volume), Heatmap (service x control), Table (top risk drivers).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.27.13 · Third-party dependency concentration monitoring (FCA operational resilience)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** FCA SS1/21 operational resilience
- **Value:** Supports UK FCA operational resilience supervisory expectations by correlating technical telemetry with governance records for third-party dependency concentration monitoring.

- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails (AWS, Azure), network infrastructure logs, financial application logs, identity management systems, vulnerability scan outputs, incident response logs, operational resilience evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cmdb sourcetype="vendor:dependency" earliest=-7d
| stats dc(critical_service) as fanout by vendor_name
| eventstats max(fanout) as maxf
| eval concentration=round(100*fanout/maxf,2)
| where fanout>=5
| sort - fanout
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `uk_oes_inventory.csv` with OES identifiers, essential services, and CAF mappings. (2) Tag correlation searches with `nis_relevant` or `fca_relevant` for workflow routing. (3) Integrate IR ticketing milestones for competent authority and FCA clocks. - **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** FCA SS1/21 operational resilience
- **Value:** Supports UK FCA operational resilience supervisory expectations by correlating technical telemetry with governance records for fca notification timeline tracking.

- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails (AWS, Azure), network infrastructure logs, financial application logs, identity management systems, vulnerability scan outputs, incident response logs, operational resilience evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="fca:notification" earliest=-730d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval filed=strptime(filed_regulator_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval hours=round((filed-opened)/3600,2)
| where hours>24 OR isnull(filed)
| table case_id, opened_at, filed_regulator_at, hours
```

(2) Tag correlation searches with `nis_relevant` or `fca_relevant` for workflow routing. (3) Integrate IR ticketing milestones for competent authority and FCA clocks. - **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Regulations:** FCA SM&CR
- **Value:** Supports UK SM&CR supervisory expectations by correlating technical telemetry with governance records for senior manager responsibility mapping.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails (AWS, Azure), network infrastructure logs, financial application logs, identity management systems, vulnerability scan outputs, incident response logs, operational resilience evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype="smcr:smf" earliest=-90d
| stats values(smf_role) as roles, dc(department) as depts by senior_manager
| mvexpand roles
| lookup smcr_prescribed_responsibilities.csv smf_role OUTPUT required
| where required="true" AND depts>3
| table senior_manager, roles, depts
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `uk_oes_inventory.csv` with OES identifiers, essential services, and CAF mappings. (2) Tag correlation searches with `nis_relevant` or `fca_relevant` for workflow routing. (3) Integrate IR ticketing milestones for competent authority and FCA clocks. (4) Monthly review with compliance, technology risk, and resilience forums.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (event volume), Heatmap (service x control), Table (top risk drivers).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.27.25 · Certification regime compliance monitoring (SM&CR)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** FCA SM&CR
- **Value:** Supports UK SM&CR supervisory expectations by correlating technical telemetry with governance records for certification regime compliance monitoring.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails (AWS, Azure), network infrastructure logs, financial application logs, identity management systems, vulnerability scan outputs, incident response logs, operational resilience evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=learning sourcetype="smcr:certification" earliest=-180d
| stats latest(cert_status) as st, latest(expiry) as ex by employee_id, cert_type
| eval exp_epoch=strptime(ex,"%Y-%m-%d")
| where st!="Active" OR exp_epoch<relative_time(now(),"+60d@d")
| table employee_id, cert_type, st, ex
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `uk_oes_inventory.csv` with OES identifiers, essential services, and CAF mappings. (2) Tag correlation searches with `nis_relevant` or `fca_relevant` for workflow routing. (3) Integrate IR ticketing milestones for competent authority and FCA clocks. (4) Monthly review with compliance, technology risk, and resilience forums.
- **Visualization:** Time chart (event volume), Heatmap (service x control), Table (top risk drivers).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails (AWS, Azure), network infrastructure logs, financial application logs, identity management systems, vulnerability scan outputs, incident response logs, operational resilience evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=defender sourcetype="DefenderATP:Alert" OR index=crowdstrike sourcetype="crowdstrike:hosts" earliest=-7d
| stats latest(last_seen) as ls, latest(rtr_state) as rtr by hostname
| eval stale=if((now()-ls)>604800,1,0)
| lookup cyberessentials_endpoints.csv hostname OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true" AND (stale=1 OR rtr!="enabled")
| table hostname, ls, rtr, stale
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `uk_oes_inventory.csv` with OES identifiers, essential services, and CAF mappings. (2) Tag correlation searches with `nis_relevant` or `fca_relevant` for workflow routing. (3) Integrate IR ticketing milestones for competent authority and FCA clocks. - **Visualization:** Time chart (event volume), Heatmap (service x control), Table (top risk drivers).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.28.1 · KRITIS operator reporting evidence (IT-SiG 2.0; BSI)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IT-SiG 2.0
- **Value:** Demonstrates alignment with IT-Sicherheitsgesetz 2.0 expectations for kritis operator reporting evidence using auditable Splunk evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="bsi:kritis:report" earliest=-365d
| eval submitted=strptime(submitted_bsi_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval due=strptime(reporting_deadline,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| where isnull(submitted) OR submitted>due
| table operator_id, reporting_deadline, submitted_bsi_at
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners.
- **Visualization:** Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.28.4 · BSI notification within 24 hours tracking (IT-SiG 2.0; BSI)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** IT-SiG 2.0
- **Value:** Demonstrates alignment with IT-Sicherheitsgesetz 2.0 expectations for bsi notification within 24 hours tracking using auditable Splunk evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=incident sourcetype="bsi:notification" earliest=-180d
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval bsi_sent=strptime(bsi_notified_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval hours=round((bsi_sent-detected)/3600,2)
| where hours>24 OR isnull(bsi_sent)
| table incident_id, hours
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners.
- **Visualization:** Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

```spl
| timechart span=1d max(utilization_pct) as u by sector
```

```spl
index=vulns sourcetype IN ("tenable:sc","qualys:vm") tag=kritis earliest=-14d
```

- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

```spl
index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" earliest=-24h tag=kritis_interface

```

### UC-22.28.19 · User access management for banking systems (BAIT/KAIT)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** BAIT/KAIT
- **Value:** Demonstrates alignment with BAIT/KAIT expectations for user access management for banking systems using auditable Splunk evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4624,4720,4738) earliest=-24h
| stats dc(ComputerName) as hosts by Account_Name, Privilege_List
| lookup bait_user_access_scope.csv Account_Name OUTPUT role
| where isnotnull(role) AND hosts>15
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners.
- **Visualization:** Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls).
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Vulnerabilities.Vulnerabilities by Vulnerabilities.severity, Vulnerabilities.signature, Vulnerabilities.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [CIM: Vulnerabilities](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Vulnerabilities)

### UC-22.28.20 · Critical infrastructure reporting for insurance sector (BAIT/KAIT)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** BAIT/KAIT
- **Value:** Demonstrates alignment with BAIT/KAIT expectations for critical infrastructure reporting for insurance sector using auditable Splunk evidence.

- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, critical infrastructure system logs, BSI IT-Grundschutz compliance scans, SIEM correlation events, vulnerability scan outputs, change management records, incident response logs, security audit evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="kritis:finance_report" earliest=-365d
| stats latest(submitted) as sub by institution_id, period
| where sub!="true"
| table institution_id, period, sub
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `de_kritis_assets.csv` with sector classification and KRITIS identifiers. (2) Link ITSI services to KRITIS objects for business impact overlays. (3) Export monthly control posture snapshots for BSI engagement. (4) Tune thresholds with enterprise risk management and OT security owners.
- **Visualization:** Matrix (host x module), Bar chart (severity), Table (controls).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk OT Security Add-on](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5151), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.29.1 · China PIPL Art.38 localization boundary monitoring (PIPL Art.38; ASEAN CBPR)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PIPL Art.38
- **Value:** Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for china pipl art.38 localization boundary monitoring aligned with PIPL Art.38; ASEAN CBPR.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=db_audit sourcetype="oracle:audit" Action="SELECT" earliest=-36h
| stats count by DBUserName, Obj_Name, client_ip
| iplocation client_ip
| where Country!="China" AND match(Obj_Name,"(?i)resident")
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)

---

### UC-22.29.2 · Cross-border transfer impact assessment logging (PIPL Art.38; ASEAN CBPR)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PIPL Art.38

```spl

| stats sum(bytes_out) as ob by dest_region, tenant

| eval pipl_art38_review="required"
| sort - ob
```

### UC-22.29.7 · Breach discovery and severity classification (APAC breach laws)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** APAC breach laws
- **Value:** Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for breach discovery and severity classification aligned with APAC breach laws.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="breach:event" jurisdiction IN ("SG","TH","JP","KR","CN") earliest=-180d
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval authority=strptime(notified_authority_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval individuals=strptime(notified_individuals_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval sla_h=case(jurisdiction="SG",72,jurisdiction="TH",72,jurisdiction="JP",120,1=1,24+7)
| eval breach=if(isnull(authority) AND (now()-detected)>sla_h*3600,1,0)
| eval register_line="7"
| table breach_id, jurisdiction, breach, register_line
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)

### UC-22.29.12 · Remediation and root-cause tracking (APAC breach laws)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** APAC breach laws
- **Value:** Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for remediation and root-cause tracking aligned with APAC breach laws.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="breach:event" jurisdiction IN ("SG","TH","JP","KR","CN") earliest=-180d
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval authority=strptime(notified_authority_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval individuals=strptime(notified_individuals_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval sla_h=case(jurisdiction="SG",72,jurisdiction="TH",72,jurisdiction="JP",120,1=1,24+12)
| eval breach=if(isnull(authority) AND (now()-detected)>sla_h*3600,1,0)
| eval register_line="12"
| table breach_id, jurisdiction, breach, register_line
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)

(2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)

### UC-22.29.29 · Consent record retention compliance (PIPL; PDPA)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PIPL
- **Value:** Provides APAC privacy supervisory evidence for consent record retention compliance aligned with PIPL; PDPA.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365 (4055), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, cloud audit trails, DLP system outputs, consent management platforms, data classification tools, cross-border transfer logs, privacy impact assessments, breach notification records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cmp sourcetype="consent:archive" earliest=-730d
| stats min(_time) as first, max(_time) as last by consent_id
| eval retained_days=round((last-first)/86400,0)
| where retained_days>2555
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `apac_transfer_register.csv` and consent or DPIA lookups referenced in runbooks. (2) Map technical identifiers to records of processing activities with legal review. (3) Restrict indexes to regional privacy roles with least privilege. (4) Quarterly DPIA and consent linkage review with local counsel.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (jurisdiction x action), Table (open reviews), Single value (exports without mechanism).
- **CIM Models:** N/A

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Office 365](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4055)

### UC-22.30.1 · Technology risk management governance metrics (MAS TRM)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** MAS TRM
- **Value:** Supports MAS TRM supervisory examination readiness for technology risk management governance metrics.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Banking system audit trails, cloud infrastructure logs, vulnerability scan outputs, privileged access management logs, change management records, business continuity evidence, incident response logs, outsourcing management records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=bank sourcetype="core:audit" regulator="MAS" earliest=-37h
| stats count by function_id, user, action
| lookup mas_trm_critical_functions.csv function_id OUTPUT tier
| where tier="Material" AND action="FUNDS_TRANSFER"
| sort - count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `apac_material_services.csv` for important business services and regulator tags. (2) Send weekly digests to CRO and technology risk committees. (3) Validate DB Connect concurrency for audit peak windows. (4) Archive results to a restricted summary index for retention governance.
- **Visualization:** Column chart (events by regulator), Table (privileged actions), Timeline (notification milestones).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.user, All_Changes.action | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)


- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert


### UC-22.30.11 · Internet banking security event monitoring (HKMA TM-G-2)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HKMA TM-G-2
- **Value:** Supports HKMA TM-G-2 supervisory examination readiness for internet banking security event monitoring.

- **Data Sources:** Banking system audit trails, cloud infrastructure logs, vulnerability scan outputs, privileged access management logs, change management records, business continuity evidence, incident response logs, outsourcing management records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=web sourcetype="ib:access" earliest=-24h
| stats count by uri, user, geo
| search uri="*/ib/*" AND geo!="HK"
| sort - count

```

### UC-22.30.12 · HKMA incident reporting timeline compliance (HKMA TM-G-2)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HKMA TM-G-2
- **Value:** Supports HKMA TM-G-2 supervisory examination readiness for hkma incident reporting timeline compliance.

- **Data Sources:** Banking system audit trails, cloud infrastructure logs, vulnerability scan outputs, privileged access management logs, change management records, business continuity evidence, incident response logs, outsourcing management records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype="hkma:incident" earliest=-180d
| eval hrs=(now()-strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))/3600
| where isnull(hkma_filed_at) AND hrs>2
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `apac_material_services.csv` for important business services and regulator tags. (2) Send weekly digests to CRO and technology risk committees. (3) Validate DB Connect concurrency for audit peak windows. (4) Archive results to a restricted summary index for retention governance.

- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.30.13 · Cyber security framework compliance monitoring (RBI cyber security framework)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** RBI cyber security framework
- **Value:** Supports RBI cyber security framework supervisory examination readiness for cyber security framework compliance monitoring.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk DB Connect (2686), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Banking system audit trails, cloud infrastructure logs, vulnerability scan outputs, privileged access management logs, change management records, business continuity evidence, incident response logs, outsourcing management records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=rbi sourcetype="bank:cyber:event" earliest=-30d
| eval reported=strptime(reported_cert_in_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| where isnull(reported) OR (reported-detected)>6*3600
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `apac_material_services.csv` for important business services and regulator tags. (2) Send weekly digests to CRO and technology risk committees. (3) Validate DB Connect concurrency for audit peak windows. (4) Archive results to a restricted summary index for retention governance.

- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

- **Criticality:** 🟠 High

- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance

- **Regulations:** APRA CPS 234

- **Data Sources:** Banking system audit trails, cloud infrastructure logs, vulnerability scan outputs, privileged access management logs, change management records, business continuity evidence, incident response logs, outsourcing management records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=board sourcetype="cps234:board_report" earliest=-400d
| stats latest(submitted) as s by quarter
| where s!="true"
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `apac_material_services.csv` for important business services and regulator tags. (2) Send weekly digests to CRO and technology risk committees. (3) Validate DB Connect concurrency for audit peak windows. (4) Archive results to a restricted summary index for retention governance.
- **Visualization:** Column chart (events by regulator), Table (privileged actions), Timeline (notification milestones).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

### UC-22.31.1 · Notifiable data breach assessment workflow timing (Privacy Act 1988 (Cth); NDB)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Privacy Act 1988 (Cth); NDB
- **Value:** Evidences Privacy Act 1988 (Cth); NDB obligations for notifiable data breach assessment workflow timing using endpoint, identity, and cloud telemetry.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, endpoint protection logs, cloud audit trails, vulnerability scan outputs, email security logs, network infrastructure logs, identity management systems, incident response records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=privacy sourcetype="oaic:ndb" earliest=-365d
| eval eligible=if(serious_harm="true",1,0)
| eval filed=strptime(filed_oaic_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| where eligible=1 AND isnull(filed)
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest CrowdStrike policy posture via TA 5082 and map hosts to NDB and NZISM tiers. (2) Tune alerts with ACSD Essential Eight and APRA-aligned severities. (3) Coordinate privacy and security ownership for dual-reporting workflows. (4) Monthly export for privacy officer and prudential working groups.
- **Visualization:** Single value (% compliant hosts), Bar chart (fails by control), Table (worst hosts).
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint, Vulnerabilities
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.process_name, Processes.user, Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, endpoint protection logs, cloud audit trails, vulnerability scan outputs, email security logs, network infrastructure logs, identity management systems, incident response records
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode=4688 earliest=-24h
| stats count by New_Process_Name, host
| lookup asd_e8_scope.csv host OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="true" AND NOT match(New_Process_Name,"(?i)\\\\Program Files")
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest CrowdStrike policy posture via TA 5082 and map hosts to NDB and NZISM tiers. (2) Tune alerts with ACSD Essential Eight and APRA-aligned severities. - **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Endpoint.Processes by Processes.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/5082), [CIM: Endpoint](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Endpoint)

### UC-22.32.1 · LGPD consent management audit trail (Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD))
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD)
- **Value:** Supports Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD) compliance evidence for lgpd consent management audit trail in Splunk dashboards and scheduled reports.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Cloud audit trails, Windows Security Event Logs, vulnerability scan outputs, access control logs, encryption status reports, continuous monitoring feeds, incident response logs, privacy management platforms, law enforcement system logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cmp sourcetype="lgpd:consent" earliest=-90d
| stats latest(consent_status) as cs by data_subject_id, purpose_id
| where cs!="recorded"
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `amer_authorization_boundary.csv` with ATO, CUI, and CJIS scope flags. (2) Integrate POA&M exports via scheduled output and lookup refresh for ISSM workflows. (3) Separate LGPD and federal datasets with index- and role-level controls. (4) Quarterly control mapping workshop with ISSM, ISO, and privacy counsel.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (control family), Table (users and events), Single value (open findings).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.32.2 · Data subject rights fulfillment SLA monitoring (Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD))
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD)
- **Value:** Supports Lei Geral de Proteção de Dados (LGPD) compliance evidence for data subject rights fulfillment sla monitoring in Splunk dashboards and scheduled reports.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for AWS (1876), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Cloud Services (3110), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Cloud audit trails, Windows Security Event Logs, vulnerability scan outputs, access control logs, encryption status reports, continuous monitoring feeds, incident response logs, privacy management platforms, law enforcement system logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=ticketing sourcetype="snow:sc_req_item" cat_item="*LGPD*DSAR*" earliest=-180d
| eval opened=strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval closed=strptime(closed_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval age=round((now()-opened)/86400,0)
| where isnull(closed) AND age>15
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `amer_authorization_boundary.csv` with ATO, CUI, and CJIS scope flags. (2) Integrate POA&M exports via scheduled output and lookup refresh for ISSM workflows. (3) Separate LGPD and federal datasets with index- and role-level controls. (4) Quarterly control mapping workshop with ISSM, ISO, and privacy counsel.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (control family), Table (users and events), Single value (open findings).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

```spl
| stats latest(evidence_status) as es by practice_id
| where es!="complete"
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain `amer_authorization_boundary.csv` with ATO, CUI, and CJIS scope flags. (2) Integrate POA&M exports via scheduled output and lookup refresh for ISSM workflows. (3) Separate LGPD and federal datasets with index- and role-level controls. (4) Quarterly control mapping workshop with ISSM, ISO, and privacy counsel.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar (control family), Table (users and events), Single value (open findings).
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t latest(All_Changes.status) as agg_value from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object_category, All_Changes.user | sort - agg_value
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Change](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Change)

- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" EventCode IN (4624,4648) earliest=-24h
| lookup cjis_workstation.csv host OUTPUT cjis_flag

| eval mfa=if(match(AuthenticationPackage,"Negotiate"),1,0)
| where mfa=0
```

- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for AWS](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1876), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.33.1 · National cybersecurity standard compliance monitoring (NESA UAE IAS)
- **Criticality:** 🟢 Low
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NESA UAE IAS
- **Value:** Aligns NESA UAE IAS supervisory expectations with measurable Splunk evidence for national cybersecurity standard compliance monitoring.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk ITSI (1841), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** Windows Security Event Logs, network infrastructure logs, cloud audit trails, national cybersecurity platform feeds, financial system logs, data protection compliance records, incident response logs, security assessment evidence
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=audit sourcetype="nesa:control" earliest=-365d
| stats latest(evidence_status) as es by control_id, system
| where es!="Collected"
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize bilingual field extractions in props.conf where required. (2) Map IP ranges and business units to national critical infrastructure and banking registers. (3) Route SAMA, QCB, NESA, and PDPL alerts to regional bilingual runbooks. (4) Weekly leadership digest with regulator-specific views.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (sector), Table (top flows), Single value (policy denies).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

```spl
index=bank sourcetype="sama:csf:event" earliest=-30d
| eval reported=strptime(reported_sama_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| eval detected=strptime(detected_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S")
| where isnull(reported) OR (reported-detected)>4*3600
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalize bilingual field extractions in props.conf where required. (2) Map IP ranges and business units to national critical infrastructure and banking registers. (3) Route SAMA, QCB, NESA, and PDPL alerts to regional bilingual runbooks. (4) Weekly leadership digest with regulator-specific views.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (sector), Table (top flows), Single value (policy denies).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk ITSI](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/1841), [CIM: Network_Traffic](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Network_Traffic)

(3) Route SAMA, QCB, NESA, and PDPL alerts to regional bilingual runbooks. (4) Weekly leadership digest with regulator-specific views.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart (sector), Table (top flows), Single value (policy denies).
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Network_Traffic.All_Traffic by All_Traffic.action, All_Traffic.src, All_Traffic.dest, All_Traffic.dest_port | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.34.1 · SWIFT secure zone logical isolation monitoring (SWIFT CSCF mandatory)
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SWIFT CSP
- **Value:** Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for swift secure zone logical isolation monitoring ahead of independent assessment.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=swift sourcetype="swift:zone:flow" OR index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" tag=swift earliest=-24h
| stats sum(bytes) as b by src_zone, dest_zone
| where src_zone!="SWIFT_DMZ" AND dest_zone="SWIFT_SECURE"
| sort - b
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Segment SWIFT CSP evidence into dedicated indexes with strict role-based access. (2) Join operators to HR screening and vendor risk lookups refreshed weekly. (3) Validate MFA and PAM sourcetypes are CIM-mapped for Authentication models. (4) Retain evidence for at least 13 months aligned to attestation cycles.
- **Visualization:** Table (operators), Single value (sessions), Bar chart (CSCF controls).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

- **SPL:**
```spl
index=wineventlog sourcetype="WinEventLog:Security" host="SWIFT-*" EventCode IN (4720,4722,4738) earliest=-24h

```

- **Data Sources:** SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=pacs sourcetype="badge:swipe" building="SWIFT_OPS" earliest=-24h
| join type=left user [| search index=swift sourcetype="swift:operator:login" earliest=-24d | stats latest(_time) as last_swift by user]
| eval gap=now()-last_swift
| where gap>7776000
| table user, last_swift
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Segment SWIFT CSP evidence into dedicated indexes with strict role-based access. (2) Join operators to HR screening and vendor risk lookups refreshed weekly. (3) Validate MFA and PAM sourcetypes are CIM-mapped for Authentication models. (4) Retain evidence for at least 13 months aligned to attestation cycles.
- **Visualization:** Table (operators), Single value (sessions), Bar chart (CSCF controls).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.

### UC-22.34.4 · Operator MFA and session integrity monitoring (SWIFT CSCF mandatory)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SWIFT CSP
- **Value:** Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for operator mfa and session integrity monitoring ahead of independent assessment.

- **Data Sources:** SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cyberark sourcetype="cyberark:pta" OR index=o365 sourcetype="ms:o365:management" earliest=-7d
| stats count by user, action, src
| search user="swift*" (action="Logon" OR Operation="UserLoggedIn")
| eval mfa=if(match(_raw,"MFA"),1,0)
| where mfa=0
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Segment SWIFT CSP evidence into dedicated indexes with strict role-based access. (2) Join operators to HR screening and vendor risk lookups refreshed weekly. (3) Validate MFA and PAM sourcetypes are CIM-mapped for Authentication models. (4) Retain evidence for at least 13 months aligned to attestation cycles.
- **Visualization:** Table (operators), Single value (sessions), Bar chart (CSCF controls).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

- **SPL:**

```spl
index=app sourcetype="backoffice:transfer" OR index=mq sourcetype="ibm:mq:error" earliest=-24h

```

### UC-22.34.7 · External transmission data protection monitoring (SWIFT CSCF advisory)
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔴 Expert
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SWIFT CSP
- **Value:** Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for external transmission data protection monitoring ahead of independent assessment.

- **Data Sources:** SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=network sourcetype="pan:traffic" OR index=ssl sourcetype="stream:tls" earliest=-24h
| stats dc(cipher) as ciphers, values(ssl_version) as tls by dest_ip, dest_port
| where dest_port=443 AND mvfind(tls,"TLS1_0")>=0
| lookup swift_external_endpoints.csv dest_ip OUTPUT approved
| where approved!="true"
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Segment SWIFT CSP evidence into dedicated indexes with strict role-based access. (2) Join operators to HR screening and vendor risk lookups refreshed weekly. (3) Validate MFA and PAM sourcetypes are CIM-mapped for Authentication models. (4) Retain evidence for at least 13 months aligned to attestation cycles.
- **Visualization:** Table (operators), Single value (sessions), Bar chart (CSCF controls).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.dest | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

### UC-22.34.12 · Counterparty CSP score monitoring for correspondent risk (SWIFT KYC-SA)
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SWIFT CSP
- **Value:** Produces SWIFT Customer Security Programme evidence for counterparty csp score monitoring for correspondent risk ahead of independent assessment.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security (263), Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for CyberArk (4295), Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757), Splunk Common Information Model Add-on (1621)
- **Data Sources:** SWIFT Alliance/Lite2 transaction logs, operator session audit trails, network infrastructure logs, endpoint security logs, privileged access management, change management records, vulnerability scan outputs, multi-factor authentication logs
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vendor sourcetype="swift:csp_score" earliest=-30d
| stats latest(score) as s, latest(trend) as t by counterparty_bic
| where s<75 OR t="down"
| sort s
| head 200
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Segment SWIFT CSP evidence into dedicated indexes with strict role-based access. (2) Join operators to HR screening and vendor risk lookups refreshed weekly. (3) Validate MFA and PAM sourcetypes are CIM-mapped for Authentication models. (4) Retain evidence for at least 13 months aligned to attestation cycles.
- **Visualization:** Table (operators), Single value (sessions), Bar chart (CSCF controls).
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **CIM SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication by Authentication.action, Authentication.user, Authentication.src | sort - count
```

- **Known false positives:** Administrative tasks, scheduled jobs or platform updates can match this pattern — correlate with change management, maintenance windows and user role before raising severity.
- **References:** [Splunk Enterprise Security](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/263), [Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/742), [Splunk Add-on for CyberArk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/4295), [CIM: Authentication](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/CIM/latest/User/Authentication)

---
