### 22.35 — additional UCs (Evidence continuity and log integrity)

### UC-22.35.4 · Log signing chain integrity — cryptographic signature drift on evidence archive
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1070
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Replaces the quarterly 'we sampled 10 logs and checked hashes' audit procedure with a continuous control whose output is either 'zero breaks for the period' or a named remediation record. This is the evidence regulators actually ask for.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Evidence archive signature-verification events (e.g., sourcetype=archive:signer emitted by the signer service), WORM repository state REST API, KMS signing audit logs.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=compliance sourcetype=archive:signer earliest=-1h
| eval block_parent_hash=coalesce(parent_hash,"GENESIS")
| stats values(block_hash) as block_hashes latest(expected_parent_hash) as expected_parent by block_id archive_id
| where expected_parent!=block_parent_hash AND block_parent_hash!="GENESIS"
| table archive_id block_id block_parent_hash expected_parent block_hashes
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure the archive signer emits a per-block event with block_id, block_hash, parent_hash, and expected_parent_hash; (2) schedule the UC hourly; (3) route hits to compliance_summary; (4) SOAR auto-opens a P1 with the relevant archive owner; (5) run an automated rebuild drill monthly to ensure verification code itself is not silently broken.
- **Visualization:** Timeline of verified vs broken blocks, scatter of block_id × hash-mismatch severity, single-value '% of archive blocks verified (30d)'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Archive signer upgrades that rotate the hash function produce a one-off legitimate 'parent-missing' at the rotation boundary — record the rotation in archive_rotations.csv and allow-list by timestamp.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [45 CFR Part 164 — HIPAA](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164), [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1070 Indicator Removal](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1070/)

---

### UC-22.35.5 · Search-head audit-trail completeness — deleted or rewritten search jobs
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1070.006
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** HIPAA, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Gives the Splunk admin team a defensible 'every search run is accounted for' control rather than the weaker 'we have search logging enabled'.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** _audit (action=search), /services/search/jobs REST for current artifacts, dispatch directory state via Splunk REST.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=_audit action=search info=completed earliest=-24h
| stats values(artifact_id) as artifact_ids earliest(_time) as first_seen latest(_time) as last_seen by search_id user
| mvexpand artifact_ids
| join type=outer artifact_ids [| rest /services/search/jobs splunk_server=* | fields sid dispatchState | rename sid as artifact_ids]
| where isnull(dispatchState) AND (now()-last_seen)<86400
| table user search_id artifact_ids first_seen last_seen
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure _audit is retained for ≥90 days on the evidence cluster; (2) adjust dispatch_ttl per risk tier; (3) schedule every 15 min; (4) on hit, SOAR opens a P2 for the Splunk admin team.
- **Visualization:** Time chart of job-vs-artifact reconciliation, table of missing artifacts by user, single-value 'search jobs with artifact present (7d)'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Cluster re-balancing or search-head restart can transiently remove dispatch artifacts before the next sync; tolerate with a 30-minute post-restart grace via maintenance_windows.csv.
- **References:** [45 CFR Part 164 — HIPAA](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164), [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [AICPA Trust Services Criteria](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1070.006 Indicator Removal: Timestomp](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1070/006/)

---

### 22.36 — additional UCs (Data subject rights fulfillment)

### UC-22.36.4 · DSAR identity-verification friction — failed-verification anomaly
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** CCPA, GDPR
- **Value:** Takes the single most common enforcement narrative ('the verification process itself denied the right') and turns it into a measurable metric.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** DSAR platform identity-verification events (OneTrust, TrustArc, ServiceNow Privacy), IdP step-up audit logs, manual review tickets.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=dsar sourcetype=onetrust:dsar OR sourcetype=servicenow:privacy action=verification earliest=-14d
| eval pass=if(result="success",1,0), fail=if(result="failure",1,0)
| stats sum(pass) as passes sum(fail) as failures values(ticket_id) as ticket_ids by subject_token
| where failures>=3 AND passes<1
| table subject_token failures ticket_ids
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure DSAR verification events emit subject_token and result; (2) run daily; (3) feed findings to a DPO review dashboard; (4) after a fixed threshold, escalate via SOAR for legal review.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart of verification failure rate per channel, table of repeat-failure subjects, single-value 'repeat-failure subjects (14d)'.
- **CIM Models:** Ticket_Management
- **Known false positives:** Genuine impersonation attempts will (correctly) produce multiple failures for the same subject_token; pair with IdP risk signals before escalating.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [California CCPA/CPRA](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displayText.xhtml?lawCode=CIV&division=3.&title=1.81.5.&part=4.&chapter=&article=)

---

### UC-22.36.5 · DSAR request-type mix anomaly — zero-deletion skew indicating broken workflow
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** CCPA, GDPR
- **Value:** Makes the common 'silent bug in the privacy portal' failure mode visible without waiting for a complaint.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** DSAR ticketing system request-type field, historic aggregates from compliance summary index.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=dsar sourcetype=onetrust:dsar OR sourcetype=servicenow:privacy earliest=-84d
| bin _time span=7d
| stats count by _time request_type
| eventstats sum(count) as total by _time
| eval ratio=count/total
| stats avg(ratio) as baseline, stdev(ratio) as baseline_sd latest(ratio) as current by request_type
| eval drift=abs(current-baseline)
| where drift>(3*baseline_sd) AND baseline_sd>0
| table request_type baseline current drift baseline_sd
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure DSAR events emit a canonical request_type from an allow-list; (2) run weekly; (3) on fire, open a DPO + engineering review ticket.
- **Visualization:** Stacked area of request-type mix over 12 weeks, bar chart of current-vs-baseline deltas, single-value 'drift categories this week'.
- **CIM Models:** Ticket_Management
- **Known false positives:** Seasonal campaigns (e.g., year-end 'delete my data' reminders) legitimately shift the mix; maintain a known-campaign lookup to dampen the signal for those windows.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [California CCPA/CPRA](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displayText.xhtml?lawCode=CIV&division=3.&title=1.81.5.&part=4.&chapter=&article=)

---

### 22.37 — additional UCs (Consent lifecycle and lawful basis)

### UC-22.37.4 · Purpose-limitation enforcement — processing not matching declared purpose
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1552
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** CCPA, GDPR, LGPD
- **Value:** Converts an otherwise-abstract regulatory principle into a continuously-measurable control with evidentiary value.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Application audit logs carrying the purpose-bearing access token, system-purpose catalogue in purpose_catalogue.csv.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=app_activity earliest=-1h token_purpose=*
| lookup purpose_catalogue.csv system_id OUTPUT allowed_purposes
| eval ok=if(match(allowed_purposes,token_purpose),1,0)
| where ok=0
| stats count values(user) as users values(subject_token) as subjects by system_id token_purpose allowed_purposes
| table system_id token_purpose allowed_purposes count users subjects
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Mint OAuth/OIDC tokens with a purpose claim; (2) publish purpose_catalogue.csv keyed by system_id; (3) run hourly; (4) alert DPO on mismatches and drive remediation via SOAR.
- **Visualization:** Sankey of token-purpose → system, heatmap of mismatches by system, single-value 'mismatch events (24h)'.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **Known false positives:** Shared service accounts without per-request purpose context cannot be evaluated; filter them out via service_account_allowlist.csv.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [California CCPA/CPRA](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displayText.xhtml?lawCode=CIV&division=3.&title=1.81.5.&part=4.&chapter=&article=), [LGPD (Brazil Law 13,709/2018)](https://www.planalto.gov.br/ccivil_03/_ato2015-2018/2018/lei/l13709.htm), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1552 Unsecured Credentials](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1552/)

---

### UC-22.37.5 · IAB TCF consent string mutation without user interaction
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1557
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** CCPA, GDPR
- **Value:** Catches a pattern that has surfaced repeatedly in EDPB enforcement decisions (Meta, Google ad-tech) before it becomes a supervisory finding.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Web access logs capturing Set-Cookie: euconsent-v2, CMP interaction events (onetrust:cmp), client-side analytics.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=web sourcetype=access_combined earliest=-1h euconsent_v2=*
| sort 0 session_id _time
| streamstats current=f last(euconsent_v2) as prior_consent by session_id
| where isnotnull(prior_consent) AND euconsent_v2!=prior_consent
| join type=outer session_id _time [search index=cmp sourcetype=onetrust:cmp action=interaction earliest=-1h | fields session_id _time | rename _time as interaction_time]
| where isnull(interaction_time) OR abs(_time-interaction_time)>60
| table _time session_id prior_consent euconsent_v2
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Preserve Set-Cookie headers in access logs; (2) ingest CMP interaction events; (3) run hourly; (4) on hit, freeze the vendor via ad-tech allow-list and open a DPO ticket.
- **Visualization:** Table of mutating sessions by vendor, bar chart of mutations per ad-tech partner, single-value 'sessions with genuine consent (24h)'.
- **CIM Models:** Web
- **Known false positives:** CMP token refreshes (same purpose mask, new expiry) can legitimately rewrite the TC string; allow-list based on purpose-bitmap equality.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [California CCPA/CPRA](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displayText.xhtml?lawCode=CIV&division=3.&title=1.81.5.&part=4.&chapter=&article=), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1557 Adversary-in-the-Middle](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1557/)

---

### 22.38 — additional UCs (Cross-border transfer controls)

### UC-22.38.4 · Transfer Impact Assessment currency — stale Schrems II assessments
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** GDPR, UK-GDPR
- **Value:** Converts a calendar reminder into a demonstrable control with evidence per-transfer.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** TIA register (tia_register.csv), transfer_register.csv, DPO review events.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup tia_register.csv
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_reviewed,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>cadence_days
| lookup transfer_register.csv tia_id OUTPUT transfer_id destination
| table tia_id destination last_reviewed age_days cadence_days transfer_id
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain tia_register.csv; (2) run weekly; (3) on miss, DPO re-opens the TIA review workflow.
- **Visualization:** Table of stale TIAs with remediation owner, bar chart of TIAs by age bucket, single-value 'TIAs within cadence'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** TIAs under active review (with a workflow_state='in_review' flag) should be excluded via workflow_state.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [ICO International Transfers Guidance](https://ico.org.uk/for-organisations/uk-gdpr-guidance-and-resources/international-transfers/)

---

### UC-22.38.5 · Bulk regulated-data export targeting non-adequate jurisdiction
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1567
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, GDPR
- **Value:** Turns post-hoc 'where did our customer dataset go?' incident forensics into real-time prevention.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** DB export audit logs, cloud storage PUT events, DLP bulk-operation classifications, sanctioned_transfer_jurisdictions.csv.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=db_audit action=export OR (index=cloud_storage action=PutObject bytes>1073741824) earliest=-10m
| lookup data_classification.csv object_key OUTPUT classification regulated_scope
| where isnotnull(regulated_scope)
| `get_geolocation(dest_ip)`
| lookup sanctioned_transfer_jurisdictions.csv country as dest_country OUTPUT scc_active adequacy_decision
| where isnull(scc_active) AND isnull(adequacy_decision)
| table _time user object_key classification regulated_scope dest_country bytes
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure DB and cloud storage layers emit size + destination; (2) maintain classification and jurisdiction lookups; (3) schedule every 10 min; (4) SOAR auto-blocks destination if exception policy permits.
- **Visualization:** World-map of bulk exports, table of findings with destination geography, single-value 'bulk exports within sanctioned geographies'.
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **Known false positives:** Bona-fide DR or migration operations authorised under a dedicated exception ID must be logged to a migrations.csv allow-list.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — DORA](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1567 Exfiltration Over Web Service](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1567/)

---

### 22.39 — additional UCs (Incident notification timeliness)

### UC-22.39.4 · Cross-regulator consistency — divergent material facts across submissions
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, GDPR, NIS2
- **Value:** Prevents the 'regulator cross-reference' problem that has escalated several prominent supervisory cases from technical fines to broader corporate sanction.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** regulator:submission sourcetype records with normalised material-fact fields (affected_subjects, data_classes, root_cause).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=notifications sourcetype=regulator:submission earliest=-30d
| stats values(affected_subjects) as subjects_set values(data_classes) as data_set dc(affected_subjects) as subjects_uniques dc(data_classes) as data_uniques by incident_id
| where subjects_uniques>1 OR data_uniques>1
| table incident_id subjects_set data_set
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalise submission events to carry canonical material-fact fields; (2) run 24h after any new submission for the incident_id; (3) alert DPO + legal if divergence found.
- **Visualization:** Table of divergent incidents, bar chart of divergence magnitude, single-value 'incidents with consistent submissions'.
- **CIM Models:** Ticket_Management
- **Known false positives:** Genuine refinement of scope between the initial 72h notice and the 30-day follow-up is legitimate and expected; parametrise a tolerance window.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — NIS2](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — DORA](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj)

---

### UC-22.39.5 · Regulator-portal authentication failure during submission window
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1110
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** GDPR, NIS2
- **Value:** Converts a class of silent failures (an expired portal API key, a stale MFA token) that has materialised in past incidents into an always-on monitoring pattern.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** IdP audit logs (okta:audit, azuread:audit), incident_register.csv with open_status flag.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=idp sourcetype=okta:audit OR sourcetype=azuread:audit earliest=-15m result=failure
| search user IN (`regulator_service_accounts`)
| join user [| inputlookup incident_register.csv | where status="open" | fields user=regulator_user incident_id]
| table _time user incident_id src_ip failure_reason
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain a named macro regulator_service_accounts listing the IdP principals; (2) run every 5-10 min; (3) SOAR pages the DPO on hit.
- **Visualization:** Time chart of portal authentications by account, table of failures, single-value 'successful authentications in open windows'.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **Known false positives:** Portal-initiated credential rotation can cause a single expected failure; require ≥2 failures in 5 min.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — NIS2](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1110 Brute Force](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1110/)

---

### 22.40 — additional UCs (Privileged access evidence)

### UC-22.40.4 · Standing-privilege credential vaulting drift — admin accounts outside PAM
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078.003
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST-800-53, PCI-DSS, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Replaces the quarterly 'access-review export + spreadsheet join' with a daily evidence feed.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** IdP privileged-group membership (Azure AD, Okta, LDAP), PAM vault inventory (CyberArk, BeyondTrust, Delinea).
- **SPL:**
```spl
| rest /services/identity/privileged-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group
| join type=outer user [| rest /services/pam/vault-inventory splunk_server=* | fields user vault_id]
| where isnull(vault_id)
| stats values(group) as groups by user
| table user groups
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Expose IdP privileged groups and PAM vault inventory via REST; (2) run daily; (3) on miss, auto-open a PAM onboarding ticket with the CISO.
- **Visualization:** Table of drift accounts by group, bar chart of drift count by tenant, single-value '% of admins vaulted'.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Change
- **Known false positives:** Break-glass accounts are intentionally outside the vault; maintain breakglass_accounts.csv to allow-list.
- **References:** [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078.003 Valid Accounts: Local Accounts](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/003/)

---

### UC-22.40.5 · High-risk privileged-session command without JIT approval
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078.004
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST-800-53, PCI-DSS, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Makes 'we require JIT approval for dangerous commands' an observable, always-on control.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** PAM session keystroke/command logs, GRC JIT ticketing events, high-risk command catalogue (high_risk_commands.csv).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:command OR sourcetype=beyondtrust:command earliest=-5m
| lookup high_risk_commands.csv command_pattern AS command OUTPUT risk_class
| where risk_class IN ("destructive","mass-data","security-control")
| join session_id [search index=grc sourcetype=jit:approval earliest=-1h | fields session_id ticket_id approver approved_at]
| where isnull(ticket_id)
| table _time session_id user command risk_class target_host
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Curate high_risk_commands.csv with regex patterns; (2) instrument GRC JIT to emit session_id; (3) run every 5 min; (4) SOAR auto-terminates session on hit.
- **Visualization:** Table of unapproved high-risk commands, bar chart of commands by risk class, single-value 'high-risk commands approved (7d)'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Automated runbook executions need a runbook_id that looks like a JIT ticket; ensure runbook automation is integrated with the JIT ledger.
- **References:** [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078.004 Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/004/)

---

### 22.41 — additional UCs (Encryption and key management attestation)

### UC-22.41.4 · TLS downgrade / legacy-cipher handshake spike
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1562.006
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA, NIST-800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Catches silent downgrade issues introduced by load-balancer config drift before they become an audit finding or data-exposure incident.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** F5/NGINX/HAProxy TLS handshake logs, cloud-provider LB telemetry, TLS scanner longitudinal results.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=tls sourcetype=f5:tls OR sourcetype=nginx:tls OR sourcetype=haproxy:tls earliest=-1h
| eval legacy=if(match(protocol,"TLSv1\.(0|1)") OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT"),1,0)
| stats sum(legacy) as legacy_hs count as total_hs by host
| eval legacy_pct=round(legacy_hs/total_hs*100,2)
| where legacy_pct>0.5 AND total_hs>100
| table host legacy_hs total_hs legacy_pct
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure LBs emit handshake telemetry; (2) schedule hourly; (3) route hits to security ops; (4) block via config-as-code rollback.
- **Visualization:** Line chart of legacy-handshake percentage per endpoint, table of offending endpoints, single-value '% handshakes on strong TLS (24h)'.
- **CIM Models:** Certificates
- **Known false positives:** Legacy client populations (POS devices) can inflate TLS 1.0 usage in scope of a documented risk-acceptance; allow-list such endpoints.
- **References:** [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [45 CFR Part 164 — HIPAA](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.006 Impair Defenses: Indicator Blocking](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1562/006/)

---

### UC-22.41.5 · Key custodian SoD — same identity creates AND approves a key
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST-800-53, PCI-DSS, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Provides daily evidence of key-custodian SoD rather than the common annual sample.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** KMS audit logs (aws:kms, azure:keyvault, gcp:kms), HSM audit logs.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=kms earliest=-30m (action=CreateKey OR action=ScheduleKeyActivation)
| stats values(user) as users values(action) as actions by key_id
| eval distinct_users=mvcount(mvdedup(users))
| where distinct_users<2 AND mvcount(actions)>=2
| table key_id users actions
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest KMS audit logs; (2) run every 30 min; (3) escalate to the crypto custodian.
- **Visualization:** Table of SoD violations, bar chart of violations by key type, single-value 'SoD-compliant key operations (30d)'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Automated key provisioning pipelines that run under a single service account may trigger this UC; they must be structured as two-stage with distinct approver accounts.
- **References:** [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201)

---

### 22.42 — additional UCs (Change management and configuration baseline)

### UC-22.42.3 · Change rollback execution evidence — declared rollback vs actual
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** NIST-800-53, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Turns rollback-plan existence from a documentation artefact into a tested capability.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** ITSM CR records (ServiceNow), CI/CD pipeline audit (rollback_test events).
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup itsm_changes.csv
| where rollback_plan_required="true"
| lookup rollback_tests.csv change_id OUTPUT last_tested
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_tested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_tested) OR age_days>180
| table change_id rollback_plan_required last_tested age_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Export CR metadata to itsm_changes.csv; (2) have CI/CD emit rollback_test events; (3) run weekly; (4) on miss, schedule a rollback rehearsal.
- **Visualization:** Aging bar chart of changes without rollback test, table of at-risk CRs, single-value 'CRs with tested rollback'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Emergency hotfix CRs may legitimately have a 'forward-fix-only' rollback strategy; annotate with rollback_type to exclude them.
- **References:** [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [AICPA Trust Services Criteria](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022), [PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201)

---

### UC-22.42.4 · CAB approval bypass — change pushed before scheduled window
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1562.001
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIST-800-53, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Turns CAB approval from a calendar event into an operational control with per-change evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** CI/CD pipeline apply events tagged with change_id, ServiceNow CR schedule data.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=change sourcetype=prod:apply earliest=-1h
| join change_id [search index=itsm sourcetype=servicenow:change | fields change_id window_start window_end approval_type]
| eval start_s=strptime(window_start,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"), end_s=strptime(window_end,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
| where approval_type!="emergency" AND (_time<start_s OR _time>end_s)
| table _time change_id applied_by window_start window_end
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Force CI/CD pipelines to emit change_id; (2) ingest CR schedule data; (3) run hourly; (4) SOAR auto-freezes the pipeline on hit.
- **Visualization:** Table of out-of-window applies, bar chart of offending pipelines, single-value '% of changes applied in window'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Timezone-normalised window_start/window_end is critical; a TZ mismatch will falsely flag correct changes.
- **References:** [AICPA Trust Services Criteria](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.001 Impair Defenses: Disable Tools](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1562/001/)

---

### UC-22.42.5 · Infrastructure-as-code drift — applied state diverges from merged plan
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1562.001
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** ISO-27001, NIST-800-53, SOC-2
- **Value:** Turns 'we use Terraform' into a continuously-verified statement.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Terraform/OpenTofu state snapshots, cloud provider inventory (AWS Config, Azure Resource Graph, GCP Asset Inventory), CI/CD merge events.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=iac sourcetype=tf:state_snapshot earliest=-30m
| stats latest(plan_checksum) as expected_checksum by resource_id
| join resource_id [search index=cloud sourcetype=aws:config OR sourcetype=azure:rg OR sourcetype=gcp:asset earliest=-30m | stats latest(state_checksum) as actual_checksum by resource_id]
| where expected_checksum!=actual_checksum
| table resource_id expected_checksum actual_checksum
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Export per-plan checksums from CI on merge; (2) enable cloud inventory export; (3) run every 30 min; (4) SOAR auto-remediates via `terraform plan && apply` if allowed.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap of resources × drift, table of drifting resources, single-value '% of resources matching plan'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Cloud-provider control-plane metadata changes (timestamps, auto-generated IDs) can fire; exclude from checksum calculation at plan-emission time.
- **References:** [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [AICPA Trust Services Criteria](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022), [ISO/IEC 27001:2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/82875.html), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1562.001 Impair Defenses: Disable Tools](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1562/001/)

---

### 22.43 — additional UCs (Vulnerability management and patch SLAs)

### UC-22.43.3 · Internet-facing asset × unpatched critical CVE
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1190
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** NIS2, NIST-800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Eliminates the gap between scanner output and risk-based patch-SLA tracking.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Vulnerability scanner feeds, external-facing inventory (Shodan, Censys, AWS ALB/NLB, cloud LBs).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vuln earliest=-24h cvss_v3_base>=9.0
| lookup external_asset_inventory.csv host OUTPUT external_exposed
| where external_exposed="true"
| stats min(_time) as first_seen values(cve) as cves by host
| eval age_days=round((now()-first_seen)/86400,0)
| where age_days>0
| table host cves age_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain external_asset_inventory.csv refreshed daily from LB config or external attack-surface tooling; (2) run hourly; (3) SOAR auto-escalates on hit.
- **Visualization:** Table of Internet-facing hosts with critical CVEs, bar chart of patch-age buckets, single-value 'Internet-facing hosts fully patched'.
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **Known false positives:** Honeypot / deliberately-exposed research assets can be excluded via an inventory annotation.
- **References:** [Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — NIS2](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1190 Exploit Public-Facing Application](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1190/)

---

### UC-22.43.4 · Scanner coverage gap — regulated hosts without a recent scan
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO-27001, NIST-800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Surfaces silent scanner onboarding failures before they become audit findings.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Vulnerability scanner inventory, asset_inventory.csv with regulated flag, per-tier SLA lookup.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup asset_inventory.csv
| where regulated="true"
| lookup scan_history.csv host OUTPUT last_scan
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_scan,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| lookup tier_sla.csv tier OUTPUT scan_sla_days
| where isnull(last_scan) OR days_since>scan_sla_days
| table tier host last_scan days_since scan_sla_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain asset_inventory.csv; (2) export scanner state to scan_history.csv; (3) run daily.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap of hosts × days-since-scan, table of offenders, single-value 'regulated coverage'.
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **Known false positives:** Hosts mid-onboarding should carry an onboarding_date; allow-list until scan credentials propagate.
- **References:** [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [ISO/IEC 27001:2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/82875.html)

---

### UC-22.43.5 · SBOM vendor-component CVE exposure
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1195.002
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** EU-CRA, NIS2, NIST-800-53
- **Value:** Closes the gap between the day a CVE is published and the day a vendor responds — crucial for CRA and NIS2 supply-chain narratives.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Vendor SBOMs (CycloneDX/SPDX), CVE feed (NVD, vendor advisories).
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup vendor_sbom.csv
| lookup cve_feed.csv component version OUTPUT cve cvss_v3_base
| where isnotnull(cve) AND cvss_v3_base>=7.0
| stats values(cve) as cves, max(cvss_v3_base) as max_cvss by vendor component version
| table vendor component version cves max_cvss
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ingest vendor SBOMs from the procurement workflow; (2) subscribe to NVD feed into cve_feed.csv; (3) run daily; (4) SOAR opens a case per vendor-affected component.
- **Visualization:** Table of exposed components, bar chart of CVE count by vendor, single-value 'SBOM-indexed components with no open CVE'.
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **Known false positives:** Version ranges expressed in SPDX are sometimes imprecise; maintain a normalisation pre-step to avoid double counting.
- **References:** [Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — NIS2](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [EU Cyber Resilience Act](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/cyber-resilience-act), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1195.002 Supply Chain Compromise: Compromise Software Supply Chain](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1195/002/)

---

### 22.44 — additional UCs (Third-party and supply-chain risk)

### UC-22.44.4 · Vendor access telemetry — principals active outside contracted hours/geos
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078.004
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, NIS2, NIST-800-53
- **Value:** Materialises contractual access controls into operational telemetry.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** IdP authentication events for named vendor principals, vendor contracts lookup with contracted hours/geos.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=idp earliest=-1h (sourcetype=okta:audit OR sourcetype=azuread:audit) result=success
| search user IN (`vendor_principals`)
| `get_geolocation(src_ip)`
| lookup vendor_contract.csv user OUTPUT allowed_hours allowed_countries
| eval hour=strftime(_time,"%H"), weekday=strftime(_time,"%A"), window_ok=if(match(allowed_hours,weekday.":".hour),1,0), geo_ok=if(match(allowed_countries,country),1,0)
| where window_ok=0 OR geo_ok=0
| table _time user country hour weekday allowed_countries allowed_hours
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain vendor_principals macro; (2) extract contracted windows/geos from contracts and publish to vendor_contract.csv; (3) run hourly.
- **Visualization:** World map of vendor logins with off-geo markers, bar chart of vendors over time, single-value 'vendor logins within contract (24h)'.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **Known false positives:** Planned after-hours maintenance windows need a pre-declared exception with vendor_contract.exception_id.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — DORA](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — NIS2](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078.004 Valid Accounts: Cloud Accounts](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/004/)

---

### UC-22.44.5 · SBOM attestation completeness — critical vendors without signed SBOM
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** EU-CRA, NIS2, NIST-800-53
- **Value:** Makes the CRA/NIS2 SBOM requirement a tracked operational metric.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Vendor SBOM register (vendor_sbom.csv), signature-validation tooling outputs, VRM criticality.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup vrm_attestations.csv
| where criticality="critical"
| lookup vendor_sbom_attestations.csv vendor OUTPUT sbom_sha256 signed signed_by signed_at
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(signed_at,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(sbom_sha256) OR signed!="true" OR age_days>180
| table vendor criticality sbom_sha256 signed signed_by age_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Onboard vendor SBOMs through procurement workflow; (2) validate signatures with sigstore or equivalent; (3) run weekly.
- **Visualization:** Table of at-risk vendors, bar chart of signature freshness, single-value 'critical vendors with signed SBOM'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Vendors mid-re-signing can appear stale; allow a 7-day grace after signed_attempted_at.
- **References:** [EU Cyber Resilience Act](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/policies/cyber-resilience-act), [Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — NIS2](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final)

---

### 22.45 — additional UCs (Backup integrity and recovery testing)

### UC-22.45.4 · Backup repository TLS posture — aged or weak-cipher endpoints
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1040
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** DORA, HIPAA, NIST-800-53
- **Value:** Closes a gap pattern observed in DORA inspections where the backup plane had older TLS than the production plane.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** TLS scanner output for backup endpoint inventory.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=tls sourcetype=tls:scan earliest=-24h
| search host IN (`backup_repository_endpoints`)
| eval days_to_expiry=round((expires-now())/86400,0)
| where days_to_expiry<45 OR protocol="TLSv1.0" OR protocol="TLSv1.1" OR match(cipher,"3DES|RC4|NULL|DES|EXPORT")
| table host days_to_expiry protocol cipher
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Inventory backup endpoints; (2) scan daily; (3) on finding, ticket to platform engineering.
- **Visualization:** Table of endpoints with weak posture, time chart of scan results, single-value '% of backup endpoints with strong TLS'.
- **CIM Models:** Certificates
- **Known false positives:** Appliance-managed endpoints with vendor-pinned TLS libraries may need a supply-chain exception.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — DORA](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [45 CFR Part 164 — HIPAA](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1040 Network Sniffing](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1040/)

---

### UC-22.45.5 · Business-continuity rehearsal evidence — BCP/DR exercise execution logged
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** DORA, NIS2, NIST-800-53
- **Value:** Converts a calendar item into evidence with a signed attestation per rehearsal.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** BCP rehearsal ticketing system, signed rehearsal attestations.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup business_units.csv
| lookup bcp_rehearsals.csv bu_id OUTPUT last_rehearsal_at signed_by
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_rehearsal_at,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_rehearsal_at) OR days_since>cadence_days
| table bu_id owner last_rehearsal_at days_since cadence_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain business_units.csv with owner + cadence; (2) emit bcp_rehearsals.csv after each exercise with a signed_by; (3) run quarterly.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap of BU × days-since-rehearsal, table of at-risk BUs, single-value 'BUs within cadence'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** New BUs need an onboarding_date carve-out until the first scheduled rehearsal.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — DORA](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — NIS2](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final)

---

### 22.46 — additional UCs (Training and awareness)

### UC-22.46.3 · Privileged-role specialist training — admins lacking annual deep-training
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** HIPAA, NIST-800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Makes the 'role-based training' requirement evidence-backed rather than policy-on-paper.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** LMS specialist-course completions, PAM vault inventory with owner mapping.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| rest /services/pam/vault-inventory splunk_server=*
| fields user account_type
| where account_type="privileged"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=priv_security_training earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user account_type last_completion
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag PAM vault entries with the owner; (2) map course completions to users in the LMS; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-enrol overdue owners.
- **Visualization:** Table of overdue admins, bar chart of departments, single-value 'admins with current specialist training'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** New privileged users within 30 days of onboarding have a grace window; allow-list via onboarding_date.
- **References:** [45 CFR Part 164 — HIPAA](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164), [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final)

---

### UC-22.46.4 · Tabletop rehearsal evidence — IR plan exercise frequency
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, NIS2, NIST-800-53
- **Value:** Shifts IR readiness evidence from 'we rehearse' to 'here is the per-playbook cadence record'.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** IR playbook register, rehearsal signed attestations.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup ir_playbooks.csv
| lookup ir_rehearsals.csv playbook_id OUTPUT last_rehearsal signed_by
| eval days_since=round((now()-strptime(last_rehearsal,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where isnull(last_rehearsal) OR days_since>cadence_days
| table playbook_id owner last_rehearsal days_since cadence_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain ir_playbooks.csv with cadence; (2) emit rehearsal completion records; (3) run monthly.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap of playbook × days-since-rehearsal, single-value '% of playbooks within cadence'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Newly-authored playbooks need an onboarding grace window.
- **References:** [Directive (EU) 2022/2555 — NIS2](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 — DORA](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final)

---

### UC-22.46.5 · Developer data-handling training — prod-access engineers lacking training
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** GDPR, HIPAA, ISO-27001
- **Value:** Links IdP posture to training posture — the evidence auditors actually ask for.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** IdP prod-access group membership, LMS completion records for DPO-approved course ids.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| rest /services/identity/prod-access-groups splunk_server=*
| fields user group scope
| where scope="regulated"
| join user [search index=lms sourcetype=completion course_id=data_handling_v* earliest=-365d | stats max(_time) as last_completion by user]
| where (now()-last_completion)>31536000 OR isnull(last_completion)
| table user group scope last_completion
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag IdP groups for prod access with regulated scope; (2) publish DPO-approved course IDs; (3) run weekly; (4) auto-revoke access after configured overdue window.
- **Visualization:** Table of engineers without training, bar chart by team, single-value 'prod-access engineers with current training'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Freshly-onboarded engineers in the 30-day grace window should be allow-listed by onboarding_date.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [45 CFR Part 164 — HIPAA](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164), [ISO/IEC 27001:2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/82875.html)

### 22.47 — additional UCs (Control testing evidence freshness)

### UC-22.47.3 · Control owner attestation freshness
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** ISO-27001, NIST-800-53, SOC-2
- **Value:** Delivers the internal-audit team a continuous view of 'who has signed what'.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** GRC owner attestation ledger, control_inventory.csv.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup control_inventory.csv
| lookup owner_attestations.csv control_id OUTPUT last_attested signed_by
| eval age_days=round((now()-strptime(last_attested,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| where age_days>attestation_cadence_days OR isnull(last_attested)
| table control_id owner last_attested age_days attestation_cadence_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Emit owner_attestations.csv from GRC workflow; (2) run weekly.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap of control × age buckets, table of overdue controls, single-value '% of controls with fresh attestation'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** New controls in onboarding need a grace window.
- **References:** [AICPA Trust Services Criteria](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [ISO/IEC 27001:2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/82875.html)

---

### UC-22.47.4 · Evidence-pack drift — auditor-facing vs pre-production evidence
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1070
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Protects the integrity of the evidence that underpins the external audit.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Evidence-pack build pipeline, publication manifest, auditor-delivery signature events.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=evidence_pack:publish earliest=-30d
| stats latest(sha256) as published_sha latest(pack_id) as pack by _time
| join pack [search index=grc sourcetype=evidence_pack:preprod | stats latest(sha256) as preprod_sha by pack]
| where published_sha!=preprod_sha
| table pack published_sha preprod_sha
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Sign both preprod and published packs; (2) run post-publish; (3) on mismatch, alert internal audit and QA pipeline.
- **Visualization:** Table of mismatches, single-value 'packs with matching SHA (30d)'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Legitimate last-mile redactions can produce intended SHA changes; capture them in a redaction_log.csv.
- **References:** [AICPA Trust Services Criteria](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022), [PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1070 Indicator Removal](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1070/)

---

### UC-22.47.5 · Continuous control monitoring anomaly — failure-rate trending up
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** ISO-27001, NIST-800-53, SOC-2
- **Value:** Gives internal audit a quantitative view of control health rather than a point-in-time pass/fail.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** GRC continuous-control-monitoring events (pass/fail per execution).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=ccm:execution earliest=-90d
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count(eval(result="fail")) as fails count as total by _time control_id
| eval fail_rate=fails/total
| eventstats avg(fail_rate) as avg7d by control_id
| stats avg(fail_rate) as baseline stdev(fail_rate) as baseline_sd latest(fail_rate) as current latest(avg7d) as avg7d by control_id
| where avg7d > baseline+3*baseline_sd
| table control_id baseline baseline_sd avg7d current
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Instrument CCM per control; (2) run daily; (3) open a deep-dive ticket for anomalies.
- **Visualization:** Line charts of failure rate with baseline bands, table of controls above threshold, single-value 'controls within baseline'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Controls with naturally bimodal distributions (business-hours only) benefit from a bucketed baseline.
- **References:** [AICPA Trust Services Criteria](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [ISO/IEC 27001:2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/82875.html)

---

### 22.48 — additional UCs (Segregation of duties enforcement)

### UC-22.48.3 · Developer-to-production SoD — same developer submits AND approves merge
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO-27001, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Makes a core deployment-pipeline SoD requirement a continuously-enforced technical control.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Git provider audit logs (GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket), CI/CD deployment events.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=git sourcetype=github:audit OR sourcetype=gitlab:audit OR sourcetype=bitbucket:audit action=merge earliest=-1h
| search target_branch IN (main,master,release*)
| where author=approver
| table _time repo target_branch author approver commit_sha
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure Git provider captures author + approver; (2) run hourly; (3) on fire, revert merge via pipeline automation.
- **Visualization:** Table of violations, bar chart by repo, single-value 'merges SoD-compliant (30d)'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Solo-maintainer repos legitimately have author=approver; these must carry a risk-acceptance annotation.
- **References:** [PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [AICPA Trust Services Criteria](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022), [ISO/IEC 27001:2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/82875.html), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078 Valid Accounts](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/)

---

### UC-22.48.4 · Financial SoD — same identity posts AND approves a journal entry
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Provides continuous evidence of a universally-audited SOX control.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** ERP financial-journal audit events (SAP, Oracle E-Business, Workday Financials).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=erp sourcetype=sap:gl OR sourcetype=oracle:gl OR sourcetype=workday:gl action=post_and_approve earliest=-1h
| where poster=approver
| table _time entry_id poster approver amount
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure ERP emits both events; (2) run hourly; (3) on fire, escalate to CFO + controller.
- **Visualization:** Table of violations, bar chart by business unit, single-value 'SoD-compliant journals (30d)'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** System-generated entries (automated accruals) post under a service account that must not be counted; filter by account type.
- **References:** [PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [AICPA Trust Services Criteria](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/download/2017-trust-services-criteria-with-revised-points-of-focus-2022), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078 Valid Accounts](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/)

---

### UC-22.48.5 · Vendor-master SoD — same identity creates vendor AND approves payment
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** PCI-DSS, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Continuously enforces a control most organisations only sample.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** ERP vendor-master events, AP payment approval events.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=erp sourcetype=sap:vendor_master OR sourcetype=oracle:vendor_master action=create earliest=-30d
| stats min(_time) as created_at values(created_by) as creators by vendor_id
| join vendor_id [search index=erp sourcetype=*:ap_payment action=approve earliest=-30d | stats values(approved_by) as approvers by vendor_id]
| eval overlap=mvfilter(match(creators,approvers))
| where mvcount(overlap)>0
| table vendor_id created_at creators approvers overlap
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure ERP emits both events; (2) run hourly; (3) auto-freeze suspicious vendor payments via AP workflow.
- **Visualization:** Table of same-person violations, single-value 'vendors with SoD-compliant lifecycle'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Small-organisation setups with a single AP resource must carry a documented compensating control.
- **References:** [PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [PCI DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078 Valid Accounts](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/)

---

### 22.49 — additional UCs (Retention and disposal automation)

### UC-22.49.4 · Retention policy drift — system config vs policy catalogue
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** CCPA, GDPR, HIPAA
- **Value:** Turns retention policy adherence into a continuously-measured metric per system.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** System retention configuration snapshots, retention_policy.csv.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=datastore sourcetype=retention:config earliest=-1d
| lookup retention_policy.csv data_domain OUTPUT policy_days
| eval drift_days=abs(configured_days-policy_days)
| where drift_days>0
| table store data_domain configured_days policy_days drift_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Export retention configuration nightly; (2) maintain retention_policy.csv; (3) run daily.
- **Visualization:** Table of drift stores, bar chart of drift magnitude by data-domain, single-value 'stores aligned to policy'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Legal-hold flagged systems legitimately have longer retention; exclude via hold_in_force=true.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [California CCPA/CPRA](https://leginfo.legislature.ca.gov/faces/codes_displayText.xhtml?lawCode=CIV&division=3.&title=1.81.5.&part=4.&chapter=&article=), [45 CFR Part 164 — HIPAA](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164)

---

### UC-22.49.5 · Cryptographic erasure attestation — per-asset destruction evidence
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** GDPR, HIPAA, NIST-800-53
- **Value:** Solves the 'how do I prove we actually erased that data?' question that every DPO faces.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Asset disposal workflow events, KMS key destruction events.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=assets sourcetype=asset:disposal earliest=-7d method=cryptographic_erasure
| join asset_id [search index=kms action=DeleteKey earliest=-8d | fields asset_id key_id _time | rename _time as key_deleted_at]
| eval delta=abs(_time-key_deleted_at)
| where isnull(key_deleted_at) OR delta>86400
| table asset_id key_id _time key_deleted_at delta
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Tag asset-disposal events with asset_id and key_id; (2) ingest KMS DeleteKey; (3) run daily.
- **Visualization:** Table of orphan disposal events, single-value '% of disposals with matched key destruction'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Shared-key assets require manual validation; exclude via shared_key_assets.csv.
- **References:** [Regulation (EU) 2016/679 — GDPR](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj), [NIST SP 800-53 Rev. 5](https://csrc.nist.gov/pubs/sp/800/53/r5/upd1/final), [45 CFR Part 164 — HIPAA](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164)

---

### 22.3 — DORA (extended clauses)

### UC-22.3.41 · DORA Art.6 — ICT risk-management framework evidence: control catalogue drift detection
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1562
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Replaces the annual 'do we still have the framework' attestation with a continuous reconciliation — regulators under DORA Art.6 expect the framework to be 'soundly, comprehensively and well-documented', meaning drift must be detected and remediated, not just discovered at the next audit.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:control, sourcetype=servicenow:grc_control; `index=cmdb` sourcetype=cmdb:ci_ict_service; controlFamily catalogue lookup `dora_control_catalogue.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype IN (archer:control,servicenow:grc_control) earliest=-1d
| stats latest(control_family) AS control_family latest(state) AS state BY control_id ict_service
| lookup dora_control_catalogue.csv control_family OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR state="inactive"
| table ict_service control_id control_family state approved
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Export control inventory daily from Archer/ServiceNow GRC into Splunk; (2) maintain dora_control_catalogue.csv as the Board-approved baseline with approval date and approver; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) drift >0 opens a risk-register entry assigned to the CISO with 30-day remediation SLA; (5) quarterly sign-off by the ICT risk committee.
- **Visualization:** Table of drifted controls, timechart of drift count by day, single value 'days since last zero-drift state'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Catalogue refreshes legitimately introduce new control_family values until the GRC platform syncs — maintain a 24h grace window in the lookup's effective-from date.
- **References:** [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1562](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1562/)

---

### UC-22.3.42 · DORA Art.7 — ICT systems inventory completeness: unmanaged endpoints attached to financial services
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Availability, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078
- **Splunk Pillar:** IT Operations
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIS2
- **Value:** Turns the quarterly 'asset inventory reconciliation' into a continuous signal, closing the time window during which an unmanaged host can process financial transactions unnoticed.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows, Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux
- **Data Sources:** `index=endpoint` sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security / sourcetype=linux:audit; `index=cmdb` sourcetype=cmdb:ci_server; authoritative financial-service CMDB view `financial_services_servers.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count FROM datamodel=Authentication WHERE Authentication.app IN ("payments","trading","settlement") BY Authentication.dest
| rename Authentication.dest AS hostname
| lookup financial_services_servers.csv hostname OUTPUT asset_id owner team
| where isnull(asset_id)
| eval inventory_gap_reason="not in ICT asset inventory"
| table hostname inventory_gap_reason count
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Onboard auth data to CIM Authentication DM; (2) maintain financial_services_servers.csv from the ICT asset register nightly; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) hit opens a ServiceNow CMDB task to register the host; (5) SLA: resolve within 72h or isolate; (6) exclude build/imaging windows with an effective-from allowlist.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart of inventory gaps by financial service, table of hosts with count, single value 'hosts in gap'.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Inventory
- **Known false positives:** Short-lived CI/CD build runners used for payments tests may legitimately appear off-inventory — allow-list by hostname-pattern in financial_services_servers.csv with a 24h TTL.
- **References:** [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/)

---

### UC-22.3.43 · DORA Art.8 — ICT risk identification: newly discovered high-severity exposure on critical financial services
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1190
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Produces per-finding evidence that critical-service exposure is identified and logged within the DORA-aligned window, rather than relying on monthly scanner review meetings.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for Tenable, Splunk Add-on for Qualys
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln OR sourcetype=qualys:host; `index=cmdb` sourcetype=cmdb:ci_ict_service; DORA critical-service lookup `dora_critical_services.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN (tenable:sc:vuln,qualys:host) cvss>=7 earliest=-15m
| rename dest AS hostname
| lookup dora_critical_services.csv hostname OUTPUT ict_service criticality_tier
| where criticality_tier="critical"
| eval registration_sla_met=if(relative_time(now(),"-24h")<=_time,1,0)
| stats count BY ict_service cvss cve hostname registration_sla_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Onboard scanner output; (2) tag hosts with ict_service + criticality_tier nightly; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) each hit opens a risk-register entry (ServiceNow) within 24h; (5) weekly report to ICT risk committee; (6) breach of 24h SLA escalates to the Head of IR and Board/Audit Committee.
- **Visualization:** Timechart of new criticals per ICT service, table of unresolved findings, single value 'findings past 24h SLA'.
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **Known false positives:** Scanner re-baselines after signature updates can produce a one-time spike of 're-discovered' findings — cross-reference against first_seen and exclude.
- **References:** [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1190](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1190/)

---

### UC-22.3.44 · DORA Art.17 — ICT incident classification timeliness: major-incident clock evidence
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security, Availability
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1090
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIS2
- **Value:** Supervisors under DORA request the per-incident clock evidence. This UC produces it automatically, so the entity can prove the notification workflow was operated within the required windows or identify the exact root cause of a miss.
- **App/TA:** Splunk SOAR, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=soar` sourcetype=servicenow:sir_incident OR sourcetype=phantom:incident; `index=ticketing` sourcetype=jira:issue (IR project); regulator notification log `index=compliance` sourcetype=dora:notification.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=soar sourcetype IN (servicenow:sir_incident,phantom:incident) tag::major_incident=true earliest=-1d
| stats min(detected_at) AS detected_at min(classified_at) AS classified_at min(notified_at) AS notified_at min(intermediate_at) AS intermediate_at min(final_at) AS final_at BY incident_id
| eval detect_to_classify_h=round((classified_at-detected_at)/3600,2)
| eval classify_to_initial_h=round((notified_at-classified_at)/3600,2)
| eval initial_to_inter_h=round((intermediate_at-notified_at)/3600,2)
| eval inter_to_final_h=round((final_at-intermediate_at)/3600,2)
| eval SLA_breached=if(detect_to_classify_h>24 OR classify_to_initial_h>24 OR initial_to_inter_h>72 OR inter_to_final_h>720,1,0)
| table incident_id detected_at classified_at notified_at intermediate_at final_at detect_to_classify_h classify_to_initial_h initial_to_inter_h inter_to_final_h SLA_breached
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalise detected_at/classified_at/notified_at/intermediate_at/final_at in the IR tool; (2) tag major incidents at classification; (3) schedule UC every 5m; (4) SLA_breached=1 escalates to CISO + Head of IR; (5) monthly roll-up to Board; (6) annual tabletop exercise validates clock fidelity.
- **Visualization:** Per-incident Gantt of phase durations, timechart of breaches per quarter, single value 'open incidents at risk of SLA breach in next 4h'.
- **CIM Models:** Alerts, Ticket_Management
- **Known false positives:** Incidents re-classified as non-major after initial raise can show a misleading 'breach' until tag::major_incident is cleared — dedup on latest tag state before computing SLAs.
- **References:** [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/dir/2022/2555/oj), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1090](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1090/)

---

### UC-22.3.45 · DORA Art.24 — Digital operational-resilience testing: test-plan execution attestation
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1562
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Gives the CISO and the ICT risk committee a single-query attestation that the operational-resilience testing programme executed as planned, replacing an email-based reconciliation that typically slips by weeks.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
- **Data Sources:** `index=testing` sourcetype=test:plan_execution (BAS/TLPT/DR runs); `index=ticketing` sourcetype=jira:issue (findings tickets); test-plan register `dora_test_plan.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup dora_test_plan.csv
| eval planned_date_et=strptime(planned_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| join type=outer test_id
  [search index=testing sourcetype=test:plan_execution
   | stats latest(_time) AS actual_run_time latest(pass_fail) AS pass_fail sum(finding_count) AS finding_count BY test_id]
| eval evidence_complete=if(isnotnull(actual_run_time),1,0)
| eval on_time=if(actual_run_time<=planned_date_et+86400*14,1,0)
| table test_id test_type scope_service planned_date actual_run_time pass_fail finding_count evidence_complete on_time
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain dora_test_plan.csv as the approved annual programme; (2) normalise execution output from BAS/DR tools to sourcetype=test:plan_execution; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) evidence_complete=0 past planned_date + 14d escalates to CISO; (5) per-quarter report to Board; (6) missing TLPT for significant entities escalates immediately.
- **Visualization:** Calendar heatmap of test executions, bar chart of findings per scope service, single value 'tests past due'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Tests rescheduled due to change freeze produce a legitimate gap — record the rescheduled_date and rebase the 14d tolerance.
- **References:** [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1562](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1562/)

---

### 22.6 — ISO/IEC 27001 (extended clauses)

### UC-22.6.46 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.1 — Risk-assessment evidence: live risk register decay
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Produces the evidence the external auditor expects at stage-2: proof that each risk line in the ISMS register was reviewed within its cadence, identifying exact owners of overdue entries.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:risk OR sourcetype=servicenow:grc_risk; ISMS cadence lookup `iso_27001_risk_cadence.csv` (per category, review frequency in days).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype IN (archer:risk,servicenow:grc_risk) earliest=-1d
| stats latest(last_reviewed) AS last_reviewed latest(category) AS category latest(owner) AS owner BY risk_id
| lookup iso_27001_risk_cadence.csv category OUTPUT cadence_days
| eval days_overdue=round((now()-last_reviewed)/86400,0)-cadence_days
| where days_overdue>0
| table risk_id category owner last_reviewed cadence_days days_overdue
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Export GRC risk register daily to Splunk; (2) maintain iso_27001_risk_cadence.csv by risk category; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) overdue > 0 opens a task to the risk owner; (5) Board quarterly report uses the rolling 90-day mean of overdue risks.
- **Visualization:** Histogram of days_overdue by category, table of top-overdue risks, single value 'total risks overdue today'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Category cadence changes introduced via ISMS committee need a 24h propagation grace — suppress new overdue hits for 24h after the lookup change.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj)

---

### UC-22.6.47 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 — Information-security objectives: measurable-target attainment
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Replaces the manual spreadsheet most ISMS programmes use to report objective attainment. Auditors see the live measurement, the target, and the last update in a single reproducible query.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance` sourcetype=isms:objective_result (push from ISMS KPIs); objective targets lookup `iso_27001_objectives.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup iso_27001_objectives.csv
| join type=outer objective_id
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=isms:objective_result earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(value) AS current latest(_time) AS measured_at BY objective_id
   | eval measured_at=strftime(measured_at,"%Y-%m-%d")]
| eval attainment_pct=round(100*current/target,1)
| eval status=if(attainment_pct>=100,"met","below_target")
| table objective_id target current attainment_pct measured_at status
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Push each KPI to index=compliance nightly with objective_id tag; (2) maintain iso_27001_objectives.csv as the ISMS-committee-approved target list; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) status=below_target opens an ISMS action; (5) quarterly Board pack exports the table.
- **Visualization:** Bullet chart of attainment % vs target, line chart of 30-day trend per objective, single value 'objectives below target'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Objective targets revised by the ISMS committee within the look-back window create an apparent regression — use the effective-from column in the lookup to compute status.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5)

---

### UC-22.6.48 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 8.2 — Operational risk-assessment: per-change risk-score recalculation
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Gives Clause 8.2 an operational verification signal instead of an annual narrative. Audit objection 'where is the evidence the risk was reassessed after the 2024-Q3 PCI change?' becomes a one-query answer.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=change` sourcetype=servicenow:change; `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:risk_score; change-to-risk mapping `iso_27001_change_to_risk.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change state=closed earliest=-14d
| rename affected_ci AS asset
| lookup iso_27001_change_to_risk.csv asset OUTPUT risk_id
| join type=outer risk_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype=archer:risk_score earliest=-14d
   | stats latest(score) AS new_score earliest(score) AS old_score latest(_time) AS recalc_time BY risk_id]
| eval delta=new_score-old_score
| eval recalc_performed=if(recalc_time>=_time AND recalc_time<=_time+604800,1,0)
| where recalc_performed=0 AND abs(delta)>0
| table change_id asset risk_id old_score new_score delta recalc_performed
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Link every CMDB change to affected risks in iso_27001_change_to_risk.csv; (2) normalise Archer risk_score events; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) recalc_performed=0 opens an ISMS action to the risk owner; (5) monthly report to risk committee.
- **Visualization:** Scatter of change impact vs recalc latency, table of changes missing recalc, single value 'changes in 14d missing recalc'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Changes categorised 'standard' (no risk implication) should be excluded — filter on change_category!='standard' in the base search.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5)

---

### UC-22.6.49 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 9.1 — Monitoring programme coverage: KPI telemetry uptime
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Availability
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, SOC-2
- **Value:** Prevents the worst external-audit outcome (monitoring 'was in place' but had silently died) and gives the Head of Platform a single dashboard tile showing ISMS telemetry health.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
