- **Data Sources:** `index=_internal` sourcetype=splunkd (scheduler logs); `index=compliance` sourcetype=isms:objective_result; KPI freshness lookup `iso_27001_kpi_freshness.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup iso_27001_kpi_freshness.csv
| join type=outer kpi_id
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=isms:objective_result earliest=-7d
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen BY kpi_id]
| eval age_min=round((now()-last_seen)/60,0)
| eval is_stale=if(age_min>sla_minutes OR isnull(last_seen),1,0)
| table kpi_id last_seen age_min sla_minutes is_stale
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Push each KPI into index=compliance; (2) maintain iso_27001_kpi_freshness.csv per KPI with its SLA; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) is_stale=1 pages the data-engineering owner of the KPI; (5) weekly ISMS report attaches the latest state.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap of KPI staleness, table of stale KPIs, single value 'stale KPIs right now'.
- **CIM Models:** Performance
- **Known false positives:** KPI suspended during maintenance windows legitimately shows as stale — exclude with a maintenance_calendar.csv lookup.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services)

---

### UC-22.6.50 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 — Internal audit coverage: control sample rotation
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, SOC-2
- **Value:** Closes the typical gap where internal audit over-samples 'hot' controls and silently skips administrative ones. The rotation evidence is what ISO 9.2 requires and what the registrar looks for.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:audit_test OR sourcetype=servicenow:audit_finding; ISMS control catalogue `iso_27001_control_catalog.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup iso_27001_control_catalog.csv
| join type=outer control_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype IN (archer:audit_test,servicenow:audit_finding) earliest=-365d
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_audited count AS audit_count_365d BY control_id]
| eval days_since=round((now()-last_audited)/86400,0)
| eval due_for_audit=if(isnull(last_audited) OR days_since>365,1,0)
| table control_id category last_audited days_since audit_count_365d due_for_audit
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain iso_27001_control_catalog.csv; (2) require internal-audit output to index=grc with control_id tag; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) due_for_audit=1 adds the control to the next quarter audit plan; (5) annual Audit Committee report.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar of audit count by category, table of overdue controls, single value 'controls never audited'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Controls retired in-year but still in catalog produce false 'overdue' entries — mark retired=true in the lookup.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5)

---

### UC-22.6.51 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A.5.24 — Incident-management planning: runbook currency attestation
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53
- **Value:** Satisfies the A.5.24 'planning and preparation' sub-requirement: incident procedures are continuously refreshed, not one-time written.
- **App/TA:** Splunk SOAR
- **Data Sources:** `index=soar` sourcetype=phantom:playbook OR sourcetype=runbook:metadata; runbook register lookup `ir_runbook_register.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup ir_runbook_register.csv
| eval last_reviewed=strptime(last_reviewed,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval age_days=round((now()-last_reviewed)/86400,0)
| eval is_stale=if(age_days>cadence_days,1,0)
| where is_stale=1
| table runbook_id owner last_reviewed age_days cadence_days is_stale
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain ir_runbook_register.csv with owner + cadence_days; (2) schedule UC daily; (3) stale runbooks open a recertification task to the owner with 14d SLA; (4) monthly IR committee review.
- **Visualization:** Table of stale runbooks by owner, single value 'stale runbooks', bar chart of age by runbook family.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Runbooks under active revision have an incomplete last_reviewed — exclude where state=in_review.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5)

---

### UC-22.6.52 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Annex A.5.25 — Event classification decisions: SIEM-to-incident triage traceability
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1070
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, SOC-2
- **Value:** Gives the Audit Committee a single number: 'classification SLA adherence %'. Analysts know their backlog, auditors see the proof.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk SOAR
- **Data Sources:** `index=notable` (ES notable events); `index=soar` sourcetype=phantom:incident (classification decisions).
- **SPL:**
```spl
search `notable` severity IN ("high","critical") earliest=-7d
| rename event_id AS alert_id
| join type=outer alert_id
  [search index=soar sourcetype=phantom:incident earliest=-7d
   | stats latest(_time) AS classified_at latest(decision) AS decision BY alert_id]
| eval decision_sla_met=if(classified_at<=_time+3600,1,0)
| where isnull(decision) OR decision_sla_met=0
| table alert_id severity _time classified_at decision decision_sla_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure SOAR writes classification decisions with alert_id tag; (2) schedule UC every 10m; (3) SLA miss escalates to shift lead; (4) daily SOC report of adherence %; (5) monthly to Audit Committee.
- **Visualization:** Funnel from total alerts → classified → on-time, line chart of adherence %, single value 'open unclassified alerts'.
- **CIM Models:** Alerts, Ticket_Management
- **Known false positives:** Alerts suppressed by tuning rules do not need classification decisions — filter out suppression_reason IS NOT NULL.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1070](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1070/)

---

### UC-22.6.53 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.2 — Competence evidence: role-based training completion
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** HIPAA Security Rule, ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Replaces the annual LMS export with a daily compliance signal. HR can notify managers proactively rather than after the audit finding.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` sourcetype=lms:completion; `index=hr` sourcetype=hr:role_assignment; required-module matrix `iso_27001_training_matrix.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype=hr:role_assignment earliest=-30d
| stats latest(role) AS role BY user
| lookup iso_27001_training_matrix.csv role OUTPUT required_modules
| eval required_modules=split(required_modules,",")
| join user
  [search index=hr sourcetype=lms:completion status=completed earliest=-365d
   | stats values(module) AS completed_modules BY user]
| eval covered=mvmap(required_modules, if(isnotnull(mvfind(completed_modules,x)),1,0))
| eval compliance_pct=round(100*sum(covered)/mvcount(required_modules),0)
| where compliance_pct<100
| table user role required_modules completed_modules compliance_pct
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Onboard LMS completions; (2) maintain iso_27001_training_matrix.csv per role; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) compliance_pct<100 triggers an HR email; (5) quarterly report to ISMS committee.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar by role, table of sub-100% users, single value 'users below 100%'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** New hires in their grace window (30d) appear as non-compliant — exclude by hire_date.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [HIPAA Security Rule 2013-final](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164), [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf)

---

### UC-22.6.54 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 7.5 — Documented information control: policy register approval trail
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, SOC-2
- **Value:** Prevents the common deficiency of policies that 'used to be' approved but whose approval trail has expired — a direct Clause 7.5 non-conformance.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` sourcetype=policyhub:policy (change of state); policy cadence lookup `iso_27001_policy_cadence.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=policyhub:policy action=approved earliest=-400d
| stats latest(_time) AS approved_at latest(version) AS version latest(approved_by) AS approved_by BY policy_id
| lookup iso_27001_policy_cadence.csv policy_id OUTPUT cadence_days owner
| eval days_to_next_review=cadence_days-round((now()-approved_at)/86400,0)
| where days_to_next_review<=30 OR isnull(approved_at)
| table policy_id version approved_by approved_at days_to_next_review owner
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Route policyhub approval events into index=grc; (2) maintain iso_27001_policy_cadence.csv; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) days_to_next_review<=30 opens a review task to policy owner; (5) quarterly CISO review.
- **Visualization:** Table of near-expiry policies, bar chart by owner, single value 'policies expired'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Newly-issued policies within 30 days of issue appear as near-expiry until the first cadence period completes — exclude if age<30d.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5)

---

### UC-22.6.55 · ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 8.1 — Operational planning: change advisory board (CAB) approval evidence
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** IT Operations
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Turns the 'post-implementation review' into a pre-emptive signal. The Head of IT Operations sees the breach list within the hour.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=change` sourcetype=servicenow:change; approver-role lookup `cab_roles.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change state IN (implemented,closed) earliest=-7d
| stats latest(type) AS type latest(_time) AS implemented_at latest(approval_status) AS approval_status latest(approver) AS approver latest(approval_date) AS cab_approved_at BY change_id
| lookup cab_roles.csv approver OUTPUT is_authorised_cab
| eval approval_met=if(type="emergency",1,if(approval_status="approved" AND is_authorised_cab=1 AND cab_approved_at<=implemented_at,1,0))
| where approval_met=0
| table change_id type implemented_at cab_approved_at approver approval_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Export ServiceNow change events; (2) maintain cab_roles.csv listing authorised approvers; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) approval_met=0 opens a deficiency record with 7d remediation; (5) monthly Audit Committee report.
- **Visualization:** Table of unapproved changes, bar chart of breach count by team, single value 'unapproved changes in last 7d'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Emergency changes legitimately bypass CAB; their follow-up retrospective approval must exist separately — this UC excludes type=emergency by design.
- **References:** [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services)

---

### 22.8 — SOC 2 (extended clauses)

### UC-22.8.31 · SOC 2 CC6.6 — Encryption-in-transit validation: cleartext protocols crossing the trust boundary
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1071
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA Security Rule, ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Replaces annual TLS-compliance scans with a continuous signal, so drift (a new container accidentally exposing http) is caught within 30 minutes.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Stream
- **Data Sources:** `index=stream` sourcetype=stream:tcp OR sourcetype=stream:http OR sourcetype=stream:tls; trust-zone lookup `trust_zones.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=stream sourcetype=stream:tls earliest=-30m
| rename src_ip AS src dest_ip AS dest
| lookup trust_zones.csv ip=src OUTPUT zone AS src_zone
| lookup trust_zones.csv ip=dest OUTPUT zone AS dest_zone
| where src_zone!=dest_zone
| eval is_deprecated=if(tls_version IN ("SSLv3","TLS1.0","TLS1.1"),1,0)
| where is_deprecated=1 OR protocol IN ("http","ftp","telnet","smtp")
| stats count BY src dest dest_port protocol tls_version is_deprecated
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Onboard Splunk Stream TLS data; (2) maintain trust_zones.csv; (3) schedule UC every 30m; (4) each hit opens a Platform ticket with src/dest/dest_port; (5) SLA: remediate within 24h.
- **Visualization:** Sankey of src_zone → dest_zone by protocol, table of deprecated sessions, single value 'deprecated TLS sessions right now'.
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic, Certificates
- **Known false positives:** Explicit plaintext management LANs (backup target, ILO) are allowlisted in trust_zones.csv via category='management-cleartext-allowed'.
- **References:** [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [HIPAA Security Rule 2013-final](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1071](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1071/)

---

### UC-22.8.32 · SOC 2 CC6.7 — System boundary & data-transmission control: unapproved egress destinations
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1041
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Provides the evidence SOC 2 auditors want: proof that outbound flows are governed and deviations are captured. Supports reduction of shadow SaaS risk.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` sourcetype=pan:traffic; ASN/country enrichment via `asn_country.csv`; approved-egress registry `approved_egress.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype=pan:traffic action=allow direction=outbound earliest=-15m
| rename src AS src_ip dest AS dest_ip
| lookup service_by_src.csv src_ip OUTPUT src_service
| lookup asn_country.csv ip=dest_ip OUTPUT asn AS dest_asn country AS dest_country
| lookup approved_egress.csv src_service dest_asn OUTPUT approved
| where isnull(approved) OR approved!="yes"
| stats count sum(bytes_out) AS bytes BY src_service dest_ip dest_asn dest_country
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enrich Palo Alto traffic with service/ASN/country; (2) maintain approved_egress.csv; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) hit opens a ticket to service owner; (5) 48h SLA; (6) weekly review by Platform team.
- **Visualization:** Geo map of unapproved destinations, bar chart by service, single value 'unapproved egress flows (1h)'.
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **Known false positives:** Public CDNs and package mirrors that use anycast may produce transient country churn — allowlist by ASN in approved_egress.csv.
- **References:** [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1041](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1041/)

---

### UC-22.8.33 · SOC 2 CC7.1 — System-operations monitoring: uptime attestation and alert-noise governance
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Availability
- **Splunk Pillar:** IT Operations
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, SOC-2
- **Value:** Lets the Head of IT Operations present the Audit Committee with coverage % (target 100% for production) and alert-noise % (target <5/day/CI). The same query replaces three spreadsheets.
- **App/TA:** Splunk IT Service Intelligence
- **Data Sources:** `index=monitoring` sourcetype=heartbeat:agent; `index=alerts` sourcetype=opsgenie:alert; CMDB lookup `cmdb_ci_list.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup cmdb_ci_list.csv
| join type=outer ci_id
  [search index=monitoring sourcetype=heartbeat:agent earliest=-1d
   | stats latest(_time) AS heartbeat_last_seen BY ci_id]
| join type=outer ci_id
  [search index=alerts sourcetype=opsgenie:alert earliest=-1d
   | stats count AS alerts_24h BY ci_id]
| eval coverage_status=case(isnull(heartbeat_last_seen),"no-heartbeat",heartbeat_last_seen<now()-1800,"stale",true(),"healthy")
| table ci_id heartbeat_last_seen alerts_24h coverage_status
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Nightly CMDB sync; (2) heartbeat agent installed on every CI; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) no-heartbeat CIs open a task to IT Ops; (5) quarterly Audit Committee report.
- **Visualization:** Donut of coverage_status, top-10 noisiest CIs, single value 'coverage %'.
- **CIM Models:** Performance, Alerts
- **Known false positives:** Decommissioned CIs legitimately stop heartbeats — mark retired=true in CMDB lookup.
- **References:** [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj)

---

### UC-22.8.34 · SOC 2 CC7.3 — Evaluated events: threshold breaches without documented rationale
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, SOC-2
- **Value:** Gives leadership proof that events are not only closed but justifiably closed. Auditors consider an 'auto-closed without rationale' event a material weakness.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security, Splunk SOAR
- **Data Sources:** `index=notable` (ES notable); `index=soar` sourcetype=phantom:incident.
- **SPL:**
```spl
search `notable` severity IN ("medium","high","critical") earliest=-7d
| rename event_id AS alert_id
| join type=outer alert_id
  [search index=soar sourcetype=phantom:incident state=closed earliest=-7d
   | stats latest(closed_at) AS closed_at latest(analyst) AS analyst latest(rationale) AS rationale BY alert_id]
| eval rationale_present=if(len(rationale)>20,1,0)
| where isnotnull(closed_at) AND rationale_present=0
| table alert_id severity closed_at analyst rationale_present
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce rationale field on SOAR close action; (2) schedule UC hourly; (3) rationale_present=0 reopens the alert for review; (4) weekly SOC report.
- **Visualization:** Stacked bar of alerts by analyst × rationale_present, single value 'closed-without-rationale'.
- **CIM Models:** Alerts
- **Known false positives:** Alerts closed by automation rules must use rationale='auto-closed:<rule_id>' rather than blank — reject blank in SOAR.
- **References:** [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj)

---

### UC-22.8.35 · SOC 2 CC7.4 — Incident response: post-incident review completion SLA
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, SOC-2
- **Value:** Surfaces PIR backlog before it becomes an audit finding. Board sees a rolling '% of PIRs on time' metric.
- **App/TA:** Splunk SOAR
- **Data Sources:** `index=soar` sourcetype=phantom:incident; `index=ticketing` sourcetype=jira:issue (PIR project).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=soar sourcetype=phantom:incident state=closed earliest=-30d
| stats latest(closed_at) AS closed_at BY incident_id
| join type=outer incident_id
  [search index=ticketing sourcetype=jira:issue project=PIR earliest=-60d
   | stats latest(_time) AS pir_completed_at BY incident_id]
| eval pir_sla_met=if(isnotnull(pir_completed_at) AND pir_completed_at<=closed_at+604800,1,0)
| where pir_sla_met=0
| table incident_id closed_at pir_completed_at pir_sla_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Ensure PIR Jira project is populated; (2) schedule UC daily; (3) pir_sla_met=0 opens a reminder ticket; (4) weekly SOC report; (5) quarterly Board report.
- **Visualization:** Waterfall of incidents → PIR completed → on time, single value 'open incidents overdue PIR'.
- **CIM Models:** Ticket_Management
- **Known false positives:** Incidents reopened and later re-closed legitimately extend the window — use latest(closed_at).
- **References:** [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj)

---

### UC-22.8.36 · SOC 2 CC1.1 — Integrity and ethical values: code-of-conduct acknowledgement trail
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, SOC-2
- **Value:** Removes the audit finding 'CoC acknowledgement trail is incomplete' by making HR's coverage measurable daily.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` sourcetype=hr:employee (active roster); `index=hr` sourcetype=lms:acknowledgement; policy-version lookup `coc_versions.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype=hr:employee status=active earliest=-30d
| stats latest(hire_date) AS hire_date BY user
| join user type=outer
  [search index=hr sourcetype=lms:acknowledgement module=code_of_conduct earliest=-2y
   | stats latest(version) AS latest_ack_version latest(_time) AS latest_ack_date BY user]
| lookup coc_versions.csv AS latest OUTPUT current_version
| eval is_compliant=if(latest_ack_version==current_version AND latest_ack_date>now()-31536000,1,0)
| where is_compliant=0
| table user hire_date latest_ack_version latest_ack_date is_compliant
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Nightly HR roster sync; (2) maintain coc_versions.csv; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) is_compliant=0 triggers an HR reminder email; (5) quarterly compliance report.
- **Visualization:** Table of non-acknowledging users, bar chart by department, single value 'non-compliant users'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Employees within their 30-day grace window are excluded via hire_date.
- **References:** [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001)

---

### UC-22.8.37 · SOC 2 CC9.1 — Risk-mitigation activity: vendor-risk action closure SLA
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, SOC-2
- **Value:** Replaces the quarterly Procurement PowerPoint with a continuous signal. Top vendors with overdue actions are the first line of an Audit Committee brief.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` sourcetype=servicenow:vendor_risk; vendor register lookup `vendor_register.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=grc sourcetype=servicenow:vendor_risk state IN (open,closed) earliest=-120d
| stats latest(state) AS state latest(severity) AS severity latest(due_at) AS due_at latest(closed_at) AS closed_at BY action_id vendor
| eval sla_met=case(state="closed" AND closed_at<=due_at,1,state="closed",0,now()>due_at,0,true(),1)
| where sla_met=0
| table action_id vendor severity due_at closed_at sla_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Route vendor_risk events from ServiceNow GRC; (2) maintain vendor_register.csv; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) sla_met=0 escalates per severity matrix; (5) monthly CISO report.
- **Visualization:** Table of overdue actions by vendor, bar chart of SLA adherence by severity, single value 'overdue vendor-risk actions'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Actions waiting on vendor response can be legitimately late — mark state='awaiting-vendor' and exclude.
- **References:** [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj)

---

### UC-22.8.38 · SOC 2 C1.1 — Confidentiality: sensitive-data exposure at the egress boundary
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1048
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** HIPAA Security Rule, ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** C1.1 confidentiality assurance hinges on continuous detection of data leaving the boundary. This UC gives the DPO a queryable control output.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Purview, Splunk Add-on for Symantec DLP
- **Data Sources:** `index=dlp` sourcetype=symantec:dlp OR sourcetype=microsoft:purview:dlp; approved-destination lookup `approved_egress.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=dlp sourcetype IN (symantec:dlp,microsoft:purview:dlp) severity IN ("high","critical") earliest=-30m
| rename destination_ip AS dest policy_name AS dlp_policy
| lookup approved_egress.csv dest OUTPUT approved
| where approved!="yes" OR isnull(approved)
| stats count BY dlp_policy severity user dest approved
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Onboard DLP; (2) maintain approved_egress.csv; (3) schedule UC every 30m; (4) hit opens a P1 to DPO + user's manager; (5) weekly DPO report.
- **Visualization:** Table of unapproved egress detections, bar chart by policy, single value 'new unapproved egress (1h)'.
- **CIM Models:** Data Loss Prevention
- **Known false positives:** Legitimate cross-tenant file shares with approved partners can produce detections — allowlist by partner in approved_egress.csv.
- **References:** [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [HIPAA Security Rule 2013-final](https://www.ecfr.gov/current/title-45/subtitle-A/subchapter-C/part-164), [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1048](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1048/)

---

### UC-22.8.39 · SOC 2 P1.1 — Privacy notice: consent-record freshness for privacy-notice version changes
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** GDPR, SOC-2
- **Value:** Prevents P1.1 findings: 'consent records reference a superseded notice'. Satisfies the '<v-current>' regulator question asked at every annual audit.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
- **Data Sources:** `index=consent` sourcetype=onetrust:consent; privacy-notice version lookup `privacy_notice_versions.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=consent sourcetype=onetrust:consent earliest=-14d
| stats latest(consent_version) AS consent_version latest(_time) AS consent_time BY subject_id
| lookup privacy_notice_versions.csv AS latest OUTPUT current_version
| eval days_stale=round((now()-consent_time)/86400,0)
| eval needs_refresh=if(consent_version!=current_version,1,0)
| where needs_refresh=1
| table subject_id consent_version current_version days_stale needs_refresh
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Push consent changes to index=consent; (2) maintain privacy_notice_versions.csv; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) batch subjects needing refresh by cohort; (5) DPO owns the refresh campaign.
- **Visualization:** Timechart of stale consent backlog, table of cohorts, single value 'subjects needing refresh'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Deceased/closed subject accounts legitimately have stale consent — exclude by account_state.
- **References:** [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [GDPR 2016/679](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2016/679/oj)

---

### 22.11 — PCI DSS v4.0 (extended clauses)

### UC-22.11.91 · PCI-DSS 1.3 — CDE network boundary: unauthorised flows between CDE and untrusted networks
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1021
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Provides QSAs the direct evidence that CDE boundary controls are intact and deviations are captured within 15 minutes.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=netfw` sourcetype IN (pan:traffic,cisco:asa); zone lookup `cde_zones.csv`; approved-flows lookup `pci_approved_flows.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=netfw sourcetype IN (pan:traffic,cisco:asa) action=allow earliest=-15m
| lookup cde_zones.csv ip=src OUTPUT zone AS src_zone
| lookup cde_zones.csv ip=dest OUTPUT zone AS dest_zone
| where (src_zone="CDE" AND dest_zone="UNTRUSTED") OR (src_zone="UNTRUSTED" AND dest_zone="CDE")
| lookup pci_approved_flows.csv src_zone dest_zone dest_port protocol OUTPUT approved
| where approved!="yes" OR isnull(approved)
| stats count BY src src_zone dest dest_zone dest_port protocol approved
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Onboard Palo Alto + ASA traffic; (2) maintain cde_zones.csv (CIDR → zone) and pci_approved_flows.csv; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) hit opens a P1 to NSC team; (5) daily report to compliance officer.
- **Visualization:** Sankey of src_zone→dest_zone with unapproved highlighted, table of unapproved flows, single value 'unapproved CDE flows (1h)'.
- **CIM Models:** Network_Traffic
- **Known false positives:** Cold-failover rules activated during DR days produce ephemeral unapproved flows — add DR-day allowlist with effective-from/to timestamps.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1021](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1021/)

---

### UC-22.11.92 · PCI-DSS 2.2 — Secure configuration baseline: drift from approved hardening template
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Turns the quarterly 'baseline assessment' into a 30-minute signal. Platform team sees regressions before the QSA.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** `index=configmgmt` sourcetype=chef:compliance OR sourcetype=ansible:callback; baseline lookup `pci_baselines.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=configmgmt sourcetype IN (chef:compliance,ansible:callback) earliest=-30m
| stats latest(role) AS role latest(baseline_version) AS baseline_version latest(resource_failed) AS resource_failed latest(_time) AS last_converged BY hostname
| eval drift_count=coalesce(resource_failed,0)
| where drift_count>0 OR last_converged<now()-86400
| table hostname role baseline_version drift_count last_converged
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce Chef/Ansible on all CDE hosts; (2) emit convergence events; (3) maintain pci_baselines.csv with the current approved baseline_version per role; (4) schedule UC every 30m; (5) drift opens a Platform ticket.
- **Visualization:** Bar of drift_count by role, table of top-drift hosts, single value 'hosts in drift'.
- **CIM Models:** Change, Endpoint
- **Known false positives:** Baseline upgrades take effect over a staged rollout — support multiple active baseline_versions in the lookup.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5)

---

### UC-22.11.93 · PCI-DSS 3.3 — Sensitive authentication data: cleartext PAN/CVV detection in logs
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1005
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Reduces the window between a logging regression introducing a cleartext PAN and the compliance team's discovery from 'next log review' to 10 minutes.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** `index=app` sourcetypes from payment applications; `index=web` access logs; redaction-pipeline attestation events.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=app OR index=web earliest=-10m
| eval pan_match=if(match(_raw,"(?<![0-9])(?:4[0-9]{12}(?:[0-9]{3})?|5[1-5][0-9]{14}|3[47][0-9]{13})(?![0-9])"),1,0)
| eval cvv_match=if(match(_raw,"(?i)cvv=[0-9]{3,4}"),1,0)
| where pan_match=1 OR cvv_match=1
| eval match_type=case(pan_match=1 AND cvv_match=1,"pan+cvv",pan_match=1,"pan",true(),"cvv")
| stats count BY sourcetype host match_type
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Apply a separate restrictive index for hits (ACL); (2) schedule UC every 10m; (3) hit creates a P1 to DPO + app owner; (4) index-time redaction must be fixed + backfill scrub; (5) monthly DPO report.
- **Visualization:** Timechart of match_count, table of top offending sourcetypes, single value 'detections in last hour'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Synthetic test data may look like a PAN — allow-list via the synthetic_test flag in the application.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1005](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1005/)

---

### UC-22.11.94 · PCI-DSS 5.2 — Anti-malware: EDR coverage + detection-queue attestation
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Converts the annual 'EDR deployed' claim into a 30-minute ground-truth signal — directly auditable against PCI-DSS 5.2 requirement for deployed anti-malware.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CrowdStrike FDR (5082)
- **Data Sources:** `index=edr` sourcetype IN (crowdstrike:hosts,microsoft:defender:host); `index=cmdb` CDE host list.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup cde_hosts.csv
| join type=outer hostname
  [search index=edr sourcetype IN (crowdstrike:hosts,microsoft:defender:host) earliest=-1h
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_event latest(agent_state) AS agent_state BY hostname]
| eval edr_seen=if(isnotnull(last_event) AND last_event>now()-1800,1,0)
| where edr_seen=0 OR agent_state!="healthy"
| table hostname role edr_seen last_event agent_state
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain cde_hosts.csv from CMDB; (2) schedule UC every 30m; (3) gap opens a ticket to the host owner; (4) 24h SLA; (5) weekly CISO report.
- **Visualization:** Bar chart of coverage by role, table of gaps, single value 'CDE hosts without EDR'.
- **CIM Models:** Endpoint
- **Known false positives:** Hosts under image-build or maintenance windows legitimately show no EDR — use maintenance_calendar.csv.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5)

---

### UC-22.11.95 · PCI-DSS 6.2 — Bespoke-software SDLC: code-review + SAST completion before CDE deploy
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1195
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Replaces the pre-release tick-box with continuous gate-enforcement evidence. Auditors see the commit→gate→deploy chain for every CDE deploy.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for GitHub
- **Data Sources:** `index=cicd` sourcetype=github:actions OR sourcetype=gitlab:pipeline; SAST output `index=security` sourcetype=veracode:scan; CDE-service lookup `cde_services.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=cicd sourcetype IN (github:actions,gitlab:pipeline) event_type=deployment target_env IN ("prod-cde") earliest=-24h
| stats latest(_time) AS deployed_at latest(commit_sha) AS commit_sha BY artifact service
| join type=outer commit_sha
  [search index=security sourcetype=veracode:scan earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(overall_state) AS sast_state BY commit_sha]
| join type=outer commit_sha
  [search index=cicd event_type=pr_review state=approved earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(reviewer) AS reviewer BY commit_sha
   | eval review_state="approved"]
| eval gates_met=if(sast_state="pass" AND review_state="approved",1,0)
| where gates_met=0
| table artifact commit_sha service deployed_at sast_state review_state gates_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Route CI/CD + SAST events; (2) maintain cde_services.csv; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) gates_met=0 opens a security ticket; (5) pipeline policy revisions add gates.
- **Visualization:** Table of gate-missing deploys, bar chart by service, single value 'recent deploys missing gates'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Hotfix deploys via break-glass workflow have an alternate review path — exclude with the workflow label hotfix=true when attested separately.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1195](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1195/)

---

### UC-22.11.96 · PCI-DSS 8.3 — Strong authentication: password-only logins against privileged accounts
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Catches the exact condition that has historically resulted in CDE breaches (privileged password-only sessions). MTTR shrinks from weeks to minutes.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Okta
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security (4624); `index=linux` sourcetype=auditd; IDP `index=idp` sourcetype=okta:system_log.
- **SPL:**
```spl
search (index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4624) OR (index=idp sourcetype=okta:system_log eventType=user.session.start) earliest=-10m
| lookup privileged_accounts.csv user OUTPUT is_privileged
| where is_privileged="yes"
| eval auth_method=case(isnotnull(LogonType) AND LogonType IN ("2","10"),"interactive",isnotnull(factor),factor,true(),"password")
| eval mfa_used=if(auth_method IN ("mfa_push","mfa_totp","webauthn","smartcard","token:hardware"),1,0)
| where mfa_used=0
| table user src dest auth_method mfa_used
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain privileged_accounts.csv; (2) ensure IDP emits factor evidence; (3) schedule UC every 10m; (4) hit opens a P1 and disables the session; (5) monthly CISO review of patterns.
- **Visualization:** Timechart of non-MFA privileged logins, table of top accounts, single value 'non-MFA priv logins (1h)'.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **Known false positives:** Service-account logins using smart-card-equivalent client cert may not show 'factor'; capture via cert-login sourcetype and mark mfa_used=1.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/)

---

### UC-22.11.97 · PCI-DSS 8.4 — MFA coverage: administrative access to CDE without MFA
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** The #1 QSA finding in failed assessments. Converting this to a 10-minute detection reduces remediation cost 100×.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for CyberArk
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` EventCode 4624/4672; `index=linux` auditd; bastion/PAM `index=pam` sourcetype=cyberark:pam; CDE-dest lookup `cde_hosts.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
search (index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode IN (4624,4672)) OR (index=pam sourcetype=cyberark:pam event_type=session_start) earliest=-10m
| rename target_host AS dest
| lookup cde_hosts.csv hostname=dest OUTPUT in_cde
| lookup privileged_accounts.csv user AS admin OUTPUT is_privileged
| where in_cde="yes" AND is_privileged="yes"
| eval mfa_used=if(match(_raw,"(?i)mfa|2fa|push|webauthn|smartcard"),1,0)
| where mfa_used=0
| table admin dest session_type mfa_used
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Integrate PAM/bastion; (2) maintain cde_hosts.csv + privileged_accounts.csv; (3) schedule UC every 10m; (4) hit revokes session; (5) monthly CISO review.
- **Visualization:** Heatmap of admins × destinations, table of non-MFA sessions, single value 'non-MFA admin in CDE'.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **Known false positives:** Break-glass emergency access with compensating controls (hardware-token-only) must log mfa_used=1 via its own sourcetype — route to the field correctly.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/)

---

### UC-22.11.98 · PCI-DSS 8.6 — Application and system accounts: interactive use of a service account
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078.002
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Removes the common post-audit-finding 'we didn't know that account was used interactively' narrative by making the misuse visible in 10 minutes.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` EventCode 4624; `index=linux` auditd; service-account lookup `service_accounts.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=4624 LogonType IN (2,10,11) earliest=-10m
| rename TargetUserName AS user
| lookup service_accounts.csv user OUTPUT account_type
| where account_type="service-only"
| table user src dest LogonType account_type
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain service_accounts.csv with account_type tag; (2) schedule UC every 10m; (3) hit opens a P1 and disables the account; (4) weekly IAM review.
- **Visualization:** Table of interactive service-account logins, time chart, single value 'events in last hour'.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication
- **Known false positives:** Admin password rotations performed by a service-account-using tool may be reported as interactive — exclude via tool's user-as-tool mapping.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078.002](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/)

---

### UC-22.11.99 · PCI-DSS 10.3 — Audit log integrity: tampering/deletion detection on CDE log source
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1070.001
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** A cleared Windows Security log on a CDE host is a near-certain indicator of attacker presence. Closing the detection gap to 10 minutes saves forensics time.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` EventCode IN (1102,104); `index=linux` auditd log clears, syslog `rotated-by-hand`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
search (index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:Security EventCode=1102) OR (index=windows sourcetype=WinEventLog:System EventCode=104) OR (index=linux sourcetype=auditd type=CONFIG_CHANGE op=remove_rule) earliest=-10m
| eval action=case(EventCode=1102,"audit_log_cleared",EventCode=104,"event_log_cleared",op="remove_rule","auditd_rule_removed",true(),"unknown")
| rename TargetUserName AS actor host AS host
| table host action actor target_log
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Forward Windows Security + System + Linux auditd to Splunk; (2) schedule UC every 10m; (3) hit opens a P1 to IR + isolates host; (4) quarterly IR drill validates response.
- **Visualization:** Table of events by action, time chart, single value 'clears in last hour'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Host-retirement scripts may legitimately clear logs — exclude only with scheduled-retirement ticket linked.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1070.001](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1070/)

---

### UC-22.11.100 · PCI-DSS 10.4 — Time synchronisation: NTP drift on CDE hosts
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🟢 Beginner
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** IT Operations
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Prevents the classic forensic nightmare of 'when exactly did the event occur' because the CDE clocks disagreed.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Microsoft Windows (742), Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux (833)
- **Data Sources:** `index=windows` sourcetype=w32time; `index=linux` sourcetype=chronyd OR sourcetype=ntpd; CDE lookup `cde_hosts.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
search (index=windows sourcetype=w32time) OR (index=linux sourcetype IN (chronyd,ntpd)) earliest=-15m
| stats latest(offset_ms) AS offset_ms latest(stratum) AS stratum latest(_time) AS last_ntp_sync BY hostname
| lookup cde_hosts.csv hostname OUTPUT in_cde
| where in_cde="yes" AND (abs(offset_ms)>1000 OR last_ntp_sync<now()-3600)
| table hostname offset_ms stratum last_ntp_sync
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Enforce w32time/chrony logs to Splunk; (2) maintain cde_hosts.csv; (3) schedule UC every 15m; (4) drift opens a Platform ticket; (5) quarterly sampling for auditor.
- **Visualization:** Scatter of offset_ms per host, table of drifted hosts, single value 'hosts over 1s drift'.
- **CIM Models:** Performance
- **Known false positives:** Virtualisation drift during host migration may cause short bursts — smooth with 5-minute averaging before trigger.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5)

---

### UC-22.11.101 · PCI-DSS 10.6 — Log review: daily-review evidence for CDE data sources
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Auditors frequently disallow 'we review daily' without evidence. The review artefact lookup gives the evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
- **Data Sources:** `index=compliance` sourcetype=review:daily (from SOC daily review tool); CDE-source list `pci_cde_log_sources.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup pci_cde_log_sources.csv
| join type=outer source
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=review:daily earliest=-1d
   | stats latest(reviewer) AS reviewer latest(_time) AS reviewed_at latest(artefact_id) AS artefact_id BY source]
| eval review_today=if(reviewed_at>=relative_time(now(),"@d"),1,0)
| where review_today=0
| table source reviewer reviewed_at artefact_id review_today
```
- **Implementation:** (1) SOC tool must write review artefacts to index=compliance; (2) maintain pci_cde_log_sources.csv; (3) schedule UC daily at 23:55 UTC; (4) gap opens a ticket for the next morning's shift; (5) monthly CISO report.
- **Visualization:** Table of sources missing review, time chart by day, single value 'days with gaps (30d)'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** Automated high-fidelity systems (e.g., PAN unified logging with correlation) may substitute for daily review — allow-list via source_type='automated-attest' in the lookup.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services)

---

### UC-22.11.102 · PCI-DSS 10.7 — Log retention: CDE data-source retention + immutability attestation
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Platform
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Prevents the audit-killing discovery that retention was silently reduced during an index rebuild. Daily attestation beats the quarterly spot-check.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise / Splunk Cloud Platform
- **Data Sources:** Splunk REST /services/data/indexes; `index=compliance` sourcetype=worm:signer.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| rest /services/data/indexes
| where match(title,"^(cde|pci)_")
| eval configured_days=round(frozenTimePeriodInSecs/86400,0)
| table title configured_days minTime maxTime
| rename title AS index
| eval oldest_event_age_d=round((now()-strptime(minTime,"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S%z"))/86400,0)
| join type=outer index
  [search index=compliance sourcetype=worm:signer earliest=-7d
   | stats latest(_time) AS worm_last_write BY index]
| where configured_days<365 OR oldest_event_age_d<365 OR worm_last_write<now()-86400
| table index configured_days oldest_event_age_d worm_last_write
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Name CDE indexes with cde_/pci_ prefix; (2) WORM signer writes per-index heartbeat; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) gap opens a Platform ticket; (5) monthly CISO review.
- **Visualization:** Bar of retention days by index, table of gaps, single value 'indexes below 365d'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** New indexes legitimately have oldest_event_age_d<365 until one year passes — mark age_exempt=true in an index-metadata lookup.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201)

---

### UC-22.11.103 · PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence and unremediated high-severity
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1595
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, NIST 800-53, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Closes the #1 open-finding reason at QSAs — 'the scan history was incomplete'. Continuous measurement is the intended replacement.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060)
- **Data Sources:** `index=vm` sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln, qualys:host; scan-scope lookup `pci_scan_scope.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN (tenable:sc:vuln,qualys:host) earliest=-120d
| stats latest(_time) AS last_scan_at count(eval(severity IN ("high","critical") AND state="open")) AS open_high_findings avg(eval(if(state="open" AND severity IN ("high","critical"),now()-first_seen,null))) AS mean_age_s BY scan_scope
| eval mean_age_days=round(mean_age_s/86400,0)
| lookup pci_scan_scope.csv scan_scope OUTPUT cadence_days sla_days
| eval sla_met=if(last_scan_at>=now()-cadence_days*86400 AND mean_age_days<=sla_days,1,0)
| where sla_met=0
| table scan_scope last_scan_at sla_met open_high_findings mean_age_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Normalise scanner feeds; (2) maintain pci_scan_scope.csv (quarterly external, per-change internal); (3) schedule UC daily; (4) sla_met=0 opens a P2; (5) quarterly QSA report.
- **Visualization:** Bullet chart of SLA adherence by scope, table of overdue scopes, single value 'scopes overdue'.
- **CIM Models:** Vulnerabilities
- **Known false positives:** Immaterial findings whose exception was approved in the risk register show as 'open' — tag state='accepted_risk' and exclude.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [NIST 800-53 Rev. 5](https://doi.org/10.6028/NIST.SP.800-53r5), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1595](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1595/)

---

### UC-22.11.104 · PCI-DSS 11.4 — Intrusion detection: IDS signature/health attestation + untuned alert monitoring
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Transforms annual sign-off into 30-minute ground-truth. IR team sees stale signatures before they become an audit issue.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Suricata, Splunk Add-on for Palo Alto Networks (2757)
- **Data Sources:** `index=ids` sourcetype IN (suricata:eve,snort:ids,palo:threat); sensor inventory `pci_ids_sensors.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup pci_ids_sensors.csv
| join type=outer sensor
  [search index=ids earliest=-30m
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_seen latest(sig_version) AS sig_version BY sensor]
| eval age_d=round((now()-strptime(sig_version,"%Y-%m-%d"))/86400,0)
| eval state=case(isnull(last_seen),"offline",last_seen<now()-1800,"stale",age_d>7,"signatures-stale",true(),"healthy")
| where state!="healthy"
| table sensor last_seen sig_version age_d state
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain pci_ids_sensors.csv; (2) onboard sensor telemetry; (3) schedule UC every 30m; (4) non-healthy state opens a P1/P2; (5) monthly CISO review.
- **Visualization:** Donut of state, table of non-healthy sensors, single value 'stale sensors'.
- **CIM Models:** Intrusion_Detection
- **Known false positives:** Sensor maintenance windows legitimately show 'offline' — allowlist with maintenance_calendar.csv.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services)

---

### UC-22.11.105 · PCI-DSS 12.10 — Incident response: IR readiness — playbook exercise evidence
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2
- **Value:** Converts the 'we did a tabletop' email into indexed evidence auditors can query directly.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
- **Data Sources:** `index=testing` sourcetype=ir:drill; playbook register `ir_playbook_register.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup ir_playbook_register.csv
| where scope_cde="yes"
| join type=outer playbook_id
  [search index=testing sourcetype=ir:drill earliest=-14mon
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_exercise_at latest(finding_count) AS finding_count latest(exercise_lead) AS exercise_lead BY playbook_id]
| eval cadence_met=if(last_exercise_at>=now()-365*86400,1,0)
| table playbook_id scope_cde last_exercise_at finding_count exercise_lead cadence_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) IR exercise tool writes drill events to index=testing; (2) maintain ir_playbook_register.csv; (3) schedule UC weekly; (4) cadence_met=0 schedules a drill; (5) annual Board report.
- **Visualization:** Table of playbooks with drill history, bar chart of findings per exercise, single value 'playbooks overdue'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Combined multi-playbook drills may link to one of several playbook_ids — maintain a drill_to_playbook mapping.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services)

---

### UC-22.11.106 · PCI-DSS 12.3 — Targeted risk analysis: frequency adherence for per-requirement TRAs
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** DORA, ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS
- **Value:** Gives compliance an explicit overdue list — the v4.0 TRA is a common reason for 'in progress' at year-two assessments.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
- **Data Sources:** `index=grc` sourcetype=archer:tra_record; TRA register `pci_tra_register.csv`.
- **SPL:**
```spl
| inputlookup pci_tra_register.csv
| join type=outer tra_id
  [search index=grc sourcetype=archer:tra_record earliest=-2y
   | stats latest(_time) AS last_refresh_at BY tra_id]
| eval overdue_days=round((now()-last_refresh_at)/86400,0)-frequency_days
| where overdue_days>0 OR isnull(last_refresh_at)
| table tra_id requirement frequency_days last_refresh_at overdue_days
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain pci_tra_register.csv with requirement and frequency_days; (2) GRC writes each refresh event; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) overdue TRAs open a CISO-owned task; (5) quarterly compliance report.
- **Visualization:** Table of overdue TRAs, bar chart by requirement, single value 'TRAs overdue'.
- **CIM Models:** N/A
- **Known false positives:** TRAs whose requirement is marked 'not applicable' should be excluded via scope_applicable=false.
- **References:** [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [DORA Regulation (EU) 2022/2554](https://eur-lex.europa.eu/eli/reg/2022/2554/oj)

---

### 22.12 — SOX / ITGC (extended clauses)

### UC-22.12.36 · SOX-ITGC AccessMgmt.Provisioning — Financial-system user provisioning SLA & workflow adherence
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1098
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Closes the #1 SOX deficiency 'access provisioned without an approved ticket'. Internal audit gets a daily report rather than a quarterly spot-check.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Okta, Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=iam` sourcetype=okta:system_log OR sourcetype=ad:group_change; ticketing `index=ticketing` sourcetype=servicenow:request.
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=iam eventType=group.user_membership.add earliest=-7d
| lookup financial_systems_groups.csv group OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="yes"
| rename actor AS added_by target.id AS user
| eval added_at=_time
| join type=outer user group
  [search index=ticketing sourcetype=servicenow:request request_type=access_request state=approved earliest=-30d
   | stats latest(ticket_id) AS ticket_id latest(approved_at) AS approved_at BY user target_group
   | rename target_group AS group]
| eval sla_met=if(isnotnull(approved_at) AND added_at<=approved_at+24*3600,1,0)
| where sla_met=0
| table group user added_at ticket_id approved_at sla_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain financial_systems_groups.csv; (2) enforce ServiceNow workflow; (3) schedule UC daily; (4) sla_met=0 opens a SOX deficiency record; (5) monthly CFO report.
- **Visualization:** Table of provisioning without approval, bar chart by group, single value 'unapproved provisions (7d)'.
- **CIM Models:** Change, Authentication
- **Known false positives:** Break-glass adds via approved emergency-access workflow populate approved_at via a separate sourcetype — ensure the join covers both.
- **References:** [SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1098](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1098/)

---

### UC-22.12.37 · SOX-ITGC AccessMgmt.Termination — Deprovisioning SLA after HR termination event
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Security
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1078
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** The most common SOX finding — ex-employees retaining access — becomes measurable hour-by-hour.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for Okta, Splunk Add-on for Workday
- **Data Sources:** `index=hr` sourcetype=workday:termination; `index=iam` sourcetype=okta:system_log (user.lifecycle.deactivate + group.user_membership.remove).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=hr sourcetype=workday:termination earliest=-7d
| stats latest(_time) AS term_date BY user
| join user type=outer
  [search index=iam (eventType=user.lifecycle.deactivate OR eventType=group.user_membership.remove) earliest=-7d
   | stats max(_time) AS last_access_revoked BY user]
| eval elapsed_hours=round((last_access_revoked-term_date)/3600,1)
| eval sla_met=if(elapsed_hours<=24 AND isnotnull(last_access_revoked),1,0)
| where sla_met=0
| table user term_date last_access_revoked elapsed_hours sla_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Route Workday terminations to Splunk; (2) route Okta lifecycle events; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) sla_met=0 pages the IAM team; (5) monthly CFO report.
- **Visualization:** Timechart of SLA adherence %, table of breaches, single value 'open SLA breaches'.
- **CIM Models:** Authentication, Change
- **Known false positives:** Contractor terminations with delayed HR events may produce a false breach — backfill term_date from contract_end_date in vendor_register.csv.
- **References:** [SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1078](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1078/)

---

### UC-22.12.38 · SOX-ITGC ChangeMgmt.Testing — Financial-system change test-evidence completeness
- **Criticality:** 🟠 High
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Converts a common sampling control into full-coverage evidence, reducing audit sampling and cost.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=change` sourcetype=servicenow:change; test evidence sub-table sourcetype=servicenow:change_task (type=UAT).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change state=implemented earliest=-7d
| lookup financial_systems_apps.csv system OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="yes"
| stats latest(_time) AS deployed_at latest(change_id) AS change_id BY change_id system
| join type=outer change_id
  [search index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change_task task_type=uat state=closed_complete earliest=-60d
   | stats latest(assigned_to) AS tester count AS uat_tasks BY change_id]
| eval test_evidence_present=if(uat_tasks>=1,1,0)
| where test_evidence_present=0
| table change_id system tester deployed_at test_evidence_present
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain financial_systems_apps.csv; (2) configure ServiceNow to require a UAT subtask; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) test_evidence_present=0 triggers a deficiency record.
- **Visualization:** Table of missing-test changes, bar chart by system, single value 'missing test evidence'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Automated changes (configuration as code) may use automated test jobs rather than UAT tasks — accept sourcetype=cicd:tests result=pass as equivalent evidence.
- **References:** [SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf)

---

### UC-22.12.39 · SOX-ITGC ChangeMgmt.Approval — Segregation of duties in financial-system change approval
- **Criticality:** 🔴 Critical
- **Difficulty:** 🟠 Advanced
- **Monitoring type:** Security, Compliance
- **MITRE ATT&CK:** T1098
- **Splunk Pillar:** Security
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, PCI-DSS, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Eliminates the two patterns that SOX auditors most frequently cite. The daily report is the control evidence.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Add-on for ServiceNow
- **Data Sources:** `index=change` sourcetype=servicenow:change (workflow events).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=change sourcetype=servicenow:change earliest=-7d
| stats latest(implementer) AS implementer latest(approver) AS approver latest(approval_date) AS approved_at latest(state_date) AS implemented_at BY change_id system
| lookup financial_systems_apps.csv system OUTPUT in_scope
| where in_scope="yes"
| eval sod_violation=if(implementer=approver,1,0)
| eval approval_before_implementation=if(approved_at<=implemented_at,1,0)
| where sod_violation=1 OR approval_before_implementation=0
| table change_id system implementer approver approved_at implemented_at sod_violation approval_before_implementation
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Maintain financial_systems_apps.csv; (2) capture ServiceNow workflow events; (3) schedule UC hourly; (4) violations open an immediate deficiency; (5) monthly CFO report.
- **Visualization:** Table of violations, bar chart by system, single value 'SoD + post-implementation approvals'.
- **CIM Models:** Change
- **Known false positives:** Standard-pre-approved changes legitimately have matching implementer/approver — filter on type!='standard'.
- **References:** [SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001), [PCI-DSS v4.0](https://listings.pcisecuritystandards.org/documents/PCI-DSS-v4_0.pdf), [MITRE ATT&CK — T1098](https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1098/)

---

### UC-22.12.40 · SOX-ITGC Operations.JobSchedule — Batch-schedule monitoring: financial-job exception visibility
- **Criticality:** 🟡 Medium
- **Difficulty:** 🔵 Intermediate
- **Monitoring type:** Compliance, Availability
- **Splunk Pillar:** IT Operations
- **Regulations:** ISO/IEC 27001, SOC-2, SOX-ITGC
- **Value:** Makes the typically-spreadsheet-based batch monitoring queryable — Ops has a live queue, auditors have a history.
- **App/TA:** Splunk Enterprise Security
- **Data Sources:** `index=batch` sourcetype=autosys:event OR sourcetype=controlm:job; operator ack `index=ticketing` sourcetype=servicenow:incident (category=batch-ack).
- **SPL:**
```spl
index=batch sourcetype IN (autosys:event,controlm:job) earliest=-30m
| lookup financial_batch_jobs.csv job_id OUTPUT in_scope expected_window
| where in_scope="yes"
| stats latest(state) AS state latest(_time) AS run_at BY job_id
| where state IN ("FAILED","LATE","WARNING")
| join type=outer job_id
  [search index=ticketing sourcetype=servicenow:incident category=batch-ack earliest=-24h
   | stats latest(assigned_to) AS ack_operator latest(_time) AS ack_at BY job_id]
| eval ack_met=if(isnotnull(ack_operator) AND ack_at>=run_at AND ack_at<=run_at+4*3600,1,0)
| where ack_met=0
| table job_id run_at state ack_operator ack_at ack_met
```
- **Implementation:** (1) Route Autosys/Control-M events; (2) maintain financial_batch_jobs.csv; (3) schedule UC every 30m; (4) unacked exceptions open an incident; (5) monthly CFO + Head of IT Ops review.
- **Visualization:** Table of unacked exceptions, time chart of exception rate, single value 'open batch exceptions'.
- **CIM Models:** Performance, Alerts
- **Known false positives:** Jobs whose expected_window is currently paused (month-end blackout) should be excluded via the lookup's active window.
- **References:** [SOX-ITGC PCAOB AS 2201](https://pcaobus.org/oversight/standards/auditing-standards/details/AS2201), [SOC-2 2017 TSC](https://www.aicpa-cima.com/resources/landing/system-and-organization-controls-soc-suite-of-services), [ISO/IEC 27001 2022](https://www.iso.org/standard/27001)

---

<!-- PHASE-2.3 END -->
